title.subtitle_en: Encyclical Letter of His Holiness Pope Leo XIV on Safeguarding the Human Person in the Time of Artificial Intelligence
title.subtitle_zh: 教宗良十四世論「在人工智能時代守護人性尊嚴」通諭
title.date_zh: 梵蒂岡，二○二六年五月十五日

blocks.en: Humanity, created by God in all its grandeur, is today facing a pivotal choice: either to construct a new Tower of Babel or to build the city in which God and humanity dwell together. Each generation inherits the task of shaping its own era, of guiding history to become a place where the dignity of every person is safeguarded, justice is promoted and fraternity is made possible. Yet every era also runs the risk of creating an inhumane and more unjust world. Whenever humanity is in danger of marring its true identity, we Christians lift our eyes to the Incarnate God, knowing that it is “only in the mystery of the Word made flesh that the mystery of humanity truly becomes clear.” [1] In Jesus Christ, this humanity in its grandeur becomes the Way, the Truth and the Life, opening the path for each of us to grow toward fullness.
blocks.zh: 天主在祂的全部壯麗中所創造的人類，今日正面臨一個關鍵的抉擇：是要興建一座新的「巴貝耳塔」，還是要建設一座天主與人共居的城。每一代都承繼着塑造自己時代的使命，引導歷史成為一個守護每個人的尊嚴、推動正義、實現兄弟情誼的場所。然而，每一個時代亦同樣面對危機，可能造就一個非人性、更為不義的世界。每當人性有失其真實身分的危險之際，我們基督徒便舉目仰望那降生成人的天主，深知「唯獨在那聖言成了血肉的奧蹟中，人類的奧蹟才真正得以彰顯」[1]。在耶穌基督內，這壯麗的人性成了道路、真理和生命，為我們每一個人開闢一條走向圓滿成全的道路。
blocks.html: 1. Humanity, created by God in all its grandeur, is today facing a pivotal choice: either to construct a new Tower of Babel or to build the city in which God and humanity dwell together. Each generation inherits the task of shaping its own era, of guiding history to become a place where the dignity of every person is safeguarded, justice is promoted and fraternity is made possible. Yet every era also runs the risk of creating an inhumane and more unjust world. Whenever humanity is in danger of marring its true identity, we Christians lift our eyes to the Incarnate God, knowing that it is “only in the mystery of the Word made flesh that the mystery of humanity truly becomes clear.” [1] In Jesus Christ, this humanity in its grandeur becomes the Way, the Truth and the Life, opening the path for each of us to grow toward fullness.

blocks.en: Founded on Christ, the living stone, we experience the powerful and mysterious action of the Holy Spirit, and we believe that every authentic human effort to cooperate with him for the good will be blessed by our heavenly Father, in whom we place our hope. For this reason, we can diligently contribute to every initiative that builds a more just world, and we can call others to collaborate in promoting the integral development of every human being. We wish to engage in dialogue with all men and women of our time, with whom we share in the events, questions and aspirations of humanity. [2] Together with them, we seek to identify new paths for the common good and for promoting a dignified life for all. Indeed, openness to dialogue is an integral part of the Church’s vocation because, constituted in Christ as “a sacrament… of communion with God and of the unity of the entire human race,” [3] she recognizes history as the place where the Gospel challenges and directs human experience.
blocks.zh: 我們以基督，那「活石」，為根基，體驗到聖神大能而奧妙的工作；我們深信，凡為善而與祂同工的一切真誠的人性努力，必蒙我們在天大父賜福——我們的希望，正寄於祂內。為此，我們可以不懈地為一切建設更公義世界的計劃作出貢獻，並可以呼籲他人共同推動每一個人的全人發展。我們願與當代所有的男女展開對話——我們與他們同處於人類的各種事件、提問與盼望之中[2]。我們同他們一起，尋求新的途徑，以促進公益和維護人人的尊嚴生活。誠然，對話的開放是教會聖召不可分割的一部分，因為教會在基督內被立為「與天主共融、並使全人類合一的聖事」[3]，故視歷史為福音向人類經驗發出挑戰並指引方向的場所。
blocks.html: 2. Founded on Christ, the living stone, we experience the powerful and mysterious action of the Holy Spirit, and we believe that every authentic human effort to cooperate with him for the good will be blessed by our heavenly Father, in whom we place our hope. For this reason, we can diligently contribute to every initiative that builds a more just world, and we can call others to collaborate in promoting the integral development of every human being. We wish to engage in dialogue with all men and women of our time, with whom we share in the events, questions and aspirations of humanity. [2] Together with them, we seek to identify new paths for the common good and for promoting a dignified life for all. Indeed, openness to dialogue is an integral part of the Church’s vocation because, constituted in Christ as “a sacrament… of communion with God and of the unity of the entire human race,” [3] she recognizes history as the place where the Gospel challenges and directs human experience.

blocks.en: In this spirit, Pope Leo XIII published his Encyclical Rerum Novarum in 1891, the 135 th anniversary of which we celebrate with deep gratitude this year. With that document, my beloved predecessor gave impetus to the reflection on society, the economy and politics, which is now known as the “Social Doctrine of the Church.” When some objected that the Church should not waste energy on worldly matters, but instead focus on communicating the message of eternal life, Leo XIII responded with realism and wisdom, saying that the proclamation of the Gospel cannot overlook the concrete lives of people. [4] Many decades have passed since then, and the Magisterium, pastors, theologians and faithful have continued to reflect on social issues in the light of the Gospel. Today, the Social Doctrine of the Church is a legacy of wisdom, where we find principles for thought, criteria for discernment and judgment, and concrete guidelines for action. Founded on Sacred Scripture and Tradition, and in engagement with the sciences, it helps us clearly interpret the challenges of the present and identify appropriate ways for living out a clear Christian witness, with joy and in service to the world. It is not an inert set of concepts, but a living corpus of truth that safeguards and interprets humanity’s vocation to a full and just life. I therefore wish to add my own voice to this living tradition, invoking the help of the Spirit of wisdom, who has dwelt in the world since its beginning (cf. Prov 8:22-31).
blocks.zh: 本着這份精神，教宗良十三世於一八九一年頒布了《新事》通諭——今年，我們以深切的感恩之心慶祝該通諭頒布一百三十五週年。藉着這份文獻，我這位敬愛的前任教宗推動了對社會、經濟與政治的反省，今日被稱為「教會社會訓導」。當時有人反對說，教會不應浪費精力於塵世事務，而應專注於宣講永生的訊息；對此良十三世以實事求是的智慧回應：宣講福音不能忽視人民的具體生活[4]。多少年代過去了，歷代訓導、牧者、神學家和信眾，繼續在福音的光照下省思社會問題。今日，教會社會訓導已成為一份智慧的傳承：我們在其中找到思考的原則、辨明與判斷的準則，以及行動的具體方針。它以聖經和聖傳為根基，並與各門科學對話，幫助我們清晰地解讀當代的挑戰，並指出與之相應的方式，去喜樂地、為服務世界而活出明確的基督徒見證。它並不是一套僵死的概念，而是一個有生命的真理體系，守護並闡釋人類獲享圓滿而正義生活的召叫。為此，我願將自己的聲音加入這個活的傳統，懇求那智慧之神的協助——祂自世界肇始以來，已居於世間（參箴 8:22-31）。
blocks.html: 3. In this spirit, Pope Leo XIII published his Encyclical Rerum Novarum in 1891, the 135 th anniversary of which we celebrate with deep gratitude this year. With that document, my beloved predecessor gave impetus to the reflection on society, the economy and politics, which is now known as the “Social Doctrine of the Church.” When some objected that the Church should not waste energy on worldly matters, but instead focus on communicating the message of eternal life, Leo XIII responded with realism and wisdom, saying that the proclamation of the Gospel cannot overlook the concrete lives of people. [4] Many decades have passed since then, and the Magisterium, pastors, theologians and faithful have continued to reflect on social issues in the light of the Gospel. Today, the Social Doctrine of the Church is a legacy of wisdom, where we find principles for thought, criteria for discernment and judgment, and concrete guidelines for action. Founded on Sacred Scripture and Tradition, and in engagement with the sciences, it helps us clearly interpret the challenges of the present and identify appropriate ways for living out a clear Christian witness, with joy and in service to the world. It is not an inert set of concepts, but a living corpus of truth that safeguards and interprets humanity’s vocation to a full and just life. I therefore wish to add my own voice to this living tradition, invoking the help of the Spirit of wisdom, who has dwelt in the world since its beginning (cf. Prov 8:22-31).

blocks.en: The res novae of our time
blocks.zh: 我們這時代的「新事」
blocks.html: The res novae of our time

blocks.en: While Leo XIII spoke in his time of “new things” ( rerum novarum), today we cannot limit ourselves simply to repeating his insightful teachings. Instead, we must ask God for the wisdom to interpret the great trends of our time, particularly technological advances. In recent years, it has become increasingly evident how rapidly and profoundly digitalization, artificial intelligence (AI) and robotics are transforming our world. Technology should not be considered, in itself, as a force antagonistic to humanity. On the contrary, it has formed part of our history since the beginning as “a profoundly human reality, linked to the autonomy and freedom of man.” [5] Over the centuries, technological development has significantly improved the living conditions of humanity. At the same time, each phase of progress has also revealed the ambiguity of tools that can cause harm when not oriented toward the good. Today, however, we find ourselves facing a new situation. The power and prevalence of emerging technologies are interwoven into the fabric of daily life, shaping decision-making processes and deeply affecting the collective imagination: “Never has humanity had such power over itself.” [6] New technologies open up a horizon extending in directions that are imaginable but not yet fully predictable. This complicates the assessment of their potential impact and the long-term effects they may have on both the dignity of individuals and the common good.
blocks.zh: 雖然良十三世在他的時代曾論及「新事」（rerum novarum），但我們今天不能僅僅滿足於重述他富有洞見的訓導。相反，我們必須祈求天主賜下智慧，俾能解讀我們這時代的重大趨勢，尤其是科技的進展。近年來，數碼化、人工智能（AI）和機械人技術，正以何等迅速而深刻的方式轉變我們的世界，已愈來愈顯而易見。科技本身不應被視為與人性對立的力量。相反，科技自人類歷史之初便是其一部分，是「一個與人的自主和自由密切相連的、深具人性的事實」[5]。多個世紀以來，科技的發展顯著改善了人類的生活條件。同時，每一階段的進步，也揭示了工具本身的雙重性——一旦不導向善，便能造成傷害。然而今日，我們發現自己面對一個新的處境。新興科技的力量與普遍性，已交織於日常生活的肌理之中，塑造着決策的過程，並深刻影響着集體的想像：「人類從未對自身擁有過如此巨大的力量」[6]。新科技打開的視域，雖可想像，卻尚未能完全預測。這使得評估其潛在影響和長期效應，無論對個人的尊嚴抑或公益，都變得錯綜複雜。
blocks.html: 4. While Leo XIII spoke in his time of “new things” ( rerum novarum), today we cannot limit ourselves simply to repeating his insightful teachings. Instead, we must ask God for the wisdom to interpret the great trends of our time, particularly technological advances. In recent years, it has become increasingly evident how rapidly and profoundly digitalization, artificial intelligence (AI) and robotics are transforming our world. Technology should not be considered, in itself, as a force antagonistic to humanity. On the contrary, it has formed part of our history since the beginning as “a profoundly human reality, linked to the autonomy and freedom of man.” [5] Over the centuries, technological development has significantly improved the living conditions of humanity. At the same time, each phase of progress has also revealed the ambiguity of tools that can cause harm when not oriented toward the good. Today, however, we find ourselves facing a new situation. The power and prevalence of emerging technologies are interwoven into the fabric of daily life, shaping decision-making processes and deeply affecting the collective imagination: “Never has humanity had such power over itself.” [6] New technologies open up a horizon extending in directions that are imaginable but not yet fully predictable. This complicates the assessment of their potential impact and the long-term effects they may have on both the dignity of individuals and the common good.

blocks.en: It now falls to us to face the challenges of our time with clarity of thought and responsibility. It is necessary to establish adequate regulatory tools capable of upholding justice and curbing the distorting effects of technological power. Nevertheless, the issue is not limited to regulation. As Pope Francis warned, we must realistically ask ourselves who holds this power today and how they use it: “It must also be recognized that nuclear energy, biotechnology, information technology, knowledge of our own DNA, and many other abilities which we have acquired… have given those with the knowledge, and especially the economic resources to use them, an impressive dominance over the whole of humanity and the entire world.” [7] In the past, it was largely up to the State to guide and direct innovation. Today, however, the main drivers of development are private, often transnational, parties that are endowed with resources and the capacity to intervene that surpass those of many Governments. Technological power thus takes on an unprecedented, predominantly “private” aspect, which makes it even more challenging to discern, govern and direct such power toward the common good.
blocks.zh: 如今擺在我們眼前的，是以清晰的思想和負責任的態度面對當代的挑戰。我們必須建立適當的監管工具，以維護正義並遏制科技力量所帶來的扭曲效應。然而，問題不能僅限於監管。正如教宗方濟各曾警告的，我們必須切實地問自己：今日的這份力量掌握在誰的手中？又是如何被使用？「我們亦必須承認，核能、生物科技、資訊科技、對人類自身 DNA 的認識，以及我們所取得的許多其他能力……已使那些擁有知識的人，特別是擁有經濟資源運用這些能力的人，對整個人類和整個世界享有令人驚嘆的支配。」[7] 在過往，引導和指揮創新的角色大致由國家承擔；而今天，發展的主要推動者卻是私人——通常是跨國的——團體，他們所擁有的資源和介入能力，已超越許多政府。因此，科技力量呈現出前所未有的、以「私人」性質為主的面貌，這使得辨明、管治和將其導向公益的工作更加困難。
blocks.html: 5. It now falls to us to face the challenges of our time with clarity of thought and responsibility. It is necessary to establish adequate regulatory tools capable of upholding justice and curbing the distorting effects of technological power. Nevertheless, the issue is not limited to regulation. As Pope Francis warned, we must realistically ask ourselves who holds this power today and how they use it: “It must also be recognized that nuclear energy, biotechnology, information technology, knowledge of our own DNA, and many other abilities which we have acquired… have given those with the knowledge, and especially the economic resources to use them, an impressive dominance over the whole of humanity and the entire world.” [7] In the past, it was largely up to the State to guide and direct innovation. Today, however, the main drivers of development are private, often transnational, parties that are endowed with resources and the capacity to intervene that surpass those of many Governments. Technological power thus takes on an unprecedented, predominantly “private” aspect, which makes it even more challenging to discern, govern and direct such power toward the common good.

blocks.en: For this reason it is necessary to begin a shared discernment process for identifying the spiritual and cultural roots of ongoing transformations. If we focus only on contingencies, we risk letting the succession of emergencies dictate the direction of our path. We are living through a rapid phase of transition, a “change of era,” in which — while some are vying for the future of new technologies and others dedicate themselves to reflecting on the matter — most people are watching and waiting, observing from afar and merely hoping for the best. For this very reason, crucial questions impose themselves on our conscience and can no longer be avoided: Where are we going? Toward what goal do we wish to orient ourselves? What direction should we choose as a people and as a human community?
blocks.zh: 為此，我們必須開展一個共同的辨明過程，以識別現今這場轉化在精神和文化上的根源。如果我們只專注於眼前的偶然事件，便冒險讓接踵而至的「緊急情況」決定我們的路向。我們正處於一個迅速過渡的階段，一個「時代的轉變」：在這當中，有人爭奪新科技的未來，有人致力於對此進行反省，而大多數人卻只是在觀望、等待，從遠處旁觀，僅僅希望事情能往好的方向發展。正因如此，那些攸關性的問題已加諸我們的良知之上，再不能迴避：我們要往哪裏去？我們希望將自己引向甚麼目標？作為一個民族、作為人類的群體，我們應選擇怎樣的方向？
blocks.html: 6. For this reason it is necessary to begin a shared discernment process for identifying the spiritual and cultural roots of ongoing transformations. If we focus only on contingencies, we risk letting the succession of emergencies dictate the direction of our path. We are living through a rapid phase of transition, a “change of era,” in which — while some are vying for the future of new technologies and others dedicate themselves to reflecting on the matter — most people are watching and waiting, observing from afar and merely hoping for the best. For this very reason, crucial questions impose themselves on our conscience and can no longer be avoided: Where are we going? Toward what goal do we wish to orient ourselves? What direction should we choose as a people and as a human community?

blocks.en: Two biblical images
blocks.zh: 兩幅聖經圖像
blocks.html: Two biblical images

blocks.en: In order to answer these questions and discern how to navigate responsibly the era of AI, I would like to bring to mind two scenes from the Bible: the construction of the Tower of Babel (cf. Gen 11:1-9) and the rebuilding of the walls of Jerusalem (cf. Neh 2–6). The story of Babel appears in the Book of Genesis, at the origins of humanity, immediately after the genealogies of Noah’s sons. After settling in a plain in the land of Shinar, the people decided to build a city and a tower “with its top in the heavens” (Gen 11:4). Fearing being scattered across the earth, they sought to guarantee stability and power for themselves, and above all to “make a name” for themselves. It was an impressive feat: a single language, a single technology, a single direction. However, the project concealed a profound danger. It was a project conceived without reference to God, supported by a uniformity that eliminated diversity and that chose homogenization over communion. When a city is built on pride and the claim to self-sufficiency, communication breaks down, languages are confused and people no longer understand each other. The result is not unity, but dispersion. Babel thus reveals the limits of any effort that, however grandiose, arises from self-affirmation, sacrifices human dignity for efficiency and aspires to reach heaven without God’s blessing.
blocks.zh: 為了回應這些問題，並辨明在人工智能時代如何負責任地航行，我願喚起聖經中兩個場景：建造巴貝耳塔的故事（參創 11:1-9）和重建耶路撒冷城牆的故事（參厄下 2-6）。巴貝耳的故事，出現在《創世紀》中，置於人類的起源，緊接諾厄諸子的族譜之後。當人們在史納爾地的平原定居後，便決定建造一座城和一座「塔頂摩天」的塔（創 11:4）。他們害怕分散到全地面上，便謀求為自己保證安定和力量，並尤其想為自己「立名」。這是一項令人印象深刻的事業：一種語言、一種科技、一個方向。然而，這個計劃隱藏着深刻的危險。它是一個沒有顧及天主的計劃，建基於一種抹殺差異的劃一性之上，以同質取代了共融。當一座城建在驕傲與自足的主張之上時，溝通便會崩潰，語言便會混亂，人與人便不再彼此理解。其結果並非合一，而是分散。巴貝耳由此揭示了一切自我中心的努力——無論多麼宏偉——的界限：以效率犧牲人的尊嚴，妄圖不憑天主的祝福而直達天庭。
blocks.html: 7. In order to answer these questions and discern how to navigate responsibly the era of AI, I would like to bring to mind two scenes from the Bible: the construction of the Tower of Babel (cf. Gen 11:1-9) and the rebuilding of the walls of Jerusalem (cf. Neh 2–6). The story of Babel appears in the Book of Genesis, at the origins of humanity, immediately after the genealogies of Noah’s sons. After settling in a plain in the land of Shinar, the people decided to build a city and a tower “with its top in the heavens” (Gen 11:4). Fearing being scattered across the earth, they sought to guarantee stability and power for themselves, and above all to “make a name” for themselves. It was an impressive feat: a single language, a single technology, a single direction. However, the project concealed a profound danger. It was a project conceived without reference to God, supported by a uniformity that eliminated diversity and that chose homogenization over communion. When a city is built on pride and the claim to self-sufficiency, communication breaks down, languages are confused and people no longer understand each other. The result is not unity, but dispersion. Babel thus reveals the limits of any effort that, however grandiose, arises from self-affirmation, sacrifices human dignity for efficiency and aspires to reach heaven without God’s blessing.

blocks.en: The Book of Nehemiah, in turn, opens at a time of great vulnerability in the history of ancient Israel. After the Babylonian exile, a portion of the people returned to Jerusalem, but the city was still in ruins, the walls collapsed and the gates burned (cf. Neh 1–2). Nehemiah, a Jew in the service of the Persian King Artaxerxes, received news of the disastrous state of his ancestral city. Before taking action, he fasted, prayed and interceded for the people. He then asked the king for permission to return to Jerusalem and, upon arriving, examined the destroyed areas in silence. He did not impose solutions from above. He convened the families, assigned each of them a section of the wall to rebuild, listened to their concerns, coordinated their efforts and addressed any opposition. The narrative shows how the city is reborn, not through the initiative of one man, but through the shared responsibility of all: men, women, priests, artisans, heads of households and young people all play a part. It is an undertaking with God at the center, which rebuilds relationships before rebuilding with stones. Thus, ancient Jerusalem rediscovers a common language — not one of uniformity, but one of communion, namely the harmony that arises when all persons assume their own role and recognize that their strength comes from the Lord.
blocks.zh: 《厄斯德拉下》一卷則於古代以色列歷史中一個極為脆弱的時刻開卷。經歷了巴比倫流亡之後，一部分子民返回耶路撒冷，但城仍是廢墟：城牆倒塌，城門被火燒毀（參厄下 1-2）。乃赫米雅，這位事奉波斯王阿塔薛西斯的猶太人，得到祖城淒慘景況的消息。在採取行動之前，他禁食、祈禱、為人民代禱。其後，他求王准許自己返回耶路撒冷；抵達後，他靜默地視察了被毀的地方。他並沒有自上而下強加方案，而是召集各家庭，分派他們每家一段城牆來重建，傾聽他們的憂慮，協調他們的努力，並處理一切反對的聲音。敘述中可見：這座城的重生，不是憑藉一個人的倡議，而是憑藉眾人共同的責任——男人、女人、司祭、工匠、家長和年輕人——人人都有自己的角色。這是一項以天主為中心的事業：先重建關係，然後才以石頭重建。如此，古耶路撒冷重新尋回一種共同的語言——並非劃一的語言，而是共融的語言：當每一個人承擔起自己的角色，並承認自己的力量來自上主時，所自然湧現的那份和諧。
blocks.html: 8. The Book of Nehemiah, in turn, opens at a time of great vulnerability in the history of ancient Israel. After the Babylonian exile, a portion of the people returned to Jerusalem, but the city was still in ruins, the walls collapsed and the gates burned (cf. Neh 1–2). Nehemiah, a Jew in the service of the Persian King Artaxerxes, received news of the disastrous state of his ancestral city. Before taking action, he fasted, prayed and interceded for the people. He then asked the king for permission to return to Jerusalem and, upon arriving, examined the destroyed areas in silence.  He did not impose solutions from above. He convened the families, assigned each of them a section of the wall to rebuild, listened to their concerns, coordinated their efforts and addressed any opposition. The narrative shows how the city is reborn, not through the initiative of one man, but through the shared responsibility of all: men, women, priests, artisans, heads of households and young people all play a part. It is an undertaking with God at the center, which rebuilds relationships before rebuilding with stones. Thus, ancient Jerusalem rediscovers a common language — not one of uniformity, but one of communion, namely the harmony that arises when all persons assume their own role and recognize that their strength comes from the Lord.

blocks.en: In light of these two images, the Holy Spirit challenges us today regarding our relationship with technology and the ongoing digital revolution. Scientific discoveries are talents entrusted to humanity so that they may bear fruit (cf. Mt 25:14-30). Technology has the power to heal, connect, educate and protect our common home; but it can also divide, exclude and generate new forms of injustice. In the abstract, technology in and of itself is not a solution to humanity’s problems, just as it is not inherently evil. In practice, however, technology is never neutral, because it takes on the characteristics of those who devise, finance, regulate and use it. Therefore, the primary choice is not between a “yes” or “no” to technology, but rather between constructing Babel or rebuilding Jerusalem; between a power that claims to dominate the heavens and a people who work together in the presence of God to rebuild the walls of fraternal coexistence.
blocks.zh: 在這兩幅圖像的光照下，聖神今日就我們與科技的關係，以及這場正在進行的數碼革命，向我們發出挑戰。科學的發現是託付給人類的「塔冷通」，為使它們結出果實（參瑪 25:14-30）。科技有能力醫治、聯繫、教育和保護我們的共同家園；但它也能分裂、排斥，並催生新形式的不義。在抽象層面上，科技本身既不是人類問題的答案，也不是本質上邪惡的；但實際上，科技從來不是中立的，因為它承載着那些設計、資助、規範和使用者的特質。因此，根本的選擇並不在於對科技說「是」還是「否」，而在於：是要建造巴貝耳，還是要重建耶路撒冷？是要那妄圖支配諸天的力量，還是那共同在天主面前同工、重建兄弟共處之牆的子民？
blocks.html: 9. In light of these two images, the Holy Spirit challenges us today regarding our relationship with technology and the ongoing digital revolution. Scientific discoveries are talents entrusted to humanity so that they may bear fruit (cf. Mt 25:14-30). Technology has the power to heal, connect, educate and protect our common home; but it can also divide, exclude and generate new forms of injustice. In the abstract, technology in and of itself is not a solution to humanity’s problems, just as it is not inherently evil. In practice, however, technology is never neutral, because it takes on the characteristics of those who devise, finance, regulate and use it. Therefore, the primary choice is not between a “yes” or “no” to technology, but rather between constructing Babel or rebuilding Jerusalem; between a power that claims to dominate the heavens and a people who work together in the presence of God to rebuild the walls of fraternal coexistence.

blocks.en: We must, then, avoid the “Babel syndrome,” namely the idolatry of profit that sacrifices the weak, a uniformity that neutralizes differences, and the pretense that a single language — even a digital one — can translate everything, including the mystery of the person, into data and performance. The risk of dehumanization — of building a future that excludes God and reduces the other to a means — is an ancient and ever-new temptation that today takes on a technical guise. Instead, let us choose the “way of Nehemiah,” which highlights the importance of working together to make the City of God a safe place for returning exiles. Rebuilding today means recognizing that, precisely from the plurality of voices and visions which, even though they sometimes remind us of the confusion caused by the diversity of spoken languages, a bright possibility emerges. Indeed, this is the possibility of building together, of transforming diversity into a resource and of making listening and dialogue the common ground upon which to cultivate justice and fraternity. Within this shared task, Christians discover their unique role of guiding actions toward God so that, in his light, pluralism does not dissipate into disorder, but instead, through the practice of synodality, it becomes the space in which humanity rediscovers its solid foundations and its final end. In the Book of Revelation, John sees the New Jerusalem “coming down out of heaven from God” (Rev 21:2) as a gift for all humanity. And this vision of grace is an invitation for us Christians to work together in order to foster a peaceful, just and dignified life in community within today’s “cities.”
blocks.zh: 因此，我們應避免「巴貝耳症候群」：即犧牲弱者的利潤崇拜、抹平差異的劃一性，以及那妄稱單一語言——即使是數碼語言——便可以將一切，包括人格的奧秘，化約為數據和表現的傲慢。非人性化的危機——建造一個排除天主、把他人化約為手段的未來——是一個古老而恆新的誘惑，它今日披上了科技的外衣。讓我們選擇「乃赫米雅之道」：強調共同合作，使天主之城成為流亡歸來者的安全居所。今日的重建，意味着承認：正是從那有時令我們想起語言混亂的多元聲音與視野之中，一個光明的可能性正在浮現——這是一同建設、把差異化為資源、以聆聽與對話為共同基礎以培育正義與兄弟情誼的可能。在這項共同的事業中，基督徒發現其獨特的角色：將行動導向天主，使多元在祂的光照下不致渙散為混亂，而是透過共議性的實踐，成為人類重新找到其堅實根基與終向的空間。在《默示錄》中，若望看見新耶路撒冷「從天上由天主那裏降下」（默 21:2），作為對全人類的恩賜。這恩寵的異象，是對我們基督徒的一個邀請：齊心合力，在今日的種種「城市」中，培育和平、正義、有尊嚴的社群生活。
blocks.html: 10. We must, then, avoid the “Babel syndrome,” namely the idolatry of profit that sacrifices the weak, a uniformity that neutralizes differences, and the pretense that a single language — even a digital one — can translate everything, including the mystery of the person, into data and performance. The risk of dehumanization — of building a future that excludes God and reduces the other to a means — is an ancient and ever-new temptation that today takes on a technical guise. Instead, let us choose the “way of Nehemiah,” which highlights the importance of working together to make the City of God a safe place for returning exiles. Rebuilding today means recognizing that, precisely from the plurality of voices and visions which, even though they sometimes remind us of the confusion caused by the diversity of spoken languages, a bright possibility emerges. Indeed, this is the possibility of building together, of transforming diversity into a resource and of making listening and dialogue the common ground upon which to cultivate justice and fraternity. Within this shared task, Christians discover their unique role of guiding actions toward God so that, in his light, pluralism does not dissipate into disorder, but instead, through the practice of synodality, it becomes the space in which humanity rediscovers its solid foundations and its final end. In the Book of Revelation, John sees the New Jerusalem “coming down out of heaven from God” (Rev 21:2) as a gift for all humanity. And this vision of grace is an invitation for us Christians to work together in order to foster a peaceful, just and dignified life in community within today’s “cities.”

blocks.en: Building for the common good
blocks.zh: 為公益而興建
blocks.html: Building for the common good

blocks.en: Building a city founded on the common good implies, first and foremost, building on a firm relationship with God. It means recognizing that the truth of his love calls us to life “in all its fullness” ( Jn 10:10) and communion with him. Like Saint Augustine, we too can say, “You have made us for yourself, O Lord, and our heart is restless until it rests in you.” [8] Indeed, God has inscribed in our hearts a desire for happiness that embraces all the dimensions of life. The Church, in dialogue with the men and women of our time, recognizes the urgent need to safeguard and guide this aspiration toward its deepest truth.
blocks.zh: 建立一個以公益為基礎的城，首先意味着建基於與天主穩固的關係之上。它意味着承認：祂愛的真理召叫我們進入「豐盛的」生命（若 10:10），並與祂共融。正如聖奧斯定所言，我們也可以說：「上主，禰造了我們是為了禰；我們的心如不安息於禰，便不會得到安寧。」[8] 確實，天主已將對幸福的渴求銘刻於我們心中——這份渴求擁抱生命的一切層面。教會在與當代男女的對話中，認識到我們有迫切的需要去守護並引導這份渴望，使它走向其至深的真理。
blocks.html: 11. Building a city founded on the common good implies, first and foremost, building on a firm relationship with God. It means recognizing that the truth of his love calls us to life “in all its fullness” ( Jn 10:10) and communion with him. Like Saint Augustine, we too can say, “You have made us for yourself, O Lord, and our heart is restless until it rests in you.” [8] Indeed, God has inscribed in our hearts a desire for happiness that embraces all the dimensions of life. The Church, in dialogue with the men and women of our time, recognizes the urgent need to safeguard and guide this aspiration toward its deepest truth.

blocks.en: Secondly, building for the common good means accepting the limits and weakness of humanity without considering them an error to be corrected. Today, the human desire for fullness of life is at risk of being misled by deceitful goals, such as the prospect of a technology that promises to free us from all weakness, and models of wellbeing that leave behind entire populations. All too often, we place our hope in unlimited “upgrades,” in forms of progress that exacerbate inequalities, and in immediate solutions incapable of healing people’s wounds. As a result, while some pursue the illusion of unlimited self-assertion, many are deprived of basic necessities. The Church reminds us, with a firm yet humble voice, that true fulfilment is not achieved by eliminating weakness but through harmonious growth. It is found where freedom and responsibility are intertwined with mutual care and true solidarity, and where progress is measured by the dignity of each person and the good of all peoples.
blocks.zh: 其次，為公益而興建意味着接納人性的限度和軟弱，不視之為需要修正的錯誤。今日，人對生命圓滿的渴求，正冒着被虛偽目標所誤導的危險：例如那承諾能將我們從一切軟弱中解脫的科技前景，或那將整批人口拋諸腦後的安適模式。我們常常把希望寄託於無止境的「升級」、加劇不平等的進步形式，以及那些無能醫治人傷痕的即時方案。其結果是：當有些人追逐着無限自我肯定的幻象時，許多人卻被剝奪了基本的所需。教會以堅定而謙卑的聲音提醒我們：真正的成全並非藉消除軟弱而達致，而是藉和諧的成長而獲得。它存於自由與責任，與相互關懷和真誠團結，交織之處；存於以每個人的尊嚴和諸民族的福祉為衡量的進步當中。
blocks.html: 12. Secondly, building for the common good means accepting the limits and weakness of humanity without considering them an error to be corrected. Today, the human desire for fullness of life is at risk of being misled by deceitful goals, such as the prospect of a technology that promises to free us from all weakness, and models of wellbeing that leave behind entire populations. All too often, we place our hope in unlimited “upgrades,” in forms of progress that exacerbate inequalities, and in immediate solutions incapable of healing people’s wounds. As a result, while some pursue the illusion of unlimited self-assertion, many are deprived of basic necessities. The Church reminds us, with a firm yet humble voice, that true fulfilment is not achieved by eliminating weakness but through harmonious growth. It is found where freedom and responsibility are intertwined with mutual care and true solidarity, and where progress is measured by the dignity of each person and the good of all peoples.

blocks.en: Thirdly, building a world in which everyone can flourish requires shared responsibility and courage. No one can single-handedly bear the weight of the challenges the world is facing, just as no one is so weak that they cannot play their part, for “power is made perfect in weakness” (2 Cor 12:9). All are given their own section of the wall: scientists and researchers, entrepreneurs and workers, educators and legislators, civil society, popular movements and faith communities. This is the logic of subsidiarity, which values the cooperation between generations, peoples, disciplines and cultures as the best way for fostering stability, prosperity and peace. We should not be intimidated by tensions or differences because they can become creative forces when guided by shared responsibility.
blocks.zh: 第三，建設一個人人皆能興盛的世界，需要共同的責任和勇氣。沒有任何一個人能獨自承擔世界所面對的這些挑戰的重量；同樣，沒有任何人軟弱到不能盡其一份力，因為「我的德能在軟弱中纔全顯出來」（格後 12:9）。每一個人都分得屬於自己的那段城牆：科學家與研究員、企業家與工人、教育家與立法者、公民社會、民間運動，以及信仰團體。這便是輔助性原則的邏輯——它珍視世代、民族、學科和文化之間的合作，視之為孕育安定、繁榮與和平的最佳途徑。我們不該被張力或差異所嚇倒，因為當這些張力與差異由共同的責任引導時，便能成為富創造力的力量。
blocks.html: 13. Thirdly, building a world in which everyone can flourish requires shared responsibility and courage. No one can single-handedly bear the weight of the challenges the world is facing, just as no one is so weak that they cannot play their part, for “power is made perfect in weakness” (2 Cor 12:9). All are given their own section of the wall: scientists and researchers, entrepreneurs and workers, educators and legislators, civil society, popular movements and faith communities. This is the logic of subsidiarity, which values the cooperation between generations, peoples, disciplines and cultures as the best way for fostering stability, prosperity and peace. We should not be intimidated by tensions or differences because they can become creative forces when guided by shared responsibility.

blocks.en: Finally, building for the common good requires an evangelical language. We must avoid humiliating or antagonistic words, opting rather for a clarity that sheds light and a frankness that unlocks new possibilities. We cannot condone naïve enthusiasms, nor fuel unfounded fears. Instead, let us establish standards for discernment — the dignity of the human person, the universal destination of goods, the preferential option for the poor, care for our common home and peace — and let us translate these standards into practices such as responsible planning, the assessment of human and social impact, the inclusion of the most vulnerable, the promotion of digital literacy and guiding research and industry toward justice and peace.
blocks.zh: 最後，為公益而興建需要一種福音性的語言。我們必須避免羞辱性或對抗性的言辭，而選擇能照亮的清晰與能開啟新可能的坦率。我們不能縱容天真的狂熱，也不能助長毫無根據的恐懼。讓我們確立辨明的準則——人格的尊嚴、財富的普世用途、優先選擇貧者、照顧我們共同家園、和平——並讓我們把這些準則化為實踐：負責任的規劃、人文與社會影響的評估、納入最脆弱者、推動數碼素養、引導研究和產業走向正義與和平。
blocks.html: 14. Finally, building for the common good requires an evangelical language. We must avoid humiliating or antagonistic words, opting rather for a clarity that sheds light and a frankness that unlocks new possibilities. We cannot condone naïve enthusiasms, nor fuel unfounded fears. Instead, let us establish standards for discernment — the dignity of the human person, the universal destination of goods, the preferential option for the poor, care for our common home and peace — and let us translate these standards into practices such as responsible planning, the assessment of human and social impact, the inclusion of the most vulnerable, the promotion of digital literacy and guiding research and industry toward justice and peace.

blocks.en: Remaining human
blocks.zh: 保持人性
blocks.html: Remaining human

blocks.en: In the recent Ordinary Jubilee Year of 2025, we walked as pilgrims of hope and were blessed with many graces. Strengthened by these gifts, we can move forward with confidence to face the arduous tasks and demanding challenges that lie ahead. In the era of artificial intelligence, when human dignity is threatened by new forms of dehumanization, ours is the pressing duty to remain profoundly human. We must lovingly safeguard the grandeur of humanity bestowed upon us and revealed in its fullness in Christ, the splendor of which no machine can ever replace. True progress always stems from a heart open to others, an intelligence willing to listen and a will that seeks what unites rather than what separates.
blocks.zh: 在剛過去的二○二五年常規禧年中，我們以「希望的朝聖者」的身分前行，並蒙獲眾多恩寵。藉這些恩賜的堅固，我們可以滿懷信心，前去面對橫亘前路的艱辛任務和苛嚴挑戰。在人工智能的年代，當人的尊嚴正被新形式的非人性化所威脅之際，我們的責任迫切：保持深刻的人性。我們必須以愛去守護那賜給我們、並在基督內以圓滿向我們啟示的人性壯麗——這份光輝，是任何機器都無從取代的。真正的進步常源自一顆向他人敞開的心，一種樂於聆聽的智慧，以及一份尋求那合一而非分裂之事物的意志。
blocks.html: 15. In the recent Ordinary Jubilee Year of 2025, we walked as pilgrims of hope and were blessed with many graces. Strengthened by these gifts, we can move forward with confidence to face the arduous tasks and demanding challenges that lie ahead. In the era of artificial intelligence, when human dignity is threatened by new forms of dehumanization, ours is the pressing duty to remain profoundly human. We must lovingly safeguard the grandeur of humanity bestowed upon us and revealed in its fullness in Christ, the splendor of which no machine can ever replace. True progress always stems from a heart open to others, an intelligence willing to listen and a will that seeks what unites rather than what separates.

blocks.en: I address this heartfelt appeal to all the Catholic faithful, to all Christians and to all men and women of goodwill. Let us not be afraid to get our hands dirty on the “construction site” of our time. Like Nehemiah, let us pray, plan wisely and work perseveringly, placing God at the forefront of our actions and the human person at the center of our choices. Thus, the “rejected stones” — the poor, the sick, the migrants and the least among us — will become the cornerstone, and a solid, welcoming common home will emerge on the earth, where love and faithfulness will finally meet, and righteousness and peace will embrace (cf. Ps 85:10). This is the blessing we implore from God; and the task that stands before us is that of being builders of communion, rather than architects of Babel. We are to be servants of the coming Kingdom, instead of lords of towers destined for ruin. With the heart of a shepherd and a father, I ask everyone to abandon the construction of yet another Tower of Babel and to join forces in building up the common good, so that humanity will never lose its beauty, and the world once again will come to recognize the human heart as the place where God desires to dwell.
blocks.zh: 我向所有公教信眾、所有基督徒、所有善心的男女，發出這份由衷的呼籲。讓我們不要害怕在這個時代的「工地」上弄髒雙手。讓我們效法乃赫米雅：祈禱、明智地計劃、堅毅地工作，把天主置於我們行動的首位，把人放在我們抉擇的中心。如此，那些「被棄置的石頭」——貧者、病者、移民、最微小者——將成為棟梁；一個堅實而好客的共同家園將在大地上湧現，在那裏「仁愛和忠信必彼此相迎，正義與和平必互相親吻」（參詠 85:10）。這便是我們向天主祈求的祝福；而擺在我們眼前的任務是：作共融的建造者，而非巴貝耳的設計師；作那即將來臨之神國的僕人，而非那注定傾倒之諸塔的主子。懷着一顆牧者與父親的心，我懇請每一個人：放下另一座巴貝耳塔的興建，攜手共同建設公益；如此，人類將永不失去其美麗，世界亦將再次認識到：人的心，正是天主願居住之地。
blocks.html: 16. I address this heartfelt appeal to all the Catholic faithful, to all Christians and to all men and women of goodwill. Let us not be afraid to get our hands dirty on the “construction site” of our time. Like Nehemiah, let us pray, plan wisely and work perseveringly, placing God at the forefront of our actions and the human person at the center of our choices. Thus, the “rejected stones” — the poor, the sick, the migrants and the least among us — will become the cornerstone, and a solid, welcoming common home will emerge on the earth, where love and faithfulness will finally meet, and righteousness and peace will embrace (cf. Ps 85:10). This is the blessing we implore from God; and the task that stands before us is that of being builders of communion, rather than architects of Babel. We are to be servants of the coming Kingdom, instead of lords of towers destined for ruin. With the heart of a shepherd and a father, I ask everyone to abandon the construction of yet another Tower of Babel and to join forces in building up the common good, so that humanity will never lose its beauty, and the world once again will come to recognize the human heart as the place where God desires to dwell.

sections.title_en: A DYNAMIC APPROACH FAITHFUL TO THE GOSPEL

blocks.en: In this first chapter, I intend to present synthetically how the Social Doctrine of the Church has taken shape in the recent Papal Magisterium and in the Second Vatican Council, in order to demonstrate its dynamic character. Indeed, in each era the res novae require that this teaching address historical questions in the light of revealed Truth. In this regard, artificial intelligence, too, should not be considered as merely yet another theme to be studied or a crisis to be managed, but rather as a development that challenges the categories of Social Doctrine from within, calling for their further development in fidelity to the Gospel.
blocks.zh: 在這第一章中，我打算綜合地闡述教會社會訓導如何在近代教宗訓導當局及梵蒂岡第二屆大公會議中逐步形成，以展示其動態的特質。事實上，在每一個時代，**res novae**（新事物）都要求這項訓導在啟示真理的光照下，回應歷史提出的問題。就此而言，人工智能也不應僅被視為又一個有待研究的主題，或一場有待處理的危機，而應被視為一種從內部挑戰社會訓導諸範疇的發展，要求我們在忠於福音的前提下，對這些範疇作出進一步的發展。
blocks.html: 17. In this first chapter, I intend to present synthetically how the Social Doctrine of the Church has taken shape in the recent Papal Magisterium and in the Second Vatican Council, in order to demonstrate its dynamic character. Indeed, in each era the res novae require that this teaching address historical questions in the light of revealed Truth. In this regard, artificial intelligence, too, should not be considered as merely yet another theme to be studied or a crisis to be managed, but rather as a development that challenges the categories of Social Doctrine from within, calling for their further development in fidelity to the Gospel.

blocks.en: This overview, however, would not be very comprehensible if, before reflecting on the contribution of individual popes and their most relevant documents, we do not first clarify some fundamental principles concerning the way in which the Church exists in history and relates to the world. Failing to do so would expose Social Doctrine to the risk of being perceived as an undue interference in “worldly” matters or as an external code of ethics imposed from above. In reality, it stems from a Church that walks alongside humanity, recognizing the autonomy of earthly realities and the distinction between ecclesial and political communities. Indeed, it is for this very reason that she strives to serve the common good.
blocks.zh: 然而，在我們反思歷任教宗個人及其最重要文件的貢獻之前，若不曾先闡明關於教會在歷史中存在並與世界建立關係的方式的若干基本原則，這番回顧便不太容易理解。若不如此，便會使教會社會訓導面臨被視為對「世俗」事務的不當干涉，或一套從上而下強加的外在倫理規範的風險。事實上，它源自一個與人類同行的教會，她承認現世現實的自主性，以及教會團體與政治團體之間的區別。的確，正是為此緣故，她竭力服務公益。
blocks.html: 18. This overview, however, would not be very comprehensible if, before reflecting on the contribution of individual popes and their most relevant documents, we do not first clarify some fundamental principles concerning the way in which the Church exists in history and relates to the world. Failing to do so would expose Social Doctrine to the risk of being perceived as an undue interference in “worldly” matters or as an external code of ethics imposed from above. In reality, it stems from a Church that walks alongside humanity, recognizing the autonomy of earthly realities and the distinction between ecclesial and political communities. Indeed, it is for this very reason that she strives to serve the common good.

blocks.en: A Church journeying through human history
blocks.zh: 與人類歷史同行的教會
blocks.html: A Church journeying through human history

blocks.en: The Church is present in the world as a sign of unity for the entire human family. She recognizes today’s questions and challenges as the current setting in which to carry out her particular vocation of listening, dialogue and service, and of being responsive to everything concerning the lives of contemporary men and women. This involvement in people’s lives helps the Church understand ever more clearly that her mission has a historical scope and entails a responsibility for the way in which social relations are built. For this reason, she cannot consider herself a stranger to the forces shaping society. On the contrary, the Church actively participates in the processes by which society grows and is organized, and she offers her own contribution to the creation of a more just and fraternal society. Pope Francis emphasized this historical dimension of the Church’s mission: “No one can demand that religion should be relegated to the inner sanctum of personal life, without influence on societal and national life, without concern for the soundness of civil institutions, without a right to offer an opinion on events affecting society.” [9]
blocks.zh: 教會臨在於世界，作為整個人類大家庭合一的標記。她體認到今日的種種疑問與挑戰，正是她履行其獨特聖召的當前環境——那便是聆聽、交談與服務，並對當代男女生活所涉及的一切作出回應。這種對人們生活的投入，幫助教會日益清晰地領悟到，她的使命具有歷史的幅度，並蘊含著對社會關係構建方式的責任。為此緣故，她不能自視為塑造社會各種力量的旁觀者。相反地，教會積極參與社會成長與組織的進程，並為創建一個更公義、更友愛的社會獻上自己的貢獻。教宗方濟各強調了教會使命的這一歷史幅度：「沒有人能要求宗教被貶抑至個人生活的內在聖所，對社會和國家生活不產生影響，不關心公民體制的健全，無權對影響社會的事件發表意見。」[9]
blocks.html: 19. The Church is present in the world as a sign of unity for the entire human family. She recognizes today’s questions and challenges as the current setting in which to carry out her particular vocation of listening, dialogue and service, and of being responsive to everything concerning the lives of contemporary men and women. This involvement in people’s lives helps the Church understand ever more clearly that her mission has a historical scope and entails a responsibility for the way in which social relations are built. For this reason, she cannot consider herself a stranger to the forces shaping society. On the contrary, the Church actively participates in the processes by which society grows and is organized, and she offers her own contribution to the creation of a more just and fraternal society. Pope Francis emphasized this historical dimension of the Church’s mission: “No one can demand that religion should be relegated to the inner sanctum of personal life, without influence on societal and national life, without concern for the soundness of civil institutions, without a right to offer an opinion on events affecting society.” [9]

blocks.en: The Church’s vocation and duty to accompany humanity in the specifics of history leads her to recognize that earthly realities possess their own proper character and order. The Second Vatican Council expressed this principle with particular precision in the Pastoral Constitution Gaudium et Spes, whose sixtieth anniversary we remembered and celebrated with gratitude on 7 December 2025: “If by the autonomy of earthly affairs is meant that created things and societies themselves enjoy their own laws and values… then the demand for autonomy is perfectly in order.” [10] This affirmation shows that creation bears the imprint of an original goodness that our human outlook must preserve, cultivate and bring to fulfilment. In this regard, the Church offers herself in a way that helps to interpret reality in all its depth. She supports with humble firmness the choices that promote the dignity of every person, the cohesion of communities and the good of all. The Church thus stands alongside the world without overpowering it, so that the promise of justice and peace that the Holy Spirit continues to sustain in the heart of humanity may come to fruition in every human endeavor.
blocks.zh: 教會在歷史的具體處境中陪伴人類的使命與責任，使她認識到現世事物具有其固有的特性和秩序。梵蒂岡第二屆大公會議在《論教會在現代世界》牧職憲章（*Gaudium et Spes*）中，以格外精確的方式表達了這一原則——我們懷著感恩之心，於2025年12月7日紀念並慶祝了該憲章頒布六十週年：「所謂現世事務的自主，如果是指受造物和社會團體本身享有其自身的規律和價值……那麼，要求這種自主是完全合理的。」[10] 這一肯定表明，創造帶有原始美善的印記，我們人類的目光必須保存、培養並使之達至圓滿。在這方面，教會以一種有助於深入詮釋現實的方式奉獻自己。她以謙遜而堅定的態度支持那些促進每一個人尊嚴、團體凝聚力及眾人福祉的抉擇。教會因此立於世界之旁而不凌駕其上，好使聖神持續在人性心中維繫的正義與和平的許諾，能在人類的每一項努力中結出果實。
blocks.html: 20. The Church’s vocation and duty to accompany humanity in the specifics of history leads her to recognize that earthly realities possess their own proper character and order. The Second Vatican Council expressed this principle with particular precision in the Pastoral Constitution Gaudium et Spes, whose sixtieth anniversary we remembered and celebrated with gratitude on 7 December 2025: “If by the autonomy of earthly affairs is meant that created things and societies themselves enjoy their own laws and values… then the demand for autonomy is perfectly in order.” [10] This affirmation shows that creation bears the imprint of an original goodness that our human outlook must preserve, cultivate and bring to fulfilment. In this regard, the Church offers herself in a way that helps to interpret reality in all its depth. She supports with humble firmness the choices that promote the dignity of every person, the cohesion of communities and the good of all. The Church thus stands alongside the world without overpowering it, so that the promise of justice and peace that the Holy Spirit continues to sustain in the heart of humanity may come to fruition in every human endeavor.

blocks.en: Recognizing that God upholds the freedom of men and women in the unfolding of history, the Second Vatican Council affirmed the distinction between the ecclesial community and the political community, emphasizing that each must operate with full autonomy. The Church’s presence in the world is also expressed through her relationship with civil society and public institutions. By engaging with these entities, the Church acknowledges the value of social and political realities and honors their specific responsibilities, supporting everything that fosters the wellbeing of individuals and strengthens the fabric of society. The Church does not claim to assume the functions belonging to the State. On the contrary, she esteems those who serve the common good, and she firmly acknowledges the responsibility that civil institutions hold within society. At the same time, the mission entrusted to the Church prompts her to address the real suffering of the men and women of our time. This closeness does not stem from an intent to supplant civil institutions, much less from an implicit criticism of their work. Rather, it stems from evangelical charity, which impels the Church to draw near to the wounds of humanity whenever they surface with greater severity. When the Church intervenes, she does so following the example of the Good Samaritan, with discretion and closeness, aware that what arises from urgent necessity cannot become the norm, nor replace the institutional responsibilities proper to the civil community.
blocks.zh: 梵蒂岡第二屆大公會議確認天主在歷史的演進中維護男女的自由，同時申明教會團體與政治團體之間的區別，強調兩者必須完全自主地運作。教會在世界的臨在，也透過她與公民社會及公共機構的關係表達出來。透過與這些實體的交往，教會承認社會和政治現實的價值，尊重它們各自的責任，並支持一切促進個人福祉及強化社會結構的事務。教會並不意圖承擔屬於國家的職能。相反地，她敬重那些為公益服務的人，並堅定地承認公民機構在社會中所肩負的責任。與此同時，託付給教會的使命催迫她回應我們這時代男女的實際苦難。這種關懷並非出於取代公民機構的意圖，更非隱含對其工作的批評。相反地，這是源於福音的愛德，這愛德驅使教會每當人類的創傷以更大嚴重性顯露時，便走近這些傷口。當教會介入時，她效法善牧的榜樣，以謹慎和關懷的態度行事，深知出於迫切需要的行動不能成為常規，也不能取代屬於公民團體的體制責任。
blocks.html: 21. Recognizing that God upholds the freedom of men and women in the unfolding of history, the Second Vatican Council affirmed the distinction between the ecclesial community and the political community, emphasizing that each must operate with full autonomy. The Church’s presence in the world is also expressed through her relationship with civil society and public institutions. By engaging with these entities, the Church acknowledges the value of social and political realities and honors their specific responsibilities, supporting everything that fosters the wellbeing of individuals and strengthens the fabric of society. The Church does not claim to assume the functions belonging to the State. On the contrary, she esteems those who serve the common good, and she firmly acknowledges the responsibility that civil institutions hold within society. At the same time, the mission entrusted to the Church prompts her to address the real suffering of the men and women of our time. This closeness does not stem from an intent to supplant civil institutions, much less from an implicit criticism of their work. Rather, it stems from evangelical charity, which impels the Church to draw near to the wounds of humanity whenever they surface with greater severity. When the Church intervenes, she does so following the example of the Good Samaritan, with discretion and closeness, aware that what arises from urgent necessity cannot become the norm, nor replace the institutional responsibilities proper to the civil community.

blocks.en: Starting from this twofold acknowledgment — the autonomy of earthly realities and the distinction between ecclesiastical and political spheres of competence — allows for a clearer understanding of the direction that the Second Vatican Council set for the Church in her relationship with the world. Gaudium et Spes reminds us that “it is the task of the whole People of God, particularly of its pastors and theologians, to listen to and distinguish the many voices of our times and to interpret them in the light of God’s word, in order that the revealed Truth may be more deeply penetrated, better understood and more suitably presented.” [11] Listening to the “many voices” is no mere sociological exercise, but instead requires spiritual discernment. Guided by the Spirit, the People of God come to recognize in cultural and social transformations both the signs of the presence of Christ, who comes and guides history toward its fulfilment, and those aberrations that obscure his face. In this way, the essential core of revealed Truth is not altered, but made explicit and adopted as a living standard for guiding concrete choices, inspiring paths of personal and communal conversion, promoting structural reforms and supporting new forms of evangelical witness in public life. History is thus understood as one of the places in which the Church allows herself to be taught by the Spirit about the humanizing power of the Gospel; and she learns to develop her own teaching at the service of the dignity of every person and the good of all peoples.
blocks.zh: 從這雙重承認出發——即現世現實的自主性，以及教會領域與政治領域職權範圍的區分——我們可以更清晰地理解梵蒂岡第二屆大公會議為教會所設定、在其與世界的關係中應循的方向。《論教會在現代世界》牧職憲章提醒我們：「全體天主子民——尤其是牧者與神學家——的任務，是聆聽並分辨我們時代的種種聲音，並在天主聖言的光照下加以詮釋，好使啟示的真理能更深入地為人所領悟、更妥善地為人所理解、更合宜地為人所呈現。」[11] 聆聽「種種聲音」絕非僅僅是一項社會學的操練，而是需要屬靈的分辨。在聖神的引導下，天主子民在文化與社會的轉變中，既能認出基督臨在的標記——祂正在來臨，並引導歷史走向其圓滿實現——也能辨別那些遮蔽祂面容的偏差。如此，啟示真理的核心本質並未被改變，反而得以顯明，並被採納為活生生的準則，用以引導具體的抉擇、激發個人與團體皈依的途徑、推動結構性的改革，並支持在公共生活中以福音作證的新形式。歷史因此被理解為教會容讓自己受教於聖神的場所之一，從中學習福音使人成聖的人性化力量；教會也在其中學習發展自己的訓導，以服務每一個人的尊嚴和所有民族的福祉。
blocks.html: 22. Starting from this twofold acknowledgment — the autonomy of earthly realities and the distinction between ecclesiastical and political spheres of competence — allows for a clearer understanding of the direction that the Second Vatican Council set for the Church in her relationship with the world. Gaudium et Spes reminds us that “it is the task of the whole People of God, particularly of its pastors and theologians, to listen to and distinguish the many voices of our times and to interpret them in the light of God’s word, in order that the revealed Truth may be more deeply penetrated, better understood and more suitably presented.” [11]  Listening to the “many voices” is no mere sociological exercise, but instead requires spiritual discernment. Guided by the Spirit, the People of God come to recognize in cultural and social transformations both the signs of the presence of Christ, who comes and guides history toward its fulfilment, and those aberrations that obscure his face. In this way, the essential core of revealed Truth is not altered, but made explicit and adopted as a living standard for guiding concrete choices, inspiring paths of personal and communal conversion, promoting structural reforms and supporting new forms of evangelical witness in public life. History is thus understood as one of the places in which the Church allows herself to be taught by the Spirit about the humanizing power of the Gospel; and she learns to develop her own teaching at the service of the dignity of every person and the good of all peoples.

blocks.en: The wisdom of the word of God in dialogue with the human sciences
blocks.zh: 與人文科學對話的天主聖言之智慧
blocks.html: The wisdom of the word of God in dialogue with the human sciences

blocks.en: The Church regards all who sincerely seek “truth, goodness and beauty” as companions on the journey, and considers them as “precious allies” [12] in defending the dignity of every person and in caring for creation. Adopting the pastoral approach of the Second Vatican Council, which invites us to listen, discern and interpret the signs of the times, and enlightened by the wisdom of the word, the Church is not afraid to encounter human knowledge. Indeed, the word of God provides reliable standards for establishing paths of justice and opening ways of reconciliation and peace among peoples. When it comes to applying these standards to the complex situations of our time, the contributions of philosophy and of the human and social sciences is essential. These disciplines help us understand and analyze cultural, economic and political dynamics more deeply. Saint John Paul II recalled that the Church welcomes the contributions of the social sciences in order “to draw from them concrete insights that help her carry out her magisterial office.” [13] A dialogue with such kinds of knowledge does not diminish the power of the Gospel. On the contrary, it makes it possible to identify with greater clarity what genuinely fosters the lives of individuals and communities. Following this perspective, Pope Francis emphasized that when dealing with many specific questions, the Church does not claim to offer “a definitive opinion,” [14] but recognizes the importance of listening to scientific research and of encouraging a serious and honest debate among experts while welcoming a diversity of opinions.
blocks.zh: 教會視所有真誠尋求「真、善、美」的人為旅途上的同伴，並將他們視為「寶貴的盟友」[12]，共同捍衛每一個人的尊嚴，關懷受造界。教會採納梵蒂岡第二屆大公會議的牧民方針——邀請我們聆聽、分辨和詮釋時代的徵兆，並在天主聖言的智慧光照下——毫不畏懼與人類知識相遇。事實上，天主的聖言提供了可靠的準則，以建立正義的道路，並在民族之間開闢和解與和平的途徑。當需要將這些準則應用於我們時代的複雜處境時，哲學以及人文和社會科學的貢獻是不可或缺的。這些學科幫助我們更深入地理解和分析文化、經濟和政治的動態。聖若望保祿二世曾提醒我們，教會接納社會科學的貢獻，是為了「從中汲取具體的洞見，以協助她履行其訓導職務。」[13] 與這些知識領域的對話，並不會減損福音的力量。相反地，它使我們能夠更清晰地辨識出什麼才是真正促進個人和團體生活的事物。依循此一觀點，教宗方濟各強調，在處理許多具體問題時，教會並不聲稱能提供「一個決定性的意見」[14]，而是承認聆聽科學研究、鼓勵專家之間進行嚴肅而誠實的辯論，並在接納多元意見的同時，均具有其重要性。
blocks.html: 23. The Church regards all who sincerely seek “truth, goodness and beauty” as companions on the journey, and considers them as “precious allies” [12] in defending the dignity of every person and in caring for creation. Adopting the pastoral approach of the Second Vatican Council, which invites us to listen, discern and interpret the signs of the times, and enlightened by the wisdom of the word, the Church is not afraid to encounter human knowledge. Indeed, the word of God provides reliable standards for establishing paths of justice and opening ways of reconciliation and peace among peoples. When it comes to applying these standards to the complex situations of our time, the contributions of philosophy and of the human and social sciences is essential. These disciplines help us understand and analyze cultural, economic and political dynamics more deeply.  Saint John Paul II recalled that the Church welcomes the contributions of the social sciences in order “to draw from them concrete insights that help her carry out her magisterial office.” [13] A dialogue with such kinds of knowledge does not diminish the power of the Gospel. On the contrary, it makes it possible to identify with greater clarity what genuinely fosters the lives of individuals and communities. Following this perspective, Pope Francis emphasized that when dealing with many specific questions, the Church does not claim to offer “a definitive opinion,” [14] but recognizes the importance of listening to scientific research and of encouraging a serious and honest debate among experts while welcoming a diversity of opinions.

blocks.en: Nourished by this fruitful dialogue between the Gospel and human knowledge, the Church has progressively developed her Social Doctrine, cultivating in history a wise patrimony marked by theological and anthropological coherence rooted in the Christian understanding of the person. Precisely because this patrimony arises from faith and a corresponding vision of reality, it does not amount to a repertoire of technical solutions or an economic or political model to be set against others. Instead, it belongs to a different order, [15] namely that of the principles that guide the interpretation of events and sustain an evangelical understanding of historical processes and the choices these entail. Herein lies the proper function of Social Doctrine, which does not claim to supplant the responsibilities of politics or institutions, but offers itself as a foundation for collective discernment, helping to recognize and promote whatever serves the dignity of persons, the vitality of communities and the common good.
blocks.zh: 在福音與人類知識之間這種豐碩對話的滋養下，教會逐步發展了她的社會訓導，在歷史中培育出一份睿智的遺產，其特色是建基於基督信仰對人的理解之上的神學與人學連貫性。正因為這份遺產源自信仰以及與之相應的現實觀，它並非一套技術解決方案彙編，也不是一個用來對抗其他模式的經濟或政治模式。相反地，它屬於另一個層次，[15] 即指導我們詮釋事件的原則層次，並支持我們以福音精神理解歷史進程及其所帶來的抉擇。這正是社會訓導的固有功能：它無意取代政治或制度的責任，而是提供一個集體分辨的基礎，幫助我們辨認並促進一切有助於人格尊嚴、團體活力與公益的事物。
blocks.html: 24. Nourished by this fruitful dialogue between the Gospel and human knowledge, the Church has progressively developed her Social Doctrine, cultivating in history a wise patrimony marked by theological and anthropological coherence rooted in the Christian understanding of the person. Precisely because this patrimony arises from faith and a corresponding vision of reality, it does not amount to a repertoire of technical solutions or an economic or political model to be set against others.  Instead, it belongs to a different order, [15] namely that of the principles that guide the interpretation of events and sustain an evangelical understanding of historical processes and the choices these entail. Herein lies the proper function of Social Doctrine, which does not claim to supplant the responsibilities of politics or institutions, but offers itself as a foundation for collective discernment, helping to recognize and promote whatever serves the dignity of persons, the vitality of communities and the common good.

blocks.en: Social Doctrine as a shared discernment
blocks.zh: 作為共同辨明的社會訓導
blocks.html: Social Doctrine as a shared discernment

blocks.en: Understanding that the truth is a gift to be shared, not a possession to be monopolized, frees the Church from the temptation of seeking forms of presence based on power. In order to rediscover the evangelical approach of a gentle proclamation of truth that is not imposed, Saint John Paul II invited us to examine honestly the times when acquiescence was given to “intolerance and even the use of violence in the service of truth.” [16] In this same vein, I too have reaffirmed that the Church “does not claim to possess a monopoly on truth,” [17] because truth is not a territory to be defended, but a good to be shared. For his part, Pope Francis expressed this same perspective in his striking phrase, “time is greater than space.” [18] What matters most is not occupying positions of power or defending cultural strongholds, but initiating good processes and enabling them to mature. In this way, the truth of the Gospel is not imposed from above, but grows over time within the concrete interweaving of lives, communities and cultures. This is not a truth that fears diversity, but instead welcomes and guides it. It does not eliminate conflicts, but transforms them, reuniting that which history tends to scatter. This concept can also be illustrated by the image of a multifaceted polyhedron, [19] in which the one truth of the Gospel is reflected from different angles.
blocks.zh: 明白真理是一份需要分享的禮物，而非一項需要壟斷的擁有物，這使教會免受誘惑，不去謀求那些立基於權力的臨在形式。為了重新發現那種溫柔宣講真理、而非強加於人的福音方式，聖若望保祿二世邀請我們誠實地省察那些曾屈服於「不寬容，甚至為真理服務而使用暴力」[16] 的時刻。循著同樣的精神，我也曾重申，教會「並不宣稱對真理擁有壟斷權」[17]，因為真理並非一片需要捍衛的領土，而是一份需要分享的美善。教宗方濟各則以他一句意味深長的話表達了這同一觀點：「時間大於空間。」[18] 最重要的不是佔據權力位置或捍衛文化堡壘，而是啟動良好的進程並使其日趨成熟。這樣，福音的真理並非自上而下強加於人，而是在具體交織的生活、團體和文化中隨著時間而成長。這並非一個畏懼多樣性的真理，相反地，它接納並引導多樣性。它不消除衝突，而是轉化衝突，將歷史趨向於分散的事物重新聚合起來。這個概念也可以用一個多面體的形象來說明[19]，在其中，福音的同一真理從不同的角度被反映出來。
blocks.html: 25. Understanding that the truth is a gift to be shared, not a possession to be monopolized, frees the Church from the temptation of seeking forms of presence based on power. In order to rediscover the evangelical approach of a gentle proclamation of truth that is not imposed, Saint John Paul II invited us to examine honestly the times when acquiescence was given to “intolerance and even the use of violence in the service of truth.” [16] In this same vein, I too have reaffirmed that the Church “does not claim to possess a monopoly on truth,” [17] because truth is not a territory to be defended, but a good to be shared. For his part, Pope Francis expressed this same perspective in his striking phrase, “time is greater than space.” [18] What matters most is not occupying positions of power or defending cultural strongholds, but initiating good processes and enabling them to mature.  In this way, the truth of the Gospel is not imposed from above, but grows over time within the concrete interweaving of lives, communities and cultures. This is not a truth that fears diversity, but instead welcomes and guides it. It does not eliminate conflicts, but transforms them, reuniting that which history tends to scatter. This concept can also be illustrated by the image of a multifaceted polyhedron, [19] in which the one truth of the Gospel is reflected from different angles.

blocks.en: This attitude of openness to truth, which is at the same time both one and diverse, profoundly expresses the catholicity of the Church, for she embraces the entire human family yet is also immersed in the concrete situations of peoples and cultures. The Second Vatican Council reminds us that, in virtue of this very catholicity, “each part contributes its own gifts to other parts and to the entire Church.” [20] In this way, the Church grows as a whole and as individual communities thanks to a mutual exchange and to shared efforts toward an ever fuller communion. It follows, then, that the People of God are not only gathered together from many peoples, but are also intertwined through different functions, vocations, cultures and traditions, each being called to support and enrich one another. From this perspective, Saint Paul VI acknowledged that, given the great variety of historical situations, it is unrealistic to think that the Church’s Social Doctrine can propose a single response that is valid in all contexts. [21] For this reason, he invited each Christian community to interpret the reality in its own country with clarity and responsibility. The fruitful tension between the universality of the Church’s mission and her local roots is an intrinsic aspect of her life, for she encompasses the whole world, while addressing the specific issues of each context as the real setting in which the Gospel takes shape.
blocks.zh: 這種同時既是一體又是多元的向真理開放的態度，深刻地體現了教會的大公性，因為她既懷抱整個人類大家庭，卻又置身於各民族與文化具體的境況之中。梵蒂岡第二屆大公會議提醒我們，正是憑藉這大公性，「每一部分都向其他部分及整個教會貢獻自己的禮物。」[20] 如此，教會透過彼此交流以及為邁向更圓滿的共融而共同努力，得以作為一個整體和作為個別團體而成長。由此可見，天主子民不僅由眾多民族聚集而成，更透過不同的職務、聖召、文化與傳統交織在一起，每一份子都蒙召去支持和豐富彼此。從這個角度來看，聖保祿六世承認，鑑於歷史處境的多樣性，若以為教會社會訓導能夠提出一個在所有環境中都適用的單一回應，那是不切實際的。[21] 為此，他邀請每一個基督徒團體，以清晰和責任心去解讀自己國家的現實。教會使命的普世性與地方根源之間富有成果的張力，是她生命的內在本質，因為她既涵蓋整個世界，同時又回應每一個處境中的具體問題，以之作為福音具體成形的真實場所。
blocks.html: 26. This attitude of openness to truth, which is at the same time both one and diverse, profoundly expresses the catholicity of the Church, for she embraces the entire human family yet is also immersed in the concrete situations of peoples and cultures. The Second Vatican Council reminds us that, in virtue of this very catholicity, “each part contributes its own gifts to other parts and to the entire Church.” [20] In this way, the Church grows as a whole and as individual communities thanks to a mutual exchange and to shared efforts toward an ever fuller communion. It follows, then, that the People of God are not only gathered together from many peoples, but are also intertwined through different functions, vocations, cultures and traditions, each being called to support and enrich one another. From this perspective, Saint Paul VI acknowledged that, given the great variety of historical situations, it is unrealistic to think that the Church’s Social Doctrine can propose a single response that is valid in all contexts. [21] For this reason, he invited each Christian community to interpret the reality in its own country with clarity and responsibility. The fruitful tension between the universality of the Church’s mission and her local roots is an intrinsic aspect of her life, for she encompasses the whole world, while addressing the specific issues of each context as the real setting in which the Gospel takes shape.

blocks.en: In light of what has been said so far, the Church’s Social Doctrine can be seen more authentically. It is not a handbook of principles and norms to be applied, but a process of shared discernment. It is born from the encounter between the eternal truth of the Gospel and the questions of history. It allows itself to be challenged by the signs of the times, and draws nourishment from the contributions of science, culture and human experience. Therefore, when the dignity of our brothers and sisters is violated, when politics fails to address the tragedies of humanity, when the economy turns against the person or science oversteps the limits of its competence, [22] the Church — together with other Christian denominations and believers of other religions — must make her voice heard, not in order to dominate, but to promote communion. Understood in this way, Social Doctrine becomes a theology of communion in history, a history in which the Word made flesh continues to be present through dialogue, memory and prophecy.
blocks.zh: 鑑於以上所述，我們可以更真切地理解教會的社會訓導。它不是一本供人應用的原則與規範手冊，而是一項共同分辨的進程。它誕生於福音的永恆真理與歷史的提問之間的相遇。它讓自己接受時代徵兆的挑戰，並從科學、文化及人類經驗的貢獻中汲取滋養。因此，當我們弟兄姊妹的尊嚴遭受侵犯時，當政治無法應對人類的悲劇時，當經濟反過來壓迫人，或科學逾越其權限時，[22] 教會——連同其他基督宗派及其他宗教的信徒——必須發出自己的聲音，不是為支配他人，而是為促進共融。如此理解之下，社會訓導便成為歷史中的一門共融神學，而在這段歷史中，聖言成了血肉通過交談、記憶與先知之言，持續臨在其中。
blocks.html: 27. In light of what has been said so far, the Church’s Social Doctrine can be seen more authentically. It is not a handbook of principles and norms to be applied, but a process of shared discernment. It is born from the encounter between the eternal truth of the Gospel and the questions of history. It allows itself to be challenged by the signs of the times, and draws nourishment from the contributions of science, culture and human experience. Therefore, when the dignity of our brothers and sisters is violated, when politics fails to address the tragedies of humanity, when the economy turns against the person or science oversteps the limits of its competence, [22] the Church — together with other Christian denominations and believers of other religions — must make her voice heard, not in order to dominate, but to promote communion. Understood in this way, Social Doctrine becomes a theology of communion in history, a history in which the Word made flesh continues to be present through dialogue, memory and prophecy.

blocks.en: The development of Social Doctrine from Leo XIII to the present
blocks.zh: 教會社會訓導自良十三世以來的發展
blocks.html: The development of Social Doctrine from Leo XIII to the present

blocks.en: Having outlined the way in which the Church is present in history and engages in dialogue with the world, I would now like to consider the development of Social Doctrine in the Magisterium, which has responded to the major social transformations from the nineteenth century to the present day. Naturally, I cannot do justice to the full richness of this teaching, whose fundamental principles are presented in the Compendium of the Social Doctrine of the Church and have been further examined by recent Magisterial teaching. Nor can I systematically explore everything that has been developed in the Encyclicals of my late venerable predecessors, especially in Laudato Si’and Fratelli Tutti. Nevertheless, I will emphasize some essential points in order to show how the present text stands in continuity with that tradition. I would also like to stress how, within this tradition, the unchanging core of revealed truths regarding the human person and society is constantly intertwined with a renewed capacity for listening to historical situations and for responding to contemporary issues. I will now review some of the significant stages of this development, beginning with the period inaugurated by the Encyclical Rerum Novarum.
blocks.zh: 在概述了教會如何在歷史中存在並與世界對話之後，我現在希望考察教會訓導中社會訓導的發展，這訓導回應了從十九世紀至今的各項重大社會轉變。當然，我無法完全展現這項教導的全部豐富內容；其基本原則已在《教會社會訓導彙編》中論述，並由近期的訓導文件進一步探討。我也無法系統地探討我可敬的前任教宗們在通諭中所闡述的一切，特別是在《願祢受讚頌》和《眾位弟兄》中的內容。儘管如此，我將強調若干要點，以顯示本文本如何與該傳統一脈相承。我也希望強調，在此傳統中，關乎人及社會的啟示真理的不變核心，是如何不斷地與一種更新的能力交織在一起的——這能力就是聆聽歷史處境，並回應當代議題。我現在將回顧這發展過程中的若干重要階段，從《新事》通諭所開啟的時期開始。
blocks.html: 28. Having outlined the way in which the Church is present in history and engages in dialogue with the world, I would now like to consider the development of Social Doctrine in the Magisterium, which has responded to the major social transformations from the nineteenth century to the present day. Naturally, I cannot do justice to the full richness of this teaching, whose fundamental principles are presented in the Compendium of the Social Doctrine of the Church and have been further examined by recent Magisterial teaching. Nor can I systematically explore everything that has been developed in the Encyclicals of my late venerable predecessors, especially in Laudato Si’and Fratelli Tutti. Nevertheless, I will emphasize some essential points in order to show how the present text stands in continuity with that tradition. I would also like to stress how, within this tradition, the unchanging core of revealed truths regarding the human person and society is constantly intertwined with a renewed capacity for listening to historical situations and for responding to contemporary issues. I will now review some of the significant stages of this development, beginning with the period inaugurated by the Encyclical Rerum Novarum.

blocks.en: The first stages of the Church’s Social Doctrine
blocks.zh: 教會社會訓導的最初階段
blocks.html: The first stages of the Church’s Social Doctrine

sections.title_en: FOUNDATIONS AND PRINCIPLES OF THE SOCIAL DOCTRINE OF THE CHURCH
sections.title_zh: 教會社會訓導的基礎與原則

blocks.en: The Social Doctrine of the Church is a living reality, in dialogue with history, cultures and sciences. At the same time, it enshrines a core set of unchanging truths. For this reason, it can be considered a form of wisdom that is capable of guiding the personal and societal lives of believers even today. In this second chapter, I would like to focus on some of the foundations and principles of the Church’s Social Doctrine that will help us to interpret the “new things” of our time, particularly in view of the inherent dignity of the human person. In order to protect the human person in the age of artificial intelligence, I believe that today we must once again reflect on the common good, the universal destination of goods, subsidiarity, solidarity and social justice. I am convinced that a harmonious relationship between these principles requires that they be considered collectively, so that it becomes clear how they relate to and complement each other.
blocks.zh: 教會社會訓導是一個活生生的現實，與歷史、文化和科學持續對話。與此同時，它蘊含著一套不可改變的核心真理。為此，它可以被視為一種智慧，即使在今日，也能夠引導信眾的個人生活和社會生活。在這第二章中，我想集中探討教會社會訓導的某些基礎和原則，它們將幫助我們詮釋我們時代的「新事物」，特別著眼於人位格與生俱來的尊嚴。為了在人工智能時代保護人位格，我認為今天我們必須再次省思公益、財物的普遍歸向、輔助原則、團結關懷，以及社會正義。我深信，這些原則之間的和諧關係，要求我們將它們集體地考量，好能清楚地看出它們如何互相關聯和彼此補足。
blocks.html: 46. The Social Doctrine of the Church is a living reality, in dialogue with history, cultures and sciences. At the same time, it enshrines a core set of unchanging truths. For this reason, it can be considered a form of wisdom that is capable of guiding the personal and societal lives of believers even today. In this second chapter, I would like to focus on some of the foundations and principles of the Church’s Social Doctrine that will help us to interpret the “new things” of our time, particularly in view of the inherent dignity of the human person. In order to protect the human person in the age of artificial intelligence, I believe that today we must once again reflect on the common good, the universal destination of goods, subsidiarity, solidarity and social justice. I am convinced that a harmonious relationship between these principles requires that they be considered collectively, so that it becomes clear how they relate to and complement each other.

blocks.en: In offering these reflections, my hope is, first and foremost, to help the lay faithful and people of goodwill rediscover their duty of implementing the above-mentioned principles in their daily lives, family relationships, work and involvement in society. Thus, they will let themselves be inspired by the aim of embodying God’s love in the concrete events of life. At the same time, I would like to encourage academic institutions and universities to give fresh impetus to these principles, and to apply them in a way that will be relevant and effective in addressing the digital revolution. In this way, theological and philosophical enquiry will be able to further explore and support the Church’s pastoral journey, and contribute to the Magisterium’s task of enlightening the consciences of the faithful and guiding their efforts to make the life of our societies more just and fraternal.
blocks.zh: 我提出這些反思，首先和最重要的希望是幫助平信徒和善心人士，重新發現他們在日常生活、家庭關係、工作和社會參與中，實踐上述原則的責任。這樣，他們將讓自己受到在生活的具體事件中體現天主聖愛這一目標所啟發。與此同時，我願鼓勵學術機構和大學，為這些原則注入新的動力，並以切合和有效的方式應用它們來應對數碼革命。如此，神學和哲學的探索將能進一步深化和支持教會的牧民旅程，並為教會訓導當局光照信友良心、引導他們努力使我們的社會生活更加公義和友愛的任務作出貢獻。
blocks.html: 47. In offering these reflections, my hope is, first and foremost, to help the lay faithful and people of goodwill rediscover their duty of implementing the above-mentioned principles in their daily lives, family relationships, work and involvement in society. Thus, they will let themselves be inspired by the aim of embodying God’s love in the concrete events of life. At the same time, I would like to encourage academic institutions and universities to give fresh impetus to these principles, and to apply them in a way that will be relevant and effective in addressing the digital revolution. In this way, theological and philosophical enquiry will be able to further explore and support the Church’s pastoral journey, and contribute to the Magisterium’s task of enlightening the consciences of the faithful and guiding their efforts to make the life of our societies more just and fraternal.

blocks.en: The foundations of Social Doctrine
blocks.zh: 社會訓導的基礎
blocks.html: The foundations of Social Doctrine

blocks.en: The human person: image of the Triune God
blocks.zh: 人類：三位一體天主的肖像
blocks.html: The human person: image of the Triune God

blocks.en: The Church’s Social Doctrine brings us to the very heart of our faith: the mystery of the living God, revealed in Jesus Christ, who, as a communion of Persons — Father, Son and Holy Spirit — is love itself in relationship, expressed in the mutual gift of self and in sharing with the world.
