[51] As the Council recalled, human persons are called to communion with God and “can fully discover their true selves only in sincere self-giving.” [52] Indeed their deepest vocation is to enter into the Trinitarian dynamic of love received and shared.
blocks.zh: 教會社會訓導將我們帶到信仰的核心：生活的天主的奧蹟，即在耶穌基督內啟示出來的天主，祂作為位格的共融——聖父、聖子及聖神——是關係中的愛本身，並在彼此的自我交付和與世界的分享中表達出來。[51] 正如大公會議所提醒的，人被召叫與天主共融，並且「只有在真誠的自我交付中，才能完全發現自己的真實自我。」[52] 事實上，人最深的聖召就是進入那接受並分享的愛——聖三的愛的動力之中。
blocks.html: 48. The Church’s Social Doctrine brings us to the very heart of our faith: the mystery of the living God, revealed in Jesus Christ, who, as a communion of Persons — Father, Son and Holy Spirit — is love itself in relationship, expressed in the mutual gift of self and in sharing with the world. [51] As the Council recalled, human persons are called to communion with God and “can fully discover their true selves only in sincere self-giving.” [52] Indeed their deepest vocation is to enter into the Trinitarian dynamic of love received and shared.

blocks.en: If the mystery of God as Love is the source of Social Doctrine, we see its most concrete expression in the face of Jesus Christ, the Incarnate Word. By becoming man, the Son of God enters our history and takes on human flesh, bringing with him the love that unites him to the Father and the Holy Spirit. In him, “the mystery of humanity truly becomes clear” [53] because his humanity is completely free, open to others, capable of building healthy and beautiful relationships and committed to the total gift of self. Those who believe in him are engaged in the great work of renewal that began with the mystery of his passion, death and resurrection, and they cooperate in building up the Kingdom of God, learning to embrace all men and women as brothers and sisters, children of one Father. In this way, both the proclamation of the Gospel and Christian life, guided by the action of the Holy Spirit, tend to bring about social consequences in the world. [54]
blocks.zh: 倘若天主是愛這奧蹟乃教會社會訓導的泉源，那麼我們便在耶穌基督——降生成人的聖言——的面容上，看到這奧蹟最具體的表述。天主子藉著成為人，進入我們的歷史，取了人的血肉，並將那使祂與聖父及聖神合一的爱帶給我們。在祂內，「人性的奧蹟得以真正彰顯」[53]，因為祂的人性是完全自由的、向他人開放的、能夠建立健康而美好的關係，並全然致力於自我的徹底交付。凡相信祂的人，都參與了那始於祂的苦難、死亡與復活奧蹟的偉大更新工程，並協力建設天主的國，學習接納所有男男女女為兄弟姊妹、同一天父的子女。這樣，無論是傳揚福音，抑或是受聖神行動所引導的基督徒生活，都傾向於在世間結出社會性的果實。[54]
blocks.html: 49. If the mystery of God as Love is the source of Social Doctrine, we see its most concrete expression in the face of Jesus Christ, the Incarnate Word. By becoming man, the Son of God enters our history and takes on human flesh, bringing with him the love that unites him to the Father and the Holy Spirit. In him, “the mystery of humanity truly becomes clear” [53] because his humanity is completely free, open to others, capable of building healthy and beautiful relationships and committed to the total gift of self. Those who believe in him are engaged in the great work of renewal that began with the mystery of his passion, death and resurrection, and they cooperate in building up the Kingdom of God, learning to embrace all men and women as brothers and sisters, children of one Father. In this way, both the proclamation of the Gospel and Christian life, guided by the action of the Holy Spirit, tend to bring about social consequences in the world. [54]

blocks.en: At the heart of the Christian understanding of the human person lies the great biblical affirmation that men and women are created in the image and likeness (cf. Gen 1:26-27) of the Triune God. Created for relationship, every human person is planned and willed by God to enter into communion with him, with others and with creation. Human dignity does not depend on a person’s abilities, wealth or position in life, nor on the right or wrong choices made; instead, it is a gift that precedes and transcends each person, endowed by God as an expression of his unfailing love. For this reason, the human person always remains the “way for the Church” [55] and the heart of every authentic path of integral human development. [56]
blocks.zh: 基督徒對人的理解的核心，在於聖經那偉大的肯定：男人和女人都是按照三位一體天主的肖像和模樣所造（參 Gen 1:26-27）。每一個人都為關係而受造，由天主所計劃、所願意，為進入與祂、與他人、與受造界的共融之中。人的尊嚴並不取決於一個人的能力、財富或社會地位，也不取決於他所做的是對或錯的選擇；相反，這是一份先於並超越每一個人的禮物，由天主所賜予，作為祂永恆不渝的愛的彰顯。為此，人永遠是「教會的道路」[55]，也是每一條通往整全人性發展的真實途徑的核心。[56]
blocks.html: 50. At the heart of the Christian understanding of the human person lies the great biblical affirmation that men and women are created in the image and likeness (cf. Gen 1:26-27) of the Triune God. Created for relationship, every human person is planned and willed by God to enter into communion with him, with others and with creation. Human dignity does not depend on a person’s abilities, wealth or position in life, nor on the right or wrong choices made; instead, it is a gift that precedes and transcends each person, endowed by God as an expression of his unfailing love. For this reason, the human person always remains the “way for the Church” [55] and the heart of every authentic path of integral human development. [56]

blocks.en: The equal dignity of all human beings
blocks.zh: 人人尊嚴平等
blocks.html: The equal dignity of all human beings

blocks.en: Saint John Paul II stated that, “this heightened sense of the dignity of the human person and of his or her uniqueness, and of the respect due to the journey of conscience, certainly represents one of the positive achievements of modern culture.” [57] This statement follows the line already laid out by the Second Vatican Council, which had noted a growing recognition of the sublime dignity of all persons, their superiority over material things and their universal and inviolable rights and duties. [58] It is important to ensure that this growth in appreciation of human dignity is not obscured by the pressure of new ideologies or very powerful interests in today’s world. Among these ideologies, I consider particularly insidious the one that suggests that every person must earn or justify his or her own worth, to the point of attributing greater value to those who are more efficient or effective. From this perspective, persons end up being reduced to a means of achieving results, a resource to be used and exploited, and are no longer recognized as a proper end in themselves who should never be instrumentalized. The value of persons, however, does not depend on what they achieve or produce. There are rights that apply to everyone simply by virtue of being human, and no human power can legitimately deny or arbitrarily limit them. [59]
blocks.zh: 聖若望保祿二世指出：「對人格尊嚴及其獨特性，以及對良心歷程應有之尊重的高度意識，確實是現代文化的正面成果之一。」[57] 這一陳述遵循了梵蒂岡第二屆大公會議早已鋪設的路線，該會議注意到，人們日益認識到所有人的崇高尊嚴、其超越物質事物的優越性，以及其普遍而不可侵犯的權利與義務。[58] 重要的是，要確保這種對人性尊嚴日益增進的賞識，不會被當今世界的新意識形態或極其強大的利益所掩蓋。在這些意識形態中，我認為尤其陰險的一種，是那種暗示每一個人必須賺取或證明自身價值的想法，甚至演變為賦予那些更有效率或效用的人更大的價值。從這個角度來看，人最終被貶低為一種達成結果的工具、一種可供使用和剝削的資源，而不再被承認為一個本身即是目的、絕不應被工具化的存在。然而，人的價值並不取決於他們所成就或生產的東西。有些權利是單純因著身為人而適用於每一個人的，任何人間權力都不能合法地否認或任意地限制這些權利。[59]
blocks.html: 51. Saint John Paul II stated that, “this heightened sense of the dignity of the human person and of his or her uniqueness, and of the respect due to the journey of conscience, certainly represents one of the positive achievements of modern culture.” [57] This statement follows the line already laid out by the Second Vatican Council, which had noted a growing recognition of the sublime dignity of all persons, their superiority over material things and their universal and inviolable rights and duties. [58] It is important to ensure that this growth in appreciation of human dignity is not obscured by the pressure of new ideologies or very powerful interests in today’s world. Among these ideologies, I consider particularly insidious the one that suggests that every person must earn or justify his or her own worth, to the point of attributing greater value to those who are more efficient or effective. From this perspective, persons end up being reduced to a means of achieving results, a resource to be used and exploited, and are no longer recognized as a proper end in themselves who should never be instrumentalized. The value of persons, however, does not depend on what they achieve or produce. There are rights that apply to everyone simply by virtue of being human, and no human power can legitimately deny or arbitrarily limit them. [59]

blocks.en: When we speak of dignity, we do not always use the word in the same way. Sometimes we refer to moral dignity, namely the way in which a person directs his or her choices and actions. At other times, we think of social dignity, which refers to a person’s living conditions and the concrete respect received from society. In other cases, we refer to existential dignity, meaning the way in which a person perceives his or her own worth and the value of life. These aspects of dignity can be enhanced or diminished. In addition to these notions, there is also the more profound and important level of ontological dignity. This is the dignity that belongs to every human being simply by virtue of existing, of having been willed, created and loved by God. [60] No sin, failure, humiliation or exclusion can diminish the profound value of a human life that God has willed and called into being. [61]
blocks.zh: 當我們談論尊嚴時，我們並非總是以同樣的方式使用這個詞。有時我們指的是道德尊嚴，即一個人引導其選擇和行動的方式。有時我們想到的是社會尊嚴，這關乎一個人的生活條件以及從社會獲得的具體尊重。還有其他情況，我們指的是存在性尊嚴，意指一個人對自身價值和生命意義的感知方式。這些不同層面的尊嚴可以增進或減損。除了這些概念之外，還有更深刻、更重要的本體尊嚴層次。這尊嚴屬於每一個人，僅僅因為他存在，因為他蒙天主所願、所造、所愛。[60] 沒有任何罪過、失敗、羞辱或排斥，能減損一個蒙天主所願並召叫進入存有的生命之深厚價值。[61]
blocks.html: 52. When we speak of dignity, we do not always use the word in the same way. Sometimes we refer to moral dignity, namely the way in which a person directs his or her choices and actions. At other times, we think of social dignity, which refers to a person’s living conditions and the concrete respect received from society. In other cases, we refer to existential dignity, meaning the way in which a person perceives his or her own worth and the value of life. These aspects of dignity can be enhanced or diminished. In addition to these notions, there is also the more profound and important level of ontological dignity. This is the dignity that belongs to every human being simply by virtue of existing, of having been willed, created and loved by God. [60] No sin, failure, humiliation or exclusion can diminish the profound value of a human life that God has willed and called into being. [61]

blocks.en: The fundamental dignity of each person, therefore, is neither acquired nor earned, nor does it need to be justified. The recent Declaration Dignitas Infinita offers a summary of the Church’s thinking on this subject: “Every human person possesses an infinite dignity, inalienably grounded in his or her very being, which prevails in and beyond every circumstance, state, or situation the person may ever encounter” [62] — in other words, always and without exception. The dignity of every human being can be described as infinite, as Saint John Paul II stated, [63] for two reasons: first, because the love of God, who calls us to friendship with him, is infinite; and second, his love is absolutely unconditional, in the sense that, even if we search endlessly, we will never find anything that can erase or deny it.
blocks.zh: 因此，每一個人的基本尊嚴，既非獲取而得，亦非憑功績賺得，更不需要為之辯護。最近的《無限尊嚴》宣言，為教會在這課題上的思想提供了一個撮要：「每一個人都擁有一種無限的尊嚴，不可剝奪地奠基於其自身的存有之上，這尊嚴在任何處境、狀態或情況中，無論其過去、現在或未來，都始終佔據主導地位」[62] —— 換言之，無時無刻，絕無例外。正如聖若望保祿二世所言，[63] 每個人的尊嚴可被形容為無限的，原因有二：首先，因為天主召叫我們與祂建立友誼的愛，是無限的；其次，祂的愛是絕對無條件的，意思是，即使我們無窮盡地尋找，也永遠找不到任何能夠抹去或否定這愛的事物。
blocks.html: 53. The fundamental dignity of each person, therefore, is neither acquired nor earned, nor does it need to be justified. The recent Declaration Dignitas Infinita offers a summary of the Church’s thinking on this subject: “Every human person possesses an infinite dignity, inalienably grounded in his or her very being, which prevails in and beyond every circumstance, state, or situation the person may ever encounter” [62] — in other words, always and without exception. The dignity of every human being can be described as infinite, as Saint John Paul II stated, [63] for two reasons: first, because the love of God, who calls us to friendship with him, is infinite; and second, his love is absolutely unconditional, in the sense that, even if we search endlessly, we will never find anything that can erase or deny it.

blocks.en: The supreme value of human rights
blocks.zh: 人權的至高價值
blocks.html: The supreme value of human rights

blocks.en: The Church gratefully acknowledges that “the movement toward the identification and proclamation of human rights is one of the most significant attempts to respond effectively to the inescapable demands of human dignity.” [64] In this regard, Saint John Paul II stated that the Universal Declaration of Human Rights, proclaimed by the United Nations on 10 December 1948, remains one of the highest expressions of the human conscience of our time. [65] It is “a milestone on the long and difficult path of the human race.” [66] For this reason, from the Christian perspective, human rights are not an external addition to the person, but an expression of intrinsic human dignity, which the international community is called to protect and promote.
blocks.zh: 教會深懷感恩地確認：「辨認和宣告人權的運動，是有效回應人性尊嚴無可迴避的要求的最重要嘗試之一。」[64] 就此，聖若望保祿二世指出，聯合國於一九四八年十二月十日頒布的《世界人權宣言》，至今仍是我們這時代人類良知的最高表達之一。[65] 它是「人類漫長而艱難旅程上的一座里程碑」。[66] 為此，從基督信仰的角度來看，人權並非外加於人身之上的附加物，而是人性固有尊嚴的表達，國際社會有責任予以保護和促進。
blocks.html: 54. The Church gratefully acknowledges that “the movement toward the identification and proclamation of human rights is one of the most significant attempts to respond effectively to the inescapable demands of human dignity.” [64]  In this regard, Saint John Paul II stated that the Universal Declaration of Human Rights, proclaimed by the United Nations on 10 December 1948, remains one of the highest expressions of the human conscience of our time. [65] It is “a milestone on the long and difficult path of the human race.” [66] For this reason, from the Christian perspective, human rights are not an external addition to the person, but an expression of intrinsic human dignity, which the international community is called to protect and promote.

blocks.en: Human rights are inviolable, since they are “inherent in the human person and in human dignity.” [67] Consequently, they are universal and inalienable. [68] Precisely because they are grounded in the common dignity of every man and woman, they have practical consequences and legal effects, for “it would be vain to proclaim human rights if, at the same time, everything were not done to ensure the duty of respecting them, respect by all, in all places and for all.” [69] Among these rights, the first is the right to life, from conception to its natural end, [70] without which it is impossible to exercise any other right. When this fundamental right is denied — as in the cases of induced abortion, killing of the innocent and euthanasia — we are faced with choices that the Church considers gravely wrong. [71]
blocks.zh: 人權是不可侵犯的，因為它們「內在於人的位格與人性尊嚴」。[67] 因此，人權具有普世性且不可剝奪。[68] 正因為人權建基於每一個男人和女人的共同尊嚴之上，它們具有實際的後果和法律效力，因為「倘若不同時盡一切努力確保所有人、在所有地方、為所有人都有責任尊重人權，那麼宣布人權便是徒勞的」。[69] 在這些權利當中，首要的是生命權，從受孕之初直到自然終結，[70] 沒有生命權，便不可能行使任何其他權利。當這項基本權利遭到否認時——例如蓄意墮胎、殺害無辜者和安樂死的情況——我們所面對的，是教會認為極其嚴重的錯誤選擇。[71]
blocks.html: 55. Human rights are inviolable, since they are “inherent in the human person and in human dignity.” [67] Consequently, they are universal and inalienable. [68] Precisely because they are grounded in the common dignity of every man and woman, they have practical consequences and legal effects, for “it would be vain to proclaim human rights if, at the same time, everything were not done to ensure the duty of respecting them, respect by all, in all places and for all.” [69] Among these rights, the first is the right to life, from conception to its natural end, [70] without which it is impossible to exercise any other right. When this fundamental right is denied — as in the cases of induced abortion, killing of the innocent and euthanasia — we are faced with choices that the Church considers gravely wrong. [71]

blocks.en: Looking at our own time, we cannot ignore the fact that the protection of human rights has been exposed to two particularly serious dangers. The first is that these rights are declared in a purely formal sense, while technological progress continues alongside covert or overt violations of human dignity. The second, which is in fact the root of the first, is the inability to recognize the foundation of their universality, since we have abandoned “the search for the solid foundations sustaining our decisions and our laws.” [72] Pope Francis urged us not to underestimate this last issue. He pointed out that when reason seriously examines human nature, it is capable of discovering values that apply to everyone, since they derive from human nature. If this task of inquiry were abandoned, it is conceivable that rights considered untouchable today might, in the future, end up being questioned or denied by those in power, perhaps after having obtained only an apparent consensus from populations that are frightened or manipulated. [73]
blocks.zh: 環顧我們自己的時代，我們不能忽略一個事實：人權的保障已暴露於兩個格外嚴重的危險之中。第一個危險是，這些權利僅以純粹形式上的方式被宣示，而技術進步卻伴隨著或明或暗地侵犯人性尊嚴的行為繼續前行。第二個危險——事實上是第一個危險的根源——即在於我們無法承認這些權利的普世性基礎，因為我們已放棄了「為支撐我們的決定和法律而尋求堅實基礎」的努力。[72] 教宗方濟各敦促我們不要低估這最後一個問題。他指出，當理性認真地審視人性時，它有能力發現那些適用於所有人的價值，因為這些價值源自人性本身。若這項探究的任務被放棄，那麼可以想見，那些今日被視為不可侵犯的權利，將來或許會遭到掌權者的質疑或否定，而在此之前，他們可能只是從受驚嚇或被操縱的民眾那裡取得了表面上的共識。[73]
blocks.html: 56. Looking at our own time, we cannot ignore the fact that the protection of human rights has been exposed to two particularly serious dangers. The first is that these rights are declared in a purely formal sense, while technological progress continues alongside covert or overt violations of human dignity. The second, which is in fact the root of the first, is the inability to recognize the foundation of their universality, since we have abandoned “the search for the solid foundations sustaining our decisions and our laws.” [72] Pope Francis urged us not to underestimate this last issue. He pointed out that when reason seriously examines human nature, it is capable of discovering values that apply to everyone, since they derive from human nature. If this task of inquiry were abandoned, it is conceivable that rights considered untouchable today might, in the future, end up being questioned or denied by those in power, perhaps after having obtained only an apparent consensus from populations that are frightened or manipulated. [73]

blocks.en: Along with a greater awareness of the value of every human person and their rights, recognition of minority rights has also grown. Yet, there is still a long way to go to ensure that the rights of a great many, namely women, are equally and genuinely guaranteed throughout the world. It is a fact that “doubly poor are those women who endure situations of exclusion, mistreatment and violence, since they are frequently less able to defend their rights.” [74] It is, therefore, not enough to state simply that men and women have equal dignity and rights; it is necessary that this be reflected in concrete decisions, such as in laws, access to employment, education, social and political responsibilities, and the way society listens to and values women’s contributions. As long as this gap persists, we cannot say that society truly and fully recognizes that women have the same dignity as men.
blocks.zh: 隨著對每一個人及其權利的價值的意識有所增長，對少數群體權利的承認也有所增長。然而，要確保為數眾多的人——即女性——的權利能在全世界獲得平等而真實的保障，仍有漫漫長路。事實上，「那些身處排斥、虐待和暴力境遇的婦女，由於她們往往更難捍衛自己的權利，是雙重貧窮的人。」[74] 因此，僅僅宣稱男女享有同等的尊嚴與權利是不夠的；這必須體現在具體的決定中，例如法律、就業機會、教育、社會與政治責任，以及社會如何聆聽並重視婦女的貢獻。只要這個差距持續存在，我們就不能說社會真正且充分地承認婦女享有與男性同等的尊嚴。
blocks.html: 57. Along with a greater awareness of the value of every human person and their rights, recognition of minority rights has also grown. Yet, there is still a long way to go to ensure that the rights of a great many, namely women, are equally and genuinely guaranteed throughout the world. It is a fact that “doubly poor are those women who endure situations of exclusion, mistreatment and violence, since they are frequently less able to defend their rights.” [74] It is, therefore, not enough to state simply that men and women have equal dignity and rights; it is necessary that this be reflected in concrete decisions, such as in laws, access to employment, education, social and political responsibilities, and the way society listens to and values women’s contributions. As long as this gap persists, we cannot say that society truly and fully recognizes that women have the same dignity as men.

blocks.en: It is individuals that matter, each and every person, together with their families. Social movements, communal ideologies and grand political proclamations in favor of a population are worthless unless they lead to the flourishing of persons — men and women — with their inalienable rights. Similarly, it is not enough to extol individual freedom or private enterprise if we then allow a multitude of people to continue living without decent work, protections or access to basic necessities.
blocks.zh: 重要的是每一個具體的人，每一個個體，以及他們的家庭。任何社會運動、集體意識形態，或為某個群體發出的堂皇政治宣言，若不能導致每一個人——男人和女人——連同其不可剝奪的權利得以茁壯成長，便毫無價值。同樣地，若我們一味頌揚個人自由或私營企業，卻任由無數的人繼續在沒有體面工作、缺乏保障或無法獲得基本生活所需的情況下過活，那也是不足夠的。
blocks.html: 58. It is individuals that matter, each and every person, together with their families. Social movements, communal ideologies and grand political proclamations in favor of a population are worthless unless they lead to the flourishing of persons — men and women — with their inalienable rights. Similarly, it is not enough to extol individual freedom or private enterprise if we then allow a multitude of people to continue living without decent work, protections or access to basic necessities.

blocks.en: The principles of Social Doctrine
blocks.zh: 社會訓導的原則
blocks.html: The principles of Social Doctrine

blocks.en: The principle of the common good
blocks.zh: 公益原則
blocks.html: The principle of the common good

blocks.en: Recognizing that every man and woman possesses an inalienable dignity, together with rights that no human power can betray or nullify, requires us to shape the way we live together, including our economic and political choices, and the makeup of our cities. From this arises the first major principle of Social Doctrine that I wish to highlight: the common good. We can describe it as the social expression of the dignity recognized in every person. When Benedict XVI referred to the non-negotiable values that the Church must always defend, he included among them “the promotion of the common good.” [75] For a Christian, going beyond the narrow confines of one’s own interests and committing oneself, within the limits of one’s ability, to the common good is a non-negotiable value, as is the promotion of life.
blocks.zh: 承認每一個男人和女人都擁有不可剝奪的尊嚴，以及任何人間權力都無法出賣或廢除的權利，要求我們塑造我們共同生活的方式，包括我們的經濟和政治選擇，以及我們城市的構成。由此產生了我希望強調的教會社會訓導的第一個主要原則：公益。我們可以將其描述為在每一個人身上所承認的尊嚴的社會表達。當本篤十六世提到教會必須始終捍衛的那些不容妥協的價值時，他將「促進公益」[75] 包括在其中。為一個基督徒而言，超越自己利益的狹隘界限，並在自身能力範圍內投身於公益，正如促進生命一樣，是一項不容妥協的價值。
blocks.html: 59. Recognizing that every man and woman possesses an inalienable dignity, together with rights that no human power can betray or nullify, requires us to shape the way we live together, including our economic and political choices, and the makeup of our cities. From this arises the first major principle of Social Doctrine that I wish to highlight: the common good. We can describe it as the social expression of the dignity recognized in every person. When Benedict XVI referred to the non-negotiable values that the Church must always defend, he included among them “the promotion of the common good.” [75] For a Christian, going beyond the narrow confines of one’s own interests and committing oneself, within the limits of one’s ability, to the common good is a non-negotiable value, as is the promotion of life.

blocks.en: The Second Vatican Council affirmed that the common good consists in “the sum total of social conditions which allow people, either as groups or as individuals, to reach their fulfillment more fully and more easily.” [76] This definition provides us with a valuable initial reference point, because the common good cannot be reduced to a mere list of conditions or institutions. It is not the sum total of individual benefits, nor the intersection of their particular interests; it is a greater good that belongs to everyone, and it can only be achieved, nurtured and protected by our collective efforts. We can say that social action reaches its fullness when it is directed toward this shared good, just as a person’s moral action finds its fulfillment in the choice of the true good. [77]
blocks.zh: 梵蒂岡第二屆大公會議確認，公益在於「社會生活的條件總和，這些條件能讓社團及個別成員，更充分、更方便地達至自身的圓滿成全」[76]。這個定義為我們提供了一個寶貴的初始參照點，因為公益不能被簡化為僅僅是一系列條件或制度的清單。它不是個人利益的總和，也不是各人特殊利益的交集；它是一個屬於所有人的更大之善，而且只能透過我們的集體努力才能實現、培育和維護。我們可以說，當社會行動導向這共享之善時，便達至其圓滿，正如一個人的道德行動在於選擇真正的善時，才獲得其成全一樣。[77]
blocks.html: 60. The Second Vatican Council affirmed that the common good consists in “the sum total of social conditions which allow people, either as groups or as individuals, to reach their fulfillment more fully and more easily.” [76] This definition provides us with a valuable initial reference point, because the common good cannot be reduced to a mere list of conditions or institutions. It is not the sum total of individual benefits, nor the intersection of their particular interests; it is a greater good that belongs to everyone, and it can only be achieved, nurtured and protected by our collective efforts. We can say that social action reaches its fullness when it is directed toward this shared good, just as a person’s moral action finds its fulfillment in the choice of the true good. [77]

blocks.en: In this sense, we can say that the whole is “greater than the sum of its parts” [78] and that, for this very reason, “the mere sum of individual interests is not capable of generating a better world for the whole human family.” [79] Indeed, it is an illusion to think that simply pursuing one’s own progress without caring for others is sufficient for contributing to the good of all. This view ignores the inherent and specific value of the common good, which is the result of an “interdependence” [80] that creates a network of social good that expands and has an impact on people. The common good is a “plus,” the result of interaction and mutual influence that connects various actions, initiatives, efforts and decisions. If we were to add up the individual goods, we could not explain the existence of this “plus” that transcends them and, at the same time, enriches them.
blocks.zh: 就此而言，我們可以說，整體「大於其各部分之總和」[78]，而且正因如此，「僅僅將個別利益相加，並不能為整個人類大家庭帶來一個更美好的世界。」[79] 事實上，以為只需追求自身的進步而無需關顧他人，便足以促進所有人的福祉，這只是一個幻象。這種觀點忽視了公益本身固有而獨特的價值。公益乃是源於一種「相互依存」[80]的關係，這種關係交織成一張不斷擴展並影響眾人的社會善之網絡。公益是一個「更多的東西」，是各種行動、倡議、努力與抉擇彼此連結、相互影響而產生的結果。如果我們只是把個別的利益相加，便無法解釋這個超越它們、同時又使它們更形豐富的「更多的東西」的存在。
blocks.html: 61. In this sense, we can say that the whole is “greater than the sum of its parts” [78] and that, for this very reason, “the mere sum of individual interests is not capable of generating a better world for the whole human family.” [79] Indeed, it is an illusion to think that simply pursuing one’s own progress without caring for others is sufficient for contributing to the good of all. This view ignores the inherent and specific value of the common good, which is the result of an “interdependence” [80] that creates a network of social good that expands and has an impact on people. The common good is a “plus,” the result of interaction and mutual influence that connects various actions, initiatives, efforts and decisions. If we were to add up the individual goods, we could not explain the existence of this “plus” that transcends them and, at the same time, enriches them.

blocks.en: It is the pursuit of the common good that gives life to a people, understood not as a mere collection of individuals, but as a living reality in which people learn to recognize that they themselves are interconnected and jointly responsible for the res publica. In this sense, every person contributes to the building up of one’s people through “a slow and arduous effort calling for a desire for integration and a willingness to achieve this through the growth of a peaceful and multifaceted culture of encounter.” [81] Working together for the common good means having a shared vision. It is clear that there are many ideological and practical differences among people, as well as differing interests and frequent disagreements, but that does not mean it is impossible to engage in dialogue to establish a set of basic agreements that enable the creation of a shared vision, upon which everyone can move forward together.
blocks.zh: 正是對公益的追求賦予一個民族生命——這裡所理解的民族，不僅僅是個體的集合，而是一個活生生的現實，在其中人們學會認識到他們彼此相連，並為公共事務共同承擔責任。就此而言，每一個人都透過「一項緩慢而艱巨的努力，這要求一種融合的渴望，以及透過一種和平而多元的相遇文化的成長來實現這一切的意願」[81]，為自己民族的建設作出貢獻。為公益而共同努力，意味著擁有一個共同的願景。顯然，人們之間存在著許多意識形態和實踐上的差異，以及不同的利益和頻繁的分歧，但這並不意味著不可能展開對話，以建立一套基本共識，從而能夠創造一個共同的願景，使眾人都能在此基礎上攜手前行。
blocks.html: 62. It is the pursuit of the common good that gives life to a people, understood not as a mere collection of individuals, but as a living reality in which people learn to recognize that they themselves are interconnected and jointly responsible for the res publica. In this sense, every person contributes to the building up of one’s people through “a slow and arduous effort calling for a desire for integration and a willingness to achieve this through the growth of a peaceful and multifaceted culture of encounter.” [81] Working together for the common good means having a shared vision. It is clear that there are many ideological and practical differences among people, as well as differing interests and frequent disagreements, but that does not mean it is impossible to engage in dialogue to establish a set of basic agreements that enable the creation of a shared vision, upon which everyone can move forward together.

blocks.en: It is the State’s responsibility to ensure cohesion, unity and the proper organization of civil society, so that the common good can be pursued with everyone’s contribution. In practical terms, this means that public authorities have the delicate duty to “harmonize the different sectoral interests with the requirements of justice,” [82] seeking a balance between individual interests and the common good, without leaving behind the most vulnerable. When politics abandons a long-term perspective and reduces itself to short-term calculations or sterile polarizations, then the language of the common good loses credibility, and, at the same time, social inequalities and divisions grow.
blocks.zh: 國家有責任確保公民社會的凝聚力、合一及妥善組織，使公益能在每個人的貢獻下得以實現。具體而言，這意味著公共當局肩負一項精細的職責，即「按正義的要求，協調各種行業利益」[82]，在個人利益與公益之間尋求平衡，不遺棄最脆弱的人。當政治放棄長遠視野，淪為短視的計算或徒然的兩極對立時，公益的語言便失去可信性，而與此同時，社會的不平等與分裂也隨之加劇。
blocks.html: 63. It is the State’s responsibility to ensure cohesion, unity and the proper organization of civil society, so that the common good can be pursued with everyone’s contribution. In practical terms, this means that public authorities have the delicate duty to “harmonize the different sectoral interests with the requirements of justice,” [82] seeking a balance between individual interests and the common good, without leaving behind the most vulnerable. When politics abandons a long-term perspective and reduces itself to short-term calculations or sterile polarizations, then the language of the common good loses credibility, and, at the same time, social inequalities and divisions grow.

blocks.en: This also applies to international politics. As the divide between nations widens, a mentality of confrontation and aggression begins to take hold, and the difficult path toward a more united and fraternal world suffers new and painful setbacks. In this context, speaking of a shared journey toward a more just development for the entire human family “sounds like madness.” [83] Yet we must not lose hope. I invite everyone to conceive of ways of cooperating and of more effective international institutions, capable of safeguarding the global common good without compromising the legitimate diversity of peoples and nations. Indeed, the promotion of the common good can never be separated from respect for the right of peoples to exist, to preserve their own identity and to contribute their unique qualities to the family of nations. [84] Moreover, any attempt or plan to eliminate or subjugate a nation is gravely immoral and therefore unacceptable.
blocks.zh: 這也適用於國際政治。隨著各國之間的鴻溝日益擴大，對抗與侵略的心態開始扎根，走向一個更加團結友愛的世界這條艱難道路，亦遭受了新的痛苦挫折。在此背景下，談論人類大家庭共同邁向更公正發展的歷程，「聽起來就像瘋話」。[83] 然而，我們絕不能失去希望。我邀請所有人構思合作的方式，並建立更有效的國際機構，使其能夠在維護各民族與國家合法多元性的同時，保障全球公益。事實上，促進公益永遠不能與尊重各民族生存的權利、保存自身身分的權利，以及為萬民大家庭貢獻其獨特稟賦的權利分割開來。[84] 再者，任何企圖或計劃消滅或征服一個民族的行為，都是嚴重不道德的，因此絕不可接受。
blocks.html: 64. This also applies to international politics. As the divide between nations widens, a mentality of confrontation and aggression begins to take hold, and the difficult path toward a more united and fraternal world suffers new and painful setbacks. In this context, speaking of a shared journey toward a more just development for the entire human family “sounds like madness.” [83] Yet we must not lose hope. I invite everyone to conceive of ways of cooperating and of more effective international institutions, capable of safeguarding the global common good without compromising the legitimate diversity of peoples and nations. Indeed, the promotion of the common good can never be separated from respect for the right of peoples to exist, to preserve their own identity and to contribute their unique qualities to the family of nations. [84] Moreover, any attempt or plan to eliminate or subjugate a nation is gravely immoral and therefore unacceptable.

blocks.en: The principle of the universal destination of goods
blocks.zh: 財富普世用途原則
blocks.html: The principle of the universal destination of goods

blocks.en: “Among the numerous implications of the common good, immediate significance is taken on by the principle of the universal destination of goods.” [85] First of all, this principle reminds us that the earth’s goods — soil, water, air and natural resources — are given by God to the entire human family to sustain the lives of all, and that every person has an inherent right to the use of such goods, both now and in the future. Saint John Paul II recalled that, “God gave the earth to the whole human race for the sustenance of all its members, without excluding or favoring anyone.” [86] Consequently, “it is not in accordance with God’s plan to use this gift in such a way that its benefits accrue solely to a select few.” [87] Today, we are called to recognize that this universal destination applies not only to material goods, but also to immaterial and cultural goods.
blocks.zh: 「在公益的眾多含義中，財貨的普遍歸向原則具有直接的意義。」[85] 首先，這項原則提醒我們，大地的財貨——土壤、水源、空氣和自然資源——是天主賜予整個人類大家庭，用以維持所有人生命的，每一個人無論是現在還是將來，都擁有使用這些財貨的固有權利。聖若望保祿二世曾提醒說：「天主將大地賜給整個人類，是為維持其所有成員的生活，不排除也不偏待任何人。」[86] 因此，「若以這樣的方式利用這份恩賜，使其利益僅歸於少數人，這並不符合天主的計劃。」[87] 今天，我們蒙召認識到，這種普遍歸向不僅適用於物質財貨，也適用於非物質和文化財貨。
blocks.html: 65. “Among the numerous implications of the common good, immediate significance is taken on by the principle of the universal destination of goods.” [85] First of all, this principle reminds us that the earth’s goods — soil, water, air and natural resources — are given by God to the entire human family to sustain the lives of all, and that every person has an inherent right to the use of such goods, both now and in the future. Saint John Paul II recalled that, “God gave the earth to the whole human race for the sustenance of all its members, without excluding or favoring anyone.” [86] Consequently, “it is not in accordance with God’s plan to use this gift in such a way that its benefits accrue solely to a select few.” [87] Today, we are called to recognize that this universal destination applies not only to material goods, but also to immaterial and cultural goods.

blocks.en: Certainly there is a right to private property, which has its own specific meaning and purpose, yet it is always subordinate to the universal destination of goods. According to John Paul II, this subordination is the golden rule of social conduct and the “first principle of the whole ethical and social order.” [88] In the Church’s tradition, property has been viewed as a means of protecting and managing goods so that they may better serve the common good. Since “the Christian tradition has never recognized the right to private property as absolute or inviolable,” [89] its social function must not be considered a mere theological opinion, but a doctrine of the Church, already present in Sacred Scripture and in the writings of the Church Fathers. For this reason, Pope Francis reminded us that solidarity, when lived out in its fullest sense, also means “to restore to the poor what belongs to them.” [90]
blocks.zh: 無疑，私有產權是存在的，且有其特定的意義與目的，然而它始終從屬於財貨的普遍歸宿。若望保祿二世指出，這種從屬關係是社會行為的黃金規律，也是「整個倫理與社會秩序的首要原則」。[88] 在教會的傳統中，財產向來被視為一種保護和管理財貨的工具，使之能更好地服務於公益。既然「基督信仰傳統從未承認私有產權是絕對或不可侵犯的」[89]，那麼其社會功能便不應被視為單純的神學見解，而是一項教會的教義，早已存在於聖經及教會聖師的著作中。為此，教宗方濟各提醒我們，當團結關懷以最圓滿的意義生活出來時，也意味著「將屬於窮人的歸還給他們」。[90]
blocks.html: 66. Certainly there is a right to private property, which has its own specific meaning and purpose, yet it is always subordinate to the universal destination of goods.  According to John Paul II, this subordination is the golden rule of social conduct and the “first principle of the whole ethical and social order.” [88] In the Church’s tradition, property has been viewed as a means of protecting and managing goods so that they may better serve the common good. Since “the Christian tradition has never recognized the right to private property as absolute or inviolable,” [89] its social function must not be considered a mere theological opinion, but a doctrine of the Church, already present in Sacred Scripture and in the writings of the Church Fathers. For this reason, Pope Francis reminded us that solidarity, when lived out in its fullest sense, also means “to restore to the poor what belongs to them.” [90]

blocks.en: Today, among the goods that are universally intended for everyone, we must also include new forms of property, such as patents, algorithms, digital platforms, technological infrastructure and data. In a context where the wealth of nations depends increasingly on knowledge and technology, when these goods remain concentrated in the hands of a few, without adequate forms of sharing and access, a new imbalance is created that contradicts the universal destination of goods. In turn, it widens the gap between the included and the excluded, between those who can participate in the digital revolution and those who remain on the margins. Furthermore, care for our common home and our responsibility toward the poor and future generations require that the use of the goods of creation and the new possibilities offered by technology be regulated in such a way as to respect the environment, avoid waste and prevent new forms of exploitation.
blocks.zh: 今日，在那些普遍為所有人而設的財物之中，我們也必須將新形式的財產納入其中，例如專利、演算法、數碼平台、技術基礎設施和數據。在國家的財富日益依賴知識與技術的背景下，當這些財物仍然集中在少數人手中，而沒有適當的分享和獲取途徑時，就會產生一種新的失衡，違背了財物的普遍歸屬。這反過來又擴大了被包容者與被排斥者之間、能夠參與數碼革命者與仍處於邊緣者之間的鴻溝。此外，對我們共同家園的關懷，以及我們對窮人和未來世代所肩負的責任，要求我們在使用受造界的財物及技術所提供的新可能性時，必須以尊重環境、避免浪費並防止新形式的剝削的方式加以規範。
blocks.html: 67. Today, among the goods that are universally intended for everyone, we must also include new forms of property, such as patents, algorithms, digital platforms, technological infrastructure and data. In a context where the wealth of nations depends increasingly on knowledge and technology, when these goods remain concentrated in the hands of a few, without adequate forms of sharing and access, a new imbalance is created that contradicts the universal destination of goods. In turn, it widens the gap between the included and the excluded, between those who can participate in the digital revolution and those who remain on the margins. Furthermore, care for our common home and our responsibility toward the poor and future generations require that the use of the goods of creation and the new possibilities offered by technology be regulated in such a way as to respect the environment, avoid waste and prevent new forms of exploitation.

blocks.en: The principle of subsidiarity
blocks.zh: 輔助原則
blocks.html: The principle of subsidiarity

blocks.en: The principle of subsidiarity stems from the very same understanding of the human person that has guided our reflection on dignity and the common good. If every woman and man is called to take ownership of his or her own life and to contribute to the formation of society, then social institutions must also respect and support this responsibility. The Social Doctrine of the Church refers to subsidiarity as the principle according to which the role of individuals, families, local communities and intermediary organizations should not be supplanted by higher-level authorities. Moreover, higher-level institutions must recognize, protect and promote the freedom and creativity of lower-level entities, coordinating their contributions so that they can cooperate effectively for the common good. [91]
blocks.zh: 輔助性原則源自對人的同一種理解——正是這種理解引導了我們對尊嚴與公益的反思。倘若每一個男女都蒙召去掌管自己的生命，並為社會的塑造作出貢獻，那麼社會制度也必須尊重並支持這份責任。教會社會訓導將輔助性原則界定為：個人、家庭、地方團體及中間組織的角色，不應被更高層級的權力當局所取代。不僅如此，更高層級的機構必須承認、保護和促進較低層級實體的自由與創造力，協調它們的貢獻，使之能有效地為公益而合作。[91]
blocks.html: 68. The principle of subsidiarity stems from the very same understanding of the human person that has guided our reflection on dignity and the common good. If every woman and man is called to take ownership of his or her own life and to contribute to the formation of society, then social institutions must also respect and support this responsibility. The Social Doctrine of the Church refers to subsidiarity as the principle according to which the role of individuals, families, local communities and intermediary organizations should not be supplanted by higher-level authorities. Moreover, higher-level institutions must recognize, protect and promote the freedom and creativity of lower-level entities, coordinating their contributions so that they can cooperate effectively for the common good. [91]

blocks.en: Starting with Leo XIII and the beginnings of modern social teaching, the Church has insisted that neither the individual nor the family should be subsumed by the State, but should be allowed to act freely, as far as possible, without harming the common good. [92] Saint John Paul II took up and developed this perspective, noting that the political community is at the service of civil society and that the State must protect the common good, intervening when necessary, but without permanently supplanting the responsibilities of intermediary organizations and social institutions. [93] Subsidiarity does not justify the State’s disengagement, but rather guides its actions. Indeed, public intervention is necessary precisely to enable all social actors to fulfill their mission without being stifled. It is the responsibility of the political community to create the conditions that allow individuals, families, associations and intermediary organizations to fulfil their mission in society, without being replaced or reduced to mere facilitators. [94]
blocks.zh: 從良十三世及現代社會訓導的萌芽時期開始，教會便堅持，個人或家庭都不應被國家所吞沒，而應在盡可能不損害公益的前提下，獲准自由地行事。[92] 聖若望保祿二世接納並發展了這一觀點，指出政治團體是為公民社會服務的，而國家必須保護公益，在必要時進行干預，但不得永久性地取代中介組織和社會機構的責任。[93] 輔助原則並不為國家的撤離提供理據，反而引導其行動。事實上，公共干預之所以必要，正是為了使所有社會行動者都能履行其使命，而不受壓制。政治團體有責任創造條件，讓個人、家庭、社團及中介組織能夠在社會中履行其使命，而不被取代或淪為純粹的促進者。[94]
blocks.html: 69. Starting with Leo XIII and the beginnings of modern social teaching, the Church has insisted that neither the individual nor the family should be subsumed by the State, but should be allowed to act freely, as far as possible, without harming the common good. [92] Saint John Paul II took up and developed this perspective, noting that the political community is at the service of civil society and that the State must protect the common good, intervening when necessary, but without permanently supplanting the responsibilities of intermediary organizations and social institutions. [93] Subsidiarity does not justify the State’s disengagement, but rather guides its actions. Indeed, public intervention is necessary precisely to enable all social actors to fulfill their mission without being stifled. It is the responsibility of the political community to create the conditions that allow individuals, families, associations and intermediary organizations to fulfil their mission in society, without being replaced or reduced to mere facilitators. [94]

blocks.en: This principle encourages us to move beyond any form of paternalistic or welfare-based management of societal life, but instead to promote a culture of shared responsibility in a State that values citizens’ initiative, and a civil society capable of forging bonds and mobilizing energies in the service of the common good. In accordance with the principle of subsidiarity, decisions are made at the closest level possible to the persons involved, thereby fostering community life and avoiding people being presented with decisions that have already been taken. In this way people can participate in the decision-making process. When families, associations, local communities, volunteer organizations and those in the so-called “third sector” are recognized and supported, social life becomes more accessible to people, services become more attuned to real needs, and solutions are more creative and respectful of the dignity of each person. [95]
blocks.zh: 這項原則鼓勵我們超越任何形式以家長式管理或福利為本的社會生活管理模式，轉而推動一種共同承擔責任的文化，在一個重視公民主動性的國家，以及一個能夠建立聯繫、動員力量以服務公益的公民社會中實現。按照輔助性原則，決策應在盡量接近當事人的層面上作出，從而促進團體生活，避免人們面對已事先決定的方案。如此一來，人們便能參與決策過程。當家庭、社團、地方社群、義工組織以及所謂「第三界別」的機構得到認可和支持時，社會生活就會變得更貼近人民，服務亦會更切合實際需要，而解決方案則會更具創意，並且更尊重每一個人的尊嚴。[95]
blocks.html: 70. This principle encourages us to move beyond any form of paternalistic or welfare-based management of societal life, but instead to promote a culture of shared responsibility in a State that values citizens’ initiative, and a civil society capable of forging bonds and mobilizing energies in the service of the common good. In accordance with the principle of subsidiarity, decisions are made at the closest level possible to the persons involved, thereby fostering community life and avoiding people being presented with decisions that have already been taken. In this way people can participate in the decision-making process. When families, associations, local communities, volunteer organizations and those in the so-called “third sector” are recognized and supported, social life becomes more accessible to people, services become more attuned to real needs, and solutions are more creative and respectful of the dignity of each person. [95]

blocks.en: The principle of subsidiarity applies especially in the context of the digital revolution. Here, the highest level is not the State, but rather major economic and technological actors that exercise de facto power over the conditions of everyday life. This level, which monopolizes expertise, data and decision-making authority, involves companies and platforms that define conditions for access, rules of visibility, forms of interaction, and even economic opportunities. The principle of subsidiarity requires that such processes not be imposed from above in an opaque and unilateral manner, but instead be directed toward the common good with transparency, accountability and meaningful forms of participation (including independent checks, transparency regarding algorithms, equitable access to data and avenues for recourse). [96]
blocks.zh: 輔助性原則尤其在數碼革命的脈絡中適用。在這裡，最高的層級並非國家，而是那些對日常生活狀況行使實際權力的大型經濟和科技行動者。這一層級壟斷了專業知識、數據和決策權力，涉及那些界定准入條件、可見度規則、互動形式，乃至經濟機會的公司和平台。輔助性原則要求，這類過程不得以不透明和單方面的方式自上而下強加於人，反而必須以透明度、問責制及有意義的參與形式（包括獨立檢查、演算法的透明度、公平的數據取用及申訴途徑）為指引，邁向公益。[96]
blocks.html: 71. The principle of subsidiarity applies especially in the context of the digital revolution. Here, the highest level is not the State, but rather major economic and technological actors that exercise de facto power over the conditions of everyday life. This level, which monopolizes expertise, data and decision-making authority, involves companies and platforms that define conditions for access, rules of visibility, forms of interaction, and even economic opportunities. The principle of subsidiarity requires that such processes not be imposed from above in an opaque and unilateral manner, but instead be directed toward the common good with transparency, accountability and meaningful forms of participation (including independent checks, transparency regarding algorithms, equitable access to data and avenues for recourse). [96]

blocks.en: In this context, States and transnational institutions are called to ensure fair rules and effective safeguards, so that local communities, intermediary organizations, schools, universities, religious institutions and associations have a voice and can contribute to the discernment of choices that affect people’s daily lives, such as employment, access to services, data management and digital environments. When it comes to decisions regarding economic flows and digital platforms, as well as the governance of data and algorithms, we cannot allow a handful of actors to dictate these processes on their own; instead, we must build forms of cooperation that respect the various levels of the global community and make them jointly responsible for the common good. [97]
blocks.zh: 在此背景下，各國和跨國機構蒙召制定公平的規則和有效的保障措施，使地方社群、中介組織、學校、大學、宗教機構和社團能夠發聲，並能夠協助分辨那些影響人民日常生活的選擇，例如就業、獲取服務的機會、數據管理和數碼環境。在關乎經濟流動和數碼平台的決策，以及數據和演算法的治理方面，我們不能讓少數參與者獨自支配這些過程；相反，我們必須建立各種合作形式，既尊重全球社群的不同層面，又使他們共同為公益承擔責任。[97]
blocks.html: 72. In this context, States and transnational institutions are called to ensure fair rules and effective safeguards, so that local communities, intermediary organizations, schools, universities, religious institutions and associations have a voice and can contribute to the discernment of choices that affect people’s daily lives, such as employment, access to services, data management and digital environments. When it comes to decisions regarding economic flows and digital platforms, as well as the governance of data and algorithms, we cannot allow a handful of actors to dictate these processes on their own; instead, we must build forms of cooperation that respect the various levels of the global community and make them jointly responsible for the common good. [97]

blocks.en: The principle of solidarity
blocks.zh: 團結原則
blocks.html: The principle of solidarity

blocks.en: Having considered the common good and subsidiarity, I would like to reflect on the principle of solidarity. This emerges from a vision of the human person generated by faith, namely that every human being is created in the image of God and is part of a network of relationships that bind him or her to others, to specific populations and to creation. Saint Paul VI observed that the obligations of solidarity, justice and charity are rooted in the human and supernatural fraternal bonds that unite individuals and populations. [98] Fraternity is not merely an aspiration of believers, but is a social and political reality to be embodied in communal choices and endeavors. Solidarity, then, is the concrete recognition that the future of each individual is connected to the future of all; indeed, “no one is saved alone.” [99] The close link between subsidiarity and solidarity thereby becomes evident. It is thus clear that there is an intimate link between subsidiarity and solidarity. When subsidiarity is not linked to solidarity, it ends up becoming merely the protection of particular interests; when solidarity is not supported by subsidiarity, it degenerates into a form of welfare that does not foster responsibility. [100] This interconnectedness also pertains to the responsibility of authentic participation. Solidarity is expressed when each person, both individually and collectively, takes part in the life of the community — by staying informed, engaging with others, making their voice heard and contributing to public decisions and choices — while also assuming real responsibility so that the common good is achieved through shared decision-making.
blocks.zh: 在思考過公益和輔助性原則之後，我願反思團結關懷的原則。這原則是從信仰所孕育的人觀中產生出來的，即每一個人都是按天主的肖像所造，並置身於一個關係網絡中，將他或她與他人、與特定的民族及與受造界聯繫在一起。聖保祿六世指出，團結關懷、正義和愛德的義務，植根於將個人和民族聯合在一起的人性與超性的兄弟情誼。[98] 兄弟情誼不僅是信友的願景，更是一個社會和政治的現實，必須體現在共同體的抉擇和努力中。因此，團結關懷就是具體地認識到，每一個人的未來都與所有人的未來息息相關；事實上，「沒有人能獨自得救。」[99] 輔助性原則與團結關懷之間的密切聯繫由此變得顯而易見。因此很清楚，輔助性原則與團結關懷之間存在著內在的聯繫。當輔助性原則不與團結關懷相連時，它最終只會成為對特殊利益的維護；而當團結關懷得不到輔助性原則的支持時，它便會退化為一種無法培養責任感的福利形式。[100] 這種相互關聯性也關係到真正參與的責任。當每一個人，無論是個人還是集體，都參與到團體生活中——透過了解情況、與他人互動、表達意見，並為公共決策和選擇作出貢獻——同時承擔起真正的責任，好使公益能透過共同的決策得以實現時，團結關懷便得以體現。
blocks.html: 73. Having considered the common good and subsidiarity, I would like to reflect on the principle of solidarity. This emerges from a vision of the human person generated by faith, namely that every human being is created in the image of God and is part of a network of relationships that bind him or her to others, to specific populations and to creation. Saint Paul VI observed that the obligations of solidarity, justice and charity are rooted in the human and supernatural fraternal bonds that unite individuals and populations. [98] Fraternity is not merely an aspiration of believers, but is a social and political reality to be embodied in communal choices and endeavors. Solidarity, then, is the concrete recognition that the future of each individual is connected to the future of all; indeed, “no one is saved alone.” [99] The close link between subsidiarity and solidarity thereby becomes evident. It is thus clear that there is an intimate link between subsidiarity and solidarity. When subsidiarity is not linked to solidarity, it ends up becoming merely the protection of particular interests; when solidarity is not supported by subsidiarity, it degenerates into a form of welfare that does not foster responsibility. [100] This interconnectedness also pertains to the responsibility of authentic participation. Solidarity is expressed when each person, both individually and collectively, takes part in the life of the community — by staying informed, engaging with others, making their voice heard and contributing to public decisions and choices — while also assuming real responsibility so that the common good is achieved through shared decision-making.

blocks.en: In many areas, we are already experiencing a kind of “ de facto solidarity,” for our lives are intertwined; digital networks connect people and communities across the world in real time, and global economies and communications mean that events in one place have a far-reaching impact. This network of relationships, however, only constitutes solidarity in the fullest sense of the word when it becomes a conscious choice. Faith invites us to see this reality as a call: we are not merely neighbors to one another, but entrusted to each other, so that each of us may take responsibility, as best we can, for the lives and wounds of our brothers and sisters. Solidarity arises precisely when we decide not to remain indifferent to what happens to our neighbor but instead to transform unavoidable bonds — economic, cultural and technological — into paths of sharing, cooperation and mutual care, embracing the idea of “thinking and acting in terms of community.” [101]
blocks.zh: 在許多領域中，我們已然體會到某種「事實上的休戚與共」，因為我們的生命交織相連；數碼網絡將世界各地的人與群體實時連結起來，而全球經濟與通訊更意味著，一個地方發生的事件會產生深遠的影響。然而，這張關係網唯有在成為一項有意識的抉擇時，才構成最完整意義上的休戚與共。信仰邀請我們將這現實視為一項召叫：我們不僅彼此為鄰，更被彼此交託，好使我們每個人都能盡己所能，為我們弟兄姊妹的生命與創傷承擔責任。休戚與共的真正產生，恰恰在於我們決意不再對近人身上所發生的事無動於衷，而是將那些無可避免的聯繫——經濟的、文化的和技術的——轉化為分享、合作與互相關懷的道路，擁抱「以團體的角度思考和行動」的理念。[101]
blocks.html: 74. In many areas, we are already experiencing a kind of “ de facto solidarity,” for our lives are intertwined; digital networks connect people and communities across the world in real time, and global economies and communications mean that events in one place have a far-reaching impact. This network of relationships, however, only constitutes solidarity in the fullest sense of the word when it becomes a conscious choice. Faith invites us to see this reality as a call: we are not merely neighbors to one another, but entrusted to each other, so that each of us may take responsibility, as best we can, for the lives and wounds of our brothers and sisters. Solidarity arises precisely when we decide not to remain indifferent to what happens to our neighbor but instead to transform unavoidable bonds — economic, cultural and technological — into paths of sharing, cooperation and mutual care, embracing the idea of “thinking and acting in terms of community.” [101]

blocks.en: The Church’s social teaching emphasizes that solidarity is both a principle and a virtue. As a principle, it expresses the objective order of relationships among individuals, groups and peoples, pointing to an awareness of interdependence whereby the good of each person depends on the good of others. As a virtue, it requires a “firm and persevering determination” [102] to strive for the common good, with particular attention to those most in need. Pope Francis noted that solidarity is “a way of making history” [103] that creates communities and not just masses of individuals. For this reason, it requires a modest and shared way of life, the ability to forego immediate benefits in order to create opportunities for others in the future, and a willingness to challenge habits and privileges — including those related to digital consumption and the use of technology — when they prevent others from living with dignity.
blocks.zh: 教會的社會訓導強調，團結關懷既是一項原則，也是一種德行。作為原則，它表達了個人、群體和民族之間關係的客觀秩序，指向一種對相互依存的自覺——每個人的福祉取決於他人的福祉。作為德行，它要求一種「堅定而持久的決心」[102] 去為公益奮鬥，並特別關顧那些最有需要的人。教宗方濟各指出，團結關懷是「一種造就歷史的方式」[103]，它創造的是真正的團體，而不只是個體的群眾。為此緣故，它要求一種簡樸而共享的生活方式，具備捨棄當下利益以在未來為他人締造機會的能力，並願意挑戰那些妨礙他人有尊嚴地生活的習慣和特權——包括與數碼消費及科技使用相關的習慣和特權。
blocks.html: 75. The Church’s social teaching emphasizes that solidarity is both a principle and a virtue. As a principle, it expresses the objective order of relationships among individuals, groups and peoples, pointing to an awareness of interdependence whereby the good of each person depends on the good of others. As a virtue, it requires a “firm and persevering determination” [102] to strive for the common good, with particular attention to those most in need. Pope Francis noted that solidarity is “a way of making history” [103] that creates communities and not just masses of individuals. For this reason, it requires a modest and shared way of life, the ability to forego immediate benefits in order to create opportunities for others in the future, and a willingness to challenge habits and privileges — including those related to digital consumption and the use of technology — when they prevent others from living with dignity.

blocks.en: In a world marked by increasingly close connections between people, communities and nations, solidarity also takes on a global dimension. Benedict XVI strongly emphasized the link between development, justice and responsibility toward future generations, stating that authentic development requires solidarity and inter-generational justice, [104] as well as an awareness of the bonds that unite us to the natural environment. Today, this responsibility also extends to digital and information infrastructure. Like the natural environment, the “digital ecosystem” can be preserved or exploited, shared or monopolized. Solidarity demands that decisions regarding data, algorithms, platforms and artificial intelligence take into account not only the immediate benefit for a few, but also the impact on all peoples and on future generations.
blocks.zh: 在人與人、團體與團體、國與國之間的聯繫日益緊密的世界中，團結關懷亦具有全球性的幅度。本篤十六世極力強調發展、正義與對後代責任之間的連繫，申明真正的發展要求團結關懷與代際正義，[104] 以及對我們與自然環境之間聯繫的意識。今天，這份責任也延伸至數碼和資訊基礎設施。如同自然環境一樣，「數碼生態系統」可以被保存，也可以被剝削；可以被分享，也可以被壟斷。團結關懷要求，在作出有關數據、演算法、平台和人工智能的決定時，不僅要考慮少數人的當下利益，更要考慮對所有民族及後代子孫的影響。
blocks.html: 76. In a world marked by increasingly close connections between people, communities and nations, solidarity also takes on a global dimension. Benedict XVI strongly emphasized the link between development, justice and responsibility toward future generations, stating that authentic development requires solidarity and inter-generational justice, [104] as well as an awareness of the bonds that unite us to the natural environment. Today, this responsibility also extends to digital and information infrastructure. Like the natural environment, the “digital ecosystem” can be preserved or exploited, shared or monopolized. Solidarity demands that decisions regarding data, algorithms, platforms and artificial intelligence take into account not only the immediate benefit for a few, but also the impact on all peoples and on future generations.

blocks.en: The principle of social justice
blocks.zh: 社會正義原則
blocks.html: The principle of social justice

blocks.en: For the Christian community, social justice is a concrete way of following Jesus and remaining faithful to the Gospel. In the New Testament, Jesus proclaims the “good news to the poor” ( Lk 4:18) and identifies himself with the lowly, the sick, the imprisoned and strangers (cf. Mt 25:31-46). He thus teaches us that justice is born from, and fulfilled in, fraternity, because the way we approach and relate to the least among us becomes, in concrete terms, the measure of our relationship with God and with our brothers and sisters. Justice, however, concerns not only the behavior of individuals, but also the way in which the structures of society are conceived and organized. In this regard, the Second Vatican Council reminds us that every institution is called to serve the human person and his or her dignity. [105] Social justice is, therefore, characterized by the capacity of a social, economic and political order to allow everyone — particularly the weakest — to live a truly dignified life, without leaving anyone behind.
blocks.zh: 對基督信仰團體而言，社會正義是跟隨耶穌、忠於福音的一條具體道路。在新約中，耶穌向「貧窮人宣告喜訊」（路 4:18），並將自己與卑微的人、患病的人、被囚禁的人和異鄉人視為同一（參瑪 25:31-46）。祂藉此教導我們：正義生於弟兄情誼，並在弟兄情誼中得以圓滿，因為我們走近並對待我們中最弱小者的方式，具體而言，就成了我們與天主、以及與我們弟兄姊妹關係的尺度。然而，正義不僅關乎個人的行為，也關乎社會結構被構思和組織的方式。就此而言，梵蒂岡第二屆大公會議提醒我們，每一個制度都蒙召為人及其尊嚴服務。[105] 因此，社會正義的特徵，在於一種社會、經濟和政治秩序能夠使每一個人——尤其是最弱小者——度一個真正有尊嚴的生活，不讓任何人被遺棄。
blocks.html: 77. For the Christian community, social justice is a concrete way of following Jesus and remaining faithful to the Gospel. In the New Testament, Jesus proclaims the “good news to the poor” ( Lk 4:18) and identifies himself with the lowly, the sick, the imprisoned and strangers (cf. Mt 25:31-46). He thus teaches us that justice is born from, and fulfilled in, fraternity, because the way we approach and relate to the least among us becomes, in concrete terms, the measure of our relationship with God and with our brothers and sisters. Justice, however, concerns not only the behavior of individuals, but also the way in which the structures of society are conceived and organized. In this regard, the Second Vatican Council reminds us that every institution is called to serve the human person and his or her dignity. [105] Social justice is, therefore, characterized by the capacity of a social, economic and political order to allow everyone — particularly the weakest — to live a truly dignified life, without leaving anyone behind.

blocks.en: The recent Magisterium has insisted that social justice begins with the least among us. Saint John Paul II spoke of a preferential option for the poor [106] that must guide both personal and societal choices, while Pope Francis denounced a “‘throw away’ culture” [107] that generates ever new forms of exclusion. From this perspective, social justice requires us to look at individuals and communities, starting with the most vulnerable: the poor, migrants, refugees, internally displaced persons, victims of violence and people living in urban or existential peripheries.
blocks.zh: 近期的教會訓導一直堅持，社會正義始於我們中最弱小者。聖若望保祿二世論及「優先關愛窮人」[106] 的選擇，這必須引導個人和社會的抉擇，而教宗方濟各則譴責了一種不斷製造新型排斥的「丟棄文化」[107]。由此觀之，社會正義要求我們審視個人和群體，從最脆弱的開始：窮人、移民、難民、國內流離失所者、暴力受害者，以及生活在城市或生存邊緣的人。
blocks.html: 78. The recent Magisterium has insisted that social justice begins with the least among us. Saint John Paul II spoke of a preferential option for the poor [106] that must guide both personal and societal choices, while Pope Francis denounced a “‘throw away’ culture” [107] that generates ever new forms of exclusion. From this perspective, social justice requires us to look at individuals and communities, starting with the most vulnerable: the poor, migrants, refugees, internally displaced persons, victims of violence and people living in urban or existential peripheries.

blocks.en: The idea of “social justice” helps us recognize that injustices do not arise solely from the wrong choices of individuals, but also from structures, mechanisms and economic and cultural systems that produce inequality almost automatically. Saint John Paul II spoke in this vein of structures of sin [108] that oppose God’s will and require a commitment to personal and social conversion. In this perspective, justice is not merely about the fairer distribution of resources or the correction of current injustices, but also assumes a restorative dimension. It aims to mend broken bonds and reintegrate those who have been excluded, taking into account the wounds caused by injustices, such as wars, colonialism, racial or gender discrimination, violence against entire peoples and exploitation. This may include restoring dignity and a voice to those who have been ignored, fostering processes of healing for collective memory, opposing discriminatory laws and practices, and providing concrete support to those who still bear the consequences of wrongs suffered in the past.
blocks.zh: 「社會正義」的概念幫助我們認識到，不義不僅源於個人的錯誤選擇，也源於那些幾乎自動地產生不平等的結構、機制及經濟和文化體系。聖若望保祿二世循此脈絡論及「罪惡的結構」[108]，這些結構違背天主的旨意，並要求我們致力於個人與社會的皈依。從這個角度來看，正義不僅關乎資源的更公平分配或對當前不義的矯正，它還具有修復的幅度。正義旨在修補破碎的聯繫，並重新接納那些被排斥的人，同時要考慮到不義所造成的創傷，例如戰爭、殖民主義、種族或性別歧視、針對整個民族的暴力以及剝削。這可能包括恢復那些被忽略者的尊嚴和發言權，促進集體記憶的治癒過程，反對歧視性的法律和做法，並向那些仍承受著過去所受傷害後果的人提供具體的支持。
blocks.html: 79. The idea of “social justice” helps us recognize that injustices do not arise solely from the wrong choices of individuals, but also from structures, mechanisms and economic and cultural systems that produce inequality almost automatically. Saint John Paul II spoke in this vein of structures of sin [108] that oppose God’s will and require a commitment to personal and social conversion. In this perspective, justice is not merely about the fairer distribution of resources or the correction of current injustices, but also assumes a restorative dimension. It aims to mend broken bonds and reintegrate those who have been excluded, taking into account the wounds caused by injustices, such as wars, colonialism, racial or gender discrimination, violence against entire peoples and exploitation. This may include restoring dignity and a voice to those who have been ignored, fostering processes of healing for collective memory, opposing discriminatory laws and practices, and providing concrete support to those who still bear the consequences of wrongs suffered in the past.

blocks.en: In this day and age, social justice must also grapple with the environment shaped by digital technologies. The spread of global networks, platforms and artificial intelligence systems is changing the way we obtain information, communicate and access services. Justice demands that we prevent the emergence of new forms of exclusion and deprivation of freedoms: individuals and peoples hindered or denied access to basic technologies, communities exposed to invasive surveillance and social groups penalized by opaque algorithms that perpetuate prejudice and discrimination. In the digital age, a just social order guarantees everyone equal access to opportunities, protects the youngest and weakest members of society, combats hate and misinformation and subjects the use of data and technology to public oversight, so that the guiding principle is not solely profit but the dignity of every person and the common good of all people.
blocks.zh: 在此時代，社會正義亦必須面對數碼科技所塑造的環境。全球網絡、平台及人工智能系統的普及，正改變我們獲取資訊、彼此溝通及獲取服務的方式。正義要求我們防止新的排斥形式及自由被剝奪的情況出現：個人及民族被阻礙或拒絕獲得基本技術，社群被置於具侵入性的監控之下，以及社會群體因不透明的演算法而受害，這些演算法延續了偏見與歧視。在數碼時代，公正的社會秩序須保證人人有平等的機會，保護社會中最年幼及最弱小的成員，打擊仇恨與虛假資訊，並使數據及技術的使用受公眾監督，以使指導原則不單是利潤，而是每一個人的尊嚴及所有人的公益。
blocks.html: 80. In this day and age, social justice must also grapple with the environment shaped by digital technologies. The spread of global networks, platforms and artificial intelligence systems is changing the way we obtain information, communicate and access services. Justice demands that we prevent the emergence of new forms of exclusion and deprivation of freedoms: individuals and peoples hindered or denied access to basic technologies, communities exposed to invasive surveillance and social groups penalized by opaque algorithms that perpetuate prejudice and discrimination. In the digital age, a just social order guarantees everyone equal access to opportunities, protects the youngest and weakest members of society, combats hate and misinformation and subjects the use of data and technology to public oversight, so that the guiding principle is not solely profit but the dignity of every person and the common good of all people.

blocks.en: A litmus test for social justice today is the treatment of migrants, refugees and those forced to move due to poverty, violence, climate change and environmental disasters. The way a society treats them reveals whether its sense of justice is driven by fear or by the spirit of fraternity. Pope Francis urged us to see migrants not simply as a problem to be managed, but as a living image of the People of God on the move. [109] They are people with dignity, resources and dreams, who have the right to be treated with respect and to ask to become active members of the societies that welcome them. Social justice in this area entails at least two complementary commitments. On the one hand, this means protecting the rightful hopes of those forced to leave by ensuring safe and legal routes, dignified conditions for receiving them, and genuine pathways to integration. On the other hand, it means promoting the right to remain in one’s homeland in peace and security by addressing the root causes that force people to migrate, including those linked to economic injustices and the climate crisis. When these rights are respected, migration can become an opportunity for encounter and mutual enrichment among peoples.
blocks.zh: 今日社會正義的一個試金石，是對待移民、難民以及那些因貧窮、暴力、氣候變化和環境災難而被迫遷徙者的方式。一個社會如何對待他們，揭示出它的正義感究竟是受恐懼所驅使，還是源於弟兄情誼的精神。教宗方濟各敦促我們，不要只將移民視為一個有待處理的問題，而要視之為旅途中天主子民的生動圖像。[109] 他們是有尊嚴、有資源、有夢想的人，有權得到尊重的對待，並有權要求成為接納他們的社會中的積極成員。這方面的社會正義至少包含兩項相輔相成的承諾。一方面，這意味著要透過確保安全合法的途徑、有尊嚴的接待條件，以及真正融入社會的道路，來保護那些被迫離開家園者的合理希望。另一方面，這意味著要透過解決迫使人們遷徙的根本原因，包括那些與經濟不公和氣候危機相關的原因，促進人民在自己的祖國和平安全地留下的權利。當這些權利得到尊重時，遷徙就能成為各民族彼此相遇和相互充實的機會。
blocks.html: 81. A litmus test for social justice today is the treatment of migrants, refugees and those forced to move due to poverty, violence, climate change and environmental disasters. The way a society treats them reveals whether its sense of justice is driven by fear or by the spirit of fraternity. Pope Francis urged us to see migrants not simply as a problem to be managed, but as a living image of the People of God on the move. [109] They are people with dignity, resources and dreams, who have the right to be treated with respect and to ask to become active members of the societies that welcome them. Social justice in this area entails at least two complementary commitments. On the one hand, this means protecting the rightful hopes of those forced to leave by ensuring safe and legal routes, dignified conditions for receiving them, and genuine pathways to integration. On the other hand, it means promoting the right to remain in one’s homeland in peace and security by addressing the root causes that force people to migrate, including those linked to economic injustices and the climate crisis. When these rights are respected, migration can become an opportunity for encounter and mutual enrichment among peoples.

blocks.en: Integral human development
blocks.zh: 人的全人發展
blocks.html: Integral human development

blocks.en: In his Encyclical Populorum Progressio, Paul VI affirmed that development is authentic only if it is “integral,” meaning that it can “foster the development of each man and of the whole man.” [110][10] In the decades that followed, the Social Doctrine of the Church reprised and reflected on this expression in order to indicate the practical ways in which the noble principles — dignity, the common good, the universal destination of goods, subsidiarity, solidarity and social justice — are implemented in real life. By “integral human development,” we mean a process in which the growth of individuals and peoples encompasses all dimensions of existence and opens the future to subsequent generations as well.
blocks.zh: 教宗保祿六世在《民族發展》通諭中申明，發展唯有是「全人的」才是真正的發展，意即它能「促進每一個人及整個人格的發展。」[110][10] 在其後的數十年間，教會社會訓導反覆闡釋並反省這一表述，為要指出那些崇高的原則——人性尊嚴、公益、財物的普遍歸屬、輔助性、團結關懷與社會正義——在現實生活中如何具體落實。所謂「全人發展」，我們指的是一個過程，在此過程中，個人與民族的成長涵蓋存在的所有幅度，並為後代子孫開啟未來。
blocks.html: 82. In his Encyclical Populorum Progressio, Paul VI affirmed that development is authentic only if it is “integral,” meaning that it can “foster the development of each man and of the whole man.” [110][10] In the decades that followed, the Social Doctrine of the Church reprised and reflected on this expression in order to indicate the practical ways in which the noble principles — dignity, the common good, the universal destination of goods, subsidiarity, solidarity and social justice — are implemented in real life. By “integral human development,” we mean a process in which the growth of individuals and peoples encompasses all dimensions of existence and opens the future to subsequent generations as well.

blocks.en: For individuals as well as for nations, development is both a duty and a right. Minimum conditions are required for enabling every person and people to flourish in accord with their dignity, without being kept in a state of dependence or excluded from access to necessary goods. Development is truly human when it places people at the center instead of the accumulation of wealth, and when it concerns peoples as well as individuals. Justice demands the recognition of the rights of society and the rights of peoples, and includes a responsibility toward future generations. Development is not truly human if it increases consumption for some while shifting costs and burdens onto others, or relegates entire regions to subordinate roles, preventing them from realizing their full potential. [111] Development is integral when it is not limited to the economic sphere, but promotes quality of life in its spiritual, cultural, moral and relational dimensions, while respecting our common home, the diversity of peoples and their ways of life. [112]
blocks.zh: 為個人與為各民族而言，發展既是一項責任，也是一項權利。必須具備最基本的條件，好讓每一個人、每一個民族都能按照自身的尊嚴蓬勃發展，而不致陷於依賴的狀態，或被排斥而無法獲得必要的物資。當發展將人置於中心，而非以財富的累積為中心，而且關乎各民族一如關乎個人時，這樣的發展才是真正合乎人性的發展。正義要求承認社會的權利和民族的權利，並且包含對未來世代應負的責任。如果發展只增加了某些人的消費，卻將代價和負擔轉嫁到他人身上，或將整個地區貶抑為從屬角色、使它們無法實現自身的全部潛能，那麼這樣的發展便不是真正合乎人性的發展。[111] 當發展不局限於經濟領域，而是在精神、文化、道德和關係等層面上增進生活品質，同時尊重我們共同的家園、尊重各民族的多樣性及其生活方式時，這樣的發展才是整全的發展。[112]
blocks.html: 83. For individuals as well as for nations, development is both a duty and a right. Minimum conditions are required for enabling every person and people to flourish in accord with their dignity, without being kept in a state of dependence or excluded from access to necessary goods. Development is truly human when it places people at the center instead of the accumulation of wealth, and when it concerns peoples as well as individuals. Justice demands the recognition of the rights of society and the rights of peoples, and includes a responsibility toward future generations. Development is not truly human if it increases consumption for some while shifting costs and burdens onto others, or relegates entire regions to subordinate roles, preventing them from realizing their full potential. [111] Development is integral when it is not limited to the economic sphere, but promotes quality of life in its spiritual, cultural, moral and relational dimensions, while respecting our common home, the diversity of peoples and their ways of life. [112]

blocks.en: Today, the concept of integral human development is a benchmark for the evaluation of integral ecology, which has become an indispensable dimension of the Church’s Social Doctrine. Indeed, the quality of development is measured by the ability to integrate justice toward people and the care of our common home, and to promote dignified living conditions, access to necessary goods, just social relations, care of creation and consideration for future generations. It follows that true progress is not what increases the wellbeing of some by degrading ecosystems, shifting costs onto the most disadvantaged communities, or compromising the living conditions of those who will follow us.
blocks.zh: 今日，整全人性發展的概念已成為評估整全生態學的標準，而整全生態學已成為教會社會訓導不可或缺的一個幅度。事實上，發展的品質，是以能否將對人的正義與對我們共同家園的關懷融合起來，並促進有尊嚴的生活條件、獲得必需物資的途徑、公義的社會關係、對受造界的照料，以及對後代子孫的顧念來衡量。由此可見，真正的進步，並非那種透過破壞生態系統、將代價轉嫁給最弱勢的社群，或損害後代子孫的生活條件，來增加某些人福祉的進步。
blocks.html: 84. Today, the concept of integral human development is a benchmark for the evaluation of integral ecology, which has become an indispensable dimension of the Church’s Social Doctrine. Indeed, the quality of development is measured by the ability to integrate justice toward people and the care of our common home, and to promote dignified living conditions, access to necessary goods, just social relations, care of creation and consideration for future generations. It follows that true progress is not what increases the wellbeing of some by degrading ecosystems, shifting costs onto the most disadvantaged communities, or compromising the living conditions of those who will follow us.

blocks.en: Seen in this light, integral human development is the framework through which we can interpret the changes of our time, including those brought about by the digital revolution. Technological innovations, including artificial intelligence, are not neutral, for they can either foster participation and justice or exacerbate inequality, control and exclusion. For this reason, they must be evaluated by asking a crucial question: Do they truly help individuals and peoples to become more humane and fraternal, while respecting our common home and future generations? It is here that the principles of Social Doctrine become concrete criteria for discernment regarding the issues which we will address in the following chapters.
blocks.zh: 由此觀之，整全的人類發展是我們得以詮釋時代變遷——包括數碼革命所帶來的種種變化——的框架。科技創新，包括人工智能，並非中性的，因為它們要麼能促進參與和公義，要麼會加劇不平等、操控和排斥。為此，我們必須透過一個關鍵問題來評估它們：這些科技是否真正有助於個人和民族變得更具人性、更友愛，同時尊重我們共同的家園和未來的世代？正是在此，教會社會訓導的原則成為具體的辨別準則，用以審視我們將在隨後章節中探討的各項議題。
blocks.html: 85. Seen in this light, integral human development is the framework through which we can interpret the changes of our time, including those brought about by the digital revolution. Technological innovations, including artificial intelligence, are not neutral, for they can either foster participation and justice or exacerbate inequality, control and exclusion. For this reason, they must be evaluated by asking a crucial question: Do they truly help individuals and peoples to become more humane and fraternal, while respecting our common home and future generations? It is here that the principles of Social Doctrine become concrete criteria for discernment regarding the issues which we will address in the following chapters.
