blocks.en: An examen for the Church
blocks.zh: 教會的良心省察
blocks.html: An examen for the Church

blocks.en: In conclusion, I would like to touch on a point that is particularly close to my heart. Social Doctrine is not merely a message addressed to society; it is also an examination of conscience for the Church — a home and school of communion that is always called to ensure that the principles outlined in this chapter are applied, especially within its own structures. In the ecclesial context, the common good takes the form of a synodal approach for mission at the service of the Kingdom. Indeed, the Church is the “communitarian and historical subject of synodality and mission.” [113] This requires attention to the way decisions are taken and responsibilities are exercised. The Final Document of the Synod identifies a culture of transparency, accountability and evaluation as key practices for missionary transformation. [114]
blocks.zh: 最後，我想觸及一個我心中特別關切的要點。教會社會訓導不單是向社會傳達的信息，也是為教會所作的一次良心省察——教會作為共融的家與學校，常被召叫，務要在其自身的架構內，尤其實踐本章所闡述的各項原則。在教會處境中，公益以一種共議性的方式呈現，是為天國服務的使命模式。事實上，教會正是「共議性與使命的團體性及歷史性主體」。[113] 這要求我們關注決策的制定方式和權責的行使方式。世界主教會議的《最後文件》指出，透明、問責和評估的文化，是傳教轉化的關鍵實踐。[114]
blocks.html: 86. In conclusion, I would like to touch on a point that is particularly close to my heart. Social Doctrine is not merely a message addressed to society; it is also an examination of conscience for the Church — a home and school of communion that is always called to ensure that the principles outlined in this chapter are applied, especially within its own structures. In the ecclesial context, the common good takes the form of a synodal approach for mission at the service of the Kingdom.  Indeed, the Church is the “communitarian and historical subject of synodality and mission.” [113] This requires attention to the way decisions are taken and responsibilities are exercised. The Final Document of the Synod identifies a culture of transparency, accountability and evaluation as key practices for missionary transformation. [114]

blocks.en: With this in mind, subsidiarity becomes the guiding principle for governance and pastoral life. It involves recognizing and supporting the faithful and intermediary ecclesial organizations as they carry out their responsibilities, valuing charisms and skills and avoiding any form of paternalism that suffocates evangelical freedom. In practical terms, the participation of the baptized in decision-making processes and their shared responsibility in the mission are achieved through genuine, rather than merely nominal, participatory bodies. [115]
blocks.zh: 懷著這個認識，相輔相助的原則便成為治理與牧靈生活的指導原則。這項原則意味著承認並支持信眾及中介性的教會組織履行其職責，珍視各種神恩與技能，並避免任何窒息福音自由的父權式作風。在具體實踐上，已領洗者在決策過程中的參與，以及他們在使命中的共同責任，是透過真正而非僅有名義的參與性機構來實現的。[115]
blocks.html: 87. With this in mind, subsidiarity becomes the guiding principle for governance and pastoral life. It involves recognizing and supporting the faithful and intermediary ecclesial organizations as they carry out their responsibilities, valuing charisms and skills and avoiding any form of paternalism that suffocates evangelical freedom. In practical terms, the participation of the baptized in decision-making processes and their shared responsibility in the mission are achieved through genuine, rather than merely nominal, participatory bodies. [115]

blocks.en: For the Christian community, solidarity finds its source in the mystery of Christ and is nourished by the Eucharist. Solidarity emerges from communion in faith and the Sacraments: Baptism and Confirmation unite us in Christ, so that we may become one Body and one Spirit, one heart and one soul (cf. Eph 4:4; Acts 4:32). The Eucharist, which is the sacrament of unity, nurtures our belonging to the Body of Christ and teaches us how to share. The diverse sensibilities present in the Church and the strong convictions that animate each person are a source of richness if they remain anchored in the certainty that unity is a gift received and a responsibility to be fulfilled.
blocks.zh: 為基督徒團體而言，團結關懷的根源在於基督的奧蹟，並由聖體聖事所滋養。團結關懷源自信仰與聖事的共融：聖洗與堅振使我們在基督內合而為一，好使我們成為一個身體、一個聖神、一個心靈、一個靈魂（參 Eph 4:4; Acts 4:32）。聖體聖事是合一的聖事，滋養我們對基督奧體的歸屬，並教導我們如何分享。存在於教會內的各種不同感受，以及激勵每個人的堅定信念，只要它們始終錨定於「合一是領受的恩賜，也是應盡的責任」這一確信之中，便都是豐富的泉源。
blocks.html: 88. For the Christian community, solidarity finds its source in the mystery of Christ and is nourished by the Eucharist. Solidarity emerges from communion in faith and the Sacraments: Baptism and Confirmation unite us in Christ, so that we may become one Body and one Spirit, one heart and one soul (cf. Eph 4:4; Acts 4:32). The Eucharist, which is the sacrament of unity, nurtures our belonging to the Body of Christ and teaches us how to share. The diverse sensibilities present in the Church and the strong convictions that animate each person are a source of richness if they remain anchored in the certainty that unity is a gift received and a responsibility to be fulfilled.

blocks.en: Living out justice in the Church means purifying ecclesial relationships and structures from distortions that give rise to inequality, lack of transparency and abuse of power. In this regard, listening to the victims of spiritual, economic, institutional, sexual and power-based abuse, as well as abuses of conscience, is an integral part of a journey toward justice, which includes acknowledging the harm done, just reparation and taking steps to prevent it from happening again. Every power is at the service of communion and mission. All authority is at the service of the People of God. This ministry of service is expressed not only through our faith celebrated and lived in the Sacraments, and in the adoption of a synodal style, but also in the concrete sharing of goods. Following the example of the early Church, ecclesial resources need to be shared so that no one among us may be in need (cf. Acts 4:34), and so that their administration may support the mission of proclaiming the Gospel to the poorest. Regular assessments of the exercise of ministerial responsibilities should be encouraged, not as judgments on individuals, but as tools for learning and correction oriented toward mission. [116] Only to the extent that we are open to the action of the Holy Spirit will these principles of Social Doctrine become incarnate in ecclesial life. In this way, the Church will be able to bear credible witness to society that seeking the common good together, with shared responsibility and fraternity, is not a utopia, but a real possibility. [117]
blocks.zh: 在教會內活出正義，意味著要淨化那些因扭曲而滋生不平等、缺乏透明度和濫用權力的教會關係與結構。在這方面，聆聽靈性、經濟、制度、性方面以及基於權力的侵犯行為的受害者，也包括良心上的侵犯行為，是走向正義之路不可或缺的一部分，這條路包括承認所造成的傷害、作出公義的補償，並採取措施防止此類事件再次發生。一切權力都是為共融和使命服務的。一切權柄都是為天主子民服務的。這種服務的職務，不僅透過我們在聖事中所慶祝和生活的信仰，以及透過採納一種共議性的風格來表達，也體現在財物的具體分享中。跟隨初期教會的榜樣，教會的資源需要被分享，好使我們當中沒有人有所匱乏（參 Acts 4:34），並且使這些資源的管理能夠支持向最貧窮者宣講福音的使命。應鼓勵對職務責任的行使進行定期評估，不是作為對個人的判斷，而是作為學習和糾正的工具，以使命為導向。[116] 只有當我們向聖神的行動開放時，這些教會社會訓導的原則才能在教會生活中成為血肉。這樣，教會將能夠向社會作出可信的見證，表明懷著共同責任和兄弟情誼一同尋求公益，並非烏托邦，而是一個真實的可能性。[117]
blocks.html: 89. Living out justice in the Church means purifying ecclesial relationships and structures from distortions that give rise to inequality, lack of transparency and abuse of power. In this regard, listening to the victims of spiritual, economic, institutional, sexual and power-based abuse, as well as abuses of conscience, is an integral part of a journey toward justice, which includes acknowledging the harm done, just reparation and taking steps to prevent it from happening again. Every power is at the service of communion and mission. All authority is at the service of the People of God. This ministry of service is expressed not only through our faith celebrated and lived in the Sacraments, and in the adoption of a synodal style, but also in the concrete sharing of goods. Following the example of the early Church, ecclesial resources need to be shared so that no one among us may be in need (cf. Acts 4:34), and so that their administration may support the mission of proclaiming the Gospel to the poorest. Regular assessments of the exercise of ministerial responsibilities should be encouraged, not as judgments on individuals, but as tools for learning and correction oriented toward mission. [116] Only to the extent that we are open to the action of the Holy Spirit will these principles of Social Doctrine become incarnate in ecclesial life. In this way, the Church will be able to bear credible witness to society that seeking the common good together, with shared responsibility and fraternity, is not a utopia, but a real possibility. [117]

sections.title_en: THE GRANDEUR OF HUMANITY IN LIGHT OF THE PROMISES OF AI
sections.title_zh: 科技與宰制——人性的偉大與人工智能的諾言

blocks.en: TECHNOLOGY AND DOMINANCE.
blocks.zh: 科技與宰制
blocks.html: TECHNOLOGY AND DOMINANCE.

blocks.en: Having recalled the principles that shine a light on Social Doctrine, I would now like to focus on certain challenges that profoundly shape our way of living today. The biblical image accompanying these reflections is that of a building project. On the one hand, there is the Tower of Babel, where collective effort follows a plan that dominates and ultimately dehumanizes (cf. Gen 11:1-9). On the other hand, there are the ruins of Jerusalem, which under Nehemiah’s direction are rebuilt piece by piece as a project of shared responsibility (cf. Neh 2–6). We are called to reflect on the great “construction sites” of our era and ask: What are we building? As technological development rapidly transforms languages, relationships, institutions and forms of power, we believers must and can choose which projects to work on and in what manner, so as to safeguard and value the grandeur of humanity that has been given to us as a gift. This is a choice not only for our future but also for our present, since artificial intelligence and other emerging technologies are already part of our daily lives.
blocks.zh: 回顧了光照社會訓導的各項原則後，我現在願集中探討若干深刻塑造我們今日生活方式的一些挑戰。伴隨這些反思的聖經圖像是建築工程的圖像。一方面是巴貝耳塔，在那裡集體的努力遵循一項支配並最終使人失去人性的計劃（參 Gen 11:1-9）。另一方面則是耶路撒冷的廢墟，在乃赫米雅的領導下，作為一項共同承擔責任的工程，一磚一瓦地被重建起來（參厄下 2–6）。我們蒙召反思我們這個時代的各大「建築工地」，並捫心自問：我們正在建造什麼？當科技發展急速地轉化語言、人際關係、制度及權力形式之際，我們信眾必須、也能夠選擇要從事哪些工程項目，以及以何種方式進行，好能維護並珍惜那作為禮物賜予我們的人性壯麗。這不僅是為了我們的未來，也是為了我們的當下所作的抉擇，因為人工智能及其他新興科技早已是我們日常生活的一部分。
blocks.html: 90. Having recalled the principles that shine a light on Social Doctrine, I would now like to focus on certain challenges that profoundly shape our way of living today.  The biblical image accompanying these reflections is that of a building project. On the one hand, there is the Tower of Babel, where collective effort follows a plan that dominates and ultimately dehumanizes (cf. Gen 11:1-9). On the other hand, there are the ruins of Jerusalem, which under Nehemiah’s direction are rebuilt piece by piece as a project of shared responsibility (cf. Neh 2–6). We are called to reflect on the great “construction sites” of our era and ask: What are we building? As technological development rapidly transforms languages, relationships, institutions and forms of power, we believers must and can choose which projects to work on and in what manner, so as to safeguard and value the grandeur of humanity that has been given to us as a gift. This is a choice not only for our future but also for our present, since artificial intelligence and other emerging technologies are already part of our daily lives.

blocks.en: I am convinced that the concrete way of living out social relationships in the light of the Gospel is not established once and for all, but remains a task entrusted, from generation to generation, to the Christian community. Under the guidance of the Holy Spirit, the Church allows herself to be enlightened by God’s word, reads the signs of the times and creatively seeks new ways for relationships between peoples and nations to become ever more conformed to the demands of the Kingdom of God. [118] For this reason, I encourage all members of the Church not to be afraid of the present challenges, but to listen to one another and firmly embrace their responsibilities in building a more humane and fraternal society.
blocks.zh: 我深信，在福音光照下具體活出社會關係的方式，並非一勞永逸地確立，而是一項託付給基督徒團體、代代相傳的任務。在聖神的引導下，教會讓自己接受天主聖言的光照，解讀時代的記號，並以創造力尋求新的途徑，使各民族與國家之間的關係能日益符合天主國度的要求。[118] 為此，我鼓勵教會全體成員不要害怕當前的挑戰，而要彼此聆聽，並堅定地擔負起建設一個更人性化、更友愛的社會的責任。
blocks.html: 91. I am convinced that the concrete way of living out social relationships in the light of the Gospel is not established once and for all, but remains a task entrusted, from generation to generation, to the Christian community. Under the guidance of the Holy Spirit, the Church allows herself to be enlightened by God’s word, reads the signs of the times and creatively seeks new ways for relationships between peoples and nations to become ever more conformed to the demands of the Kingdom of God. [118] For this reason, I encourage all members of the Church not to be afraid of the present challenges, but to listen to one another and firmly embrace their responsibilities in building a more humane and fraternal society.

blocks.en: The technocratic paradigm and digital power
blocks.zh: 科技治理範式與數碼權力
blocks.html: The technocratic paradigm and digital power

blocks.en: In his Encyclical Laudato Si’, Pope Francis denounced the growing dominance of a technocratic paradigm [119] in our globalized world: the tendency to let the logic of efficiency, control and profit alone shape personal, social and economic decisions. This makes it clear that technology is not simply a tool. When it becomes the standard by which everything is judged, it begins to dictate what matters and what can be discarded, reducing creation to an object of exploitation and human beings to mere cogs in a system driven toward ever greater efficiency.
blocks.zh: 教宗方濟各在他的《願祢受讚頌》通諭中，譴責了在我們這全球化的世界中，科技至上範式[119]日益佔據主導地位的現象：即一種讓效率、控制和利潤的邏輯獨自塑造個人、社會和經濟決策的趨勢。這清楚表明，科技不僅僅是工具。當它成為衡量一切事物的標準時，它便開始決定什麼是重要的、什麼是可以捨棄的，將受造界貶低為剝削的對象，並將人貶抑為一個追求更高效率的系統中的單純齒輪。
blocks.html: 92. In his Encyclical Laudato Si’, Pope Francis denounced the growing dominance of a technocratic paradigm [119] in our globalized world: the tendency to let the logic of efficiency, control and profit alone shape personal, social and economic decisions. This makes it clear that technology is not simply a tool. When it becomes the standard by which everything is judged, it begins to dictate what matters and what can be discarded, reducing creation to an object of exploitation and human beings to mere cogs in a system driven toward ever greater efficiency.

blocks.en: This paradigm has spread rapidly in recent years, fueled in part by the expansion of artificial intelligence, cognitive science, nanotechnology, robotics and biotechnology. In themselves, these innovations can greatly serve integral human development and the care of our common home. Yet precisely because of their power, they can also hasten the expansion of the technocratic paradigm and therefore require a new spiritual, ethical and political framework. More power does not necessarily imply something better. In this respect, the words of Romano Guardini remain relevant: “Contemporary man has not been trained to use power well.” [120]
blocks.zh: 這種範式近年在人工智能、認知科學、納米技術、機械人技術及生物科技的擴張推動下迅速蔓延。這些創新本身大可為人的整體發展及我們共同家園的照料作出巨大貢獻。然而，正因為它們的力量，它們也可能加速科技至上範式的擴展，因而需要一個新的靈性、倫理和政治框架。更大的力量並不必然意味著更好的事物。在這方面，羅馬諾·瓜爾迪尼的話至今依然適用：「現代人並未受過善用力量的訓練。」[120]
blocks.html: 93. This paradigm has spread rapidly in recent years, fueled in part by the expansion of artificial intelligence, cognitive science, nanotechnology, robotics and biotechnology. In themselves, these innovations can greatly serve integral human development and the care of our common home. Yet precisely because of their power, they can also hasten the expansion of the technocratic paradigm and therefore require a new spiritual, ethical and political framework. More power does not necessarily imply something better. In this respect, the words of Romano Guardini remain relevant: “Contemporary man has not been trained to use power well.” [120]

blocks.en: The danger of humanity becoming a victim of its own achievements was already clearly recognized by Saint Paul VI, who warned that “the most extraordinary scientific progress, the most astounding technical feats and the most amazing economic growth, unless accompanied by authentic moral and social progress, will in the long run go against man.” [121] For this reason, technological progress — valuable in itself — requires careful discernment of the anthropological vision that guides it and the ends it pursues. If technological development advances without a corresponding ethical and social progress, the result may be an increase in means without a growth in humanity: “having more” without “being more.” In such a scenario, there is a risk that individuals will be evaluated principally according to the outcomes they produce. [122]
blocks.zh: 人類成為自身成就的犧牲品的危險，早已為聖保祿六世所清晰洞悉；他曾警告說：「最非凡的科學進步、最驚人的技術成就、最令人讚嘆的經濟增長，若沒有真正的道德和社會進步相伴，長遠而言終將與人為敵。」[121] 為此，技術進步——其本身固然可貴——需要對引導它的那個人類學願景，以及它所追求的目標，進行審慎的分辨。若技術發展不斷前進，而缺乏相應的倫理和社會進步，其結果可能是工具手段增多，而人性卻未能增長：即「擁有更多」而未能「成為更善」。在這種情況下，個人將面臨僅按其所能產出的成果來被衡量的危險。[122]
blocks.html: 94. The danger of humanity becoming a victim of its own achievements was already clearly recognized by Saint Paul VI, who warned that “the most extraordinary scientific progress, the most astounding technical feats and the most amazing economic growth, unless accompanied by authentic moral and social progress, will in the long run go against man.” [121] For this reason, technological progress — valuable in itself — requires careful discernment of the anthropological vision that guides it and the ends it pursues. If technological development advances without a corresponding ethical and social progress, the result may be an increase in means without a growth in humanity: “having more” without “being more.” In such a scenario, there is a risk that individuals will be evaluated principally according to the outcomes they produce. [122]

blocks.en: Here, we must recognize another crucial aspect, which I have noted earlier. In many cases within the digital context, control over platforms, infrastructure, data and computing power does not rest with States, but with major economic and technological actors. These entities effectively set the conditions for access, determine the rules of visibility and shape the very possibilities for participation. When such power is concentrated in the hands of a few, it tends to become opaque and evade public oversight, increasing the risk of distorted forms of development that give rise to new dependencies, exclusions, manipulations and inequalities.
blocks.zh: 在此，我們必須承認另一個關鍵層面，這是我先前已提及的。在數碼環境中，許多情況下對平台、基礎設施、數據和計算能力的控制，並非掌握在國家手中，而是掌握在主要的經濟和科技參與者手中。這些實體實際上設定了取用的條件、決定了可見度的規則，並塑造了參與的各種可能性本身。當這種權力集中在少數人手中時，它往往會變得不透明並逃避公眾監督，從而增加了發展模式扭曲的風險，導致新的依賴、排斥、操縱和不平等。
blocks.html: 95. Here, we must recognize another crucial aspect, which I have noted earlier. In many cases within the digital context, control over platforms, infrastructure, data and computing power does not rest with States, but with major economic and technological actors. These entities effectively set the conditions for access, determine the rules of visibility and shape the very possibilities for participation. When such power is concentrated in the hands of a few, it tends to become opaque and evade public oversight, increasing the risk of distorted forms of development that give rise to new dependencies, exclusions, manipulations and inequalities.

blocks.en: Faced with this concentration of power in the digital world, the criteria for judgment and discernment in this new situation are the noble principles of Social Doctrine: the inalienable dignity of the human person, the common good, the universal destination of goods, subsidiarity, solidarity and social justice. They demand that we assess whether the power of digital infrastructures and algorithms truly fosters participation and responsibility, protects the vulnerable, ensures fair access to opportunities and remains directed toward the good of all. On this basis, we can now examine more closely what artificial intelligence is, the possibilities it opens up and the risks it entails.
blocks.zh: 面對數碼世界中這種權力的集中，在這新形勢下的判斷和分辨標準，就是教會社會訓導的崇高原則：人之不可剝奪的尊嚴、公益、財富的普世共享終向、輔助原則、團結關懷，以及社會正義。這些原則要求我們評估，數碼基礎設施和演算法的權力，是否真正促進參與和責任、保護弱小者、確保公平獲取機會，並持續導向眾人的福祉。在此基礎上，我們現在可以更仔細地審視何謂人工智能、它所開啟的可能性，以及它所蘊含的風險。
blocks.html: 96. Faced with this concentration of power in the digital world, the criteria for judgment and discernment in this new situation are the noble principles of Social Doctrine: the inalienable dignity of the human person, the common good, the universal destination of goods, subsidiarity, solidarity and social justice. They demand that we assess whether the power of digital infrastructures and algorithms truly fosters participation and responsibility, protects the vulnerable, ensures fair access to opportunities and remains directed toward the good of all. On this basis, we can now examine more closely what artificial intelligence is, the possibilities it opens up and the risks it entails.

blocks.en: Artificial intelligence
blocks.zh: 人工智能
blocks.html: Artificial intelligence

blocks.en: It is not my intention here to offer a comprehensive treatment of artificial intelligence, nor to give an overview of the extensive relevant literature, since authoritative contributions already exist, including within the ecclesial context. [123] I limit myself to recalling a few essential elements for a moral and social discernment that safeguards the primacy of the human person, in order to ensure that it will always be human intelligence, with its conscience and freedom, that guides technical innovations and responsibly determines their use and limits.
blocks.zh: 在這裡，我無意對人工智能作出全面的論述，也無意概述大量相關的文獻，因為在這方面已有權威性的貢獻，包括在教會範圍內的貢獻。[123] 我只限於重提幾個基本要素，以便進行一種維護人之位元尊嚴優先性的道德與社會分辨，從而確保引導技術革新、並負責任地決定其運用與界限的，始終是人的理智、連同其良心與自由。
blocks.html: 97. It is not my intention here to offer a comprehensive treatment of artificial intelligence, nor to give an overview of the extensive relevant literature, since authoritative contributions already exist, including within the ecclesial context. [123]  I limit myself to recalling a few essential elements for a moral and social discernment that safeguards the primacy of the human person, in order to ensure that it will always be human intelligence, with its conscience and freedom, that guides technical innovations and responsibly determines their use and limits.

blocks.en: It is appropriate to preface this discussion with two considerations. First, any statement regarding AI risks becoming quickly outdated, given the remarkable pace at which these systems are developing. Second, all of us, including those who design them, possess only a limited understanding of their actual functioning. Indeed, current AI systems are more “cultivated” than “built,” for developers do not directly design every detail, but instead create a framework within which the intelligence “grows.” As a result, fundamental scientific aspects — such as the internal representations and computational processes of these systems — remain, at present, unknown. There thus emerges an urgent need for a twofold commitment: on the one hand, a deepening of scientific research; on the other, the exercise of moral and spiritual discernment.
blocks.zh: 在此討論之前，宜先提出兩項考量。首先，鑑於這些系統發展的速度驚人，任何關於人工智能的陳述都隨時可能過時。其次，我們所有人，包括那些設計這些系統的人，對其實際運作方式的了解都相當有限。事實上，當前的人工智能系統與其說是「建造」出來的，不如說是「培育」出來的，因為開發者並非直接設計每一個細節，而是創建一個框架，讓智能在其中「生長」。因此，一些基本的科學面向——例如這些系統的內部表徵和計算過程——至今仍屬未知。於是，一項雙重的急迫任務便隨之浮現：一方面是深化科學研究；另一方面則是運用道德與靈性的辨別。
blocks.html: 98. It is appropriate to preface this discussion with two considerations. First, any statement regarding AI risks becoming quickly outdated, given the remarkable pace at which these systems are developing. Second, all of us, including those who design them, possess only a limited understanding of their actual functioning. Indeed, current AI systems are more “cultivated” than “built,” for developers do not directly design every detail, but instead create a framework within which the intelligence “grows.” As a result, fundamental scientific aspects — such as the internal representations and computational processes of these systems — remain, at present, unknown. There thus emerges an urgent need for a twofold commitment: on the one hand, a deepening of scientific research; on the other, the exercise of moral and spiritual discernment.

blocks.en: It is not possible to provide a single, comprehensive definition of AI. What can be stated, however, is that we must avoid the misconception of equating this type of “intelligence” with that of human beings. These systems merely imitate certain functions of human intelligence. In doing so, they often surpass human intelligence in speed and computational capacity, offering tangible benefits across many fields. Yet this power remains entirely tied to data processing. So-called artificial intelligences do not undergo experiences, do not possess a body, do not feel joy or pain, do not mature through relationships and do not know from within what love, work, friendship or responsibility mean. Nor do they have a moral conscience, since they do not judge good and evil, grasp the ultimate meaning of situations, or bear responsibility for consequences. They may imitate language, behavior and analytical skills, or even simulate empathy and understanding, but they do not understand what they produce, for they lack the affective, relational and spiritual perspective through which human beings grow in wisdom. Even when these tools are described as capable of “learning,” their way of doing so is different from that of a human person. It is not the experience of those who allow themselves to be shaped by life and grow over time through choices, mistakes, forgiveness and fidelity. Rather, it is a form of statistical adaptation based on data and feedback, which can be very effective, but does not imply inner growth.
blocks.zh: 我們無法為人工智能提供一個單一且全面的定義。然而可以明確指出的是，我們必須避免將這類「智能」等同於人類智能的錯誤觀念。這些系統僅是模仿人類智能的某些功能。在此過程中，它們往往在速度和計算能力上超越人類智能，為許多領域帶來了實際益處。然而，這種能力完全局限於數據處理。所謂的人工智能不會經歷體驗，不擁有身體，感受不到喜悅或痛苦，不會透過關係走向成熟，也無法從內在體會愛、工作、友誼或責任的意義。它們也不具備道德良心，因為它們不會判斷善惡，無法領會境遇的終極意義，也不會為後果承擔責任。它們可以模仿語言、行為和分析能力，甚至模擬同理心和理解力，但它們並不理解自己所產出的內容，因為它們缺乏人類藉以在智慧中成長的情感、關係和靈性視角。即使這些工具被描述為具有「學習」能力，其學習方式也與人的方式截然不同。這不是那種讓自己被生命所塑造、並透過選擇、錯誤、寬恕和忠誠隨著時間而成長的經驗，而是一種基於數據和反饋的統計調適方式，這種方式可能非常有效，但並不意味著內在的成長。
blocks.html: 99. It is not possible to provide a single, comprehensive definition of AI. What can be stated, however, is that we must avoid the misconception of equating this type of “intelligence” with that of human beings. These systems merely imitate certain functions of human intelligence. In doing so, they often surpass human intelligence in speed and computational capacity, offering tangible benefits across many fields. Yet this power remains entirely tied to data processing. So-called artificial intelligences do not undergo experiences, do not possess a body, do not feel joy or pain, do not mature through relationships and do not know from within what love, work, friendship or responsibility mean. Nor do they have a moral conscience, since they do not judge good and evil, grasp the ultimate meaning of situations, or bear responsibility for consequences. They may imitate language, behavior and analytical skills, or even simulate empathy and understanding, but they do not understand what they produce, for they lack the affective, relational and spiritual perspective through which human beings grow in wisdom. Even when these tools are described as capable of “learning,” their way of doing so is different from that of a human person. It is not the experience of those who allow themselves to be shaped by life and grow over time through choices, mistakes, forgiveness and fidelity. Rather, it is a form of statistical adaptation based on data and feedback, which can be very effective, but does not imply inner growth.

blocks.en: A valuable tool that requires vigilance
blocks.zh: 一項寶貴但須警惕的工具
blocks.html: A valuable tool that requires vigilance

blocks.en: In light of what has been said, we can better understand why AI can be a valuable tool and, at the same time, why it calls for a measured and vigilant approach. In recent years, its private use has expanded significantly, prompting growing reflection on both the opportunities it offers and the risks tied to its rapid spread. In personal use, three aspects in particular deserve careful consideration: the ease with which results are obtained, the impression of objectivity and the simulation of human communication. The speed and simplicity with which information, complex analyses, media content and practical assistance can be accessed undoubtedly makes life easier. Yet they can also encourage excessive reliance and the search for ready-made answers, and weaken personal creativity and judgment. The apparent objectivity of the responses and suggestions these systems provide can lead us to overlook the fact that they reflect the cultural assumptions of those who designed and trained them, with all their strengths and limitations. The artificial imitation of positive human communication — words of advice, empathy, friendship and even love — can be engaging and at times genuinely helpful. However, for less discerning users, it can also be misleading, creating the illusion of a relationship with a real personal subject. When words are simulated, they do not build genuine relationships, but only their appearance. The artificial imitation of care or support can become particularly risky when it enters contexts where real relationships and emotional bonds are lacking. Here, the danger is not so much that a person may believe they are communicating with another person, but rather that they may gradually lose the very desire to form genuine human connections.
blocks.zh: 根據以上所述，我們可以更清楚地理解為何人工智能可以是一件寶貴的工具，同時又為何要求我們以審慎和警惕的態度對待它。近年來，人工智能的私人使用顯著擴展，促使人們日益反思它所帶來的機遇，以及與其迅速普及相關的風險。在個人使用方面，有三個方面尤其值得仔細考量：獲取結果的便捷性、客觀性的假象，以及對人類溝通所作的模擬。資訊、複雜分析、媒體內容和實用協助能夠以快速簡單的方式取得，無疑使生活更為便利。然而，這些便利也可能促使人們過度依賴和尋求現成的答案，並削弱個人的創造力和判斷力。這些系統所提供的回應和建議表面上的客觀性，可能使我們忽略了一個事實，即它們反映了設計和訓練這些系統的人的文化假設，連同其所有長處和限制。對正面人際溝通——包括勸勉之言、同理心、友誼甚至愛——的人工模擬，可能引人入勝，有時確實有所幫助。然而，對於辨別能力較弱的使用者來說，它也可能造成誤導，製造出一種與真實位格主體建立關係的幻覺。當言語被模擬時，它們不會建立起真正的關係，而只是關係的表象。當這種對關懷或支持的人工模擬進入缺乏真實關係和情感聯繫的環境時，便可能變得尤其危險。在此，危險並不在於人可能相信自己正在與另一個人溝通，而是在於他們可能逐漸失去建立真誠人際聯繫的渴望本身。
blocks.html: 100. In light of what has been said, we can better understand why AI can be a valuable tool and, at the same time, why it calls for a measured and vigilant approach. In recent years, its private use has expanded significantly, prompting growing reflection on both the opportunities it offers and the risks tied to its rapid spread. In personal use, three aspects in particular deserve careful consideration: the ease with which results are obtained, the impression of objectivity and the simulation of human communication. The speed and simplicity with which information, complex analyses, media content and practical assistance can be accessed undoubtedly makes life easier. Yet they can also encourage excessive reliance and the search for ready-made answers, and weaken personal creativity and judgment. The apparent objectivity of the responses and suggestions these systems provide can lead us to overlook the fact that they reflect the cultural assumptions of those who designed and trained them, with all their strengths and limitations. The artificial imitation of positive human communication — words of advice, empathy, friendship and even love — can be engaging and at times genuinely helpful. However, for less discerning users, it can also be misleading, creating the illusion of a relationship with a real personal subject. When words are simulated, they do not build genuine relationships, but only their appearance. The artificial imitation of care or support can become particularly risky when it enters contexts where real relationships and emotional bonds are lacking. Here, the danger is not so much that a person may believe they are communicating with another person, but rather that they may gradually lose the very desire to form genuine human connections.

blocks.en: Broadening our perspective to the use of AI in society, we see that it is now embedded in decision-making processes across many sectors and at multiple levels: in communication, management and control. The gains in efficiency and the potential to improve certain services are clear, yet rapidly and uncritically adopting them exposes us to a range of risks, including the tendency to overlook the environmental impact. Current AI systems require enormous amounts of energy and water, significantly influencing carbon dioxide emissions, and place heavy demands on natural resources. As their complexity increases, especially in the case of large language models, the need for computing power and storage capacity grows too, which requires an extensive network of machines, cables, data centers and energy-intensive infrastructure. For this reason, it is essential to develop more sustainable technological solutions that reduce environmental impact and help protect our common home. [124]
blocks.zh: 將視野擴展至人工智能在社會中的應用，我們看到它目前已嵌入許多領域及多個層面的決策過程之中：在通訊、管理及監控方面。效率的提升及改善某些服務的潛力固然顯而易見，然而過急且不加批判地採用這些技術，卻使我們暴露於一系列風險之中，包括傾向於忽視其對環境的影響。目前的人工智能系統消耗極大量的能源和水，顯著影響二氧化碳排放，並對自然資源造成沉重需求。隨著其複雜性不斷增加，尤其是大型語言模型的情況，對運算能力和儲存容量的需求也隨之增長，這需要廣泛的機器、電纜、數據中心及耗能巨大的基礎設施網絡。為此緣故，開發更具可持續性的技術解決方案，以減少環境影響並有助保護我們的共同家園，實屬至關重要。[124]
blocks.html: 101. Broadening our perspective to the use of AI in society, we see that it is now embedded in decision-making processes across many sectors and at multiple levels: in communication, management and control. The gains in efficiency and the potential to improve certain services are clear, yet rapidly and uncritically adopting them exposes us to a range of risks, including the tendency to overlook the environmental impact. Current AI systems require enormous amounts of energy and water, significantly influencing carbon dioxide emissions, and place heavy demands on natural resources. As their complexity increases, especially in the case of large language models, the need for computing power and storage capacity grows too, which requires an extensive network of machines, cables, data centers and energy-intensive infrastructure. For this reason, it is essential to develop more sustainable technological solutions that reduce environmental impact and help protect our common home. [124]

blocks.en: Responsibility, transparency and the governance of AI
blocks.zh: 人工智能的責任、透明度與管治
blocks.html: Responsibility, transparency and the governance of AI

blocks.en: The use of AI is never a purely technical matter: when it enters processes that affect people’s lives, it touches on rights, opportunities, status and freedom. Important and sensitive decisions — concerning employment, credit, access to public services or even a person’s reputation — risk being fully delegated to automated systems that do not know “compassion, mercy, forgiveness, and above all, the hope that people are able to change,” [125] and can therefore give rise to new forms of exclusion. There are clearly harmful uses, such as the manipulation of information or violations of privacy. Yet there is also a subtler danger, for when AI systems present themselves as neutral and objective, they end up reflecting and reinforcing the stereotypes or ideological bias of their designers and developers.
blocks.zh: 人工智能的使用絕非純粹的技術問題：當它進入影響人的生命的過程時，便觸及權利、機會、地位和自由。重要而敏感的決定——關乎就業、信貸、取得公共服務的門徑，乃至一個人的名譽——都有被完全委託給自動化系統的風險，而這些系統並不認識「同情、憐憫、寬恕，以及最重要的，就是人能夠改變的希望」[125]，因此可能產生新型式的排斥。有些用途顯然是有害的，例如操縱資訊或侵犯隱私。然而，還有一種更微妙隱伏的危險，因為當人工智能系統將自己呈現為中立客觀時，它們最終會反映並強化其設計者和開發者所持有的定型觀念或意識形態偏見。
blocks.html: 102. The use of AI is never a purely technical matter: when it enters processes that affect people’s lives, it touches on rights, opportunities, status and freedom. Important and sensitive decisions — concerning employment, credit, access to public services or even a person’s reputation — risk being fully delegated to automated systems that do not know “compassion, mercy, forgiveness, and above all, the hope that people are able to change,” [125] and can therefore give rise to new forms of exclusion. There are clearly harmful uses, such as the manipulation of information or violations of privacy. Yet there is also a subtler danger, for when AI systems present themselves as neutral and objective, they end up reflecting and reinforcing the stereotypes or ideological bias of their designers and developers.

blocks.en: Indeed, entrusting an algorithm in practice with the power to select who is worthy or not, without anyone bearing responsibility for that judgment, is to hand over the task of redefining the boundaries of human possibilities. In this process, political responsibility is also lost, not just empathy toward those excluded, which can, after all, be simulated. The exclusion of the vulnerable becomes cloaked in a veneer of neutrality and objectivity, against which it becomes difficult to raise objections. In this way, injustice goes unnoticed, and compassion, mercy and forgiveness — understood not as mere appearances but as real political actions — gradually disappear from view.
blocks.zh: 在實踐中將甄別誰有價值、誰無價值的權力交託給一個演算法，卻無任何人為該判斷承擔責任，無異於將重新界定人類可能性的疆界這項任務拱手交出。在此過程中，政治責任亦隨之喪失，而不僅是對被排斥者的同理心——畢竟那同理心是可以被模擬的。對弱勢者的排斥被披上一層中立與客觀的外衣，使人難以對此提出反對。如此一來，不公義就在不知不覺中發生，而同情、憐憫和寬恕——這些不應被視為表面的偽裝，而是真實的政治行動——便逐漸從視野中消失。
blocks.html: 103. Indeed, entrusting an algorithm in practice with the power to select who is worthy or not, without anyone bearing responsibility for that judgment, is to hand over the task of redefining the boundaries of human possibilities. In this process, political responsibility is also lost, not just empathy toward those excluded, which can, after all, be simulated. The exclusion of the vulnerable becomes cloaked in a veneer of neutrality and objectivity, against which it becomes difficult to raise objections. In this way, injustice goes unnoticed, and compassion, mercy and forgiveness — understood not as mere appearances but as real political actions — gradually disappear from view.

blocks.en: From this follows a simple but compelling consequence: we cannot consider AI to be morally neutral. In reality, every technical tool embodies choices and priorities through what it measures, ignores and optimizes, and how it classifies people and situations. If a system is designed or used in a way that treats some lives as less worthy, or excludes them without the possibility of appeal, then it is not merely a tool “to be used well,” since it has already introduced criteria that contradict the inalienable dignity of the human person. For this reason, ethical discernment cannot be limited to asking whether we are using a system for good or bad purposes; it must also examine how that system is designed and what vision of the human person and society is embedded in the data and models that guide it. [126]
blocks.zh: 由此得出一個簡單卻具說服力的結論：我們不能視人工智能為道德上中立的。事實上，每一項技術工具都透過它所衡量、忽略和優化的內容，以及它對人和處境進行分類的方式，體現出特定的選擇和優先次序。如果一個系統在設計或使用上，將某些生命視為較不值得尊重，或將他們排除在外而不容申訴，那麼它就不僅是一件「需要善用」的工具而已，因為它已引入了與人不可剝奪的尊嚴相抵觸的準則。為此，倫理分辨不應僅限於追問我們使用一個系統是出於善意還是惡意的目的；它還必須審視該系統是如何設計的，以及在引導它的數據和模型中，嵌入了怎樣的關於人和社會的願景。[126]
blocks.html: 104. From this follows a simple but compelling consequence: we cannot consider AI to be morally neutral. In reality, every technical tool embodies choices and priorities through what it measures, ignores and optimizes, and how it classifies people and situations. If a system is designed or used in a way that treats some lives as less worthy, or excludes them without the possibility of appeal, then it is not merely a tool “to be used well,” since it has already introduced criteria that contradict the inalienable dignity of the human person. For this reason, ethical discernment cannot be limited to asking whether we are using a system for good or bad purposes; it must also examine how that system is designed and what vision of the human person and society is embedded in the data and models that guide it. [126]

blocks.en: For AI to respect human dignity and truly serve the common good, responsibility must be clearly defined at every stage: from those who design and develop these systems to those who use them and rely on them for concrete decisions. In many cases, however, the internal processes leading to a result remain opaque, making it harder to assign responsibility and correct errors. This is where accountability becomes crucial: the possibility of identifying who must “account” for decisions, justify them, monitor them, and, when necessary, challenge them and remedy any harm caused. [127]
blocks.zh: 要讓人工智能尊重人性尊嚴並真正服務於公益，在每一個階段都必須清晰界定責任：從設計和開發這些系統的人，到使用並依賴它們作出具體決定的人。然而在許多情況下，通往結果的內部過程仍然是不透明的，這使得追究責任和糾正錯誤變得更加困難。這正是問責制變得至關重要的原因：必須有可能確定誰該為決策「負責」、為決策提出理據、進行監察，並在必要時提出質疑以及對所造成的損害作出補救。[127]
blocks.html: 105. For AI to respect human dignity and truly serve the common good, responsibility must be clearly defined at every stage: from those who design and develop these systems to those who use them and rely on them for concrete decisions. In many cases, however, the internal processes leading to a result remain opaque, making it harder to assign responsibility and correct errors. This is where accountability becomes crucial: the possibility of identifying who must “account” for decisions, justify them, monitor them, and, when necessary, challenge them and remedy any harm caused. [127]

blocks.en: Calling for prudence, rigorous evaluation and even, at times, a slower pace in adopting AI does not mean opposing progress; instead, it is an exercise of responsible care for the human family. This need is all the more urgent given the frequent imbalance between the speed of technological growth and the slower development of awareness, norms, safeguards and institutions capable of governing its effects. It is not enough to invoke ethics in the abstract; robust legal frameworks, independent oversight, informed users and a political system that does not abdicate its responsibility are required. Otherwise, change will be governed only by technocratic thinking and presented as necessary and inevitable, ultimately imposing rules shaped by those who control data, infrastructure and computing power.
blocks.zh: 呼籲在採用人工智能時保持審慎、嚴格評估，甚至在某些時候放緩步伐，並非反對進步；相反，這是對人類大家庭負責任的關懷之舉。鑑於科技發展的速度與能夠治理其影響的意識、規範、保障措施及制度的較緩慢發展之間經常失衡，這一需要便愈顯迫切。僅僅抽象地訴諸倫理是不夠的；我們需要健全的法律框架、獨立的監督機制、具備知情能力的用者，以及一個不放棄自身責任的政治體系。否則，變革將只由技術官僚思維所主導，並被呈現為必要且無可避免，最終強加由那些掌控數據、基礎設施和計算能力的人所制定的規則。
blocks.html: 106. Calling for prudence, rigorous evaluation and even, at times, a slower pace in adopting AI does not mean opposing progress; instead, it is an exercise of responsible care for the human family. This need is all the more urgent given the frequent imbalance between the speed of technological growth and the slower development of awareness, norms, safeguards and institutions capable of governing its effects. It is not enough to invoke ethics in the abstract; robust legal frameworks, independent oversight, informed users and a political system that does not abdicate its responsibility are required. Otherwise, change will be governed only by technocratic thinking and presented as necessary and inevitable, ultimately imposing rules shaped by those who control data, infrastructure and computing power.

blocks.en: We cannot be satisfied with merely calling for the moralization of machines — the so-called “alignment” of AI with human values — without also having the courage to insist on a further condition: the possibility of openly discussing the ethical frameworks involved and subjecting them to shared standards of social justice. Otherwise, those who control AI will impose their own moral vision, which will become the invisible infrastructure of these systems. A more moral AI is not enough if that morality is determined by a few. What is needed is a more active political involvement that is capable of slowing things down when everything is accelerating, and of protecting the opportunities for communities still to be able to participate and ask questions.
blocks.zh: 我們不能僅滿足於呼籲將機械道德化——即所謂的人工智能與人類價值的「對齊」——卻沒有勇氣堅持另一個條件，即：必須能夠公開討論所牽涉的倫理框架，並將之置於共同的社會正義標準之下進行審視。否則，控制人工智能的人便會將其自身的道德觀強加於人，使之成為這些系統的無形基礎設施。倘若此道德觀由少數人所決定，一個「更有道德」的人工智能是不足夠的。我們所需要的，是更積極的政治參與，這種參與能在一切加速前進時使之放緩，並保護各團體仍能參與和提問的機會。
blocks.html: 107. We cannot be satisfied with merely calling for the moralization of machines — the so-called “alignment” of AI with human values — without also having the courage to insist on a further condition: the possibility of openly discussing the ethical frameworks involved and subjecting them to shared standards of social justice. Otherwise, those who control AI will impose their own moral vision, which will become the invisible infrastructure of these systems. A more moral AI is not enough if that morality is determined by a few. What is needed is a more active political involvement that is capable of slowing things down when everything is accelerating, and of protecting the opportunities for communities still to be able to participate and ask questions.

blocks.en: In fact, as with every major technological shift, AI tends to amplify the power of those who already possess economic resources, expertise and access to data. In light of the common good and the universal destination of goods, this raises serious concerns, since small but highly influential groups can shape information and consumption patterns, influence democratic processes and steer economic dynamics to their own advantage, undermining social justice and solidarity among peoples. For this reason, it is essential that the use of AI, especially when it touches on public goods and fundamental rights, be guided by clear criteria and effective oversight, grounded in participation and subsidiarity. Communities and intermediary organizations must not be reduced to passive recipients of decisions made elsewhere; they must be able to contribute to discernment and oversight. Moreover, ownership of data cannot be left solely in private hands but must be appropriately regulated. Data is the product of many contributors and should not be treated as something to be sold off or entrusted to a select few. It is necessary to think creatively in order to manage data as a common or shared good, in a spirit of participation, as Saint John Paul II already suggested regarding collective goods. [128]
blocks.zh: 事實上，正如每一次重大的技術變革一樣，人工智能傾向於擴大那些已擁有經濟資源、專業知識和數據獲取權的人的力量。從公益和財物的普遍歸向來看，這引發了嚴重的憂慮，因為一些規模雖小但極具影響力的群體能夠左右資訊和消費模式、影響民主進程，並為了自身利益操縱經濟動態，從而損害社會正義和各民族之間的團結。為此，至關重要的是，對人工智能的使用——尤其是當它涉及公共財物和基本權利時——必須以清晰的標準和有效的監督來引導，並建基於參與和輔助性原則之上。團體和中間組織不應淪為他方決策的被動接受者；它們必須能夠為明辨和監督作出貢獻。此外，數據的所有權不能只掌握在私人手中，而必須受到適當的規範。數據是許多貢獻者的產物，不應被視為可供出售或委託給少數特權者的東西。我們有必要以創意的方式思考，本著參與的精神，將數據作為一種共同或共享的財物來管理，正如聖若望保祿二世對集體財物所曾建議的那樣。[128]
blocks.html: 108. In fact, as with every major technological shift, AI tends to amplify the power of those who already possess economic resources, expertise and access to data. In light of the common good and the universal destination of goods, this raises serious concerns, since small but highly influential groups can shape information and consumption patterns, influence democratic processes and steer economic dynamics to their own advantage, undermining social justice and solidarity among peoples. For this reason, it is essential that the use of AI, especially when it touches on public goods and fundamental rights, be guided by clear criteria and effective oversight, grounded in participation and subsidiarity. Communities and intermediary organizations must not be reduced to passive recipients of decisions made elsewhere; they must be able to contribute to discernment and oversight. Moreover, ownership of data cannot be left solely in private hands but must be appropriately regulated. Data is the product of many contributors and should not be treated as something to be sold off or entrusted to a select few. It is necessary to think creatively in order to manage data as a common or shared good, in a spirit of participation, as Saint John Paul II already suggested regarding collective goods. [128]

blocks.en: The principles of Social Doctrine offer a framework for understanding this new reality. In a world where data, computational resources and regulatory influence remain in the hands of a few, to speak of the common good means exposing this new form of epistemic, economic and political asymmetry and naming the new monopolies of AI. To speak of the universal destination of goods means finding ways of ensuring universal access to both technologies and the education needed to use them. To speak of subsidiarity calls for protecting the ability of communities to make choices and corrections, rather than confining their role to mere oversight after the standards have been set elsewhere. To speak of solidarity obliges us to recognize the hidden, often exploited workers, who sustain algorithmic systems. To speak of justice requires questioning the global distribution of power that decides who in fact can train these models and who is merely subjected to them. Likewise, it means acknowledging that social justice is not only a goal to be safeguarded after technologies are deployed, but a condition that must shape their very design from the outset.
blocks.zh: 教會社會訓導的原則為理解這個新的現實提供了框架。在數據、計算資源和監管影響力仍掌握在少數人手中的世界裡，談論公益，意味著揭露這種新形式的認知、經濟和政治不對稱，並指出人工智能的新壟斷。談論財物的普遍共享，意味著尋找途徑，確保人們既能普遍獲取這些技術，也能獲得使用這些技術所需的教育。談論輔助原則，呼籲保護各團體作出選擇和糾正的能力，而非將其角色局限於在他處已訂定標準之後的單純監督。談論團結互助，迫使我們承認那些維持演算法系統運作、卻往往遭受剝削的隱藏勞動者。談論正義，要求我們質疑全球權力分配，究竟是誰實際上能訓練這些模型，而誰只是被迫服從於它們。同樣地，這意味著要承認，社會正義不僅是在技術部署之後需要維護的目標，更是必須從一開始就塑造其設計的條件。
blocks.html: 109. The principles of Social Doctrine offer a framework for understanding this new reality. In a world where data, computational resources and regulatory influence remain in the hands of a few, to speak of the common good means exposing this new form of epistemic, economic and political asymmetry and naming the new monopolies of AI. To speak of the universal destination of goods means finding ways of ensuring universal access to both technologies and the education needed to use them. To speak of subsidiarity calls for protecting the ability of communities to make choices and corrections, rather than confining their role to mere oversight after the standards have been set elsewhere. To speak of solidarity obliges us to recognize the hidden, often exploited workers, who sustain algorithmic systems. To speak of justice requires questioning the global distribution of power that decides who in fact can train these models and who is merely subjected to them. Likewise, it means acknowledging that social justice is not only a goal to be safeguarded after technologies are deployed, but a condition that must shape their very design from the outset.

blocks.en: Finally, I would like to employ the expression “to disarm,” which is close to my heart. Disarming AI means freeing it from the mentality of “armed” competition, which today is not limited simply to the military context, but is also an economic and cognitive phenomenon. This entails a race for ever more powerful algorithms and larger datasets, driven by the desire to secure geopolitical or commercial dominance. To disarm means discrediting the assumption that technical power automatically confers the right to govern. To disarm does not mean rejecting technology, but preventing it from dominating humanity. It means freeing technology from monopolistic control and opening it to discussion and debate, therefore making it human-friendly and restoring it to the plurality of human cultures and ways of life. Our task today is not only ethical or technical. It is ecological in the deepest sense, for it concerns a new dimension of our common home. AI is already an environment in which we are immersed, as well as a force with which we must engage. For this reason, merely regulating it is insufficient; it must be disarmed, welcoming and accessible.
blocks.zh: 最後，我願使用「解除武裝」這個令我深感親切的表達。為人工智能解除武裝，意味著使其擺脫「武裝化」競爭的心態，這種競爭今日已不僅限於軍事領域，更是一種經濟和認知現象。這是一場追求更強大演算法和更大數據集的競賽，驅動力是為了確保地緣政治或商業主導地位的慾望。解除武裝意味著否定這樣一種假設：技術力量自動賦予治理的權利。解除武裝並不意味著拒絕技術，而是要防止技術支配人類。這意味著使技術擺脫壟斷性控制，使之向討論和辯論開放，從而使其對人類友善，並將其重新納入人類文化的多元性與生活方式的豐富含涵中。我們今日的任務不僅是倫理性的或技術性的，更在最深層意義上是一項生態性的任務，因為它關涉到我們共同家園的一個新維度。人工智能已然是我們沉浸其中的一個環境，也是一股我們必須與之互動的力量。為此，僅僅對其加以規範是不夠的；它必須被解除武裝，成為受歡迎的、可親近的。
blocks.html: 110. Finally, I would like to employ the expression “to disarm,” which is close to my heart. Disarming AI means freeing it from the mentality of “armed” competition, which today is not limited simply to the military context, but is also an economic and cognitive phenomenon. This entails a race for ever more powerful algorithms and larger datasets, driven by the desire to secure geopolitical or commercial dominance. To disarm means discrediting the assumption that technical power automatically confers the right to govern. To disarm does not mean rejecting technology, but preventing it from dominating humanity. It means freeing technology from monopolistic control and opening it to discussion and debate, therefore making it human-friendly and restoring it to the plurality of human cultures and ways of life. Our task today is not only ethical or technical. It is ecological in the deepest sense, for it concerns a new dimension of our common home. AI is already an environment in which we are immersed, as well as a force with which we must engage. For this reason, merely regulating it is insufficient; it must be disarmed, welcoming and accessible.

blocks.en: I wish to address a special appeal to those who develop artificial intelligence. In one sense, technological innovation can represent human participation in the divine act of creation. Developers, therefore, bear a particular ethical and spiritual responsibility, for every design choice reflects a vision of humanity. Just as the creator of an artistic or literary work must consider the values it conveys, so developers are called to embed values in their projects with due seriousness: with transparency, responsibility toward affected communities and careful attention to ensuring that what is being cultivated is a genuine good.
blocks.zh: 我願特別向那些研發人工智能的人士發出呼籲。從某種意義上說，科技創新可以代表人類對天主創造工程的參與。因此，研發者肩負著特殊的倫理與屬靈責任，因為每一個設計選擇都反映著某種對人類的觀念。正如藝術或文學作品的創作者必須考慮其作品所傳遞的價值，研發者也蒙召以應有的嚴肅態度將價值嵌入他們的項目之中：秉持透明度、對受影響群體的責任感，並謹慎留意確保所培育的是真正的善。
blocks.html: 111. I wish to address a special appeal to those who develop artificial intelligence. In one sense, technological innovation can represent human participation in the divine act of creation. Developers, therefore, bear a particular ethical and spiritual responsibility, for every design choice reflects a vision of humanity. Just as the creator of an artistic or literary work must consider the values it conveys, so developers are called to embed values in their projects with due seriousness: with transparency, responsibility toward affected communities and careful attention to ensuring that what is being cultivated is a genuine good.

blocks.en: What must not be lost
blocks.zh: 不可失落之事
blocks.html: What must not be lost

blocks.en: Having considered the issues of responsibility and governance of AI, we must now return to our central question: what does it mean to safeguard our humanity? The risk extends beyond the misuse of certain technologies. More gravely, the pervasive technocratic paradigm in which we are immersed, and that is amplified by the digital revolution and AI, threatens to normalize an anti-human vision. In that vision, the fullness of life is equated with having more, reducing weakness, eliminating uncertainty and exerting total control. When efficiency becomes the ultimate measure of value, human beings are tempted to see themselves as a project to be optimized rather than as persons called to relationship and communion.
blocks.zh: 在探討了人工智能的責任與治理問題後，我們現在必須回到我們的核心問題：維護我們的人性意味著甚麼？這個風險不僅限於某些技術的濫用。更嚴重的是，我們身處其中、且因數碼革命和人工智能而日益加劇的普遍技術官僚範式，正威脅要將一種反人性的視野正常化。在這種視野中，生命的圓滿等同於擁有更多、減少軟弱、消除不確定性以及行使全面的控制。當效率成為價值的最終標準時，人便會被誘惑將自己視為一個有待優化的項目，而非蒙召走向關係與共融的位格。
blocks.html: 112. Having considered the issues of responsibility and governance of AI, we must now return to our central question: what does it mean to safeguard our humanity? The risk extends beyond the misuse of certain technologies. More gravely, the pervasive technocratic paradigm in which we are immersed, and that is amplified by the digital revolution and AI, threatens to normalize an anti-human vision. In that vision, the fullness of life is equated with having more, reducing weakness, eliminating uncertainty and exerting total control. When efficiency becomes the ultimate measure of value, human beings are tempted to see themselves as a project to be optimized rather than as persons called to relationship and communion.

blocks.en: In reality, elevating any single dimension of human existence to an absolute is always a mistake. Indeed, disorder does not arise only from scarcity; even unchecked growth can give rise to impoverishment. In an ecosystem, balance is disrupted when one species expands at the expense of others; in human life, something similar occurs when one faculty claims to be the measure of everything. Thus, intelligence, when absolutized, overshadows other essential dimensions of life, such as affection, the will, commitment and relationships. Similarly, technical power, if left unbalanced, does not make us more capable; it makes us more isolated and more vulnerable to being dominated and excluded. This critical point does not oppose intelligence, but serves as a reminder that when intelligence becomes self-referential, its true purpose of serving life and the human person is lost.
blocks.zh: 實際上，將人類存有的任何單一幅度絕對化，總是一個錯誤。的確，混亂不僅源於匱乏；不受約束的增長同樣會導致貧乏。在生態系統中，當一個物種以犧牲其他物種為代價擴張時，平衡便遭破壞；在人性生活中，當某一官能自詡為萬物的尺度時，類似的情況便會發生。因此，理智一旦被絕對化，便會掩蓋生命的其他基本幅度，如情感、意志、委身和人際關係。同樣地，技術的力量若失去平衡，並不會使我們更有能力，反而使我們更加孤立，更容易被支配和排斥。這一批判性要點並非反對理智，而是提醒我們：當理智變得自我參照時，它便失去了服務生命和人的真正目的。
blocks.html: 113. In reality, elevating any single dimension of human existence to an absolute is always a mistake. Indeed, disorder does not arise only from scarcity; even unchecked growth can give rise to impoverishment. In an ecosystem, balance is disrupted when one species expands at the expense of others; in human life, something similar occurs when one faculty claims to be the measure of everything. Thus, intelligence, when absolutized, overshadows other essential dimensions of life, such as affection, the will, commitment and relationships. Similarly, technical power, if left unbalanced, does not make us more capable; it makes us more isolated and more vulnerable to being dominated and excluded. This critical point does not oppose intelligence, but serves as a reminder that when intelligence becomes self-referential, its true purpose of serving life and the human person is lost.

blocks.en: The quality of a civilization is measured not by the power of its means, but by the care it is able to offer, by its ability to recognize the other as a face not merely as a function. The ability to care for one another is a fundamental dimension of our humanity, one that is learned and mastered through lived experience. Reading stories to a child, offering company to an elderly person and arranging a home so that it is welcoming are simple gestures often rooted in family life. They teach us to value care at a societal level and train us to recognize others as persons worthy of attention. Technology can also support this mutual care between people, for example, by providing tools that help us anticipate and organize things, without undermining human freedom and judgment. After all, human beings are the subjects of relationships and responsible for their own decisions.
blocks.zh: 一個文明的素質，不在於其工具的威力，而在於它所能提供的關懷，在於它能夠將他人視為一張面容而非僅僅一個功能的能力。彼此關懷的能力是我們人性的一個基本向度，是透過生活的經驗學習與掌握的。為孩子講故事、陪伴長者、將家居佈置得溫馨好客——這些都是往往扎根於家庭生活中的簡單舉動。它們教導我們在社會層面上珍視關懷，並訓練我們將他人視為值得關注的人。科技同樣可以支援人與人之間這種互相關懷，例如提供工具幫助我們預測和安排事務，而不損害人的自由和判斷。畢竟，人是關係的主體，並為自己的決定負責。
blocks.html: 114. The quality of a civilization is measured not by the power of its means, but by the care it is able to offer, by its ability to recognize the other as a face not merely as a function. The ability to care for one another is a fundamental dimension of our humanity, one that is learned and mastered through lived experience. Reading stories to a child, offering company to an elderly person and arranging a home so that it is welcoming are simple gestures often rooted in family life. They teach us to value care at a societal level and train us to recognize others as persons worthy of attention. Technology can also support this mutual care between people, for example, by providing tools that help us anticipate and organize things, without undermining human freedom and judgment. After all, human beings are the subjects of relationships and responsible for their own decisions.

blocks.en: Underlying narratives: transhumanism and posthumanism
blocks.zh: 潛在的敘事：超人類主義與後人類主義
blocks.html: Underlying narratives: transhumanism and posthumanism

blocks.en: In an attempt to shed light on the cultural assumptions accompanying the ongoing digital revolution, I would now like to turn our attention to certain currents of thought that interpret progress as surpassing the human condition, and which are often grouped under the labels of transhumanism and posthumanism. These perspectives form the ideological background present in some centers of technological power and occupy the collective imagination in a simplified form, especially in the media and on social networks. They tend to foster enthusiasm for new technologies through a futuristic vision of an “enhanced human being” or “human-machine hybrid.”
blocks.zh: 為能闡明伴隨著當下數碼革命而來的文化預設，我想在此將我們的注意力轉向某些將進步理解為超越人類境況的思想潮流；這些思想通常被歸類於「超人類主義」和「後人類主義」的標籤之下。這些觀點構成了一些技術權力中心所存在的意識形態背景，並以簡化的形式佔據著集體想像，特別是在媒體和社交網絡中尤為明顯。它們往往透過一種關於「強化人類」或「人機混合體」的未來主義願景，來助長對新技術的狂熱。
blocks.html: 115. In an attempt to shed light on the cultural assumptions accompanying the ongoing digital revolution, I would now like to turn our attention to certain currents of thought that interpret progress as surpassing the human condition, and which are often grouped under the labels of transhumanism and posthumanism. These perspectives form the ideological background present in some centers of technological power and occupy the collective imagination in a simplified form, especially in the media and on social networks. They tend to foster enthusiasm for new technologies through a futuristic vision of an “enhanced human being” or “human-machine hybrid.”

blocks.en: Transhumanism and posthumanism encompass a range of currents and sensibilities, making it difficult to define them in a single, unambiguous way. They can be likened to an archipelago of conceptual “islands,” distinct yet connected by a common “sea” of assumptions, namely the central role of technology and the aspiration to transcend the limits of the human condition. In general, transhumanism envisions the enhancement of human beings through technologies — such as biomedicine, body engineering, devices and algorithms — with the aim of increasing performance and capabilities. Posthumanism, especially in its more radical forms, goes further: it challenges anthropocentrism and envisions a hybridization of human beings, machines and the environment, even anticipating a threshold where humanity surpasses itself in a new evolutionary stage. Even when such ideas remain largely speculative, they gain relevance by altering the collective imagination and thereby influence social, economic and political choices. [129]
blocks.zh: 超人類主義和後人類主義涵蓋了一系列的思潮和感受，難以用單一明確的方式加以界定。它們可以比作一個由概念性「島嶼」組成的群島，彼此分明，卻由一片共同的假設之「海」相連，即技術的核心角色，以及超越人類境況局限的渴望。一般而言，超人類主義設想通過各種技術——如生物醫學、身體工程、裝置和演算法——來增強人類，以提升其表現和能力。後人類主義，特別是在其更激進的形式中，則更進一步：它挑戰人類中心主義，並設想人類、機器和環境的混合雜交，甚至預期會達到一個門檻，使人類在一個新的進化階段中超越自身。即使這些想法在很大程度上仍是推測性的，它們卻因改變了集體想像而具有了現實意義，並由此影響了社會、經濟和政治的選擇。[129]
blocks.html: 116. Transhumanism and posthumanism encompass a range of currents and sensibilities, making it difficult to define them in a single, unambiguous way. They can be likened to an archipelago of conceptual “islands,” distinct yet connected by a common “sea” of assumptions, namely the central role of technology and the aspiration to transcend the limits of the human condition. In general, transhumanism envisions the enhancement of human beings through technologies — such as biomedicine, body engineering, devices and algorithms — with the aim of increasing performance and capabilities. Posthumanism, especially in its more radical forms, goes further: it challenges anthropocentrism and envisions a hybridization of human beings, machines and the environment, even anticipating a threshold where humanity surpasses itself in a new evolutionary stage. Even when such ideas remain largely speculative, they gain relevance by altering the collective imagination and thereby influence social, economic and political choices. [129]

blocks.en: From the perspective of the Church’s Social Doctrine, the key issue is not the use of technology as such, but the vision that underlies it. If the human being is treated as something to be perfected or surpassed, it becomes easier to accept that some lives are less useful, less desirable or less worthy. In the name of progress, “necessary sacrifices” may begin to be justified, placing the burden on the most vulnerable in pursuit of a supposed optimization of the species. In this regard, the aforementioned warning of Saint Paul VI retains great foresight: indeed, scientific and technological advances, when detached from moral and social progress, end up turning against humanity. [130] For this reason, a clear distinction must be made. It is one thing to integrate technology within a human-centered, relational vision; it is quite another to be guided by an outlook that devalues human limits and promises a purely technical form of “salvation.”
blocks.zh: 從教會社會訓導的角度來看，關鍵的問題不在於科技的使用本身，而在於其背後的願景。倘若人被視為有待完善或超越的對象，那麼就更容易接受有些生命是較無用處、較不受歡迎或較不值得的。以進步的名義，「必要的犧牲」可能開始被合理化，將重擔放在最脆弱者身上，以追求所謂的物種優化。在這方面，聖保祿六世先教宗的前述警告極具先見之明：事實上，科技進步一旦脫離道德和社會的進步，最終便會反噬人自身。[130] 因此，必須作出清晰的區分。將科技整合到一個以人為中心、重視關係的願景中，是一回事；而被一種貶低人性限度、並承諾一種純粹技術形式的「救贖」的觀念所引導，則是截然不同的另一回事。
blocks.html: 117. From the perspective of the Church’s Social Doctrine, the key issue is not the use of technology as such, but the vision that underlies it. If the human being is treated as something to be perfected or surpassed, it becomes easier to accept that some lives are less useful, less desirable or less worthy. In the name of progress, “necessary sacrifices” may begin to be justified, placing the burden on the most vulnerable in pursuit of a supposed optimization of the species. In this regard, the aforementioned warning of Saint Paul VI retains great foresight: indeed, scientific and technological advances, when detached from moral and social progress, end up turning against humanity. [130] For this reason, a clear distinction must be made. It is one thing to integrate technology within a human-centered, relational vision; it is quite another to be guided by an outlook that devalues human limits and promises a purely technical form of “salvation.”

blocks.en: The limit, the heart and the grandeur of the human person
blocks.zh: 人的限度、心靈與壯麗
blocks.html: The limit, the heart and the grandeur of the human person

blocks.en: Our relationship with life seems to be in crisis today. Everything that appears as a “limit” — incapacity, illness, old age, suffering, vulnerability — tends to be seen primarily as a defect to be corrected, rather than as a reality through which our humanity matures and opens itself to relationship. And yet we must remember that humanity flourishes not despite limitations, but often through them. The light of faith offers a perspective on reality that helps us recognize what we call the “contingency” of the things of this world. While it is right to strive to alleviate the suffering that marks human life, it is also wise to acknowledge our fundamental finitude, knowing that “religious experience, and in particular Christian faith, propose that we live, without oversimplification, this ambivalence between human greatness and limitation, interpreting it in the light of our original and fundamental relationship with God.” [131]
blocks.zh: 我們今天與生命的關係，似乎正處於危機之中。一切看似「限制」的事物——無能、疾病、衰老、苦難、脆弱——往往主要被視為有待矯正的缺陷，而非我們人性得以成熟並向關係開放的現實。然而我們必須銘記，人性之蓬勃昌盛，並非在於沒有局限，而往往是藉著局限而來。信德之光提供了一種審視現實的視角，幫助我們認清我們所謂的此世事物之「偶有性」。竭力減輕那標誌著人類生命的苦難固然是正當的，但承認我們根本上的有限性也是明智之舉，因為我們知道，「宗教經驗，特別是基督宗教信仰，提議我們在不作過度簡化的情況下，活出這種人性偉大與局限之間的矛盾，並在天主與我們原初而根本的關係光照下加以解讀。」[131]
blocks.html: 118. Our relationship with life seems to be in crisis today. Everything that appears as a “limit” — incapacity, illness, old age, suffering, vulnerability — tends to be seen primarily as a defect to be corrected, rather than as a reality through which our humanity matures and opens itself to relationship. And yet we must remember that humanity flourishes not despite limitations, but often through them. The light of faith offers a perspective on reality that helps us recognize what we call the “contingency” of the things of this world. While it is right to strive to alleviate the suffering that marks human life, it is also wise to acknowledge our fundamental finitude, knowing that “religious experience, and in particular Christian faith, propose that we live, without oversimplification, this ambivalence between human greatness and limitation, interpreting it in the light of our original and fundamental relationship with God.” [131]

blocks.en: It is precisely within our limitations that the following find a place: compassion, as well as a sincere concern for the needs of others; a generosity that can emerge even in the midst of darkness and failure; spiritual experience and the worship of God. We see this at many moments when our limits become tangible: when we face rejection, when we suffer the illness or loss of a loved one, when we encounter our own weakness or failure. Mysteriously, it is precisely in such moments that we can discover a new wisdom, tangibly experience the closeness of others and encounter the presence of the Lord.
blocks.zh: 正是在我們的限度內，以下種種得以安身：憐憫之心，以及對他人需要的真誠關懷；即使在黑暗與失敗之中仍能萌生的慷慨之情；靈性經驗和對天主的欽崇敬拜。我們在許多時刻看到這一點，就是當我們的限度變得具體可見之際：當我們面對拒絕時，當我們承受疾病或失去至親的痛苦時，當我們與自身的軟弱或失敗相遇時。奧妙的是，正是在這樣的時刻，我們能夠發現一種新的智慧，具體地體驗到他人的親近，並與上主的臨在相遇。
blocks.html: 119. It is precisely within our limitations that the following find a place: compassion, as well as a sincere concern for the needs of others; a generosity that can emerge even in the midst of darkness and failure; spiritual experience and the worship of God. We see this at many moments when our limits become tangible: when we face rejection, when we suffer the illness or loss of a loved one, when we encounter our own weakness or failure. Mysteriously, it is precisely in such moments that we can discover a new wisdom, tangibly experience the closeness of others and encounter the presence of the Lord.

blocks.en: Even when limitations are experienced as inner suffering, human wisdom teaches us not to deny or suppress it, but to integrate it. To eliminate suffering entirely would mean, in the end, extinguishing love and desire as well. Those who love and desire cannot avoid passing through trial and suffering; and over the years, we carry within us lessons that leave their mark like scars, the memories of a journey shaped by freedom and failure, dreams and disappointments. It is only thanks to the interplay of these elements that the wonders of the soul occur within us, allowing us to sense the richness of our humanity. [132] To renounce this adventure, both tragic and splendid, in the name of a presumed transcendence of all limits, could mean many things, but it would no longer be human.
blocks.zh: 即使當限制被體驗為內心的痛苦，人類的智慧教導我們不要否認或壓抑它，而要將其整合。徹底消除痛苦，最終也意味著熄滅愛與渴望。凡是愛和渴望的人，都無法避免經歷考驗和痛苦；隨著歲月流逝，我們在內心背負著那些如傷疤般留下印記的教訓，那是自由與失敗、夢想與幻滅交織而成的旅程的記憶。唯有透過這些元素之間的相互作用，靈魂的奇妙工程才能在我們內發生，使我們得以感受自身人性的豐盛。[132] 以一種假設的超越一切限制為名，放棄這場既悲壯又燦爛的歷險，可能意味著許多事情，但那將不再屬於人性。
blocks.html: 120. Even when limitations are experienced as inner suffering, human wisdom teaches us not to deny or suppress it, but to integrate it. To eliminate suffering entirely would mean, in the end, extinguishing love and desire as well. Those who love and desire cannot avoid passing through trial and suffering; and over the years, we carry within us lessons that leave their mark like scars, the memories of a journey shaped by freedom and failure, dreams and disappointments. It is only thanks to the interplay of these elements that the wonders of the soul occur within us, allowing us to sense the richness of our humanity. [132] To renounce this adventure, both tragic and splendid, in the name of a presumed transcendence of all limits, could mean many things, but it would no longer be human.

blocks.en: The moral corruption of our limitations as created beings — namely the evil that clearly agitates the human heart — ruins society and life, at times reaching extreme forms of inhumanity. Yet even these painful expressions of our limitations leave openings for the good. Even when persons dehumanize themselves and bring about tragedy, a small light continues to shine within humanity, one that can be rekindled, with God’s grace, along paths of conversion and reconciliation. As Viktor Frankl rightly observed, in moments of horror, “we have come to know man as he really is. After all, man is that being who invented the gas chambers of Auschwitz; however, he is also that being who entered those gas chambers upright, with the Lord’s Prayer or the Shema Yisrael on his lips.” [133]
blocks.zh: 我們作為受造物的有限性所導致的道德敗壞——即明顯攪動人心的邪惡——摧毀社會與生命，有時更達到極端非人道的程度。然而，即使是我們有限性這些痛苦的表現，也為善留下空隙。即使人在自我非人化並造成悲劇之時，一線微光仍繼續在人性的深處閃耀；這微光能藉著天主的恩寵，在皈依與和解的道路上重新燃起。正如維克多．弗蘭克（Viktor Frankl）所確切觀察到的，在恐怖的時刻，「我們認識到人的本來面目。畢竟，人既是發明奧斯威辛（Auschwitz）煤氣室的存有；然而，他也是挺直身軀走進那些煤氣室、口中唸著〈天主經〉或〈以色列！你要聽〉（Shema Yisrael）的存有。」[133]
blocks.html: 121. The moral corruption of our limitations as created beings — namely the evil that clearly agitates the human heart — ruins society and life, at times reaching extreme forms of inhumanity. Yet even these painful expressions of our limitations leave openings for the good. Even when persons dehumanize themselves and bring about tragedy, a small light continues to shine within humanity, one that can be rekindled, with God’s grace, along paths of conversion and reconciliation. As Viktor Frankl rightly observed, in moments of horror, “we have come to know man as he really is. After all, man is that being who invented the gas chambers of Auschwitz; however, he is also that being who entered those gas chambers upright, with the Lord’s Prayer or the Shema Yisrael on his lips.” [133]

blocks.en: Finitude, when truly accepted, does not diminish us but opens us to recognizing the face of God and others. Indeed, precisely because we experience limits — vulnerability, suffering and failure — we can recognize the inviolable dignity of every person, both our own and that of others. In this same experience, we remain capable of intuiting a fraternity greater than ourselves and of perceiving injustice as a scandal. Authentic culture and art preserve this spark, resisting the normalization of evil. For this reason, certain works have taken on an almost prophetic significance: Beethoven’s Ninth Symphony can be seen as a desire for unity; Guernica as a denunciation of dehumanization; Schindler’s List as a call not to consign the past to oblivion.
blocks.zh: 當人真正接納自身的有限性時，這並不會貶損我們，反而開啟我們去認出天主和他人的面容。的確，正因為我們體驗到各種限制——脆弱、痛苦和失敗——我們才能夠認出每一個人不可侵犯的尊嚴，包括我們自己的尊嚴和他人的尊嚴。在同一經驗中，我們依然能夠直覺到一種比我們自身更大的兄弟情誼，並將不公義視為一件令人震驚的惡事。真正的文化與藝術保存著這道火花，抵抗邪惡被正常化的趨勢。為此，某些作品幾乎具有了先知性的意義：貝多芬的第九交響曲可被視為對合一的渴望；《格爾尼卡》是對非人化的譴責；《舒特拉的名單》則是一個呼籲，叫我們不要將過去遺忘於虛無之中。
blocks.html: 122. Finitude, when truly accepted, does not diminish us but opens us to recognizing the face of God and others. Indeed, precisely because we experience limits — vulnerability, suffering and failure — we can recognize the inviolable dignity of every person, both our own and that of others. In this same experience, we remain capable of intuiting a fraternity greater than ourselves and of perceiving injustice as a scandal. Authentic culture and art preserve this spark, resisting the normalization of evil. For this reason, certain works have taken on an almost prophetic significance: Beethoven’s Ninth Symphony can be seen as a desire for unity; Guernica as a denunciation of dehumanization; Schindler’s List as a call not to consign the past to oblivion.

blocks.en: History does not appear solely as a record of human violence, but also as evidence that humanity is capable of creating institutions that protect our shared life. Over the past two centuries, this can be seen in several emblematic achievements: the founding of the International Committee of the Red Cross (1863), whose operational neutrality ensures compassionate care for all; the long process that led to the abolition of slavery, which represented not only a legal shift but a transformation of conscience; the establishment of the United Nations (1945) and the Universal Declaration of Human Rights (1948), which articulated a shared language for affirming, at least as a common ideal, the universality of human dignity; and the 1951 Refugee Convention, which recognizes the duty to protect those fleeing persecution and danger. In each of these cases, the desire for good took concrete shape in public contexts — laws, institutions and practices — capable of limiting the abuse of power and defending the vulnerable. Yet none of these developments emerged without encountering resistance, narrow interests or cultural inertia. Moral progress almost always unfolds through a long and demanding journey, often marked by setbacks. We need only think of stalled peace processes or the slow implementation of environmental commitments. The very fragility of these achievements highlights how precious the responsibility is of those who initiate and sustain them.
blocks.zh: 歷史不僅顯示為人類暴力的記錄，也見證了人類有能力創造保護我們共同生活的制度。在過去兩個世紀中，這一點體現在若干標誌性的成就上：國際紅十字委員會的成立（1863年），其運作上的中立性確保對所有人施以惻隱之心的照護；通往廢除奴隸制的漫長進程，這不僅代表法律上的轉變，更是良心的轉化；聯合國的建立（1945年）及《世界人權宣言》（1948年）的頒布，後者闡述了一種共同的語言，以肯定——至少作為一個共同的理想——人性尊嚴的普遍性；以及1951年的《難民公約》，該公約確認了保護那些逃離迫害與危險者的責任。在這些事例中的每一個，對善的渴求都在公共場域中——法律、制度與實踐——取得了具體的形式，能夠限制權力的濫用並保護弱小者。然而，這些發展沒有一項不是在遭遇抗拒、狹隘利益或文化惰性的情況下出現的。道德的進步幾乎總是經歷一段漫長而艱辛的旅程，往往充滿挫折。我們只需想一想那些停滯不前的和平進程，或環境承諾的緩慢落實。這些成就本身的脆弱性，正好凸顯出那些發起並維持這些成就的人所肩負的責任是何等寶貴。
blocks.html: 123. History does not appear solely as a record of human violence, but also as evidence that humanity is capable of creating institutions that protect our shared life. Over the past two centuries, this can be seen in several emblematic achievements: the founding of the International Committee of the Red Cross (1863), whose operational neutrality ensures compassionate care for all; the long process that led to the abolition of slavery, which represented not only a legal shift but a transformation of conscience; the establishment of the United Nations (1945) and the Universal Declaration of Human Rights (1948), which articulated a shared language for affirming, at least as a common ideal, the universality of human dignity; and the 1951 Refugee Convention, which recognizes the duty to protect those fleeing persecution and danger. In each of these cases, the desire for good took concrete shape in public contexts — laws, institutions and practices — capable of limiting the abuse of power and defending the vulnerable.  Yet none of these developments emerged without encountering resistance, narrow interests or cultural inertia. Moral progress almost always unfolds through a long and demanding journey, often marked by setbacks.
