We need only think of stalled peace processes or the slow implementation of environmental commitments. The very fragility of these achievements highlights how precious the responsibility is of those who initiate and sustain them.

blocks.en: Certain events make it clear that history can also change when individuals truly take the dignity of everyone seriously: the civil rights movement in the United States of America, closely associated with the testimony of Martin Luther King Jr., or the end of apartheid in South Africa following the release of Nelson Mandela and his decision not to surrender the future to hatred. In different contexts, many courageous and generous women have also stood out, including Saint Laura Montoya, Saint Teresa of Calcutta, Dorothy Day, Marie Skłodowska-Curie, Maria Montessori, Elisabeth Elliot, Wangari Maathai, Benazir Bhutto and countless others from every continent whose commitment has contributed to making history more humane.
blocks.zh: 某些事件清楚地表明，當人們真正認真地對待每一個人的尊嚴時，歷史也能夠轉變：美利堅合眾國的民權運動，與馬丁·路德·金（Martin Luther King Jr.）的見證密不可分；南非種族隔離政策的終結，則是在納爾遜·曼德拉（Nelson Mandela）獲釋，以及他決定不將未來交託給仇恨之後實現的。在不同的背景下，許多勇敢而慷慨的女性也脫穎而出，包括聖勞拉·蒙托亞（Saint Laura Montoya）、加爾各答的聖德肋撒（Saint Teresa of Calcutta）、多蘿西·戴（Dorothy Day）、瑪麗·斯克沃多夫斯卡-居里（Marie Skłodowska-Curie）、瑪利亞·蒙特梭利（Maria Montessori）、伊麗莎白·艾略特（Elisabeth Elliot）、旺加里·馬塔伊（Wangari Maathai）、貝娜齊爾·布托（Benazir Bhutto），以及來自各大洲的無數其他女性，她們的奉獻使歷史變得更加具有人性。
blocks.html: 124. Certain events make it clear that history can also change when individuals truly take the dignity of everyone seriously: the civil rights movement in the United States of America, closely associated with the testimony of Martin Luther King Jr., or the end of apartheid in South Africa following the release of Nelson Mandela and his decision not to surrender the future to hatred. In different contexts, many courageous and generous women have also stood out, including Saint Laura Montoya, Saint Teresa of Calcutta, Dorothy Day, Marie Skłodowska-Curie, Maria Montessori, Elisabeth Elliot, Wangari Maathai, Benazir Bhutto and countless others from every continent whose commitment has contributed to making history more humane.

blocks.en: Alongside these public signs, there is a more hidden but decisive story. We see it in religious communities that choose to serve in poor and dangerous places. We also see it in the martyrs of fraternity and justice, such as Saint Maximilian Mary Kolbe, Saint Oscar Romero and Blessed Enrique Angelelli; and in those witnesses who embodied the hope of the Gospel as well as human dignity amidst harsh, often inhumane conditions, such as Venerable Francis-Xavier Nguyễn Văn Thuận. Above all, it is visible in the “martyrs of everyday life” who care for, educate, accompany and comfort without fanfare, such as parents, nurses, doctors, volunteers and those who remain alongside an elderly person or an outcast. Their testimony demonstrates that goodness does not advance automatically, but requires the perseverance, memory and interior conversion necessary to begin anew, even after defeat.
blocks.zh: 除了這些公開的標記之外，還有一個較為隱蔽但更具決定性的故事。我們在那些選擇到貧窮和危險之地服務的宗教團體身上看到這個故事。我們也在那些為兄弟情誼和正義而殉道的人身上看到這個故事，例如聖馬希連·瑪利亞·高比、聖奧斯卡·羅梅羅和真福恩里克·安赫萊利；以及在那些於嚴酷、往往不人道的環境中，體現了福音的望德與人性尊嚴的見證人身上看到這個故事，例如可敬者方濟各·沙勿略·阮文順。最重要的是，這個故事可見於那些「日常生活中的殉道者」身上，他們默默無聞地照顧、教育、陪伴和安慰他人，例如父母、護士、醫生、義工，以及那些留守在長者或被遺棄者身邊的人。他們的見證表明，善不會自動前進，而是需要堅忍、記憶和內心的皈依，才能在即使經歷挫敗之後，重新開始。
blocks.html: 125. Alongside these public signs, there is a more hidden but decisive story. We see it in religious communities that choose to serve in poor and dangerous places.  We also see it in the martyrs of fraternity and justice, such as Saint Maximilian Mary Kolbe, Saint Oscar Romero and Blessed Enrique Angelelli; and in those witnesses who embodied the hope of the Gospel as well as human dignity amidst harsh, often inhumane conditions, such as Venerable Francis-Xavier Nguyễn Văn Thuận. Above all, it is visible in the “martyrs of everyday life” who care for, educate, accompany and comfort without fanfare, such as parents, nurses, doctors, volunteers and those who remain alongside an elderly person or an outcast. Their testimony demonstrates that goodness does not advance automatically, but requires the perseverance, memory and interior conversion necessary to begin anew, even after defeat.

blocks.en: It is this intertwining of just institutions, credible witnesses and daily fidelity that sustains hope and provides clear direction for technological progress without allowing the heart to regress. For this reason, humanity — in all its grandeur and woundedness — must never be replaced or surpassed. We can embrace the technological progress that alleviates suffering and unlocks new possibilities, provided that we do not abandon the very essence of our humanity, namely the capacity for relationship and love. This leads to a crucial question: if an authentic “more than human” exists, where is it to be found? The Christian faith answers that question by pointing to a fulfilment that does not arise from a technological divinization, but through God’s grace received in Christ.
blocks.zh: 正是這公義的制度、可信的見證與日常的忠信交織在一起，滋養著望德，並為科技的進步提供清晰的方向，同時不讓心靈倒退。為此緣故，人類——在其全部的壯麗與創傷中——絕不能被取代或超越。我們可以擁抱那些減輕痛苦、開啟新可能的科技進步，條件是我們不可摒棄我們人性的本質，即建立關係與愛的能力。這引向一個關鍵的問題：如果有一種真實的「超越人性」存在，它應在何處被尋見？基督信仰對這問題的回答，指向一種並非源於科技神化、而是透過在基督內領受的天主恩寵而來的滿全。
blocks.html: 126. It is this intertwining of just institutions, credible witnesses and daily fidelity that sustains hope and provides clear direction for technological progress without allowing the heart to regress. For this reason, humanity — in all its grandeur and woundedness — must never be replaced or surpassed. We can embrace the technological progress that alleviates suffering and unlocks new possibilities, provided that we do not abandon the very essence of our humanity, namely the capacity for relationship and love. This leads to a crucial question: if an authentic “more than human” exists, where is it to be found?  The Christian faith answers that question by pointing to a fulfilment that does not arise from a technological divinization, but through God’s grace received in Christ.

blocks.en: The authentic “more than human”: grace and Christian humanism
blocks.zh: 真正的「超越人性」：恩寵與基督徒人文主義
blocks.html: The authentic “more than human”: grace and Christian humanism

blocks.en: The expression “more than human” is not an exclusive domain of technological promise. For centuries, the Christian tradition has maintained that human beings are not confined by the boundaries of their own nature; rather, they are called to self-transcendence, not through an escape from reality or a contempt for their limitations, but through their fulfillment in love. Faith recognizes an openness toward the “beyond,” which originates as a gift from God. This transformation is a work of the Holy Spirit. As Saint Thomas Aquinas taught, this process of elevation and transformation “surpasses every capability of created nature,” [134] for an infinite disparity separates our finite nature from the life of God. [135] Nevertheless, it remains possible to enter into the heart of that inexhaustible life, even as we journey through the limitations of this world. The one who makes this passage possible can only be the Eternal One who gives of himself. Indeed, it is God himself who overcomes the “infinite” disproportion. [136] In him, the re-creation of the human person happens. “If anyone is in Christ, there is a new creation: everything old has passed away; see, everything has become new” ( 2 Cor 5:17).
blocks.zh: 「超越人性」這個表述並非科技許諾的專屬領域。多個世紀以來，基督教傳統一直堅持，人並非受其本性界限所局限；相反，人被召喚走向自我超越，不是透過逃避現實或輕蔑自身的限度，而是透過在愛內的圓滿實現。信仰認識到一種朝向「超越」的開放，這份開放源自天主的恩賜。這種轉化是聖神的工作。正如聖多瑪斯·阿奎那所教導的，這種提升與轉化的過程「超越一切受造本性的能力」，[134] 因為我們有限的本性與天主的生命之間存在著無限的差距。[135] 然而，即使在我們穿越此世種種限度的旅途中，我們仍有可能進入那無窮盡生命的核心。使這逾越成為可能的，只能是那位自我給予的永恆者。事實上，正是天主自己克服了那「無限的」不相稱。[136] 在祂內，人的重新創造得以發生。「誰若在基督內，他就是一個新受造物：舊的已成過去，看，都成了新的」（格後 5:17）。
blocks.html: 127. The expression “more than human” is not an exclusive domain of technological promise. For centuries, the Christian tradition has maintained that human beings are not confined by the boundaries of their own nature; rather, they are called to self-transcendence, not through an escape from reality or a contempt for their limitations, but through their fulfillment in love. Faith recognizes an openness toward the “beyond,” which originates as a gift from God. This transformation is a work of the Holy Spirit. As Saint Thomas Aquinas taught, this process of elevation and transformation “surpasses every capability of created nature,” [134] for an infinite disparity separates our finite nature from the life of God. [135] Nevertheless, it remains possible to enter into the heart of that inexhaustible life, even as we journey through the limitations of this world. The one who makes this passage possible can only be the Eternal One who gives of himself. Indeed, it is God himself who overcomes the “infinite” disproportion. [136] In him, the re-creation of the human person happens. “If anyone is in Christ, there is a new creation: everything old has passed away; see, everything has become new” ( 2 Cor 5:17).

blocks.en: When we embrace the possibility of transcending ourselves through God’s grace, we do not deny our nature, nor do we become less human. On the contrary, as Pope Francis explained, “We become fully human when we become more than human, when we let God bring us beyond ourselves in order to attain the fullest truth of our being.” [137] Herein lies the radical departure from Promethean dreams: what saves humanity is not enhanced self-sufficiency, but a relationship that liberates, a communion that transforms. In this light, a technology that merely classifies and optimizes what already exists can, however unintentionally, become an obstacle to change and growth. For an algorithm, an error is a flaw to be corrected; for a person, however, an error can be a catalyst for profound change. A person’s future is not calculable, but depends on one’s freedom — elevated by the inexhaustible grace of God — and on the relationships cultivated.
blocks.zh: 當我們接受藉著天主的恩寵超越自我的可能時，我們並不否認自己的本性，也不會變得不那麼具有人性。恰恰相反，正如教宗方濟各所闡釋的：「當我們變得超越人性，當我們讓天主帶領我們走出自我，以達至我們存在的最完整真理時，我們才成為完全的人。」[137] 這正是與普羅米修斯式夢想的徹底決裂：拯救人類的，不是增強的自我滿足，而是一種帶來解放的關係，一種轉化生命的共融。就此而言，僅僅對既存事物進行分類和優化的技術，無論其意圖如何，都可能成為改變和成長的障礙。對一個演算法而言，錯誤是需要修正的缺陷；然而對一個人來說，錯誤卻可以成為深刻轉變的催化劑。一個人的未來是無法計算的，它取決於個人的自由——這自由因天主無盡的恩寵而被提升——以及所培養的關係。
blocks.html: 128. When we embrace the possibility of transcending ourselves through God’s grace, we do not deny our nature, nor do we become less human. On the contrary, as Pope Francis explained, “We become fully human when we become more than human, when we let God bring us beyond ourselves in order to attain the fullest truth of our being.” [137] Herein lies the radical departure from Promethean dreams: what saves humanity is not enhanced self-sufficiency, but a relationship that liberates, a communion that transforms. In this light, a technology that merely classifies and optimizes what already exists can, however unintentionally, become an obstacle to change and growth. For an algorithm, an error is a flaw to be corrected; for a person, however, an error can be a catalyst for profound change. A person’s future is not calculable, but depends on one’s freedom — elevated by the inexhaustible grace of God — and on the relationships cultivated.

blocks.en: Two cities and two loves
blocks.zh: 兩個城、兩種愛
blocks.html: Two cities and two loves

blocks.en: Christian humanism does not reject science or technology, but embraces them with gratitude and realism, and grounds them within a higher vocation. The creative intelligence of humanity is a gift that can alleviate suffering and open up new possibilities, but it must remain ordered toward the common good, justice, the care of the vulnerable and creation. In this sense, the true alternative is not between enthusiasm and fear, but between two paths of development: a progress that serves individuals and peoples, or a progress that subjects them to the mentality of power. Ultimately, the key question remains the one posed by Saint John Paul II: does AI “make human life on earth ‘more human’ in every aspect of that life? Does it make it more worthy of man?” [138] If the answer is yes, then we can recognize it as an opportunity to be embraced responsibly, on a path of patient, shared reconstruction, akin to the rebuilding of Jerusalem narrated in the Book of Nehemiah. If, however, power grows while the heart withers and human bonds fray, then we are faced with a new form of Babel — a construction that is grandiose, yet fundamentally dehumanizing.
blocks.zh: 基督徒人文主義並不拒絕科學或技術，而是懷著感恩和現實的態度接納它們，並將它們奠基於一種更高的召叫之中。人類的創造性智慧是一份恩賜，能夠減輕痛苦並開啟新的可能性，但它必須始終朝向公益、正義、對弱勢群體和受造界的關懷。從這個意義上說，真正的抉擇並不在於熱情與恐懼之間，而是在於兩條發展道路之間：一種是服務於個人和民族的進步，另一種則是將他們置於權力心態支配之下的進步。歸根結柢，關鍵問題始終是聖若望保祿二世所提出的那個問題：人工智能是否「使人在世上的生活，在該生活的每一個層面上都『更合乎人性』？它是否使這生活更配得上人？」[138] 如果答案是肯定的，那麼我們便能認識到這是一個需要負責任地把握的機遇，它要求我們走上一條耐心、共同的修復之路，類似於《厄斯德拉下》（乃赫米雅）中所記述的耶路撒冷重建工程。然而，如果力量增長了，心靈卻枯萎了，人際關係也磨損了，那麼我們所面對的便是一種新形式的巴貝耳──一座宏偉壯觀，卻從根本上剝奪人性的建築。
blocks.html: 129. Christian humanism does not reject science or technology, but embraces them with gratitude and realism, and grounds them within a higher vocation. The creative intelligence of humanity is a gift that can alleviate suffering and open up new possibilities, but it must remain ordered toward the common good, justice, the care of the vulnerable and creation. In this sense, the true alternative is not between enthusiasm and fear, but between two paths of development: a progress that serves individuals and peoples, or a progress that subjects them to the mentality of power. Ultimately, the key question remains the one posed by Saint John Paul II: does AI “make human life on earth ‘more human’ in every aspect of that life? Does it make it more worthy of man?” [138] If the answer is yes, then we can recognize it as an opportunity to be embraced responsibly, on a path of patient, shared reconstruction, akin to the rebuilding of Jerusalem narrated in the Book of Nehemiah. If, however, power grows while the heart withers and human bonds fray, then we are faced with a new form of Babel — a construction that is grandiose, yet fundamentally dehumanizing.

blocks.en: Questioning this alternative path of progress and how we interpret and live it is ultimately a matter of examining our own hearts. The way we understand and shape relationships, work and institutions, in practice reveals our fundamental values. In the end, it all stems from what we hold most dear. This is a love that guides us as to what we truly cherish, both as individuals and as a society, and directs our lives and actions. Saint Augustine described human history as a struggle between two loves, which give rise to two ways of inhabiting the world and living together — or two “cities,” as it were: on the one hand, the love of God and neighbor; on the other, the exclusive love of self. “Two loves have built two cities: the earthly city, the love of self even to the contempt of God; the heavenly city, the love of God even to the contempt of self.” [139] As throughout history, these two loves continue to contend for dominance in our hearts today. The age of AI is no exception: the construction of Babel or the rebuilding of Jerusalem begins within each one of us.
blocks.zh: 質疑這條另類的進步道路，以及我們如何詮釋和實踐它，歸根結底是在審視我們自己的心。我們理解和塑造關係、工作及制度的方式，實際上揭示了我們的基本價值觀。最終，一切都源於我們視為最珍貴的事物。這是一種愛，引導著我們──無論是作為個人還是社會──明辨我們真正珍視的是什麼，並指引我們的生活和行動。聖奧斯定將人類歷史描述為兩種愛之間的鬥爭，這兩種愛產生了兩種居住於世界和共同生活的方式──或可說是兩座「城」：一方面是對天主和近人的愛；另一方面則是唯我獨尊的自愛。「兩種愛建造了兩座城：塵世之城，由愛自己乃至輕視天主所建造；天上之城，則由愛天主乃至輕視自己所建造。」[139] 正如貫穿整個歷史，這兩種愛今日仍在我心中爭奪主導權。人工智能時代亦不例外：巴貝耳的建造或耶路撒冷的重建，始於我們每一個人的內心。
blocks.html: 130. Questioning this alternative path of progress and how we interpret and live it is ultimately a matter of examining our own hearts. The way we understand and shape relationships, work and institutions, in practice reveals our fundamental values. In the end, it all stems from what we hold most dear. This is a love that guides us as to what we truly cherish, both as individuals and as a society, and directs our lives and actions. Saint Augustine described human history as a struggle between two loves, which give rise to two ways of inhabiting the world and living together — or two “cities,” as it were: on the one hand, the love of God and neighbor; on the other, the exclusive love of self. “Two loves have built two cities: the earthly city, the love of self even to the contempt of God; the heavenly city, the love of God even to the contempt of self.” [139] As throughout history, these two loves continue to contend for dominance in our hearts today. The age of AI is no exception: the construction of Babel or the rebuilding of Jerusalem begins within each one of us.

sections.title_zh: 在劇變時代守護人性——真理、勞動、自由

blocks.en: SAFEGUARDING HUMANITY AT A TIME OF TRANSFORMATION.
blocks.zh: 在轉型時代中守護人性。
blocks.html: SAFEGUARDING HUMANITY AT A TIME OF TRANSFORMATION.

blocks.en: Having outlined the context in which the challenge of technological transformation is situated, especially those linked to AI and to transhumanist and posthumanist currents, we cannot remain at the level of general analysis alone. When languages and tools change, so do everyday actions and social relationships. For this reason, we must focus on certain areas in which these transformations have particularly concrete, and at times tragic, consequences. In light of the principles of the Church’s Social Doctrine, the digital transformation invites us to rediscover truth as a common good, to protect the dignity of work and to safeguard freedom against all forms of dependence and commercialization.
blocks.zh: 在勾劃了科技轉型挑戰所處的脈絡之後，尤其是那些與人工智能、超人類主義及後人類主義潮流相關的挑戰，我們不能僅停留在一般性的分析層面。當語言和工具發生變化時，日常行動和社會關係也隨之改變。為此，我們必須聚焦於某些具體領域，在這些領域中，這些轉化帶來了格外具體、有時甚至是悲劇性的後果。根據教會社會訓導的原則，數碼轉型邀請我們重新發現真理作為一種公益，保護工作的尊嚴，並捍衛自由，使之免受一切形式的依賴與商品化之害。
blocks.html: 131. Having outlined the context in which the challenge of technological transformation is situated, especially those linked to AI and to transhumanist and posthumanist currents, we cannot remain at the level of general analysis alone. When languages and tools change, so do everyday actions and social relationships. For this reason, we must focus on certain areas in which these transformations have particularly concrete, and at times tragic, consequences. In light of the principles of the Church’s Social Doctrine, the digital transformation invites us to rediscover truth as a common good, to protect the dignity of work and to safeguard freedom against all forms of dependence and commercialization.

blocks.en: Truth as a common good
blocks.zh: 真理作為公益
blocks.html: Truth as a common good

blocks.en: Truth and democracy
blocks.zh: 真理與民主
blocks.html: Truth and democracy

blocks.en: The use of digital platforms and AI systems is driving profound changes in public and political communication. Tools that could foster dialogue and participation are often used to construct distorted narratives and blur the boundaries between truth and falsehood, mixing facts with opinions. Disinformation did not begin with AI, yet today it finds a powerful amplifier in AI. The ability to manipulate content, images and videos exposes people to biased or misleading perspectives. This problem has both cultural and moral dimensions, since the quality of public communication depends directly on social trust and, in turn, shapes it. At the same time, truthful information does not arise from centralized or automated control. In public discourse, the truth of facts has a rational dimension, as it requires verification, cross-checking of sources and responsible argumentation. Moreover, it is deeply relational, built through bonds of trust and shared practices, as well as an honest exchange with others and with the world. Only the shared pursuit of the veracity of facts, perceived as a common good, can provide a solid foundation for just communication.
blocks.zh: 數碼平台及人工智能系統的使用，正推動公共及政治傳播發生深遠的轉變。那些本可促進對話與參與的工具，常常被用來建構扭曲的敘事，模糊真理與虛假之間的界線，將事實與意見混為一談。假資訊並非始於人工智能，然而今日它卻在人工智能中找到了強大的擴音器。操控內容、圖像及影片的能力，使人們暴露於偏頗或誤導的觀點之下。這個問題同時具有文化與道德的層面，因為公共傳播的品質直接取決於社會信任，反過來又塑造著社會信任。與此同時，真實的資訊並非來自集中化或自動化的控制。在公共論述中，事實的真相具有理性的維度，因為它需要查證、交叉核對資料來源，以及負責任的論證。此外，它更是深度關係性的，是透過信任的紐帶和共同的實踐，以及與他人和世界的坦誠交流而建構起來的。唯有共同追求事實的真實性，並視之為一種公益，才能為正義的傳播提供堅實的基礎。
blocks.html: 132. The use of digital platforms and AI systems is driving profound changes in public and political communication. Tools that could foster dialogue and participation are often used to construct distorted narratives and blur the boundaries between truth and falsehood, mixing facts with opinions. Disinformation did not begin with AI, yet today it finds a powerful amplifier in AI. The ability to manipulate content, images and videos exposes people to biased or misleading perspectives. This problem has both cultural and moral dimensions, since the quality of public communication depends directly on social trust and, in turn, shapes it. At the same time, truthful information does not arise from centralized or automated control. In public discourse, the truth of facts has a rational dimension, as it requires verification, cross-checking of sources and responsible argumentation. Moreover, it is deeply relational, built through bonds of trust and shared practices, as well as an honest exchange with others and with the world. Only the shared pursuit of the veracity of facts, perceived as a common good, can provide a solid foundation for just communication.

blocks.en: Those who command powerful technological and economic resources, along with substantial human capital for intervention, possess significant capabilities for influencing cultural change. Ultimately, they can influence a significant number of people concerning the truth about humanity, the world, the meaning of existence, the family and even God. This is pure power detached from truth, which subtly or overtly imposes what it wishes others to accept as true. At its root lies a deeper and often unrecognized “sickness”: the fact that “modern man is wrongly convinced that he is the sole author of himself, his life and society. This is a presumption that follows from being selfishly closed in upon himself.” [140] Consequently, people believe that they can construct reality, and that whatever best suits their claims corresponds to what is true. Saint John Paul II reflected on the consequences of this “crisis of truth,” going so far as to state that “once the idea of a universal truth about the good, knowable by human reason, is lost, inevitably the notion of conscience also changes.” [141] In such a context, universally valid truths, which precede us and which conscience must accept, are no longer recognized. This led Pope Francis to ask with realism: “What is law without the conviction, born of age-old reflection and great wisdom, that each human being is sacred and inviolable?” To which he concluded: “If society is to have a future, it must respect the truth of our human dignity and submit to that truth. Murder is not wrong simply because it is socially unacceptable and punished by law, but because of a deeper conviction. This is a non-negotiable truth attained by the use of reason and accepted in conscience. A society is noble and decent, not least for its support of the pursuit of truth and its adherence to the most basic of truths.” [142]
blocks.zh: 那些掌握強大科技與經濟資源、並擁有雄厚人力資本可作干預的人，擁有影響文化變遷的巨大能力。歸根結柢，他們能在關乎人性、世界、存在的意義、家庭，乃至天主的真理上，影響為數眾多的人。這是一種脫離真理的純粹權力，以微妙或公然的方式，將他們希望別人接受為真實的事物強加於人。其根源處隱藏著一種更深層、且往往未被意識到的「病態」：即「現代人錯誤地確信自己是其自身、其生命及社會的唯一作者。這是一種源於自私地自我封閉的妄自尊大。」[140] 因此，人們相信他們能夠建構現實，而且凡是最符合他們主張的，便是真實的。聖若望保祿二世曾反思這「真理危機」的後果，甚至指出：「一旦喪失了關於善的普遍真理（這真理可為人的理性所認識）的觀念，良心的概念也必然隨之改變。」[141] 在此背景下，那些先於我們存在、且良心必須接受的普遍有效的真理，便不再被承認。這促使教宗方濟各現實地問道：「若沒有那種源於悠久反思和偉大智慧的確信——即每個人都是神聖和不可侵犯的——法律又算是甚麼呢？」他隨後總結道：「一個社會若要擁有未來，就必須尊重我們人性尊嚴的真理，並順服於這真理。謀殺之所以是錯的，不僅僅因為它在社會上不可接受且受法律懲罰，更是出於一種更深層的確信。這是一項透過理性的運用而獲得、並在良心中被接受的不容妥協的真理。一個社會之所以高貴和體面，最重要的原因之一，正是它對追求真理的支持，以及它對最基本真理的堅持。」[142]
blocks.html: 133. Those who command powerful technological and economic resources, along with substantial human capital for intervention, possess significant capabilities for influencing cultural change. Ultimately, they can influence a significant number of people concerning the truth about humanity, the world, the meaning of existence, the family and even God. This is pure power detached from truth, which subtly or overtly imposes what it wishes others to accept as true. At its root lies a deeper and often unrecognized “sickness”: the fact that “modern man is wrongly convinced that he is the sole author of himself, his life and society. This is a presumption that follows from being selfishly closed in upon himself.” [140] Consequently, people believe that they can construct reality, and that whatever best suits their claims corresponds to what is true. Saint John Paul II reflected on the consequences of this “crisis of truth,” going so far as to state that “once the idea of a universal truth about the good, knowable by human reason, is lost, inevitably the notion of conscience also changes.” [141] In such a context, universally valid truths, which precede us and which conscience must accept, are no longer recognized. This led Pope Francis to ask with realism: “What is law without the conviction, born of age-old reflection and great wisdom, that each human being is sacred and inviolable?”  To which he concluded: “If society is to have a future, it must respect the truth of our human dignity and submit to that truth. Murder is not wrong simply because it is socially unacceptable and punished by law, but because of a deeper conviction. This is a non-negotiable truth attained by the use of reason and accepted in conscience. A society is noble and decent, not least for its support of the pursuit of truth and its adherence to the most basic of truths.” [142]

blocks.en: The search for truth is an essential element of democracy, which is itself a means of contributing to the common good. When questions about what is true lose their appeal, and a pragmatism takes hold that is content with what appears useful or effective, then democratic life is weakened. After all, democracy does not consist of rules and procedures alone, but above all of a solid concordance with the facts and a genuine commitment to the good of individuals and society as a whole. Indifference to the truth leads, slowly but surely, to a descent into totalitarianism. As the philosopher Hannah Arendt wrote, the ideal subjects of such regimes are not so much those who are ideologically convinced, but rather “people for whom the distinction between fact and fiction (i.e., the reality of experience) and the distinction between true and false (i.e., the standards of thought) no longer exist.” [143]
blocks.zh: 對真理的追尋是民主的重要元素，而民主本身就是促進公益的一種方式。一旦關於何為真實的問題失去吸引力，取而代之的是一種滿足於看似有用或有效之事的實用主義，民主生活便會遭到削弱。畢竟，民主不僅僅在於規則和程序，更在於與事實之間的堅實契合，以及對個人和整個社會之美善的真誠投入。對真理的漠不關心，將緩慢而必然地導向極權主義的墮落。正如哲學家漢娜·鄂蘭所寫的，此類政權的理想臣民，與其說是那些在意識形態上確信不疑的人，不如說是「那些在他們心目中，事實與虛構（即經驗的現實）之間的區別，以及真與假（即思想的標準）之間的區別已不復存在的人」。[143]
blocks.html: 134. The search for truth is an essential element of democracy, which is itself a means of contributing to the common good. When questions about what is true lose their appeal, and a pragmatism takes hold that is content with what appears useful or effective, then democratic life is weakened. After all, democracy does not consist of rules and procedures alone, but above all of a solid concordance with the facts and a genuine commitment to the good of individuals and society as a whole.  Indifference to the truth leads, slowly but surely, to a descent into totalitarianism. As the philosopher Hannah Arendt wrote, the ideal subjects of such regimes are not so much those who are ideologically convinced, but rather “people for whom the distinction between fact and fiction (i.e., the reality of experience) and the distinction between true and false (i.e., the standards of thought) no longer exist.” [143]

blocks.en: Communication and the collective imagination
blocks.zh: 傳播與集體想像
blocks.html: Communication and the collective imagination

blocks.en: In view of this, it is important to recall that communication “is not only the transmission of information, but it is also the creation of a culture.” [144] The content that circulates within digital environments shapes how people perceive the world and introduces into the collective consciousness images and narratives that direct our desires and influence our daily choices. This is “not a parallel or purely virtual world,” [145] since what originates online now becomes a part of people’s lives, especially of the youngest.
blocks.zh: 有鑑於此，必須重申：傳播「不僅是資訊的傳遞，也是文化的創造。」[144] 在數碼環境中流通的內容，塑造了人們感知世界的方式，並將引導我們慾望、影響我們日常選擇的圖像和敘事注入集體意識之中。這「不是一個平行或純粹虛擬的世界」，[145] 因為源自網絡的事物現已成為人們生活的一部分，對最年輕的一代而言尤其如此。
blocks.html: 135. In view of this, it is important to recall that communication “is not only the transmission of information, but it is also the creation of a culture.” [144] The content that circulates within digital environments shapes how people perceive the world and introduces into the collective consciousness images and narratives that direct our desires and influence our daily choices. This is “not a parallel or purely virtual world,” [145] since what originates online now becomes a part of people’s lives, especially of the youngest.

blocks.en: For this reason, those who control digital platforms and means of communication have a considerable ability to affect the collective imagination and to present a particular vision of reality as desirable. Such power should be constantly guided by the pursuit of truth and respect for human dignity, so that the culture fostered on the internet does not become an instrument of excessive distraction, homogenization or dominance, but rather a setting in which inner freedom and critical thought can mature.
blocks.zh: 為此，那些掌控數碼平台和傳播媒體的人，擁有相當大的能力去影響集體想像力，並將某種特定的現實觀包裝成令人嚮往的模樣。這股力量應時刻受到追求真理和尊重人性尊嚴的引導，好使在互聯網上所培育的文化，不至於成為過度分心、千篇一律或宰制他人的工具，反而能成為一個讓內在自由和批判性思維得以成熟的環境。
blocks.html: 136. For this reason, those who control digital platforms and means of communication have a considerable ability to affect the collective imagination and to present a particular vision of reality as desirable. Such power should be constantly guided by the pursuit of truth and respect for human dignity, so that the culture fostered on the internet does not become an instrument of excessive distraction, homogenization or dominance, but rather a setting in which inner freedom and critical thought can mature.

blocks.en: Toward an ecology of communication
blocks.zh: 邁向一種傳播的生態學
blocks.html: Toward an ecology of communication

blocks.en: Our first task is neither to demonize nor idolize technological tools, but to utilize them on the basis of a fundamental principle, namely that truth is a common good and not the property of those with power or influence. We must therefore promote an ecology of communication. On the level of public policy, this entails establishing norms so that the decision-making behind content selection and its development becomes more transparent and protects personal data. Regarding social and cultural aspects, this requires a strengthening of intermediary organizations, serious journalism and forums for debate, where reasoned argumentation and verification carry greater weight than immediate reaction. For families and schools, there is a growing need for new educational awareness and for formation concerning the proper and critical use of digital tools, AI and online commercial and financial platforms. In universities, the principal challenge lies in the integration of knowledge, cultivating both the capacity to connect and synthesize knowledge in order to grasp complexity, and the skills necessary to verify facts.
blocks.zh: 我們的首要任務既不是將科技工具妖魔化，也不是將其偶像化，而是基於一項基本原則來善用它們：真理是公益，而非屬於那些擁有權力或影響力之人的私產。因此，我們必須促進一種傳播生態學。在公共政策層面，這意味著建立規範，使內容篩選及其開發背後的決策過程變得更透明，並保護個人資料。在社會與文化方面，這要求加強中介組織、嚴肅的新聞業及辯論平台，讓理性的論證與事實核查比即時反應更有分量。為家庭和學校而言，愈來愈需要新的教育意識，以及對數碼工具、人工智能及網上商業和金融平台的正確且具批判性地使用的培育。在大學裡，主要的挑戰在於知識的整合，既要培養連接與綜合知識以掌握複雜性的能力，也要培養核實事實所需的技能。
blocks.html: 137. Our first task is neither to demonize nor idolize technological tools, but to utilize them on the basis of a fundamental principle, namely that truth is a common good and not the property of those with power or influence. We must therefore promote an ecology of communication. On the level of public policy, this entails establishing norms so that the decision-making behind content selection and its development becomes more transparent and protects personal data. Regarding social and cultural aspects, this requires a strengthening of intermediary organizations, serious journalism and forums for debate, where reasoned argumentation and verification carry greater weight than immediate reaction. For families and schools, there is a growing need for new educational awareness and for formation concerning the proper and critical use of digital tools, AI and online commercial and financial platforms. In universities, the principal challenge lies in the integration of knowledge, cultivating both the capacity to connect and synthesize knowledge in order to grasp complexity, and the skills necessary to verify facts.

blocks.en: Christian communities, too, are called to commit themselves to transparency in communication and to the honest pursuit of facts. Sadly, this has not always been the case. We have witnessed with shame the emergence of painful truths concerning even members of the Church and ecclesial realities. In particular, some journalists, driven by a passion for truth, have played a crucial role in bringing injustices and abuses to light. To them, I wish to repeat the words that Pope Francis used in speaking to journalists: “I also thank you for what you tell us about what goes wrong in the Church, for helping us not to sweep it under the carpet, and for the voice you have given to the victims of abuse.” [146] Yet vigilance and transparency remain first and foremost a grave responsibility for the Church herself, and we must not wait for others to compel us to confront uncomfortable truths about ourselves.
blocks.zh: 基督徒團體同樣蒙召致力於傳播上的透明度，以及誠實地追求事實。可惜的是，情況並非總是如此。我們羞愧地目睹了一些痛苦的真相浮現，甚至涉及教會的成員和教會團體。尤其是，一些本著追求真理的熱忱而工作的記者，在揭露不公義和侵犯行為方面發揮了關鍵作用。對他們，我願重申教宗方濟各對記者們所說的話：「我也感謝你們告訴我們教會內出了什麼問題，幫助我們不去掩蓋問題，並感謝你們讓侵犯受害者的聲音得以發出。」[146] 然而，警醒和透明度首先仍是教會本身的一項重大責任，我們不應等待他人來迫使我們面對關於自身的令人不安的真相。
blocks.html: 138. Christian communities, too, are called to commit themselves to transparency in communication and to the honest pursuit of facts. Sadly, this has not always been the case. We have witnessed with shame the emergence of painful truths concerning even members of the Church and ecclesial realities. In particular, some journalists, driven by a passion for truth, have played a crucial role in bringing injustices and abuses to light. To them, I wish to repeat the words that Pope Francis used in speaking to journalists: “I also thank you for what you tell us about what goes wrong in the Church, for helping us not to sweep it under the carpet, and for the voice you have given to the victims of abuse.” [146] Yet vigilance and transparency remain first and foremost a grave responsibility for the Church herself, and we must not wait for others to compel us to confront uncomfortable truths about ourselves.

blocks.en: An educational alliance for the digital age
blocks.zh: 數碼時代的教育同盟
blocks.html: An educational alliance for the digital age

blocks.en: In an era when truth is often distorted in order to serve particular interests and communication strategies, the field of education assumes decisive importance. Yet rapid technological transformations reveal just how unprepared we are on the educational level. The pervasiveness of digital media fosters a culture of immediacy and hyper-stimulation, which gives rise to fatigue, boredom and apathy concerning the effort required for seeking the truth.
blocks.zh: 在這個為服務特定利益和傳播策略而使真理常遭扭曲的時代，教育領域肩負著決定性的重要意義。然而，急速的科技變革恰恰揭示我們在教育層面上的準備是何等不足。數碼媒體的普及催生了一種即時滿足和過度刺激的文化，這導致人們對尋求真理所需付出的努力產生疲憊、厭倦和冷漠。
blocks.html: 139. In an era when truth is often distorted in order to serve particular interests and communication strategies, the field of education assumes decisive importance. Yet rapid technological transformations reveal just how unprepared we are on the educational level. The pervasiveness of digital media fosters a culture of immediacy and hyper-stimulation, which gives rise to fatigue, boredom and apathy concerning the effort required for seeking the truth.

blocks.en: Education, by contrast, is a long journey requiring patience, and therefore needs time for development and for engagement with reality beyond appearances. This is a fundamental issue because every technology shapes those who use it. Educating people about the use of AI, then, involves teaching them to decide when and for what purpose it ought not to be used. The speed and ease with which answers or summaries can be obtained risk extinguishing the desire to ask questions, which is a process that bears fruit only over time. As Plato wrote, the deepest and most important things are learned only after much time and effort, by engaging in discussion with others, “striking upon” ideas and experiences together like flint until the spark of understanding is kindled within us. [147] We must learn, then, how to exercise restraint in the use of AI and to protect our young people from the promise of the perfect machine, from that subtle temptation which renders human thought seemingly superfluous precisely when it is most needed.
blocks.zh: 與之相反，教育乃是一段需要耐心的漫長旅程，因此需要時間去發展，並在表象之外與現實深度交會。這是一個根本性的議題，因為每一項科技都在塑造使用它的人。因此，教育人使用人工智能，意味著教導他們判斷何時不應使用、以及為了什麼目的而不應使用。答案或摘要獲取的速度與便利，有可能扼殺人提出問題的渴望，而提問正是需要經年累月才能結出果實的過程。正如柏拉圖所寫，最深刻與最重要的事，唯有經過大量的時間與努力，通過與他人共同討論，像敲打燧石般一同「擊打」思想與經驗，直至理解的火花在我們內心點燃，方能學到。[147] 因此，我們必須學會在使用人工智能時保持節制，保護我們的年輕人遠離那完美機器的許諾，遠離那精微的誘惑——正當人的思想最為必需之際，卻使它顯得彷彿是多餘的。
blocks.html: 140. Education, by contrast, is a long journey requiring patience, and therefore needs time for development and for engagement with reality beyond appearances. This is a fundamental issue because every technology shapes those who use it. Educating people about the use of AI, then, involves teaching them to decide when and for what purpose it ought not to be used. The speed and ease with which answers or summaries can be obtained risk extinguishing the desire to ask questions, which is a process that bears fruit only over time. As Plato wrote, the deepest and most important things are learned only after much time and effort, by engaging in discussion with others, “striking upon” ideas and experiences together like flint until the spark of understanding is kindled within us. [147] We must learn, then, how to exercise restraint in the use of AI and to protect our young people from the promise of the perfect machine, from that subtle temptation which renders human thought seemingly superfluous precisely when it is most needed.

blocks.en: In recent years, psychological and psychiatric literature has documented with growing insistence how early and unsupervised exposure to digital devices and social media can negatively impact sleep, attention span, control of emotions and relationships, especially during the most vulnerable stages of life, at times with tragic consequences. This is further aggravated by easy access to violent or degrading content that offends sensibility, to pornographic and hypersexualized material, to messages that trivialize the body and emotions, and to proposals that normalize risky behavior. Online phenomena such as grooming, blackmail and the sexual exploitation of minors are not uncommon, and are made more insidious by the use of fake profiles, algorithms that facilitate dangerous contact, and AI tools capable of manipulating images and videos. Having a personal mobile device at too early an age and using it without adult supervision can exacerbate young people’s vulnerabilities, foster addiction and expose them to isolation, bullying and cyberbullying, as well as to pressures to share intimate images or sensitive information.
blocks.zh: 近年來，心理學及精神病學文獻日益迫切地指出，過早且在無監管的情況下接觸數碼裝置和社交媒體，會對睡眠、專注力、情緒控制和人際關係產生負面影響，尤其是在生命最脆弱的階段，有時更會帶來悲慘的後果。這情況因容易接觸到冒犯感受的暴力或低俗內容、色情及過度性化的材料、貶低身體與情感的訊息，以及鼓吹將高風險行為正常化的建議而進一步加劇。網上誘騙、勒索及對未成年人的性剝削等現象並不罕見，而虛假帳戶的使用、助長危險接觸的演算法，以及能夠操縱圖像和影片的人工智能工具，更使這些行為變得愈加陰險。在過於年幼時便擁有個人流動裝置，並在沒有成人監管下使用，會加劇年輕人的弱點，助長成癮行為，並使他們陷於孤立、欺凌和網絡欺凌，以及被迫分享私密影像或敏感資訊的壓力之中。
blocks.html: 141. In recent years, psychological and psychiatric literature has documented with growing insistence how early and unsupervised exposure to digital devices and social media can negatively impact sleep, attention span, control of emotions and relationships, especially during the most vulnerable stages of life, at times with tragic consequences. This is further aggravated by easy access to violent or degrading content that offends sensibility, to pornographic and hypersexualized material, to messages that trivialize the body and emotions, and to proposals that normalize risky behavior. Online phenomena such as grooming, blackmail and the sexual exploitation of minors are not uncommon, and are made more insidious by the use of fake profiles, algorithms that facilitate dangerous contact, and AI tools capable of manipulating images and videos. Having a personal mobile device at too early an age and using it without adult supervision can exacerbate young people’s vulnerabilities, foster addiction and expose them to isolation, bullying and cyberbullying, as well as to pressures to share intimate images or sensitive information.

blocks.en: It is difficult for parents by themselves to resist the influence of business models that monetize attention and time. Therefore, it is essential to form an alliance among policy-makers, educational institutions and families that is capable of concretely supporting adults in this task. Far-sighted public policies are needed to oppose the immediate interests of platforms, concentrated in a few hands, when they conflict with the wellbeing of minors. In this regard, interventions by legislators are appropriate for setting age limits, holding service providers accountable rather than shifting the whole burden of control onto families, and for providing specific protections against all forms of online sexual exploitation and violence. Thus can children and adolescents, who are entrusted to our care, be genuinely protected as a precious treasure. [148] At the same time, it is also necessary to teach children, adolescents and young people how to recognize manipulation, defend their dignity and respect that of others in digital environments. [149]
blocks.zh: 父母單靠自身難以抗衡那些將注意力和時間貨幣化的商業模式所帶來的影響。因此，決策者、教育機構和家庭之間建立聯盟至關重要，使這聯盟能夠以具體方式支持成年人在這項任務中的努力。我們需要具遠見的公共政策，以抗衡那些集中在少數人手中的平台之即時利益，當這些利益與未成年人的福祉發生衝突時尤為如此。在這方面，立法者的干預是適切的：設定年齡限制、要求服務提供者承擔責任而非將監管的全部重擔轉嫁給家庭，以及針對一切形式的網上性剝削和暴力提供特定保護。這樣，那些託付給我們照顧的兒童和青少年，就能如同珍貴的寶藏般得到真正的保護。[148] 與此同時，也必須教導兒童、青少年和年輕人如何在數碼環境中辨識操控、維護自身尊嚴，並尊重他人的尊嚴。[149]
blocks.html: 142. It is difficult for parents by themselves to resist the influence of business models that monetize attention and time. Therefore, it is essential to form an alliance among policy-makers, educational institutions and families that is capable of concretely supporting adults in this task. Far-sighted public policies are needed to oppose the immediate interests of platforms, concentrated in a few hands, when they conflict with the wellbeing of minors. In this regard, interventions by legislators are appropriate for setting age limits, holding service providers accountable rather than shifting the whole burden of control onto families, and for providing specific protections against all forms of online sexual exploitation and violence. Thus can children and adolescents, who are entrusted to our care, be genuinely protected as a precious treasure. [148] At the same time, it is also necessary to teach children, adolescents and young people how to recognize manipulation, defend their dignity and respect that of others in digital environments. [149]

blocks.en: The central role of schools
blocks.zh: 學校的核心角色
blocks.html: The central role of schools

blocks.en: School is the place where new generations can learn to seek and love the truth, to reflect on the meaning of life and to recognize the dignity of every person. For this reason, many parents, who want their children to grow in the capacity to form relationships, develop critical thinking skills and embrace solid values, place great expectations on schools as valuable partners in their children’s education. Yet parents have the primary and inalienable right to choose the kind of education and formation for their children, in a manner consistent with their moral, cultural and religious convictions. Today, the world of education faces a number of urgent challenges.
blocks.zh: 學校是新一代學習尋求並熱愛真理、反思生命意義、並辨認每個人尊嚴的地方。為此，許多希望子女能夠在人際關係的建立、批判思考能力的培養，以及擁抱穩固價值觀等方面成長的父母，對學校寄予厚望，視之為子女教育中寶貴的合作夥伴。然而，父母擁有首要且不可剝奪的權利，得以按照符合其道德、文化和宗教信念的方式，為子女選擇教育的種類和培育模式。今日，教育界正面臨一系列迫切的挑戰。
blocks.html: 143. School is the place where new generations can learn to seek and love the truth, to reflect on the meaning of life and to recognize the dignity of every person. For this reason, many parents, who want their children to grow in the capacity to form relationships, develop critical thinking skills and embrace solid values, place great expectations on schools as valuable partners in their children’s education. Yet parents have the primary and inalienable right to choose the kind of education and formation for their children, in a manner consistent with their moral, cultural and religious convictions. Today, the world of education faces a number of urgent challenges.

blocks.en: The first challenge is socio-political. Both within individual nations and across different regions of the world, significant inequalities persist concerning access to basic education and higher studies. In many nations, Governments have not yet invested the necessary resources for guaranteeing a quality education for all, whether by adequately supporting the public school system or by assisting private institutions that offer this essential service. When a substantial portion of education, at various levels, is entrusted to private institutions, access to schooling may become overly dependent on families’ financial means, especially in the absence of adequate public support. In the face of this risk, it is nevertheless important to acknowledge and encourage the contribution of the many private Catholic educational institutions which ensure inclusive access for children and young people of every background, even when families’ economic circumstances would not otherwise allow it.
blocks.zh: 第一個挑戰是社會政治層面的。無論在個別國家內部，或是在世界不同地區之間，在獲得基礎教育和高等教育的機會方面，仍然存在著重大的不平等。許多國家的政府尚未投入必要的資源，以保障全民享有優質教育，無論是透過充分支持公立學校體系，還是透過協助提供這項基本服務的私立機構。當各級教育的相當大部分交由私立機構承擔時，入學機會便可能過度依賴家庭的經濟能力，特別是在缺乏足夠公共支持的情況下。面對這一風險，我們仍須肯定並鼓勵眾多天主教私立教育機構的貢獻，這些機構確保來自各種背景的兒童和青少年都能獲得包容性的入學機會，即使在其家庭的經濟狀況無法負擔的情況下亦然。
blocks.html: 144. The first challenge is socio-political. Both within individual nations and across different regions of the world, significant inequalities persist concerning access to basic education and higher studies. In many nations, Governments have not yet invested the necessary resources for guaranteeing a quality education for all, whether by adequately supporting the public school system or by assisting private institutions that offer this essential service. When a substantial portion of education, at various levels, is entrusted to private institutions, access to schooling may become overly dependent on families’ financial means, especially in the absence of adequate public support. In the face of this risk, it is nevertheless important to acknowledge and encourage the contribution of the many private Catholic educational institutions which ensure inclusive access for children and young people of every background, even when families’ economic circumstances would not otherwise allow it.

blocks.en: The second major challenge is pedagogical. Many educational systems struggle to keep pace with change and to support the integral development of students. The advance of information technologies and AI is rapidly rendering curricula obsolete that were designed for a different era. Meanwhile, the organization of schools, physical spaces, evaluation methods and the role of teachers themselves must be rethought in order to promote an authentically integral education that addresses every dimension of the person. It is necessary to support the ongoing formation of teachers throughout their professional lives, so that they can engage positively with new technologies, helping students to use them responsibly, critically and creatively, rather than passively succumbing to their influence.
blocks.zh: 第二個重大挑戰是教育方面的。許多教育體系難以跟上變化的步伐，也難以支持學生的整全發展。資訊科技與人工智能的快速推進，正迅速使為不同時代設計的課程變得過時。與此同時，學校的架構、物理空間、評估方法，乃至教師本身的角色，都必須重新審視，以促進一種真正整全的教育，涵蓋人的每一個面向。有必要在教師的整個職業生涯中支持他們的持續培育，使他們能夠以積極的態度與新科技互動，幫助學生以負責任、具批判性和創造性的方式使用這些科技，而非被動地屈服於其影響之下。
blocks.html: 145. The second major challenge is pedagogical. Many educational systems struggle to keep pace with change and to support the integral development of students. The advance of information technologies and AI is rapidly rendering curricula obsolete that were designed for a different era. Meanwhile, the organization of schools, physical spaces, evaluation methods and the role of teachers themselves must be rethought in order to promote an authentically integral education that addresses every dimension of the person. It is necessary to support the ongoing formation of teachers throughout their professional lives, so that they can engage positively with new technologies, helping students to use them responsibly, critically and creatively, rather than passively succumbing to their influence.

blocks.en: The third major challenge is intellectual and concerns knowledge. Without careful attention, an educational system lacking in a love for truth may emerge, in which an incessant flow of information replaces the essential exercise of research, reflection and discernment. As knowledge becomes increasingly fragmented, it becomes difficult to grasp reality as a whole, to ask profound questions about meaning, or to develop authentic, critical and creative thought. Many educators already report signs of dehumanization, where people may “know many things” but struggle to find direction in their lives, partly due to an inability to connect information with deeper knowledge or maintain a sense of purpose. A genuinely healthy attitude is needed, requiring rhythms that incorporate silence, in-depth study, reading and judicious analysis, for without these elements inner freedom may be compromised.
blocks.zh: 第三個重大的挑戰屬於理智層面，關乎知識。若不謹慎留意，一種缺乏對真理的熱愛的教育體系便可能出現；在其中，不間斷的資訊洪流取代了研究、反思與分辨這些根本的操練。隨著知識日益碎片化，人就越難將現實作為一個整體來把握，難以提出關於生命意義的深刻問題，也難以培養真正具有批判性與創造力的思想。許多教育工作者早已報告去人性化的跡象：人們可能「知道很多事情」，卻難以找到生命的方向，部分原因正是無法將資訊與更深的知識連結起來，也難以保持一份目的感。這裡需要一種真正健全的態度，要求一個包含靜默、深入研讀、閱讀和明智分析的節奏，因為若沒有這些元素，內在的自由便可能受到損害。
blocks.html: 146. The third major challenge is intellectual and concerns knowledge. Without careful attention, an educational system lacking in a love for truth may emerge, in which an incessant flow of information replaces the essential exercise of research, reflection and discernment. As knowledge becomes increasingly fragmented, it becomes difficult to grasp reality as a whole, to ask profound questions about meaning, or to develop authentic, critical and creative thought. Many educators already report signs of dehumanization, where people may “know many things” but struggle to find direction in their lives, partly due to an inability to connect information with deeper knowledge or maintain a sense of purpose. A genuinely healthy attitude is needed, requiring rhythms that incorporate silence, in-depth study, reading and judicious analysis, for without these elements inner freedom may be compromised.

blocks.en: The Church’s Social Doctrine invites families, schools, Christian communities and public institutions to form a renewed educational alliance. This takes shape when fundamental principles are translated into educational goals, including teaching students a sense of moderation and limits; recognition of the rights of others and of future generations to enjoy the goods that are either provided for us or made available by human ingenuity; freedom and responsibility; and a sense of transcendence and the common good. Schools are not called to follow the pace of the digital world, but to offer that which the digital sphere by itself cannot provide, namely a shared time for learning and developing trustworthy relationships.
blocks.zh: 教會的社會訓導邀請家庭、學校、基督徒團體和公共機構，建立一個更新的教育聯盟。只有當基本原則化為教育目標時，這聯盟才能真正成形，這些目標包括：教導學生節制和知所界限的意識；承認他人及後代子孫有權享有那些由天主賜予我們、或經由人類才智所提供的物資；自由與責任的意識；以及超越幅度與公益的意識。學校蒙召，不是要追隨數碼世界的步伐，而是要提供數碼領域本身所無法給予的東西，即一段共享的學習時光，並藉此建立可信賴的關係。
blocks.html: 147. The Church’s Social Doctrine invites families, schools, Christian communities and public institutions to form a renewed educational alliance. This takes shape when fundamental principles are translated into educational goals, including teaching students a sense of moderation and limits; recognition of the rights of others and of future generations to enjoy the goods that are either provided for us or made available by human ingenuity; freedom and responsibility; and a sense of transcendence and the common good. Schools are not called to follow the pace of the digital world, but to offer that which the digital sphere by itself cannot provide, namely a shared time for learning and developing trustworthy relationships.

blocks.en: The dignity of work at a time of digital transition
blocks.zh: 數碼轉型時代勞動的尊嚴
blocks.html: The dignity of work at a time of digital transition

blocks.en: The value of work
blocks.zh: 勞動的價值
blocks.html: The value of work

blocks.en: Since the emergence of her Social Doctrine, beginning with Rerum Novarum, the Church has emphasized the protection of workers and the need to combat all forms of exploitation. Above all, however, the Magisterium has recognized in work “the essential key” [150] to understanding the entire social question, since it is through their work that individuals develop many dimensions of their existence. In view of this, we can understand the great intuition of Saint Benedict of Nursia, who united prayer and work, showing daily activity to be a part of the human response to God’s call. Created in the image of the Creator, our own work in some way continues his, for thereby we contribute to the progress of society and the common good, put to good use the capabilities we have received, improve and beautify the world, support our families, engage in cooperative relationships and, through listening and dialogue, learn to build together something that no one could achieve alone.
blocks.zh: 自其社會訓導的發端——始於《新事》通諭——以來，教會一直強調對工人的保護，以及打擊一切形式的剝削的必要性。然而，最重要的是，教會訓導當局在勞動中認出了理解整個社會問題的「根本關鍵」[150]，因為正是通過勞動，個人才得以發展其存在的諸多幅度。有鑑於此，我們能夠理解聖本篤（努西亞的）那偉大的直覺：他將祈禱與勞動結合在一起，展現出日常活動乃是人類回應天主召叫的一部分。我們既是按照造物主的肖像受造的，我們自己的勞動便以某種方式延續祂的工程，因為藉此我們為社會的進步和公益作出貢獻，善用我們所領受的才能，改善並美化世界，供養我們的家庭，投入合作的關係，並通過聆聽與對話，學會共同建設一些無人能單獨完成的事業。
blocks.html: 148. Since the emergence of her Social Doctrine, beginning with Rerum Novarum, the Church has emphasized the protection of workers and the need to combat all forms of exploitation. Above all, however, the Magisterium has recognized in work “the essential key” [150] to understanding the entire social question, since it is through their work that individuals develop many dimensions of their existence. In view of this, we can understand the great intuition of Saint Benedict of Nursia, who united prayer and work, showing daily activity to be a part of the human response to God’s call. Created in the image of the Creator, our own work in some way continues his, for thereby we contribute to the progress of society and the common good, put to good use the capabilities we have received, improve and beautify the world, support our families, engage in cooperative relationships and, through listening and dialogue, learn to build together something that no one could achieve alone.

blocks.en: For these reasons, work is not simply an instrument; it expresses and enhances the dignity of our lives. It is a requirement of the human condition, a normal path toward maturity, development and personal fulfilment. In this regard, financial assistance to the poor may at times be necessary in emergencies, but it cannot become the sole response, since the goal is to enable each person to live with dignity through his or her own work. [151]
blocks.zh: 基於這些原因，工作並非僅是一件工具；它表達並提升了我們生命的尊嚴。它是人類處境的一項要求，是通向成熟、成長和個人實現的正常途徑。就此而言，在緊急情況下向貧困者提供經濟援助有時是必要的，但這不能成為唯一的回應，因為目標是使每一個人能透過自己的工作有尊嚴地生活。[151]
blocks.html: 149. For these reasons, work is not simply an instrument; it expresses and enhances the dignity of our lives. It is a requirement of the human condition, a normal path toward maturity, development and personal fulfilment. In this regard, financial assistance to the poor may at times be necessary in emergencies, but it cannot become the sole response, since the goal is to enable each person to live with dignity through his or her own work. [151]

blocks.en: Today, the convergence of automation, robotics and AI is rapidly transforming the very structure of work. It is said that this will bring great improvements for everyone. In reality, however, the “new ways” of working are not necessarily better, for “while AI promises to boost productivity by taking over mundane tasks, it frequently forces workers to adapt to the speed and demands of machines, rather than machines being designed to support those who work. As a result, contrary to the advertised benefits of AI, current approaches to technology can paradoxically de-skill workers, subject them to automated surveillance and relegate them to rigid and repetitive tasks. The need to keep up with the pace of technology can erode workers’ sense of agency and stifle the innovative abilities they are expected to bring to their work.” [152] Precisely in order to avoid this drift, it is necessary to design systems that are centered on the human person and not solely on performance.
blocks.zh: 時至今日，自動化、機械人技術與人工智能的匯流，正迅速改變著工作的本質結構。有人說，這將為每一個人帶來重大的改善。然而實際上，這些工作的「新方式」不一定更好，因為「雖然人工智能承諾透過接管繁瑣的工作來提高生產力，但它時常迫使工人去適應機器的速度和需求，而不是讓機器被設計成去支援工作的人。結果是，與人工智能所宣傳的益處相反，當前的技術方針可能會自相矛盾地剝奪工人的技能，使他們受到自動化監控，並將他們侷限於僵化而重複的任務中。追趕技術步伐的需要，會侵蝕工人的自主意識，並扼殺他們本應為其工作帶來的創新能力。」[152] 正是為了避免這種偏離，有必要設計以人為本、而非單以績效為本的系統。
blocks.html: 150. Today, the convergence of automation, robotics and AI is rapidly transforming the very structure of work. It is said that this will bring great improvements for everyone. In reality, however, the “new ways” of working are not necessarily better, for “while AI promises to boost productivity by taking over mundane tasks, it frequently forces workers to adapt to the speed and demands of machines, rather than machines being designed to support those who work. As a result, contrary to the advertised benefits of AI, current approaches to technology can paradoxically de-skill workers, subject them to automated surveillance and relegate them to rigid and repetitive tasks. The need to keep up with the pace of technology can erode workers’ sense of agency and stifle the innovative abilities they are expected to bring to their work.” [152] Precisely in order to avoid this drift, it is necessary to design systems that are centered on the human person and not solely on performance.

blocks.en: The problem of unemployment
blocks.zh: 失業問題
blocks.html: The problem of unemployment

blocks.en: Saint John Paul II recognized that unemployment is a grave evil. Indeed, when it reaches massive proportions, it becomes a true social calamity that especially requires the State to exercise responsibility. [153] Today, amid the “fourth industrial revolution,” this concern is even more acute, as innovation is often pursued solely for reducing costs and increasing profits. [154] In some contexts, there is a legitimate fear of a significant and rapid contraction in available jobs that would create a chain reaction deeply impacting families, young people and local economies. In many sectors, this can already be seen in new forms of job insecurity and inequality, characterized by outsized remuneration for a highly specialized minority alongside declining wages for a large portion of the workforce.
blocks.zh: 聖若望保祿二世確認，失業乃一種嚴重的邪惡。事實上，當失業達到巨大規模時，便成為一場真正的社會災難，尤其需要國家承擔起責任。[153] 今日，在「第四次工業革命」之中，這種憂慮更為尖銳，因為創新往往只是為了降低成本、提高利潤而追求的。[154] 在某些處境中，人們合理地擔憂就業機會將出現顯著而急速的萎縮，從而引發連鎖反應，深刻影響家庭、青年和地方經濟。在許多行業中，這已可從新型態的就業不穩定與不平等中看到，其特徵是為少數高度專業化的人提供過高的薪酬，與此同時，大部分勞動者的工資卻不斷下降。
blocks.html: 151. Saint John Paul II recognized that unemployment is a grave evil. Indeed, when it reaches massive proportions, it becomes a true social calamity that especially requires the State to exercise responsibility. [153] Today, amid the “fourth industrial revolution,” this concern is even more acute, as innovation is often pursued solely for reducing costs and increasing profits. [154] In some contexts, there is a legitimate fear of a significant and rapid contraction in available jobs that would create a chain reaction deeply impacting families, young people and local economies. In many sectors, this can already be seen in new forms of job insecurity and inequality, characterized by outsized remuneration for a highly specialized minority alongside declining wages for a large portion of the workforce.

blocks.en: It is certainly desirable for technology to relieve humans of arduous, repetitive or dangerous tasks and to provide intelligent support for human activity. Yet, the protection of employment opportunities and the irreplaceable role of the individual must remain the general rule. The pursuit of greater profits cannot justify choices that systematically sacrifice jobs, because the human person is an end, not a means, and the economic order must remain subordinate to human dignity and the common good.
blocks.zh: 科技固然宜於減輕人類艱苦、重複或危險的工作，並為人類活動提供智能支援。然而，保障就業機會和個人不可替代的角色，必須始終是普遍的原則。追求更高利潤不能成為系統性地犧牲工作崗位的理據，因為人是目的而非手段，而經濟秩序必須始終服膺於人性尊嚴和公益。
blocks.html: 152. It is certainly desirable for technology to relieve humans of arduous, repetitive or dangerous tasks and to provide intelligent support for human activity. Yet, the protection of employment opportunities and the irreplaceable role of the individual must remain the general rule. The pursuit of greater profits cannot justify choices that systematically sacrifice jobs, because the human person is an end, not a means, and the economic order must remain subordinate to human dignity and the common good.

blocks.en: At the same time, we must acknowledge that every real transition involves discontinuities, for it is uneven, fragmented and sometimes conflictual. Consequently, no single model of change or universal solution exists, since there are places and situations that require different responses. Given the inequality that characterizes our world, the spread of AI and computational systems produces varied effects in different places. Wealthy societies automate rapidly and chaotically, reducing the need for a workforce and creating room for unemployment and institutional friction. Vast regions of the world, by contrast, remain trapped in hybrid economies, where underpaid human labor and partial technologies coexist without achieving genuine transformation. These areas become places of precarious labor, and hotbeds of instability and forced migration. Therefore, solutions must be sought at national and local levels through the involvement of intermediary communities. We need adaptive tools, including well-structured models, local initiatives, progressive redistribution and new rights of access to essential goods. While not pursuing an abstract harmony, we must build concrete forms of human coexistence at this time of transformation.
blocks.zh: 與此同時，我們必須承認，每一次真正的轉變都包含著斷裂，因為這過程是不均衡、零散且有時充滿衝突的。因此，並不存在單一的變革模式或普世通用的解決方案，因為不同的地方和處境需要不同的回應。鑑於我們世界的特徵是不平等，人工智能和計算系統的擴散在不同的地方產生了各異的效應。富裕社會快速而混亂地進行自動化，減少了對勞動力的需求，並為失業和制度性摩擦創造了空間。相比之下，世界上廣大的地區仍然被困在混合經濟之中，報酬過低的人力勞動與不完整的技術並存，卻未能實現真正的轉變。這些地區成了勞動條件不穩定的地方，也是動盪和被迫遷徙的溫床。因此，解決方案必須在國家和地方層面，透過中間團體的參與來尋求。我們需要適應性工具，包括結構良好的模式、地方倡議、漸進式的再分配，以及獲取基本物資的新權利。我們雖然不追求一種抽象的和諧，但在這轉變的時代，我們必須建立具體的人類共存形式。
blocks.html: 153. At the same time, we must acknowledge that every real transition involves discontinuities, for it is uneven, fragmented and sometimes conflictual. Consequently, no single model of change or universal solution exists, since there are places and situations that require different responses. Given the inequality that characterizes our world, the spread of AI and computational systems produces varied effects in different places. Wealthy societies automate rapidly and chaotically, reducing the need for a workforce and creating room for unemployment and institutional friction. Vast regions of the world, by contrast, remain trapped in hybrid economies, where underpaid human labor and partial technologies coexist without achieving genuine transformation. These areas become places of precarious labor, and hotbeds of instability and forced migration. Therefore, solutions must be sought at national and local levels through the involvement of intermediary communities. We need adaptive tools, including well-structured models, local initiatives, progressive redistribution and new rights of access to essential goods. While not pursuing an abstract harmony, we must build concrete forms of human coexistence at this time of transformation.

blocks.en: Work remains a fundamental dimension of the human experience, for not only is it a means of sustenance, but it is also a context for expression, relationships and contributing to the community. Therefore, the problems related to work extend beyond the income necessary for family survival. A society that guarantees employment to only a small fraction of the population, despite having a high level of technical development, risks exposing many to forced inactivity, a lack of responsibility and the absence of daily tasks and stimuli, resulting in human and cultural impoverishment. This creates a paradox of material progress and anthropological regression that undermines the foundations of a just and stable social peace. For this reason, the Church’s Social Doctrine insists that access to work for all must be a high priority for public policies and economic processes, serving as a criterion for evaluating the human quality of any development model. [155] Moreover, in those parts of the world where work tends to diminish or change radically due to technological and organizational processes outside of democratic control, we must rethink the nature of work and its connection to citizenship, ensuring that unemployment does not jeopardize social participation.
blocks.zh: 工作依然是人類經驗的一個基本幅度，因為它不僅是維持生計的手段，也是自我表達、建立關係和貢獻社會的環境。因此，與工作相關的問題，遠不止於維持家庭生存所需的收入。一個社會若只保證一小部分人口就業，儘管其技術發展水平很高，卻可能使許多人陷入被迫無所事事、缺乏責任感、缺少日常任務和激勵的境地，最終導致人性和文化的貧瘠。這就造成了一種物質進步與人學倒退的弔詭，破壞了公正而穩定的社會和平的基礎。為此，教會的社會訓導堅持，人人得以工作必須是公共政策和經濟進程的高度優先事項，以之作為衡量任何發展模式人性品質的標準。[155] 此外，在世界那些工作因脫離民主管控的技術和組織化進程而趨於減少或徹底改變的地區，我們必須重新思考工作的本質及其與公民身分的聯繫，確保失業不會危及社會參與。
blocks.html: 154. Work remains a fundamental dimension of the human experience, for not only is it a means of sustenance, but it is also a context for expression, relationships and contributing to the community. Therefore, the problems related to work extend beyond the income necessary for family survival. A society that guarantees employment to only a small fraction of the population, despite having a high level of technical development, risks exposing many to forced inactivity, a lack of responsibility and the absence of daily tasks and stimuli, resulting in human and cultural impoverishment. This creates a paradox of material progress and anthropological regression that undermines the foundations of a just and stable social peace. For this reason, the Church’s Social Doctrine insists that access to work for all must be a high priority for public policies and economic processes, serving as a criterion for evaluating the human quality of any development model. [155] Moreover, in those parts of the world where work tends to diminish or change radically due to technological and organizational processes outside of democratic control, we must rethink the nature of work and its connection to citizenship, ensuring that unemployment does not jeopardize social participation.

blocks.en: In light of this conviction, we can better appreciate the history of the Church’s Social Doctrine after Rerum Novarum. The initiatives which emerged from that tradition, including associations, trade unions, cooperatives and welfare organizations, have contributed decisively to improving labor legislation, protecting the most vulnerable and promoting more humane conditions. [156] Today, however, these instruments are no longer sufficient by themselves in the face of the transformations driven by AI, the new organization of markets and the competitiveness that is rarely concerned with social sustainability. New collaborative efforts are needed among political leaders, labor organizations, the business world and the scientific community in order to develop rapidly adequate shared regulations and protections, including at the international level. [157] Labor unions, which the Church has consistently supported, are called upon to be open to new types of employment and the corresponding needs of workers, in order to represent and defend them. In this context, without bold decisions, the prospect of greater poverty and inequality looms large, which would leave many individuals marginalized, stranded and surrounded by the machines and automated systems that have replaced them.
blocks.zh: 懷著這信念，我們便更能領會《新事》通諭後教會社會訓導的歷史。從這傳統中萌生的種種舉措，包括協會、工會、合作社和福利組織等，都為改善勞工法例、保護最脆弱的一群、促進更合乎人性尊嚴的條件，作出了決定性的貢獻。[156] 然而，面對人工智能帶動的轉型、市場的新組織模式，以及鮮有關注社會可持續性的競爭，這些工具今天單憑自身已不再足夠。我們需要政治領袖、勞工組織、商界和科學界之間作出新的協作努力，以迅速制訂恰當的共同規範和保障，包括在國際層面上。[157] 教會一直支持的工會，蒙召向新類型的就業模式及工人相應的需求開放，好能代表和維護他們。在這樣的背景下，若不作大膽的決定，更嚴重的貧窮和不平等的陰影便會愈益逼近，使許多人被邊緣化、被遺棄，被那些取代了他們的機器和自動化系統重重包圍。
blocks.html: 155. In light of this conviction, we can better appreciate the history of the Church’s Social Doctrine after Rerum Novarum. The initiatives which emerged from that tradition, including associations, trade unions, cooperatives and welfare organizations, have contributed decisively to improving labor legislation, protecting the most vulnerable and promoting more humane conditions. [156] Today, however, these instruments are no longer sufficient by themselves in the face of the transformations driven by AI, the new organization of markets and the competitiveness that is rarely concerned with social sustainability. New collaborative efforts are needed among political leaders, labor organizations, the business world and the scientific community in order to develop rapidly adequate shared regulations and protections, including at the international level. [157] Labor unions, which the Church has consistently supported, are called upon to be open to new types of employment and the corresponding needs of workers, in order to represent and defend them. In this context, without bold decisions, the prospect of greater poverty and inequality looms large, which would leave many individuals marginalized, stranded and surrounded by the machines and automated systems that have replaced them.

blocks.en: At this time of transition, it is not enough to react only when jobs disappear; we must oversee the transformation in advance. One viable path is, first of all, to establish social criteria for innovation. Here, every introduction of automation and AI should be accompanied by verifiable measures to protect the employment, retraining and participation of workers. In this way, technology will be oriented toward freeing up human time and capabilities, rather than producing exclusion. Second, we need proactive policies that make continuous training and professional transitions accessible to all, ensuring that the cost of adaptation does not fall solely on individuals. Finally, there needs to be a corporate commitment to include quality and dignity of work among its indicators of success. When these conditions are present, innovation can serve as an ally of safer, more creative and dignified work; without them, innovation tends to become an accelerator of injustice.
blocks.zh: 在這過渡的時刻，只在工作職位消失時才作出反應是不夠的；我們必須提前管理這場轉型。一個可行的途徑，首先是為創新訂立社會準則。在此，每一次引入自動化和人工智能，都應該伴隨著可驗證的措施，以保障工人的就業、再培訓和參與。這樣，技術便會導向釋放人的時間和能力，而非製造排斥。第二，我們需要積極主動的政策，使持續培訓和職業轉型對所有人開放，確保適應的代價不會單獨落在個人身上。最後，企業需要作出承諾，將工作的質素和尊嚴納入其衡量成功的指標之中。當這些條件具備時，創新可以成為更安全、更具創意和更有尊嚴的工作的盟友；沒有這些條件，創新往往會成為不公義的加速器。
blocks.html: 156. At this time of transition, it is not enough to react only when jobs disappear; we must oversee the transformation in advance. One viable path is, first of all, to establish social criteria for innovation. Here, every introduction of automation and AI should be accompanied by verifiable measures to protect the employment, retraining and participation of workers. In this way, technology will be oriented toward freeing up human time and capabilities, rather than producing exclusion. Second, we need proactive policies that make continuous training and professional transitions accessible to all, ensuring that the cost of adaptation does not fall solely on individuals. Finally, there needs to be a corporate commitment to include quality and dignity of work among its indicators of success. When these conditions are present, innovation can serve as an ally of safer, more creative and dignified work; without them, innovation tends to become an accelerator of injustice.

blocks.en: An economy that values dignity
blocks.zh: 重視尊嚴的經濟
blocks.html: An economy that values dignity

blocks.en: The labor market is one area in which the risks associated with new technologies more clearly emerge. It is thus necessary to remember that economic freedom is not absolute; it must always be measured against the common good and the dignity of every person. Entrepreneurial initiative can indeed be a true vocation, generating wealth and improving lives, rather than a variable that is dependent only on profit. This is possible when it recognizes that the creation of dignified, valuable jobs are an essential part of its proper service to society. [158]
blocks.zh: 勞動市場是新生科技所帶來的風險最為明顯的領域之一。因此，我們有必要緊記，經濟自由並非絕對的；它必須時刻以公益和每一個人的尊嚴為衡量的標準。企業家的創業精神確實可以是一項真正的召叫，它能創造財富、改善生活，而非只是一個取決於利潤的變數。當它意識到，創造有尊嚴、有價值的工作崗位，是其服務社會的本有使命中不可或缺的一部分時，這一切方為可能。[158]
blocks.html: 157. The labor market is one area in which the risks associated with new technologies more clearly emerge. It is thus necessary to remember that economic freedom is not absolute; it must always be measured against the common good and the dignity of every person. Entrepreneurial initiative can indeed be a true vocation, generating wealth and improving lives, rather than a variable that is dependent only on profit. This is possible when it recognizes that the creation of dignified, valuable jobs are an essential part of its proper service to society. [158]

blocks.en: With prophetic spirit, Pope Francis warned against an economic freedom proclaimed in words alone, while actual conditions prevent many from benefiting from it. [159] Economic models that exalt efficiency and individual success often view investment in disadvantaged people or in those with slower development paths as useless or inconvenient, as if their futures depended solely on their ability to keep pace with the “winners.” In reality, a just society requires a vigilant State and civil institutions that are capable of overcoming the singular mentality of efficiency, and of ensuring that resources, creative solutions and regulations favor the most vulnerable. [160] Instead of waiting for the benefits of growth to reach the poor “eventually,” decisions need to be taken to ensure that growth becomes inclusive from the outset. The experience of recent decades shows that in economic and financial crises, it is always the poor who pay the highest price, while the theories that promise automatic general prosperity often prove to be illusory.
blocks.zh: 懷著先知性的精神，教宗方濟各曾警告，一種僅在言詞上宣告的經濟自由，而實際狀況卻使許多人無法從中受益，是危險的。[159] 那些推崇效率和個人成功的經濟模式，往往將投資於弱勢群體或發展步伐較慢的人視為無用或不便，彷彿他們的未來完全取決於能否跟上「贏家」的步伐。事實上，一個公義的社會需要一個警醒的國家和公民機構，它們能夠超越單一的效率思維，並確保資源、創新的解決方案和法規有利於最脆弱的人。[160] 與其等待增長的益處「最終」惠及窮人，不如從一開始就作出決定，以確保增長具有包容性。近數十年的經驗表明，在經濟和金融危機中，付出最高代價的總是窮人，而那些承諾自動帶來普遍繁榮的理論往往被證明是虛幻的。
blocks.html: 158. With prophetic spirit, Pope Francis warned against an economic freedom proclaimed in words alone, while actual conditions prevent many from benefiting from it. [159] Economic models that exalt efficiency and individual success often view investment in disadvantaged people or in those with slower development paths as useless or inconvenient, as if their futures depended solely on their ability to keep pace with the “winners.” In reality, a just society requires a vigilant State and civil institutions that are capable of overcoming the singular mentality of efficiency, and of ensuring that resources, creative solutions and regulations favor the most vulnerable. [160] Instead of waiting for the benefits of growth to reach the poor “eventually,” decisions need to be taken to ensure that growth becomes inclusive from the outset. The experience of recent decades shows that in economic and financial crises, it is always the poor who pay the highest price, while the theories that promise automatic general prosperity often prove to be illusory.

blocks.en: It is important to move beyond the current metrics of development — which for more than eighty years have been tied to the concept of Gross Domestic Product (GDP) — since these metrics almost systematically neglect aspects essential to the overall wellbeing of people and the environment. The development of parameters and metrics complementary to GDP is crucial for improving the databases used for conducting analyses, political and economic decision-making and establishing regional, national and international priorities. The introduction of new parameters will allow for a comprehensive and timely assessment of how legislative and regulatory decisions impact the dignity of work, shared prosperity, inequality reduction and environmental protection. It will also affect the concept of development, educational processes, mindsets and public opinion, as well as peace, which is only authentic when based on justice.
blocks.zh: 至關重要的是，我們必須超越現行的發展衡量標準——這些標準八十多年來一直與本地生產總值（GDP）的概念掛鉤——因為這些標準幾乎有系統地忽略了對人類整體福祉及環境至為關鍵的各個層面。發展出與本地生產總值相輔相成的參數和衡量標準，對於改進用於進行分析、作出政治和經濟決策，以及確立地區、國家和國際優先次序的數據庫至關重要。引入新的參數將使人們能夠全面而及時地評估立法及監管決策如何影響工作的尊嚴、共享繁榮、減少不平等現象，以及環境保護。這也將影響到發展的概念、教育過程、思維模式和公眾輿論，以及和平——真正的和平唯有奠基於正義之上。
blocks.html: 159. It is important to move beyond the current metrics of development — which for more than eighty years have been tied to the concept of Gross Domestic Product (GDP) — since these metrics almost systematically neglect aspects essential to the overall wellbeing of people and the environment. The development of parameters and metrics complementary to GDP is crucial for improving the databases used for conducting analyses, political and economic decision-making and establishing regional, national and international priorities. The introduction of new parameters will allow for a comprehensive and timely assessment of how legislative and regulatory decisions impact the dignity of work, shared prosperity, inequality reduction and environmental protection. It will also affect the concept of development, educational processes, mindsets and public opinion, as well as peace, which is only authentic when based on justice.
