blocks.en: In recent years, finance has increased in importance and has undergone significant innovation, driven partly by the introduction of cryptocurrencies. The reflections and observations contained in the teaching of my predecessors, particularly in their Encyclicals, have highlighted how the financial intermediation sector, “when operating without the necessary anthropological and moral foundations, has not only produced manifest abuses and injustice, but also demonstrated a capacity to create systemic and worldwide economic crisis.” [161] It is likewise the case that income from capital risks replacing income from labor, which is often confined to the margins of the economic system’s primary interests. Yet savings transformed into credit for the real economy, thereby creating both jobs and self-employed work, remain central for development and the investments that must accompany ongoing transitions. The social function of credit remains irreplaceable. Finance for its own sake is fundamentally different from finance aimed at the development, creation and evolution of work.
blocks.zh: 近年來，金融的重要性日益增加，並經歷了顯著的創新，部分是由加密貨幣的引入所推動。我的前任在他們的訓導，尤其在他們的通諭中所包含的反思與觀察，已指出金融中介行業「在缺乏必要的人類學和道德基礎的情況下運作，不僅產生了明顯的濫權和不義，還展現了製造系統性、全球性經濟危機的能力。」[161] 同樣地，資本收益有取代勞動收入的風險，而後者往往被局限於經濟體系主要利益的邊緣。然而，轉化為實體經濟信貸的儲蓄，從而創造就業機會和自僱工作，仍然是發展以及伴隨持續轉型所必需的投資的核心。信貸的社會功能仍然不可替代。為金融而金融，從根本上不同於以工作的發展、創造和演化為目標的金融。
blocks.html: 160. In recent years, finance has increased in importance and has undergone significant innovation, driven partly by the introduction of cryptocurrencies. The reflections and observations contained in the teaching of my predecessors, particularly in their Encyclicals, have highlighted how the financial intermediation sector, “when operating without the necessary anthropological and moral foundations, has not only produced manifest abuses and injustice, but also demonstrated a capacity to create systemic and worldwide economic crisis.” [161] It is likewise the case that income from capital risks replacing income from labor, which is often confined to the margins of the economic system’s primary interests.  Yet savings transformed into credit for the real economy, thereby creating both jobs and self-employed work, remain central for development and the investments that must accompany ongoing transitions. The social function of credit remains irreplaceable. Finance for its own sake is fundamentally different from finance aimed at the development, creation and evolution of work.

blocks.en: This perspective needs to become part of a broader view of global dynamics. While the world’s wealth has grown in absolute terms, it is increasingly concentrated in fewer hands, widening inequalities both within and between countries. “There are a few who have too much, and too many who have little, that is the logic of today.” [162] Scientific and technological advances, even in the medical field, are not easily accessible to the vast majority of people, as was dramatically demonstrated during the recent pandemic. While some regions spend heavily on superfluous interventions or dreams of individual enhancement accessible only to a select few, other parts of the world lack the essential equipment needed to save millions of human lives. To think that new technologies will automatically benefit everyone is to ignore the evidence. Unless transformations at the design stage prioritize the prevention of new and further disparities, technological progress will inevitably produce structural inequalities. Today, justice requires access to the benefits of innovation, including care, knowledge, tools and opportunities.
blocks.zh: 這一視野需要融入對全球動態更宏觀的理解之中。世界的財富雖然在絕對值上有所增長，卻日益集中在少數人手中，加劇了各國內部及國與國之間的不平等。「少數人擁有太多，太多人擁有太少——這就是當今的邏輯。」[162] 科學與技術的進步，即使在醫療領域，也並非絕大多數人能夠輕易獲得，最近的大流行病已戲劇性地證明了這一點。當某些地區在過度的干預措施上投入巨資，或追逐只有極少數人才能享有的個人增強之夢時，世界的其他地區卻缺乏挽救數百萬人生命所必需的基本設備。若以為新技術會自動惠及所有人，就是對明證視而不見。除非在設計階段的變革就優先考慮防止出現新的、更深遠的差距，否則技術進步將不可避免地產生結構性的不平等。今天，正義要求人們能夠獲得創新的益處，包括照護、知識、工具和機會。
blocks.html: 161. This perspective needs to become part of a broader view of global dynamics. While the world’s wealth has grown in absolute terms, it is increasingly concentrated in fewer hands, widening inequalities both within and between countries. “There are a few who have too much, and too many who have little, that is the logic of today.” [162] Scientific and technological advances, even in the medical field, are not easily accessible to the vast majority of people, as was dramatically demonstrated during the recent pandemic. While some regions spend heavily on superfluous interventions or dreams of individual enhancement accessible only to a select few, other parts of the world lack the essential equipment needed to save millions of human lives. To think that new technologies will automatically benefit everyone is to ignore the evidence. Unless transformations at the design stage prioritize the prevention of new and further disparities, technological progress will inevitably produce structural inequalities. Today, justice requires access to the benefits of innovation, including care, knowledge, tools and opportunities.

blocks.en: Just laws and methods of redistribution are certainly necessary for correcting imbalances, including tax systems that lighten the burden on the weakest and ask for more from those with greater resources. However, the pursuit of social justice should not be considered a separate issue that follows only after the production of wealth, as if the economy existed solely to create wealth, with politicians only intervening afterwards in order to distribute it. Indeed, justice concerns every phase of economic activity, from resource acquisition to financing, and from production to consumption; every choice has moral consequences. [163]
blocks.zh: 公正的法律和再分配方法，包括減輕最弱小者負擔並向擁有較多資源者徵收更多的稅收制度，對於修正失衡確實是必要的。然而，追求社會公義不應被視為一個僅在財富生產之後才處理的獨立議題，彷彿經濟只是為了創造財富而存在，而政治人物只在事後介入以進行分配。事實上，公義關乎經濟活動的每一個階段，從資源獲取到融資，從生產到消費；每一個選擇都具有道德後果。[163]
blocks.html: 162. Just laws and methods of redistribution are certainly necessary for correcting imbalances, including tax systems that lighten the burden on the weakest and ask for more from those with greater resources. However, the pursuit of social justice should not be considered a separate issue that follows only after the production of wealth, as if the economy existed solely to create wealth, with politicians only intervening afterwards in order to distribute it. Indeed, justice concerns every phase of economic activity, from resource acquisition to financing, and from production to consumption; every choice has moral consequences. [163]

blocks.en: More than ever, in the age of AI and robotics, it is no longer possible to rely solely on the “invisible hand” of the market. [164] Politics has the task of orientating economies and technologies to the common good, promoting dignified work, social inclusion and an equitable distribution of the benefits of innovation. Since many economic decisions transcend national borders, there is also a need for international cooperation capable of defining common strategies, especially in favor of the most vulnerable countries and people, in order to promote development and overcome welfare dependency. The thinking behind these choices is the immeasurable dignity of every person, the common good and a world truly governed for everyone. The interdependence between peace and development, as Saint Paul VI prophetically wrote in 1967, [165] remains applicable today, for prosperity contributes to building and reinforcing peace only if it is widespread, inclusive and sustainable.
blocks.zh: 在人工智能與機械人技術的時代，我們比以往任何時候都更不可能單純依賴市場的「無形之手」。[164] 政治有責任將經濟與科技導向公益，促進有尊嚴的工作、社會包容，以及創新利益的公平分配。由於許多經濟決策超越國界，因此也需要國際合作，能夠制定共同的策略，尤其惠及最脆弱的國家和人民，以促進發展並克服福利依賴。這些選擇背後的理念，是每一個人無可估量的尊嚴、公益，以及一個真正為所有人而治理的世界。和平與發展之間的相互依存關係，正如聖保祿六世於1967年先知性地所寫的，[165] 至今依然適用，因為繁榮唯有在普及、包容且可持續的情況下，才有助於建設並鞏固和平。
blocks.html: 163.  More than ever, in the age of AI and robotics, it is no longer possible to rely solely on the “invisible hand” of the market. [164] Politics has the task of orientating economies and technologies to the common good, promoting dignified work, social inclusion and an equitable distribution of the benefits of innovation. Since many economic decisions transcend national borders, there is also a need for international cooperation capable of defining common strategies, especially in favor of the most vulnerable countries and people, in order to promote development and overcome welfare dependency. The thinking behind these choices is the immeasurable dignity of every person, the common good and a world truly governed for everyone. The interdependence between peace and development, as Saint Paul VI prophetically wrote in 1967, [165] remains applicable today, for prosperity contributes to building and reinforcing peace only if it is widespread, inclusive and sustainable.

blocks.en: In practical terms, in the age of AI and robotics, ensuring that the economy favors human dignity means adopting certain criteria for firm action. First, transparency and accountability: when data and algorithms influence credit distribution, personnel selection or access to services and opportunities, it is necessary that decisions be understandable, contestable and subject to oversight, so that individuals are not reduced to mere profiles. Second, inclusion and access: the benefits of innovation must be paired with investments in skills, infrastructure and essential services to ensure that technology does not widen the gap between those who have and those who have not. Finally, measures to ensure equity: taxation, social protection and industrial policies must correct the imbalances created by the concentration of wealth and power. Indeed, these criteria do not constitute a curb on innovation; instead they make it civilized and humane.
blocks.zh: 在實際層面，在人工智能與機械人技術的時代，要確保經濟有利於人的尊嚴，意味著在企業行動上採納若干準則。首先是透明度與問責性：當數據和演算法影響信貸分配、人事遴選或獲取服務與機會時，決策必須是可理解的、可質疑的，並須接受監督，以免個人被簡化為純粹的數據檔案。其次是包容性與可及性：創新的益處必須與在技能、基礎設施及基本服務方面的投資相配合，以確保科技不會擴大有者與無者之間的鴻溝。最後是確保公平的措施：稅收、社會保障及產業政策必須糾正由財富與權力集中所造成的失衡。事實上，這些準則非但不是對創新的束縛，反而使之文明化且合乎人性。
blocks.html: 164. In practical terms, in the age of AI and robotics, ensuring that the economy favors human dignity means adopting certain criteria for firm action. First, transparency and accountability: when data and algorithms influence credit distribution, personnel selection or access to services and opportunities, it is necessary that decisions be understandable, contestable and subject to oversight, so that individuals are not reduced to mere profiles. Second, inclusion and access: the benefits of innovation must be paired with investments in skills, infrastructure and essential services to ensure that technology does not widen the gap between those who have and those who have not. Finally, measures to ensure equity: taxation, social protection and industrial policies must correct the imbalances created by the concentration of wealth and power. Indeed, these criteria do not constitute a curb on innovation; instead they make it civilized and humane.

blocks.en: Families and young people: the social conditions for hope
blocks.zh: 家庭與青年：希望的社會條件
blocks.html: Families and young people: the social conditions for hope

blocks.en: The family is a primary social good. Founded on the enduring union between a man and a woman, it is the first environment in which all persons develop their potential, become aware of their dignity and learn the earliest forms of truth and goodness, internalizing the habits that prepare them for life in society. [166] As the first natural society, endowed with foundational rights, the family is the fundamental and irreplaceable cell of every community organization. [167] Consequently, when political projects and major economic decisions relegate the family to a marginal or secondary role, the authentic growth of the entire social body is compromised. [168]
blocks.zh: 家庭是一項首要的社會公益。家庭奠基於一男一女之間的恆久結合，是每一個人發展其潛能、意識到自身尊嚴，並學習分辨真與善的最初形式、將預備他們投入社會生活的習慣予以內化的首要環境。[166] 作為第一個自然社會，家庭擁有其根本性的權利，是每一個社群組織基本且不可取代的細胞。[167] 因此，當政治計劃和重大的經濟決策將家庭置於邊緣或次要的地位時，整個社會團體的真正成長便會受到損害。[168]
blocks.html: 165. The family is a primary social good. Founded on the enduring union between a man and a woman, it is the first environment in which all persons develop their potential, become aware of their dignity and learn the earliest forms of truth and goodness, internalizing the habits that prepare them for life in society. [166] As the first natural society, endowed with foundational rights, the family is the fundamental and irreplaceable cell of every community organization. [167] Consequently, when political projects and major economic decisions relegate the family to a marginal or secondary role, the authentic growth of the entire social body is compromised. [168]

blocks.en: The family, however, is a fragile social good immediately affected by the economic and technological transformations reshaping the nature of work. It thus requires cultural, juridical and economic support. The devastating impact of unemployment and job insecurity on family structures is well known. In the short term, it may seem advantageous to reduce labor costs or maximize financial efficiency, but in the long term this undermines the very foundations of social coexistence. While technological successes are celebrated, the social fabric is progressively eroded, as if by a silent virus.
blocks.zh: 然而，家庭是一項脆弱的社會公益，直接受到重塑工作本質的經濟及科技變革所衝擊。因此，家庭需要文化、法律及經濟上的支持。失業及就業不穩定對家庭結構帶來的毀滅性影響，是眾所周知的。短期而言，削減勞動成本或將經濟效益最大化似乎有利，但長遠而言，這會侵蝕社會共同生活本身的基礎。正當我們慶祝科技成就之際，社會結構卻在不知不覺間逐步遭到侵蝕，猶如一種無聲的病毒。
blocks.html: 166. The family, however, is a fragile social good immediately affected by the economic and technological transformations reshaping the nature of work. It thus requires cultural, juridical and economic support. The devastating impact of unemployment and job insecurity on family structures is well known. In the short term, it may seem advantageous to reduce labor costs or maximize financial efficiency, but in the long term this undermines the very foundations of social coexistence. While technological successes are celebrated, the social fabric is progressively eroded, as if by a silent virus.

blocks.en: For young people, job insecurity is particularly devastating. As the Bishops of the United States of America have recalled, work is not merely a source of income but a crucial sphere in which identity is formed, friendships and relationships are forged, practical responsibilities are learned and one’s vocation is discerned. [169] When access to work is hindered by high levels of unemployment, inadequate systems of training or structural barriers, many young people find the path to their human and professional fulfilment blocked. The need to change jobs several times over the course of life requires that continuous updating and retraining be provided, so that new generations can competently and independently face the risks of an economic environment that is both changing and often unpredictable. [170]
blocks.zh: 對青年人來說，就業的不穩定性尤其具有毀滅性的影響。正如美國主教團所提醒的，工作不僅是收入的來源，更是身分得以形成、友誼與人際關係得以建立、實際責任得以學習，以及個人聖召得以辨明的重要領域。[169] 當獲得工作的機會因高失業率、不足的培訓制度或結構性障礙而受阻時，許多青年人發現自己走向人性與專業圓滿的道路被堵塞了。在一生中需要多次轉換工作的現實，要求社會必須提供持續的進修和再培訓，好讓新一代能夠勝任且獨立地面對一個既不斷變化又往往難以預測的經濟環境所帶來的風險。[170]
blocks.html: 167. For young people, job insecurity is particularly devastating. As the Bishops of the United States of America have recalled, work is not merely a source of income but a crucial sphere in which identity is formed, friendships and relationships are forged, practical responsibilities are learned and one’s vocation is discerned. [169] When access to work is hindered by high levels of unemployment, inadequate systems of training or structural barriers, many young people find the path to their human and professional fulfilment blocked. The need to change jobs several times over the course of life requires that continuous updating and retraining be provided, so that new generations can competently and independently face the risks of an economic environment that is both changing and often unpredictable. [170]

blocks.en: This gives rise to a specific public responsibility. The State has the duty to support business activity by fostering conditions favorable to employment, promoting work where it is lacking and defending it in times of crisis, since it is a primary good for families and for society. [171] Particularly in an age of continuous technological transformation, we need a political creativity that will promote “work” and place the family and coming generations at the center; otherwise our economic progress will translate into new forms of insecurity and exclusion.
blocks.zh: 這產生了一項特定的公共責任。國家有責任支持商業活動，透過創造有利於就業的條件、在缺乏工作機會的地方促進工作，並在危機時期加以捍衛，因為工作對家庭和社會而言是一項首要的善。[171] 尤其在一個科技持續轉型的時代，我們需要一種能促進「工作」並將家庭及後代置於中心的政治創造力；否則，我們的經濟進步將轉化為新的不安全感與排斥的新形式。
blocks.html: 168. This gives rise to a specific public responsibility. The State has the duty to support business activity by fostering conditions favorable to employment, promoting work where it is lacking and defending it in times of crisis, since it is a primary good for families and for society. [171] Particularly in an age of continuous technological transformation, we need a political creativity that will promote “work” and place the family and coming generations at the center; otherwise our economic progress will translate into new forms of insecurity and exclusion.

blocks.en: Supporting families and young people in this transition requires choices that make stability feasible. As has been noted above, labor policies need to promote continuity and the quality of employment, countering insecurity as a normal condition of life and encouraging realistic paths for entry into the workforce and for professional growth. Second, measures are needed to ensure a healthy way of living, for without a proper balance between work, leisure and rest, families are weakened and young people struggle to develop a sense of responsibility. Furthermore, it is essential to invest in accessible education and retraining, so that the professional mobility demanded by the digital economy does not become a harsh selection between those who are able to update their skills and those who cannot. Finally, social ties must be supported, with networks and educational communities that accompany life choices and prevent uncertainty from giving rise to loneliness or addictions. If implemented, these technological transformations can be navigated without undermining the capacity to build the future, which is what makes a society prosperous.
blocks.zh: 在這場轉型中支持家庭和青年，需要作出能使穩定變得可行的選擇。如前所述，勞工政策必須促進就業的連續性和品質，抗拒將不穩定視為生活的正常狀況，並鼓勵實際可行的途徑，讓人們進入勞動市場並獲得專業成長。其次，我們需要採取措施，確保一種健康的生活方式，因為若沒有工作、休閒和休息之間的適當平衡，家庭就會被削弱，青年亦難以培養責任感。再者，投資於可及的優質教育和再培訓至關重要，好讓數碼經濟所要求的職業流動性，不致淪為能夠更新技能者與無法更新技能者之間的殘酷篩選。最後，必須支持社會聯繫，建立陪伴人生抉擇的網絡和教育團體，防止不確定性衍生出孤獨或成癮問題。若能落實這些措施，這些技術轉型便能在不削弱建設未來的能力——這正是使社會繁榮昌盛的要素——的情況下安然渡過。
blocks.html: 169. Supporting families and young people in this transition requires choices that make stability feasible. As has been noted above, labor policies need to promote continuity and the quality of employment, countering insecurity as a normal condition of life and encouraging realistic paths for entry into the workforce and for professional growth. Second, measures are needed to ensure a healthy way of living, for without a proper balance between work, leisure and rest, families are weakened and young people struggle to develop a sense of responsibility.  Furthermore, it is essential to invest in accessible education and retraining, so that the professional mobility demanded by the digital economy does not become a harsh selection between those who are able to update their skills and those who cannot. Finally, social ties must be supported, with networks and educational communities that accompany life choices and prevent uncertainty from giving rise to loneliness or addictions. If implemented, these technological transformations can be navigated without undermining the capacity to build the future, which is what makes a society prosperous.

blocks.en: Protecting freedom against dependencies and commercialization
blocks.zh: 保護自由，免於依賴與商品化
blocks.html: Protecting freedom against dependencies and commercialization

blocks.en: Dependencies and societal control
blocks.zh: 依賴與社會控制
blocks.html: Dependencies and societal control

blocks.en: Having reflected on truth and education, work and families, we must now consider the impact of the digital revolution on human freedom, addressing risks to both the mental health of individuals and broader social challenges. The subtler forms of addiction linked to the “digital attention economy” should not be underestimated, since platforms and services are often designed to capture users’ time and attention, exploiting their vulnerabilities and weakening their inner freedom. When business models thrive on human weakness, the person is treated as a means rather than as an end; those who design or finance such systems bear a moral responsibility that cannot be ignored. There is an urgent need to promote technologies that strengthen interior freedom by fostering education in digital sobriety and the protection of minors, thus countering models that exploit vulnerability.
blocks.zh: 在反思過真理與教育、工作與家庭之後，我們現在必須審視數碼革命對人類自由的影響，正視它對個人精神健康以及更廣泛社會挑戰所帶來的危機。與「數碼注意力經濟」相關、較為隱晦的成癮形式不容低估，因為各種平台和服務往往被設計成要擄獲用戶的時間和注意力，利用他們的脆弱性並削弱他們內在的自由。當商業模式依附著人性的軟弱而蓬勃發展時，人便被當作工具而非目的；那些設計或資助此類系統的人，肩上負有無可推卸的道德責任。我們迫切需要推廣能強化內心自由的技術，方法是培育數碼節制方面的教育以及對未成年人的保護，從而抗衡那些利用脆弱性的模式。
blocks.html: 170. Having reflected on truth and education, work and families, we must now consider the impact of the digital revolution on human freedom, addressing risks to both the mental health of individuals and broader social challenges. The subtler forms of addiction linked to the “digital attention economy” should not be underestimated, since platforms and services are often designed to capture users’ time and attention, exploiting their vulnerabilities and weakening their inner freedom. When business models thrive on human weakness, the person is treated as a means rather than as an end; those who design or finance such systems bear a moral responsibility that cannot be ignored. There is an urgent need to promote technologies that strengthen interior freedom by fostering education in digital sobriety and the protection of minors, thus countering models that exploit vulnerability.

blocks.en: A further risk, less visible but no less serious, is that of social control made possible by the massive collection of data and use of algorithmic systems. When every action—movements, purchases, relationships and preferences—leaves a trace, a new form of power emerges, namely the power to profile, predict and influence behavior, often without individuals being fully aware of it. If such kinds of data are used to make decisions affecting concrete opportunities — such as access to credit, employment or essential services — there is a risk of undermining freedom and discriminating against the most vulnerable. Furthermore, control is exercised not only through explicit prohibitions, but also through the architecture of visibility: what is amplified or rendered invisible, what is rewarded or penalized, ultimately shapes opinions and choices, fostering conformity and self-censorship. For this reason, freedom in the digital age is not merely a matter of interiority but also a public concern. It calls for clear rules, transparency, the possibility of recourse and proportionate limits on the use of intrusive technologies, so that technology will remain at the service of the human person and not become a form of control over consciences.
blocks.zh: 另一個較不明顯但同樣嚴重的風險，是通過大規模收集數據和使用演算法系統而得以實現的社會控制。當每一個行動——移動、消費、關係和偏好——都留下痕跡時，一種新的權力形式便應運而生，即對行為進行剖析、預測和影響的權力，而個人往往對此並不完全知情。如果此類數據被用來做出影響具體機會的決定——例如獲取信貸、就業或基本服務的機會——便存在損害自由和歧視最弱勢群體的風險。此外，控制不僅通過明確的禁令來行使，也通過可見性的架構來行使：什麼被放大或隱沒、什麼被獎勵或懲罰，最終塑造了意見和選擇，助長了從眾和自我審查。為此原因，數碼時代的自由不僅是一個內心世界的問題，也是一項公共關切。它要求明確的規則、透明度、申訴的可能性，以及對侵入性技術的使用設定相稱的限制，好使技術始終為人服務，而非成為一種對良心的控制形式。
blocks.html: 171. A further risk, less visible but no less serious, is that of social control made possible by the massive collection of data and use of algorithmic systems. When every action—movements, purchases, relationships and preferences—leaves a trace, a new form of power emerges, namely the power to profile, predict and influence behavior, often without individuals being fully aware of it. If such kinds of data are used to make decisions affecting concrete opportunities — such as access to credit, employment or essential services — there is a risk of undermining freedom and discriminating against the most vulnerable. Furthermore, control is exercised not only through explicit prohibitions, but also through the architecture of visibility: what is amplified or rendered invisible, what is rewarded or penalized, ultimately shapes opinions and choices, fostering conformity and self-censorship. For this reason, freedom in the digital age is not merely a matter of interiority but also a public concern. It calls for clear rules, transparency, the possibility of recourse and proportionate limits on the use of intrusive technologies, so that technology will remain at the service of the human person and not become a form of control over consciences.

blocks.en: At the root of these problems lies a technocratic and post-humanist mentality that tends to regard the human person as an object to be manipulated or a resource to be optimized, [172] removing all safeguards against the unchecked pursuit of profit. What prevails is efficiency, rather than respect for freedom and human dignity. Some post-humanist currents even go so far as to envision “second-class” human beings, subordinate to the interests of elites who consider themselves superior. This troubling prospect becomes all the more serious when combined with technological tools that exponentially increase the capacity for control and selection. Even certain forms of structural indebtedness, which keep entire peoples in conditions of dependence, reflect the same mentality, in new forms, that tolerates relationships of subordination akin to slavery.
blocks.zh: 這些問題的根源，在於一種技術至上與後人類主義的心態，它傾向於將人視為可被操控的對象或可被優化的資源，[172] 從而取消了對利潤無限追逐的一切保障。居於主導地位的是效率，而非對自由與人性尊嚴的尊重。某些後人類主義的潮流甚至設想出「次等」人類，使之隸屬於自視為優越的精英階層的利益之下。當這種令人不安的前景與那些以指數級增長的控制和篩選能力的技術工具相結合時，就顯得愈發嚴峻。即便是某些結構性的債務形式，它們使整個民族長期處於依附狀態，也以新的形式反映出同樣的心態——這種心態容忍近乎奴役的從屬關係。
blocks.html: 172. At the root of these problems lies a technocratic and post-humanist mentality that tends to regard the human person as an object to be manipulated or a resource to be optimized, [172] removing all safeguards against the unchecked pursuit of profit. What prevails is efficiency, rather than respect for freedom and human dignity. Some post-humanist currents even go so far as to envision “second-class” human beings, subordinate to the interests of elites who consider themselves superior. This troubling prospect becomes all the more serious when combined with technological tools that exponentially increase the capacity for control and selection. Even certain forms of structural indebtedness, which keep entire peoples in conditions of dependence, reflect the same mentality, in new forms, that tolerates relationships of subordination akin to slavery.

blocks.en: Breaking the chains of new forms of slavery
blocks.zh: 打破新形式奴役的枷鎖
blocks.html: Breaking the chains of new forms of slavery

blocks.en: This distorted view of the human person is reflected today in various forms of servitude directly linked to the digital economy. Nothing in the world of AI is immaterial or magical. Every seemingly immediate and flawless response is the result of a long chain of mediation, involving vast networks of natural resources, energy infrastructure and, above all, people. A significant part of the digital economy’s functioning relies on the silent work of millions of people engaged in essential yet largely unseen activities, such as data labeling, model training and content moderation, often involving disturbing material. In many cases, these workers are young people, predominantly women, working under demanding conditions for minimal wages. Added to this invisible labor is the even harsher work of extracting the resources required for the production of the devices and microprocessors on which AI depends. In some regions of the world, children and adolescents work in dangerous conditions, crushing the materials from which rare earth elements are extracted. The bodies of these people are scarred, injured and worn down so that computational flow may continue uninterruptedly. Furthermore, criminal networks use online platforms, messaging systems, anonymous payment methods and profiling techniques in order to recruit, control and transport victims of trafficking — very often minors — reducing men and women to “data” to be tracked and “packages” to be moved around within the same digital circuits that support much of the global economy. This reality deeply challenges the moral conscience of our time. It is not enough to invoke efficiency, nor to celebrate the benefits of innovation, if they are built on a chain of exploitation that remains deliberately hidden. If technology promises emancipation, yet produces new forms of global subordination, it stands in contradiction to the fundamental principle of human dignity.
blocks.zh: 這種對人的扭曲觀點，今天反映在與數碼經濟直接相關的各種奴役形式中。在人工智能世界裡，沒有任何東西是非物質的或帶有魔法的。每一個看似即時且毫無瑕疵的回應，都是一長串中介過程的結果，牽涉到廣闊的天然資源網絡、能源基礎設施，以及最重要的——人。數碼經濟運作的相當一部分，有賴數百萬人默默無聲的勞動，他們從事著不可或缺但大多不為人知的活動，例如數據標註、模型訓練和內容審核，這些工作往往涉及令人不安的材料。在許多情況下，這些工人是年輕人，且以女性為主，他們在嚴苛的條件下工作，換取微薄的薪酬。除了這種隱形的勞動之外，還有更為艱苦的工作，即開採生產人工智能所依賴的設備和微處理器所需的資源。在世界某些地區，兒童和青少年在危險的環境中工作，粉碎著用作提取稀土元素的礦石。這些人的身體佈滿傷痕、受到傷害、備受磨損，只是為了讓運算流程能不受干擾地繼續下去。此外，犯罪網絡利用網上平台、通訊系統、匿名支付方式和特徵分析技術，來招募、控制和運送販運受害者——這些受害者往往是未成年人——將男人和女人貶抑為待追蹤的「數據」和待轉移的「包裹」，在這支撐著大部分全球經濟的同一數碼迴路中被來回運送。這一現實深深挑戰著我們時代的道德良心。若創新所建立的基礎是一條蓄意隱藏的剝削鏈條，那麼僅僅援引效率或頌揚創新的益處是遠遠不夠的。如果科技許諾解放，卻製造出全球性的新型從屬關係，那它就與人性尊嚴的基本原則背道而馳。
blocks.html: 173. This distorted view of the human person is reflected today in various forms of servitude directly linked to the digital economy. Nothing in the world of AI is immaterial or magical. Every seemingly immediate and flawless response is the result of a long chain of mediation, involving vast networks of natural resources, energy infrastructure and, above all, people. A significant part of the digital economy’s functioning relies on the silent work of millions of people engaged in essential yet largely unseen activities, such as data labeling, model training and content moderation, often involving disturbing material. In many cases, these workers are young people, predominantly women, working under demanding conditions for minimal wages. Added to this invisible labor is the even harsher work of extracting the resources required for the production of the devices and microprocessors on which AI depends. In some regions of the world, children and adolescents work in dangerous conditions, crushing the materials from which rare earth elements are extracted. The bodies of these people are scarred, injured and worn down so that computational flow may continue uninterruptedly. Furthermore, criminal networks use online platforms, messaging systems, anonymous payment methods and profiling techniques in order to recruit, control and transport victims of trafficking — very often minors — reducing men and women to “data” to be tracked and “packages” to be moved around within the same digital circuits that support much of the global economy. This reality deeply challenges the moral conscience of our time. It is not enough to invoke efficiency, nor to celebrate the benefits of innovation, if they are built on a chain of exploitation that remains deliberately hidden. If technology promises emancipation, yet produces new forms of global subordination, it stands in contradiction to the fundamental principle of human dignity.

blocks.en: The fight against new forms of slavery is a decisive test for the ethical discernment of AI and digital transformation. In continuity with the tradition inaugurated by Leo XIII, the Church renews her firm condemnation of all forms of slavery, trafficking and the commodification of persons. She likewise highlights the urgent need for reflection and action that keep the inalienable dignity of every human being and the common good, as both the focus and goal of society, as well as the guiding criteria for every personal, social and political choice. Without this ethical and humanizing reflection, the growing power of digital systems could lead us toward new atrocities that are no less shameful than those of the past that we now deplore, while we continue to present ourselves as “advanced” and “civilized” societies.
blocks.zh: 對抗新形式的奴役，是對人工智能及數碼轉型進行倫理分辨的一項決定性考驗。教會延續由良十三世開啟的傳統，再次堅定譴責一切形式的奴役、人口販運及將人商品化的行為。教會同樣強調，迫切需要進行反思和採取行動，以每一個人不可剝奪的尊嚴和公益，作為社會的中心與目標，以及一切個人、社會和政治抉擇的指導準則。缺乏這種倫理和人道化的反思，數碼系統日益增長的力量，便可能將我們引向新的暴行，其可恥程度絕不亞於我們今天所痛惜的過去那些暴行，而我們卻仍自詡為「先進」和「文明」的社會。
blocks.html: 174. The fight against new forms of slavery is a decisive test for the ethical discernment of AI and digital transformation. In continuity with the tradition inaugurated by Leo XIII, the Church renews her firm condemnation of all forms of slavery, trafficking and the commodification of persons. She likewise highlights the urgent need for reflection and action that keep the inalienable dignity of every human being and the common good, as both the focus and goal of society, as well as the guiding criteria for every personal, social and political choice. Without this ethical and humanizing reflection, the growing power of digital systems could lead us toward new atrocities that are no less shameful than those of the past that we now deplore, while we continue to present ourselves as “advanced” and “civilized” societies.

blocks.en: Human trafficking must be recognized as a contemporary form of slavery and a grave violation of human dignity. Failing to respond firmly, or tolerating these practices in any way, is in some way to become complicit in today’s sins, which are akin to those of the past when slavery was being concealed and justified. [173]
blocks.zh: 人口販運必須被認定為現代形式的奴隸制度，並構成對人性尊嚴的嚴重侵犯。如果未能堅定回應，或以任何方式容忍這些行為，便在某種程度上成了今日罪惡的同謀──這些罪惡與過去奴隸制度被掩飾及合理化時的罪惡如出一轍。[173]
blocks.html: 175. Human trafficking must be recognized as a contemporary form of slavery and a grave violation of human dignity. Failing to respond firmly, or tolerating these practices in any way, is in some way to become complicit in today’s sins, which are akin to those of the past when slavery was being concealed and justified. [173]

blocks.en: In the development of her doctrine, the Church has gradually come to a deeper awareness of the gravity of these issues. It is true that past events cannot be judged anachronistically, as though the moral criteria that matured over time had always been available. Yet neither can we deny or diminish the delay with which both society and the Church came to denounce the scourge of slavery. In antiquity and the Middle Ages many individuals and even ecclesiastical institutions had slaves. Already in the early modern period, the Apostolic See of Rome, responding to requests from Sovereigns, intervened several times in order to regulate and legitimize forms of subjugation, and, in certain cases, the enslavement of “infidels.” [174] It was only in the nineteenth century that a formal, absolute and universal condemnation of slavery was clearly articulated, notably under Pope Leo XIII. [175] This development offers a clear example of the Church’s growth in understanding the perennial truths of Revelation that she safeguards. Although there was not always consistency in practice — given that slavery was long tolerated before being unequivocally condemned — there has been a continuous affirmation throughout history of the dignity of every human being, created in the image of God, even if it took eighteen centuries for its full incompatibility with slavery to be explicitly recognized. This constitutes a wound in Christian memory, one from which we cannot consider ourselves detached. [176] It is impossible not to feel deep sorrow when contemplating the immense suffering and humiliation endured by so many in stark contrast to their immeasurable dignity as persons infinitely loved by the Lord. For this, in the name of the Church, I sincerely ask for pardon.
blocks.zh: 在教會教義的發展過程中，教會逐漸對這些問題的嚴重性有了更深刻的認識。誠然，我們不能以時代錯置的方式來評判過去的事件，彷彿隨著時間而成熟的道德標準一直存在一樣。然而，我們同樣不能否認或輕描淡寫社會和教會在譴責奴隸制這一禍害方面的遲延。在古代和中世紀，許多個人，甚至教會機構，都擁有奴隸。早在近代初期，羅馬宗座便應君主們的請求，多次進行干預，以規範和合法化各種形式的奴役，並在某些情況下，將「不信者」的奴役加以合法化。[174] 直到十九世紀，對奴隸制的正式、絕對且普遍的譴責才被明確地闡述出來，特別是在教宗良十三世任內。[175] 這一發展提供了一個清晰的例證，顯示教會在理解她所守護的啟示之永恆真理方面的成長。儘管在實踐上並非始終一致——鑑於奴隸制在被毫不含糊地譴責之前曾長期被容忍——但在整個歷史中，對每一個按照天主肖像所造之人的尊嚴，一直都有持續不斷的肯定，即使花了十八個世紀，這種尊嚴與奴隸制的完全不相容才被明確地承認。這構成了基督徒記憶中的一道創傷，我們不能認為自己與此無關。[176] 當默觀如此多的人所忍受的巨大苦難和羞辱，而這與他們作為上主所無限愛慕之人的不可估量的尊嚴形成鮮明對比時，我們不可能不感到深切的悲痛。為此，我以教會的名義，誠摯地請求寬恕。
blocks.html: 176. In the development of her doctrine, the Church has gradually come to a deeper awareness of the gravity of these issues. It is true that past events cannot be judged anachronistically, as though the moral criteria that matured over time had always been available. Yet neither can we deny or diminish the delay with which both society and the Church came to denounce the scourge of slavery. In antiquity and the Middle Ages many individuals and even ecclesiastical institutions had slaves. Already in the early modern period, the Apostolic See of Rome, responding to requests from Sovereigns, intervened several times in order to regulate and legitimize forms of subjugation, and, in certain cases, the enslavement of “infidels.” [174] It was only in the nineteenth century that a formal, absolute and universal condemnation of slavery was clearly articulated, notably under Pope Leo XIII. [175] This development offers a clear example of the Church’s growth in understanding the perennial truths of Revelation that she safeguards. Although there was not always consistency in practice — given that slavery was long tolerated before being unequivocally condemned — there has been a continuous affirmation throughout history of the dignity of every human being, created in the image of God, even if it took eighteen centuries for its full incompatibility with slavery to be explicitly recognized. This constitutes a wound in Christian memory, one from which we cannot consider ourselves detached. [176] It is impossible not to feel deep sorrow when contemplating the immense suffering and humiliation endured by so many in stark contrast to their immeasurable dignity as persons infinitely loved by the Lord. For this, in the name of the Church, I sincerely ask for pardon.

blocks.en: This is why the memory of past complicity and blindness in the face of the injustice of slavery becomes a call to vigilance. What we have learned must be translated into discernment and responsibility in the present. If we want to avoid the need to ask for pardon again in the future for having failed to respect the treasure of human dignity that is required by our faith, it falls to us today to denounce, clearly and firmly, trafficking in its many forms and, together with all who are committed to this cause, to support concrete efforts of prevention, protection, liberation and rehabilitation.
blocks.zh: 正因如此，對昔日面對奴役的不義時那種同謀與盲目的記憶，便成為一份對警醒的召喚。我們所汲取的教訓，必須轉化為當下的分辨與責任。如果我們欲避免在未來，因未能尊重我們信仰所要求的人性尊嚴這寶藏而再次需要請求寬恕，那麼今天，我們便有責任清晰而堅定地譴責各種形式的販運人口行為，並與所有投身這項事業的人一起，支持預防、保護、解救和康復的具體努力。
blocks.html: 177. This is why the memory of past complicity and blindness in the face of the injustice of slavery becomes a call to vigilance. What we have learned must be translated into discernment and responsibility in the present. If we want to avoid the need to ask for pardon again in the future for having failed to respect the treasure of human dignity that is required by our faith, it falls to us today to denounce, clearly and firmly, trafficking in its many forms and, together with all who are committed to this cause, to support concrete efforts of prevention, protection, liberation and rehabilitation.

blocks.en: Even today, colonialism assumes new forms. It no longer dominates only bodies, but appropriates data, transforming personal lives into exploitable information. Entire regions, especially those marked by structural fragility and limited geopolitical relevance, are currently subjected to a new mindset of extraction: that of health data, epidemiological profiles, genetic maps and demographic information. These have become the new “rare earths” of power: vital data which, once aggregated and analyzed, can be used to train predictive models, guide investment strategies, anticipate crises and, above all, determine who and what is deemed to matter. Those who control the health data of entire peoples — often collected under the pretext of aid, research or innovation — possess a structural leverage over the future, for they can shape needs and markets. They can also decide, before others, to whom medicines, investments and protections will be allocated. Here lies one of the most urgent moral challenges of our time: to ensure that shared knowledge becomes a true common good rather than an instrument of dominance. This requires restoring to individuals not only the data that describes them, but also the ability to decide how it is used, by whom and for whose benefit. Otherwise, the digital age will not be post-colonial, but colonial in another form.
blocks.zh: 即使到了今天，殖民主義依然以新的形式呈現。它不再僅僅支配血肉之軀，而是侵奪數據，將個人的生命轉化為可供剝削的資訊。整個地區，特別是那些結構脆弱、地緣政治影響力有限的地區，現正面臨一種新的掠取心態：健康數據、流行病學資料、基因圖譜和人口統計資訊的掠取。這些已然成為權力領域新的「稀土」：這些至關重要的數據一經匯集和分析，便可用於訓練預測模型、指導投資策略、預測危機，而最關鍵的是，它們能決定誰和甚麼被視為重要。那些掌握著整個民族健康數據的人——這些數據往往以援助、研究或創新的名義收集——手執對未來的結構性槓桿，因為他們能塑造需求和市場。他們還能在他人之前，決定藥物、投資和保障將分配給誰。我們這個時代最迫切的道德挑戰之一正在於此：確保共享的知識成為真正的公益，而非支配的工具。這不僅需要將描述個人的數據歸還給他們，更需要恢復他們決定這些數據如何使用、由誰使用及為了誰的益處而使用的能力。否則，數碼時代將不會是後殖民時代，而是另一種形式的殖民時代。
blocks.html: 178.  Even today, colonialism assumes new forms. It no longer dominates only bodies, but appropriates data, transforming personal lives into exploitable information.  Entire regions, especially those marked by structural fragility and limited geopolitical relevance, are currently subjected to a new mindset of extraction: that of health data, epidemiological profiles, genetic maps and demographic information. These have become the new “rare earths” of power: vital data which, once aggregated and analyzed, can be used to train predictive models, guide investment strategies, anticipate crises and, above all, determine who and what is deemed to matter. Those who control the health data of entire peoples — often collected under the pretext of aid, research or innovation — possess a structural leverage over the future, for they can shape needs and markets. They can also decide, before others, to whom medicines, investments and protections will be allocated. Here lies one of the most urgent moral challenges of our time: to ensure that shared knowledge becomes a true common good rather than an instrument of dominance. This requires restoring to individuals not only the data that describes them, but also the ability to decide how it is used, by whom and for whose benefit. Otherwise, the digital age will not be post-colonial, but colonial in another form.

blocks.en: New forms of slavery are fueled by economic chains and digital infrastructures. Therefore, action is required on several fronts. First, the supply chains that underpin the technological industry and the digital economy need to become more transparent, so that no competitive advantage is built upon hidden exploitation. Second, companies and investors need to adopt clear criteria for preventive ethical verification (due diligence), placing among their priorities the protection of workers, the fight against forced labor and the assessment of the social impact of data-driven business models. Furthermore, digital platforms must cooperate responsibly with authorities and civil society to prevent communication, payment and profiling tools from becoming channels for the recruitment and control of victims. When such efforts converge, the digital environment can be transformed from a space of exploitation into one of protection, prevention and the promotion of human dignity.
blocks.zh: 新的奴役形式是由經濟枷鎖和數碼基礎設施所助長的。因此，需要在多個方面採取行動。首先，支撐科技產業和數碼經濟的供應鏈必須變得更加透明，以致沒有任何競爭優勢是建立在隱蔽的剝削之上的。其次，企業和投資者需要採納明確的預防性倫理審查（盡職調查）標準，將保護工人、打擊強迫勞動，以及評估數據驅動商業模式的社會影響列為優先事項。此外，數碼平台必須負責任地與當局和公民社會合作，防止通訊、支付和用戶分析工具成為招募和控制受害者的渠道。當這些努力匯聚在一起時，數碼環境就能從一個剝削的空間，轉變為一個保護、預防和促進人性尊嚴的空間。
blocks.html: 179. New forms of slavery are fueled by economic chains and digital infrastructures.  Therefore, action is required on several fronts. First, the supply chains that underpin the technological industry and the digital economy need to become more transparent, so that no competitive advantage is built upon hidden exploitation. Second, companies and investors need to adopt clear criteria for preventive ethical verification (due diligence), placing among their priorities the protection of workers, the fight against forced labor and the assessment of the social impact of data-driven business models. Furthermore, digital platforms must cooperate responsibly with authorities and civil society to prevent communication, payment and profiling tools from becoming channels for the recruitment and control of victims. When such efforts converge, the digital environment can be transformed from a space of exploitation into one of protection, prevention and the promotion of human dignity.

blocks.en: A shared responsibility
blocks.zh: 共同的責任
blocks.html: A shared responsibility

blocks.en: The various areas just considered— the search for the truth in public life, education in the digital environment, the transformation of work, the fragility of families and new forms of slavery—are not isolated phenomena. Rather, they reflect a common underlying issue, namely that if technology becomes the ultimate criterion, the human person risks being reduced to data, a cog in a machine or a commodity. If, however, technology is integrated with a wise perspective, it can become an instrument of growth, justice and fraternity.
blocks.zh: 剛才所論及的各個範疇──公共生活中對真理的尋求、數碼環境中的教育、工作的轉型、家庭的脆弱以及新型奴役──並非孤立的現象。相反，它們反映出一個共同的深層問題，即：如果科技成為最終的標準，人便有被簡化為數據、機器中的齒輪或商品的危險。然而，如果科技與明智的視野相結合，它就能成為促進成長、正義與友愛的工具。
blocks.html: 180. The various areas just considered— the search for the truth in public life, education in the digital environment, the transformation of work, the fragility of families and new forms of slavery—are not isolated phenomena. Rather, they reflect a common underlying issue, namely that if technology becomes the ultimate criterion, the human person risks being reduced to data, a cog in a machine or a commodity. If, however, technology is integrated with a wise perspective, it can become an instrument of growth, justice and fraternity.

blocks.en: From this perspective, the Social Doctrine of the Church calls for a shared responsibility. It asks that these processes be guided with foresight: by institutions capable of regulating without stifling, and protecting without taking over; by businesses that recognize work and dignity as measures of success; by intermediary organizations and educational communities that rebuild trust and relationships; and by citizens who cultivate responsibility, moderation, discernment and a sense of truth. Only in this way can innovation genuinely serve integral human development, rather than becoming a source of exclusion and dominance. And only in this way can the promise of progress be recognized as authentic, because it is measured against the inviolable dignity of every man and woman.
blocks.zh: 從這個角度來看，教會社會訓導呼籲一種共同的責任。它要求這些進程以具前瞻性的方式加以引導：由那些能夠在規範中不致窒息的、在保護中不致包辦代替的機構來引導；由那些承認工作與尊嚴為成功之尺度的企業來引導；由那些重建信任與人際關係的中介組織和教育團體來引導；以及由那些培養責任感、節制、明辨和真理意識的公民來引導。唯有如此，創新才能真正服務於人的整體發展，而非成為排斥與支配的根源。也唯有如此，進步的承諾才能被認作是真實的，因為它是以每一個男人和女人不可侵犯的尊嚴為衡量標準的。
blocks.html: 181. From this perspective, the Social Doctrine of the Church calls for a shared responsibility. It asks that these processes be guided with foresight: by institutions capable of regulating without stifling, and protecting without taking over; by businesses that recognize work and dignity as measures of success; by intermediary organizations and educational communities that rebuild trust and relationships; and by citizens who cultivate responsibility, moderation, discernment and a sense of truth. Only in this way can innovation genuinely serve integral human development, rather than becoming a source of exclusion and dominance. And only in this way can the promise of progress be recognized as authentic, because it is measured against the inviolable dignity of every man and woman.

sections.title_en: THE CULTURE OF POWER AND THE CIVILIZATION OF LOVE
sections.title_zh: 權力的文化與愛的文明

blocks.en: Having considered how AI is transforming certain aspects of life and society, in particular the serious implications for human dignity, we must now turn our attention to the yet more tragic issue of war. Here the question is not merely the efficiency of new tools, but also the risk that technology, detached from ethics and responsibility, will render decisions about life and death more rapid and impersonal, and will present the use of force as an immediate and viable option. In an increasingly interdependent world, peace is not simply one issue among others, but a prerequisite for the universal common good and a test of the moral maturity of peoples, especially of those who bear responsibility for governing.
blocks.zh: 在探討了人工智能如何改變生活與社會的某些層面，特別是對人性尊嚴的深遠影響之後，我們現在必須將注意力轉向一個更為慘烈的問題：戰爭。在這方面，問題不僅在於新工具的效能，更在於脫離倫理與責任約束的科技，將使關乎生死存亡的決定變得更迅速、更不近人情，並將使用武力呈現為一個即時而可行的選擇。在一個日益相互依存的世界中，和平並非僅是眾多議題之一，而是普世公益的先決條件，也是考驗各民族道德成熟度的試金石，尤其是那些肩負治理責任的民族。
blocks.html: 182. Having considered how AI is transforming certain aspects of life and society, in particular the serious implications for human dignity, we must now turn our attention to the yet more tragic issue of war. Here the question is not merely the efficiency of new tools, but also the risk that technology, detached from ethics and responsibility, will render decisions about life and death more rapid and impersonal, and will present the use of force as an immediate and viable option. In an increasingly interdependent world, peace is not simply one issue among others, but a prerequisite for the universal common good and a test of the moral maturity of peoples, especially of those who bear responsibility for governing.

blocks.en: The digital revolution is changing the nature of conflict. Alongside conventional warfare, there are hybrid forms such as cyberattacks, information manipulation, campaigns of influence and the automation of strategic decisions. AI acts as an accelerating factor in these processes, particularly within a context where many technologies are intrinsically ambivalent. Consequently, what is created for defense can be rapidly repurposed for offense, and the fine line between protection and aggression becomes blurred. While AI can enhance the defense and protection of civilians, it can also lower the threshold for the use of force, shield people from responsibility and foster a culture in which the enemy is reduced to a statistic and the victim to “collateral damage.” Faced with these transformations, we must recall the principles of Social Doctrine — the dignity of the person, the common good, the universal destination of goods, subsidiarity, solidarity and justice — for they are criteria for judging whether technologies truly serve humanity or are subjugating it. We should, therefore, consider these principles as guidelines for our decision-making.
blocks.zh: 數碼革命正在改變衝突的本質。除了傳統戰爭之外，還出現了混合形式的衝突，例如網絡攻擊、資訊操縱、影響力行動，以及戰略決策的自動化。人工智能在這些過程中扮演著加速因素的角色，尤其是在許多技術本質上具有模稜兩可性的背景下。因此，為防禦而創造的東西可以迅速被重新用於進攻，保護與侵略之間的界線變得模糊不清。雖然人工智能可以加強對平民的防禦和保護，但它也可能降低使用武力的門檻，使人們免受責任的約束，並助長一種將敵人貶低為統計數字、將受害者貶低為「附帶損害」的文化。面對這些轉變，我們必須回顧教會社會訓導的原則——人格尊嚴、公益、財物的普遍歸向、輔助原則、團結互助與正義——因為這些原則是判斷科技究竟是真正服務於人類，還是在奴役人類的準則。因此，我們應將這些原則視為我們決策的指引。
blocks.html: 183. The digital revolution is changing the nature of conflict. Alongside conventional warfare, there are hybrid forms such as cyberattacks, information manipulation, campaigns of influence and the automation of strategic decisions. AI acts as an accelerating factor in these processes, particularly within a context where many technologies are intrinsically ambivalent. Consequently, what is created for defense can be rapidly repurposed for offense, and the fine line between protection and aggression becomes blurred. While AI can enhance the defense and protection of civilians, it can also lower the threshold for the use of force, shield people from responsibility and foster a culture in which the enemy is reduced to a statistic and the victim to “collateral damage.” Faced with these transformations, we must recall the principles of Social Doctrine — the dignity of the person, the common good, the universal destination of goods, subsidiarity, solidarity and justice — for they are criteria for judging whether technologies truly serve humanity or are subjugating it. We should, therefore, consider these principles as guidelines for our decision-making.

blocks.en: In this chapter, therefore, I will compare two opposing approaches, which I have already evoked through biblical imagery in the Introduction. On the one hand, there is the temptation of constructing the Tower of Babel, relying on power and pride. On the other hand, patience is required in order to rebuild Jerusalem “piece by piece,” as in the time of Nehemiah, by safeguarding humanity and the common good.
blocks.zh: 因此，在本章中，我將對比兩種對立的進路，這兩種進路我已在導言中以聖經圖像作了提示。一方面是興建巴貝耳塔的誘惑，依恃權力與驕傲。另一方面，則需要耐心，如同在乃赫米雅的時代那樣，「一塊一塊地」重建耶路撒冷，以守護人性與公益。
blocks.html: 184. In this chapter, therefore, I will compare two opposing approaches, which I have already evoked through biblical imagery in the Introduction. On the one hand, there is the temptation of constructing the Tower of Babel, relying on power and pride. On the other hand, patience is required in order to rebuild Jerusalem “piece by piece,” as in the time of Nehemiah, by safeguarding humanity and the common good.

blocks.en: If we examine global dynamics, we can recognize more clearly the spread of a culture of power characterized by polarization and violence. The modern Babel can be seen not only in the globalized technocratic paradigm, but also in the remote clash between opposing imperialisms, between powers that wish to preserve their supremacy, and those that aspire to seize that supremacy, resulting in a multiplicity of local conflicts. Moreover, there seems to be no limit to the race — driven by a dehumanizing ambition — to develop evermore powerful technologies or to secure control over them. Yet, despite this downward spiral, we can also glimpse a great part of humanity that is striving to remain human and working to build the holy city of coexistence and peace. All too often, we are unwitting builders and clumsy architects of this city, capable of generous gestures but lacking an overall vision. This building project is slower, less visible and less spectacular, and awaits a better understanding and greater coordination so that it may become the conscious and clear responsibility of every community, from families to States, and the relations between Nations. It is this prospect of commitment, this construction site of hope, that we call the “civilization of love.”
blocks.zh: 如果我們審視全球的動態，便更能清楚地辨認出一種以兩極化和暴力為特徵的權力文化的蔓延。現代的巴貝耳不但體現在全球化的技術官僚範式中，也體現在互相對立的帝國主義之間的遙遠衝突中——一方是希望維護其霸權的勢力，另一方是企圖奪取該霸權的勢力，結果引致了眾多地區性衝突。不僅如此，在日益強大的技術的開發競賽或對這些技術的掌控競賽中——這是由一種使人失去人性的野心所驅動的——似乎毫無止境。然而，儘管存在這種向下的惡性循環，我們仍能瞥見人類中有一大部分正努力保持其人性，並致力建設那座共存與和平的聖城。很多時候，我們都是這座城的不自覺的建造者和笨拙的建築師，能做出慷慨的舉動，但缺乏整體的視野。這項建設工程較為緩慢，較不顯眼，也較不壯觀，它期待著更深入的理解與更廣泛的協調，好使它能成為每一個團體——從家庭到國家，乃至各國之間的關係——的自覺而明確的責任。正是這個承擔責任的前景，這個希望的工地，我們稱之為「愛的文明」。
blocks.html: 185. If we examine global dynamics, we can recognize more clearly the spread of a culture of power characterized by polarization and violence. The modern Babel can be seen not only in the globalized technocratic paradigm, but also in the remote clash between opposing imperialisms, between powers that wish to preserve their supremacy, and those that aspire to seize that supremacy, resulting in a multiplicity of local conflicts. Moreover, there seems to be no limit to the race — driven by a dehumanizing ambition — to develop evermore powerful technologies or to secure control over them. Yet, despite this downward spiral, we can also glimpse a great part of humanity that is striving to remain human and working to build the holy city of coexistence and peace. All too often, we are unwitting builders and clumsy architects of this city, capable of generous gestures but lacking an overall vision. This building project is slower, less visible and less spectacular, and awaits a better understanding and greater coordination so that it may become the conscious and clear responsibility of every community, from families to States, and the relations between Nations. It is this prospect of commitment, this construction site of hope, that we call the “civilization of love.”

blocks.en: The civilization of love in the digital age
blocks.zh: 數碼時代的愛的文明
blocks.html: The civilization of love in the digital age

blocks.en: When Saint Paul VI coined the phrase “the civilization of love,” [177] the world was in the midst of the Cold War, an arms race and severe economic instability. In that context, the Church proposed an alternative path to that of ideological opposition between systems, and envisioned a social order in which justice and charity are intertwined and love becomes the guiding principle of economic, political and cultural life. Today, we must resolutely recover this vision, for the civilization of love is no naïve utopia, but a demanding project, which consists in translating charity into structures of justice, giving institutional form to fraternity and regarding others — whether individuals or peoples — as allies necessary for building the common good. As the Encyclical Letter Fratelli Tutti reminded us, only this social love is capable of becoming a culture and a norm, and thereby of bringing about a stable international order, transforming mere armed coexistence into a community with a shared future. [178]
blocks.zh: 當聖保祿六世提出「愛的文明」[177] 一詞時，世界正處於冷戰、軍備競賽和嚴重的經濟不穩之中。在那個背景下，教會提出了一條有別於制度間意識形態對立的替代道路，並設想了一種社會秩序，在其中正義與愛德交織，而愛成為經濟、政治及文化生活的指導原則。今天，我們必須堅決重拾這個願景，因為愛的文明並非幼稚的烏托邦，而是一項要求嚴格的計劃，其內容在於將愛德轉化為正義的架構，賦予兄弟情誼制度性的形式，並將他人——無論是個人還是民族——視為建設公益所必需的盟友。正如《眾位弟兄》通諭提醒我們的，唯有這種社會性的愛才能成為一種文化和規範，從而帶來穩定的國際秩序，將單純的武裝共存轉變為一個擁有共同未來的團體。[178]
blocks.html: 186. When Saint Paul VI coined the phrase “the civilization of love,” [177] the world was in the midst of the Cold War, an arms race and severe economic instability. In that context, the Church proposed an alternative path to that of ideological opposition between systems, and envisioned a social order in which justice and charity are intertwined and love becomes the guiding principle of economic, political and cultural life. Today, we must resolutely recover this vision, for the civilization of love is no naïve utopia, but a demanding project, which consists in translating charity into structures of justice, giving institutional form to fraternity and regarding others — whether individuals or peoples — as allies necessary for building the common good. As the Encyclical Letter Fratelli Tutti reminded us, only this social love is capable of becoming a culture and a norm, and thereby of bringing about a stable international order, transforming mere armed coexistence into a community with a shared future. [178]

blocks.en: This insight proves even more fundamental in the current context of digital transformation. Digital networks, the globalized economy and the development of AI create increasingly tighter bonds, linking — in real time — decisions made in one place to the effects they produce elsewhere. In this sense, the words of the Second Vatican Council on the growing interdependence between peoples remain timely, for the common good is taking on an increasingly universal dimension, with rights and duties concerning the entire human family. [179] The project for a civilization of love, therefore, must undertake the task of transforming this imposed interdependence into a willed and chosen solidarity. This is the guiding principle for technological processes: it is not enough for artificial intelligence to make us more efficient or connected; it must also serve to build a universal human family, with shared rights and duties, where digital proximity becomes a real opportunity for encounter and mutual care.
blocks.zh: 這項洞見在當前數碼轉型的背景下顯得更為根本。數碼網絡、全球化經濟及人工智能的發展，締造了日益緊密的聯繫，將一個地方作出的決定與其在別處產生的影響實時連結起來。在此意義下，梵蒂岡第二屆大公會議關於各民族間日益增強的相互依存關係的論述，至今依然切合時宜，因為公益正日漸呈現出愈發普世性的幅度，其權利與義務關乎整個人類大家庭。[179] 因此，建設愛的文明的計劃，必須承擔起將這種被強加的相互依存，轉化為自願選擇的精誠團結的任務。這正是引導技術進程的原則：人工智能使我們更有效率或更緊密聯繫，這還不足夠；它還必須服務於建設一個普世性的人類大家庭，其中共享權利與義務，數碼世界的臨近成為真正相遇和彼此關懷的機遇。
blocks.html: 187. This insight proves even more fundamental in the current context of digital transformation. Digital networks, the globalized economy and the development of AI create increasingly tighter bonds, linking — in real time — decisions made in one place to the effects they produce elsewhere. In this sense, the words of the Second Vatican Council on the growing interdependence between peoples remain timely, for the common good is taking on an increasingly universal dimension, with rights and duties concerning the entire human family. [179] The project for a civilization of love, therefore, must undertake the task of transforming this imposed interdependence into a willed and chosen solidarity. This is the guiding principle for technological processes: it is not enough for artificial intelligence to make us more efficient or connected; it must also serve to build a universal human family, with shared rights and duties, where digital proximity becomes a real opportunity for encounter and mutual care.

blocks.en: The culture of power
blocks.zh: 權力的文化
blocks.html: The culture of power

blocks.en: In our time, a culture of power is taking hold, in which the availability of resources and the ability to dominate tend to dictate the agenda and criteria for decision-making. In this way, the common good of humanity is relegated to the background and the concrete tragedy of peoples at war is reduced to a secondary consideration in relation to strategic interests. This culture of power infiltrates society, changes relationships and behaviors, and grows by normalizing war, pursuing ever-greater military power, taking advantage of the crisis of multilateralism and fueling a false realism that insists that there is no alternative.
blocks.zh: 在我們的時代，一種權力文化正逐漸佔據主導地位；在這種文化中，資源的可及性與支配的能力往往主導著決策的議程與標準。如此一來，人類的公益被退居次要地位，而交戰民族具體的悲劇，相較於戰略利益，則被降格為附帶考量。這種權力文化滲透社會，改變了人際關係與行為模式，並通過將戰爭常態化、追求不斷增強的軍事力量、利用多邊主義的危機，以及助長一種堅稱別無選擇的虛假現實主義，而不斷壯大。
blocks.html: 188. In our time, a culture of power is taking hold, in which the availability of resources and the ability to dominate tend to dictate the agenda and criteria for decision-making. In this way, the common good of humanity is relegated to the background and the concrete tragedy of peoples at war is reduced to a secondary consideration in relation to strategic interests. This culture of power infiltrates society, changes relationships and behaviors, and grows by normalizing war, pursuing ever-greater military power, taking advantage of the crisis of multilateralism and fueling a false realism that insists that there is no alternative.

blocks.en: The normalization of war
blocks.zh: 戰爭的常態化
blocks.html: The normalization of war

blocks.en: In 1965, the words of Saint Paul VI resounded powerfully at the UN General Assembly: “Never again war, never again war!” [180] We must acknowledge that, despite the desires and declarations for peace, the past sixty years have been marked by conflicts of astonishing brutality, often affecting civilian populations on a massive scale, leading to the death of innocent victims, mass displacement, social destabilization and long-lasting wounds. Nevertheless, in public discourse, there was a widespread conviction that war should remain a last resort, subject to strict ethical and legal limits, and always oriented toward a political vision of peace. Following developments in the immediate post-First World War period, a turning point occurred after the Second World War: peace was made the focus of the international order, as attested in particular by the United Nations Charter, with the intention to “save succeeding generations from the scourge of war.” [181] Likewise, many national constitutions restricted the use of force to extreme and strictly limited circumstances. Even during the Cold War, despite the existence of serious conflicts, there remained the awareness that a new world war had to be avoided at all costs.
blocks.zh: 一九六五年，聖保祿六世的話語在聯合國大會上鏗鏘迴響：「永遠不再有戰爭，永遠不再有戰爭！」[180] 我們必須承認，儘管人們表達了和平的渴望，並作出了和平的宣言，過去六十年卻充滿了令人震驚的殘酷衝突，這些衝突往往大規模地波及平民，導致無辜者死亡、大規模流離失所、社會動盪，以及持久不癒的創傷。然而，在公共論述中，普遍存在著一種信念，即戰爭只應是最後的手段，須受嚴格的倫理和法律限制，並且始終指向一個和平的政治願景。緊隨第一次世界大戰後的發展，第二次世界大戰後出現了一個轉折點：和平被確立為國際秩序的焦點，這尤其體現在《聯合國憲章》之中，其目的在於「欲免後世再遭戰禍」。[181] 同樣地，許多國家的憲法將使用武力的權限限制在最極端且嚴格限定的情況之下。即使在冷戰期間，儘管存在嚴重的衝突，人們仍然意識到必須不惜一切代價避免一場新的世界大戰。
blocks.html: 189. In 1965, the words of Saint Paul VI resounded powerfully at the UN General Assembly: “Never again war, never again war!” [180] We must acknowledge that, despite the desires and declarations for peace, the past sixty years have been marked by conflicts of astonishing brutality, often affecting civilian populations on a massive scale, leading to the death of innocent victims, mass displacement, social destabilization and long-lasting wounds. Nevertheless, in public discourse, there was a widespread conviction that war should remain a last resort, subject to strict ethical and legal limits, and always oriented toward a political vision of peace. Following developments in the immediate post-First World War period, a turning point occurred after the Second World War: peace was made the focus of the international order, as attested in particular by the United Nations Charter, with the intention to “save succeeding generations from the scourge of war.” [181] Likewise, many national constitutions restricted the use of force to extreme and strictly limited circumstances. Even during the Cold War, despite the existence of serious conflicts, there remained the awareness that a new world war had to be avoided at all costs.

blocks.en: Today, however, we are witnessing a real paradigm shift in public discourse and in decisions regarding rearmament, with a troubling revival of war as an instrument of international politics, while the very ethical principles that had previously limited its use are being eroded. Regional conflicts that drag on over time, escalating tensions and reciprocal threats are becoming almost commonplace, and forms of conflict driven by the desire for territorial expansion that were thought to be overcome are re-emerging. Public opinion is gradually being shaped and conditioned by polarizing media narratives, which are often amplified by algorithms that prioritize conflict and confrontation.
blocks.zh: 然而，今天我們正目睹公共論述和關於重整軍備的決策出現真正的範式轉移，戰爭作為國際政治工具的可憂復興正在發生，而此前曾限制其使用的倫理原則本身卻正被侵蝕。曠日持久的地區衝突、不斷升級的緊張局勢和相互威脅幾乎變得司空見慣，而那些由領土擴張慾望驅動、本被認為已克服的衝突形式，正在重新浮現。公眾輿論正逐漸被兩極分化的媒體敘事所塑造和制約，這些敘事往往被優先考慮衝突與對抗的演算法放大。
blocks.html: 190. Today, however, we are witnessing a real paradigm shift in public discourse and in decisions regarding rearmament, with a troubling revival of war as an instrument of international politics, while the very ethical principles that had previously limited its use are being eroded. Regional conflicts that drag on over time, escalating tensions and reciprocal threats are becoming almost commonplace, and forms of conflict driven by the desire for territorial expansion that were thought to be overcome are re-emerging. Public opinion is gradually being shaped and conditioned by polarizing media narratives, which are often amplified by algorithms that prioritize conflict and confrontation.

blocks.en: We are also witnessing a disconcerting loss of historical memory, as first-hand accounts of the Holocaust and the two World Wars are disappearing. This leads to a selective or distorted rewriting of the past, in a context where fake news and the manipulation of narratives obscure the lessons that have been learned. Without a living memory of the horrors of war, political decisions risk being made on the basis of power alone, without any consideration for the long-term consequences.
blocks.zh: 我們也正目睹一種令人不安的歷史記憶喪失——隨著對大屠殺及兩次世界大戰的第一手見證逐漸消逝，在假新聞和敘事操縱模糊了已汲取的教訓的背景下，這導致了人們選擇性或扭曲地重寫過去。若對戰爭的恐怖失去活生生的記憶，政治決策便可能純粹基於權力而作出，絲毫不考慮其長遠後果。
blocks.html: 191. We are also witnessing a disconcerting loss of historical memory, as first-hand accounts of the Holocaust and the two World Wars are disappearing. This leads to a selective or distorted rewriting of the past, in a context where fake news and the manipulation of narratives obscure the lessons that have been learned. Without a living memory of the horrors of war, political decisions risk being made on the basis of power alone, without any consideration for the long-term consequences.

blocks.en: To all of this, the media and digital dimensions are adding new and decisive elements. Communication networks, fragmented information environments and algorithms that reward conflict can magnify polarization and resentment, increase propaganda and make shared discernment more difficult. Thus, war is not only fought, but also culturally conditioned through simplistic narratives, a friend-or-foe mentality, disinformation and fear. When historical memory fades and the ethical principles that protect civilians and the most vulnerable are weakened, it becomes easier to justify violence as necessary, inevitable or even “sanitized.” It is in this context that humanity is slipping into a violent culture of power, where peace no longer appears as a responsibility to be taken on, but as a fragile interval between conflicts. Today, more than ever, without prejudice to the right to self-defense in the strictest sense, it is important to reaffirm that the “just war” theory, which has all too often been used to justify any kind of war, is now outdated. [182] Humanity possesses far more effective and capable tools for promoting human life and resolving conflicts, such as dialogue, diplomacy and forgiveness. The use of force, violence and weapons reflects a relational poverty that always has disastrous consequences for civilian populations.
blocks.zh: 對於這一切，媒體和數碼領域正在添加新的且具決定性的因素。通訊網絡、碎片化的資訊環境，以及獎勵衝突的演算法，足以放大對立與怨恨，加劇宣傳，並使共同的辨別更形困難。如此一來，戰爭不僅實際地進行，更通過簡化的敘述、敵我思維、虛假資訊與恐懼而在文化上被制約。當歷史記憶消退，保護平民和最弱勢者的倫理原則被削弱，就更容易將暴力合理化為必要的、無可避免的，甚至「被淨化過」的行為。正是在這背景下，人類正滑向一種暴力的權力文化；在這文化中，和平不再是一項應當承擔的責任，而只是衝突之間的一段脆弱間歇。今天，比任何時候都更甚，在無損於最嚴格意義上的自衛權的前提下，必須重申那屢遭濫用以合理化任何形式戰爭的「正義戰爭」理論如今已不合時宜。[182] 人類擁有更為有效和得力的工具來促進人類生命和解決衝突，例如對話、外交和寬恕。使用武力、暴力和武器所反映的是一種關係上的貧乏，而這貧乏必對平民百姓帶來災難性的後果。
blocks.html: 192. To all of this, the media and digital dimensions are adding new and decisive elements. Communication networks, fragmented information environments and algorithms that reward conflict can magnify polarization and resentment, increase propaganda and make shared discernment more difficult. Thus, war is not only fought, but also culturally conditioned through simplistic narratives, a friend-or-foe mentality, disinformation and fear. When historical memory fades and the ethical principles that protect civilians and the most vulnerable are weakened, it becomes easier to justify violence as necessary, inevitable or even “sanitized.” It is in this context that humanity is slipping into a violent culture of power, where peace no longer appears as a responsibility to be taken on, but as a fragile interval between conflicts. Today, more than ever, without prejudice to the right to self-defense in the strictest sense, it is important to reaffirm that the “just war” theory, which has all too often been used to justify any kind of war, is now outdated. [182]  Humanity possesses far more effective and capable tools for promoting human life and resolving conflicts, such as dialogue, diplomacy and forgiveness. The use of force, violence and weapons reflects a relational poverty that always has disastrous consequences for civilian populations.

blocks.en: Force without limits
blocks.zh: 無限度的武力
blocks.html: Force without limits

blocks.en: The growth of the military-industrial complex has become a defining feature of the current political landscape and has become a key sector in the economy of various countries. The close link between economic interests, the military apparatus and political decisions produces an “armed nation,” in which war appears as a natural extension of politics, and the arms market becomes an autonomous driving force behind military decisions. Nor can we ignore the enormous economic interests behind war. The armaments industry, and countries that supply weapons, profit from a market that thrives precisely on conflicts. In this sense, there are also financial interests that contribute to fueling tensions in various regions of the world.
blocks.zh: 軍事工業複合體的增長已成為當前政治格局的一個決定性特徵，並已成為各國經濟中的關鍵部門。經濟利益、軍事機器與政治決策之間的緊密聯繫，產生了一個「武裝的國家」，在其中戰爭看似政治的必然延伸，而軍火市場則成為推動軍事決策的自主驅動力。我們也不能忽視戰爭背後龐大的經濟利益。軍火工業和供應武器的國家，正是從一個依賴衝突而蓬勃發展的市場中獲利。從這個意義上說，也存在著一些金融利益，它們助長了世界各地的緊張局勢。
blocks.html: 193. The growth of the military-industrial complex has become a defining feature of the current political landscape and has become a key sector in the economy of various countries. The close link between economic interests, the military apparatus and political decisions produces an “armed nation,” in which war appears as a natural extension of politics, and the arms market becomes an autonomous driving force behind military decisions. Nor can we ignore the enormous economic interests behind war. The armaments industry, and countries that supply weapons, profit from a market that thrives precisely on conflicts. In this sense, there are also financial interests that contribute to fueling tensions in various regions of the world.

blocks.en: Military arsenals are receiving renewed attention. In the past, recognition of the threat posed by weapons capable of destroying all of humanity had promoted paths toward détente and disarmament negotiations. Unfortunately, this approach has been left behind, and the evolution of nuclear arsenals — including the prospect of its “tactical” use — makes the use of such weapons seem less improbable. In this context, the Treaty on the Prohibition of Nuclear Weapons, which came into force in 2021 with the support of over seventy countries, is an important step. However, it risks remaining largely symbolic since the major nuclear powers have not agreed to it. This has led to the widespread yet erroneous belief that nuclear deterrence is an indispensable prerequisite for security. This has also contributed to a new arms race, which is hard to control and accompanied by the gradual dismantling of nuclear reduction agreements, as well as the development of “miniaturized” weapons, that make their use seem like a more viable option.
blocks.zh: 軍事武器儲備正重新受到關注。在過去，認識到那些足以毀滅全人類的武器所構成的威脅，曾促進了緩和局勢及裁軍談判的道路。不幸的是，這一方針已被摒棄，而核武庫的演變——包括其「戰術性」使用的前景——使得動用此類武器的可能性看來不再那麼渺茫。在此背景下，在七十多個國家的支持下於二零二一年生效的《禁止核武器條約》，是重要的一步。然而，由於主要的核武大國並未同意該條約，它恐怕在很大程度上仍只是象徵性的。這導致了一種普遍卻錯誤的信念，即核威懾是安全不可或缺的先決條件。這也助長了新的軍備競賽，而這場競賽難以控制，伴隨著核裁軍協議的逐步瓦解，以及「微型化」武器的研發，使得使用這些武器看來成了一個更可行的選項。
blocks.html: 194. Military arsenals are receiving renewed attention. In the past, recognition of the threat posed by weapons capable of destroying all of humanity had promoted paths toward détente and disarmament negotiations. Unfortunately, this approach has been left behind, and the evolution of nuclear arsenals — including the prospect of its “tactical” use — makes the use of such weapons seem less improbable. In this context, the Treaty on the Prohibition of Nuclear Weapons, which came into force in 2021 with the support of over seventy countries, is an important step. However, it risks remaining largely symbolic since the major nuclear powers have not agreed to it. This has led to the widespread yet erroneous belief that nuclear deterrence is an indispensable prerequisite for security. This has also contributed to a new arms race, which is hard to control and accompanied by the gradual dismantling of nuclear reduction agreements, as well as the development of “miniaturized” weapons, that make their use seem like a more viable option.

blocks.en: The same logic applies to conventional warfare. Military force, weak diplomatic initiatives and the complexity of the interests at stake contribute to conflicts that tend to become protracted, with extremely high human and environmental costs. It is much easier to start a war than to stop it, and yet, discussion on conflict prevention remains tragically marginal.
blocks.zh: 同樣的邏輯也適用於常規戰爭。軍事力量、薄弱的外交舉措，以及牽涉利益的複雜性，共同導致了衝突往往曠日持久，造成極其高昂的人命與環境代價。發動一場戰爭遠比結束它來得容易，然而，有關預防衝突的討論，卻依然可悲地處於邊緣地位。
blocks.html: 195. The same logic applies to conventional warfare. Military force, weak diplomatic initiatives and the complexity of the interests at stake contribute to conflicts that tend to become protracted, with extremely high human and environmental costs. It is much easier to start a war than to stop it, and yet, discussion on conflict prevention remains tragically marginal.

blocks.en: The situation is further destabilized by the presence of new armed operatives, such as jihadist groups, private militias and criminal networks that mark the end of the State’s monopoly on the use of force. Often these groups intertwine vague ideological motivations with concrete economic interests, transforming war into a “way of life” for entire generations of young people and children. Here, the objective is no longer a definitive victory, but the perpetuation of conflict as a source of power and income.
blocks.zh: 局勢因新型武裝行動者的出現而更趨不穩，例如聖戰組織、私人民兵和犯罪網絡，這些都標誌著國家對武力使用的壟斷權走向終結。這些團體往往將模糊的意識形態動機與具體的經濟利益交織在一起，將戰爭轉化為整整幾代青年和兒童的「生活方式」。在此，目標不再是決定性的勝利，而是讓衝突持續下去，作為權力和收入的來源。
blocks.html: 196. The situation is further destabilized by the presence of new armed operatives, such as jihadist groups, private militias and criminal networks that mark the end of the State’s monopoly on the use of force. Often these groups intertwine vague ideological motivations with concrete economic interests, transforming war into a “way of life” for entire generations of young people and children. Here, the objective is no longer a definitive victory, but the perpetuation of conflict as a source of power and income.

blocks.en: Weapons and artificial intelligence
blocks.zh: 武器與人工智能
blocks.html: Weapons and artificial intelligence

blocks.en: The above-mentioned scenario is linked to the unceasing development of weapons systems, particularly those involving AI. The Holy See has recently observed that the growing ease with which autonomous weapons systems can be deployed makes war more “feasible” and less subject to human control. This violates the principle that armed force should be used only as a last resort in cases of legitimate self-defense. [183] For this reason, the development and use of AI in warfare must be subject to the most rigorous ethical constraints, to guarantee respect for human dignity and the sanctity of life and to avoid a race to develop such arms. [184]
blocks.zh: 上述場景與武器系統，特別是有關人工智能的武器系統的不斷發展息息相關。聖座近來觀察到，自主武器系統部署日益便捷，這使得戰爭變得更「可行」，且更少受到人為控制。這違反了武力只應在合法自衛的極端情況下作為最後手段的原則。[183] 為此，在戰爭中開發和使用人工智能必須受到最嚴格的倫理約束，以保證對人性尊嚴和生命神聖性的尊重，並避免引發此類武器的競賽。[184]
