blocks.html: 197. The above-mentioned scenario is linked to the unceasing development of weapons systems, particularly those involving AI. The Holy See has recently observed that the growing ease with which autonomous weapons systems can be deployed makes war more “feasible” and less subject to human control. This violates the principle that armed force should be used only as a last resort in cases of legitimate self-defense. [183] For this reason, the development and use of AI in warfare must be subject to the most rigorous ethical constraints, to guarantee respect for human dignity and the sanctity of life and to avoid a race to develop such arms. [184]

blocks.en: Sometimes there is talk of “artificial moral agents,” as if machines were able to distinguish between right and wrong with greater consistency than a human being. Yet moral judgment cannot be reduced to calculation, for it involves conscience, personal responsibility and the recognition of the other as a person. Therefore, it is not permissible to entrust lethal or otherwise irreversible decisions to artificial systems. No algorithm can make war morally acceptable. AI does not remove the intrinsic inhumanity of conflict; indeed it can only bring about conflict more quickly and render it more impersonal, lowering the threshold for resorting to violence, transforming defense into threat prediction and thus reducing victims to data. In this way, it will accustom us to the idea that violence is inevitable and needs only to be optimized. This does not diminish the importance of instilling, as far as possible, values and sound judgment into the artificial systems we build, so that they can contribute to a moral ecosystem in which humans are better able to listen to their own consciences, as well as allowing AI models to establish appropriate boundaries.
blocks.zh: 有時人們會談論「人工道德主體」，彷彿機器能夠比人類更一貫地區分善惡。然而，道德判斷不能被化約為計算，因為它涉及良心、個人責任，以及將他人作為一個位格予以接納。因此，將致死的或其他不可逆轉的決定委託給人工系統是不被容許的。沒有任何演算法能使戰爭在道德上成為可接受的。人工智能並不能消除衝突的固有非人性；事實上，它只會使衝突更快爆發，並使其更加非人化，降低了訴諸暴力的門檻，將防衛轉化為威脅預測，從而將受害者化約為數據。如此一來，它將使我們習慣於這樣一種觀念：暴力是不可避免的，只需將之優化即可。這並不減損以下的努力的重要性：即盡可能地將價值和健全的判斷力灌注到我們所建構的人工系統中，使之能有助於建構一個道德生態系統，在其中，人類能夠更好地聆聽自己的良心，同時也讓人工智能模型得以設立適當的界線。
blocks.html: 198. Sometimes there is talk of “artificial moral agents,” as if machines were able to distinguish between right and wrong with greater consistency than a human being. Yet moral judgment cannot be reduced to calculation, for it involves conscience, personal responsibility and the recognition of the other as a person. Therefore, it is not permissible to entrust lethal or otherwise irreversible decisions to artificial systems. No algorithm can make war morally acceptable. AI does not remove the intrinsic inhumanity of conflict; indeed it can only bring about conflict more quickly and render it more impersonal, lowering the threshold for resorting to violence, transforming defense into threat prediction and thus reducing victims to data. In this way, it will accustom us to the idea that violence is inevitable and needs only to be optimized. This does not diminish the importance of instilling, as far as possible, values and sound judgment into the artificial systems we build, so that they can contribute to a moral ecosystem in which humans are better able to listen to their own consciences, as well as allowing AI models to establish appropriate boundaries.

blocks.en: It is not enough to invoke a generic type of ethics. Concrete criteria for discernment must be established. The first such criterion concerns personal responsibility. When a decision to strike becomes automated or opaque, the risk of abdicating responsibility increases. For this reason, the chain of responsibility must be identifiable and verifiable; those who design, train, authorize and employ technology must be held accountable for their decisions. The second criterion pertains to the moral timeframe for making judgments. While AI tends to expedite the decision-making processes, speed and efficiency should never be the supreme motivating force for the irreversible decisions made in the context of war. The third criterion is the identification and protection of civilians. Any technology that facilitates attacks without seeing the face of human beings lowers the moral threshold of conflict. Target selection and the use of force must not confuse combatants and non-combatants, nor ignore the impact on defenseless populations.
blocks.zh: 僅援引某種泛泛的倫理原則是不夠的。必須建立具體的辨別準則。第一項準則關乎個人責任。當發動攻擊的決定變得自動化或隱晦不明時，推卸責任的風險便會增加。為此，責任鏈必須是可識別及可驗證的；設計、訓練、授權和使用技術的人必須為其決定負責。第二項準則關乎作出判斷的道德時間框架。雖然人工智能傾向加速決策過程，但速度和效率絕不應成為在戰爭背景下作出不可逆轉決定的至上動力。第三項準則是平民的識別和保護。任何使攻擊得以在不見人類面容的情況下進行的技術，都會降低衝突的道德門檻。目標選擇和武力使用不得混淆戰鬥人員與非戰鬥人員，亦不得忽視對手無寸鐵民眾的影響。
blocks.html: 199. It is not enough to invoke a generic type of ethics. Concrete criteria for discernment must be established. The first such criterion concerns personal responsibility. When a decision to strike becomes automated or opaque, the risk of abdicating responsibility increases. For this reason, the chain of responsibility must be identifiable and verifiable; those who design, train, authorize and employ technology must be held accountable for their decisions. The second criterion pertains to the moral timeframe for making judgments. While AI tends to expedite the decision-making processes, speed and efficiency should never be the supreme motivating force for the irreversible decisions made in the context of war. The third criterion is the identification and protection of civilians. Any technology that facilitates attacks without seeing the face of human beings lowers the moral threshold of conflict. Target selection and the use of force must not confuse combatants and non-combatants, nor ignore the impact on defenseless populations.

blocks.en: These criteria give rise to certain non-negotiable requirements. First, all systems used in a war setting must guarantee the possibility of retracing and reconstructing decision-making processes, so that accountability and blame are not collapsed into “the machine.” Second, the decision to use lethal force cannot be delegated to opaque or automated processes, but must remain under effective, self-aware and responsible human control. Finally, it is imperative to establish a shared framework — also at the international level — in order to curb the technological arms race and ensure robust protection for civilians and the infrastructures necessary for their survival.
blocks.zh: 這些標準產生了若干不容妥協的要求。首先，在戰爭環境中使用的所有系統，必須保證能夠追溯和重建決策過程的可能性，以免責任與罪責被歸結於「機器」身上。其次，使用致命武力的決定不可委託給不透明或自動化的程序，而必須始終處於有效、自覺且負責任的人類控制之下。最後，當務之急是建立一個共同的框架——亦在國際層面上——以遏止科技軍備競賽，並確保平民及其生存所必需的基礎設施得到穩固的保護。
blocks.html: 200. These criteria give rise to certain non-negotiable requirements. First, all systems used in a war setting must guarantee the possibility of retracing and reconstructing decision-making processes, so that accountability and blame are not collapsed into “the machine.” Second, the decision to use lethal force cannot be delegated to opaque or automated processes, but must remain under effective, self-aware and responsible human control. Finally, it is imperative to establish a shared framework — also at the international level — in order to curb the technological arms race and ensure robust protection for civilians and the infrastructures necessary for their survival.

blocks.en: The crisis of multilateralism
blocks.zh: 多邊主義的危機
blocks.html: The crisis of multilateralism

blocks.en: The culture of power also stems from the crisis of the multilateral system. The institutions established to safeguard the concept of a common future for all peoples and a global common good appear to have been weakened. This is due not only to structural limitations, but also to a frequent lack of shared will to support and reform them, or to recognize their moral authority. Instead of making progress, we are regressing from the significant turning point of the twentieth century. After 1989, the collapse of communist regimes in Europe was followed by a predominantly economic globalization, which lacked an adequate political framework capable of sustaining dialogue and peace. An almost blind faith was placed in the ability of the markets to generate prosperity, democracy and stability. In reality, rather than automatically generating unity and peace, globalization has provoked fundamentalist, identity-based and nationalistic reactions. The result is a far cry from genuine multilateralism; instead, what has appeared is a disorderly and conflict-ridden multipolarism with a prevailing sense of mistrust.
blocks.zh: 強權文化亦源於多邊體系的危機。為維護各民族共同未來及全球公益理念而建立的各種制度，似乎已被削弱。這不僅是由於結構上的局限，更在於經常缺乏共同的意願去支持、改革這些制度，或承認其道德權威。我們非但沒有取得進步，反而從二十世紀的重大轉折點上倒退。1989年後，歐洲共產政權的崩潰隨之以主要為經濟主導的全球化，而這場全球化缺乏一個能夠維持對話與和平的適當政治框架。人們幾乎盲目地相信市場有能力產生繁榮、民主與穩定。事實上，全球化非但沒有自發地產生團結與和平，反而引發了原教旨主義、身分認同及民族主義的反彈。其結果與真正的多邊主義相去甚遠；取而代之的，是一個無序、衝突頻仍的多極化格局，並瀰漫著普遍的不信任感。
blocks.html: 201. The culture of power also stems from the crisis of the multilateral system. The institutions established to safeguard the concept of a common future for all peoples and a global common good appear to have been weakened. This is due not only to structural limitations, but also to a frequent lack of shared will to support and reform them, or to recognize their moral authority. Instead of making progress, we are regressing from the significant turning point of the twentieth century. After 1989, the collapse of communist regimes in Europe was followed by a predominantly economic globalization, which lacked an adequate political framework capable of sustaining dialogue and peace. An almost blind faith was placed in the ability of the markets to generate prosperity, democracy and stability. In reality, rather than automatically generating unity and peace, globalization has provoked fundamentalist, identity-based and nationalistic reactions. The result is a far cry from genuine multilateralism; instead, what has appeared is a disorderly and conflict-ridden multipolarism with a prevailing sense of mistrust.

blocks.en: What has also re-emerged is the temptation to forge a collective identity in opposition to an enemy, fueled by narratives in which each party portrays itself as a victim entitled to retribution. The reduction of complex issues into simplistic categories — “me first,” “friend or foe,” “us or them” — facilitates decisions that are often irresponsible and undermine mutual trust among nations. The force of international law is thus replaced by the claim that “might makes right.” Consequently, tribunals that are competent for settling disputes between States or dealing with war crimes are often weakened or bypassed, with devastating ramifications for political culture and social cohesion. [185]
blocks.zh: 同樣再次浮現的，是那種透過樹立敵人來塑造集體身分的誘惑；這種誘惑由一些敘事所煽動，其間每一方都將自己描繪成有權復仇的受害者。將複雜問題化約為簡單的歸類——「我為先」、「是友是敵」、「我們或他們」——便利了那些往往不負責任的抉擇，並侵蝕國家之間的互信。國際法的力量遂被「強權即公理」的主張所取代。結果，那些有權解決國家間爭端或處理戰爭罪行的法庭往往被削弱或繞過，給政治文化與社會凝聚力帶來災難性的後果。[185]
blocks.html: 202. What has also re-emerged is the temptation to forge a collective identity in opposition to an enemy, fueled by narratives in which each party portrays itself as a victim entitled to retribution. The reduction of complex issues into simplistic categories — “me first,” “friend or foe,” “us or them” — facilitates decisions that are often irresponsible and undermine mutual trust among nations. The force of international law is thus replaced by the claim that “might makes right.” Consequently, tribunals that are competent for settling disputes between States or dealing with war crimes are often weakened or bypassed, with devastating ramifications for political culture and social cohesion. [185]

blocks.en: In this context, peacebuilding has been relegated to a secondary role. Cooperation for development, disarmament, conflict prevention and the establishment of mutual trust are neglected in the name of power politics. The achievements of humanitarian law are also being compromised. Indeed, the principle of proportionality in responding to aggression, the protection of access to water, food and essential goods, and respect for the lives of civilians, especially children, come to be regarded as naïve relics of the past.
blocks.zh: 在這背景下，建設和平的工作已被貶至次要地位。為發展、裁軍、衝突預防及建立互信而進行的合作，都在強權政治的名義下被忽視。國際人道法的成果同樣遭受損害。事實上，回應侵略時的相稱性原則、對獲取水源、食物及基本物資的保障，以及對平民——特別是兒童——生命的尊重，都被視為天真的歷史遺跡。
blocks.html: 203. In this context, peacebuilding has been relegated to a secondary role.  Cooperation for development, disarmament, conflict prevention and the establishment of mutual trust are neglected in the name of power politics. The achievements of humanitarian law are also being compromised. Indeed, the principle of proportionality in responding to aggression, the protection of access to water, food and essential goods, and respect for the lives of civilians, especially children, come to be regarded as naïve relics of the past.

blocks.en: A supposed political realism
blocks.zh: 所謂的政治現實主義
blocks.html: A supposed political realism

blocks.en: We live at a time of significant spiritual and cultural blindness. A false pragmatism urges us to sever the roots of our history, as if it were possible to inaugurate a kind of “new creation” detached from the past. Even those who cite important moral principles can fall into this historical nihilism, mistakenly believing that the atrocities of the twentieth century can never happen again. Yet, in reality, the same dynamics are re-emerging under new guises. The mentality of armed equilibrium and deterrence appears to be reasserting itself. Today, however, in contrast to the two-sided dynamic of the Cold War, the proliferation of operatives and battlefields makes this mentality increasingly fragile. Escalating conflicts lead to asymmetric and “hybrid” wars, fought not only on the battleground but also on the economic, financial and cyber fronts, where disinformation and campaigns that feed people’s fears are used to manipulate public opinion. In many countries, including those in the Global South, increased military spending is presented as the only response to an uncertain future or perceived threats. Meanwhile, the real cost falls on the poorest, who see resources for healthcare, education and social services being reduced.
blocks.zh: 我們生活在一個深陷靈性與文化盲目的時代。一種虛假的實用主義催促我們斬斷歷史的根源，彷彿有可能開創一種與過去脫節的「新創造」。即使是那些援引重要道德原則的人，也可能陷入這種歷史虛無主義，錯誤地相信二十世紀的暴行絕對不會重演。然而事實上，同樣的動力正以新的面貌重新出現。武裝均勢與威懾的思維似乎正在重新抬頭。可是今天，與冷戰時期雙邊對峙的格局不同，作戰人員與戰場的擴散使這種思維愈發脆弱。不斷升級的衝突導致不對稱的「混合」戰爭，不僅在戰場上進行，也在經濟、金融和網絡戰線上展開，利用虛假資訊和煽動人民恐懼的宣傳運動來操縱公眾輿論。在許多國家，包括全球南方的國家，增加軍費開支被呈現為應對不確定未來或感知威脅的唯一回應。與此同時，真正的代價卻落在最貧窮的人身上，他們眼看著醫療保健、教育和社會服務的資源日漸縮減。
blocks.html: 204. We live at a time of significant spiritual and cultural blindness. A false pragmatism urges us to sever the roots of our history, as if it were possible to inaugurate a kind of “new creation” detached from the past. Even those who cite important moral principles can fall into this historical nihilism, mistakenly believing that the atrocities of the twentieth century can never happen again. Yet, in reality, the same dynamics are re-emerging under new guises. The mentality of armed equilibrium and deterrence appears to be reasserting itself. Today, however, in contrast to the two-sided dynamic of the Cold War, the proliferation of operatives and battlefields makes this mentality increasingly fragile. Escalating conflicts lead to asymmetric and “hybrid” wars, fought not only on the battleground but also on the economic, financial and cyber fronts, where disinformation and campaigns that feed people’s fears are used to manipulate public opinion. In many countries, including those in the Global South, increased military spending is presented as the only response to an uncertain future or perceived threats. Meanwhile, the real cost falls on the poorest, who see resources for healthcare, education and social services being reduced.

blocks.en: At the core of these issues is a false realism, based not only on the prevailing mentality of force, but on the cultural and anthropological belief that war is an inevitable part of human nature. It is said that things have always been this way, except for occasional pauses, and that it will always be so! As a result, the concern is no longer the search for peace — which has been lost as a point of reference on the international stage — but rather how and when to take military action. This same argument maintains that it would be irresponsible not to prepare for conflict. I would argue, however, that what is truly irresponsible is Realpolitik, the form of political “realism” that sows in consciences and in society an attitude of resignation to the inevitability of war, and dismisses peace and dialogue as utopian or irrational positions that ignore the risks at stake. In fact, peace is neither a naïve hope nor merely the absence of war; instead, it is always possible as the fruit of justice and charity.
blocks.zh: 這些問題的核心，是一種虛假的現實主義。這種現實主義不僅建基於盛行的武力心態，更建基於一種文化和人類學的信念，即戰爭是人類本性中無可倖免的一部分。人們說，自古以來便是如此，除了偶爾的間歇，將來也永遠如此！結果，人們關心的不再是如何尋求和平——和平在國際舞台上已不再是一個參照點——而是何時以及如何採取軍事行動。這同一套論調還堅持說，不為衝突做好準備才是不負責任的。然而，我要指出，真正不負責任的，是「現實政治」（Realpolitik）——這種政治「現實主義」在人的良心和社會中播下一種對戰爭無可避免的聽天由命態度，並將和平與對話視為漠視風險的不切實際或非理性立場。事實上，和平既不是天真的盼望，也不僅僅是沒有戰爭；相反，和平作為正義與愛德的果實，永遠是可能的。
blocks.html: 205. At the core of these issues is a false realism, based not only on the prevailing mentality of force, but on the cultural and anthropological belief that war is an inevitable part of human nature. It is said that things have always been this way, except for occasional pauses, and that it will always be so! As a result, the concern is no longer the search for peace — which has been lost as a point of reference on the international stage — but rather how and when to take military action. This same argument maintains that it would be irresponsible not to prepare for conflict. I would argue, however, that what is truly irresponsible is Realpolitik, the form of political “realism” that sows in consciences and in society an attitude of resignation to the inevitability of war, and dismisses peace and dialogue as utopian or irrational positions that ignore the risks at stake. In fact, peace is neither a naïve hope nor merely the absence of war; instead, it is always possible as the fruit of justice and charity.

blocks.en: In such a climate, nihilism and pragmatism become intertwined and end up normalizing grave errors. Religious extremism and identity-based fanaticism ally themselves with irrational economic policies, while politics often turns to misinformation and ridiculing opponents, and systematically cultivating fears and resentments. Thus, diversity is increasingly perceived as a threat, which fuels a desire for possession, a will to dominate, hegemonic ambitions, abuses of power and a fear of those who are different, thereby creating an environment in which new conflicts can develop almost imperceptibly. [186]
blocks.zh: 在這樣的氛圍中，虛無主義和實用主義交織在一起，最終使嚴重的錯誤淪為常態。宗教極端主義和以身分為基礎的狂熱，與非理性的經濟政策沆瀣一氣，而政治往往訴諸於錯誤資訊和嘲弄對手，並系統性地培養恐懼與怨恨。因此，多元性愈來愈被視為一種威脅，從而助長了佔有的慾望、支配的意志、霸權野心、濫用權力以及對異己者的恐懼，由此創造出一種環境，使新的衝突幾乎在不知不覺中醞釀成形。[186]
blocks.html: 206. In such a climate, nihilism and pragmatism become intertwined and end up normalizing grave errors. Religious extremism and identity-based fanaticism ally themselves with irrational economic policies, while politics often turns to misinformation and ridiculing opponents, and systematically cultivating fears and resentments. Thus, diversity is increasingly perceived as a threat, which fuels a desire for possession, a will to dominate, hegemonic ambitions, abuses of power and a fear of those who are different, thereby creating an environment in which new conflicts can develop almost imperceptibly. [186]

blocks.en: This, then, is the fertile ground for new wars that are perhaps even more dangerous than those of the past, since they tend to disregard all ethical limits. What was once considered unacceptable can now be carried out almost without hesitation, while the international response is increasingly influenced more by the interests of individual Governments than by the objective gravity of situations. Decisions now seem to be driven almost exclusively by economic calculations, justified through media distortions, manufactured enthusiasm and “dreams” that inevitably shatter, generating frustration and further violence. When people come to believe that nothing is genuinely true and that principles are hollow words, then the fuse in their hearts is lit for new eruptions of intolerance and aggression.
blocks.zh: 這正是新戰爭滋生蔓延的肥沃土壤，這些戰爭或許比過去的戰爭更加危險，因為它們傾向於無視一切倫理底線。昔日被視為不可接受的事，如今幾乎可以毫不猶豫地進行，而國際社會的回應，與其說是基於客觀情況的嚴重性，倒不如說愈來愈受到個別政府利益的左右。如今的決策似乎幾乎完全由經濟計算所驅動，並透過傳媒的扭曲、人為製造的狂熱，以及那些無可避免會破滅並引發挫敗感與更多暴力的「夢想」加以合理化。一旦人們開始相信沒有什麼是真正真實的，並認為原則只是空洞的言辭，那麼他們心中便點燃了引信，隨時會引發新一輪的不寬容與侵犯。
blocks.html: 207. This, then, is the fertile ground for new wars that are perhaps even more dangerous than those of the past, since they tend to disregard all ethical limits. What was once considered unacceptable can now be carried out almost without hesitation, while the international response is increasingly influenced more by the interests of individual Governments than by the objective gravity of situations.  Decisions now seem to be driven almost exclusively by economic calculations, justified through media distortions, manufactured enthusiasm and “dreams” that inevitably shatter, generating frustration and further violence. When people come to believe that nothing is genuinely true and that principles are hollow words, then the fuse in their hearts is lit for new eruptions of intolerance and aggression.

blocks.en: In these situations, the issue of concrete safeguards to prevent future violence remains an open question. When a culture normalizes and justifies conflict, a dangerous pathway opens up, in that what seems unthinkable today may become acceptable tomorrow in the name of utility or security. In countries marked by serious social tensions, we cannot rule out the possibility that some leaders may consider armed conflict as an effective way of diverting attention from domestic problems and a cynical tool for managing difficulties.
blocks.zh: 在這些境況中，為預防未來暴力的具體保障措施，仍是一個懸而未決的問題。當一種文化將衝突正常化並加以合理化時，一條危險的途徑便隨之開啟：今日看來不可思議之事，明日可能以效益或安全之名變得可被接受。在那些社會矛盾嚴重的國家，我們不能排除某些領袖可能視武裝衝突為轉移國內問題視線的有效手段，並將其當作一種應對困難的犬儒式工具。
blocks.html: 208. In these situations, the issue of concrete safeguards to prevent future violence remains an open question. When a culture normalizes and justifies conflict, a dangerous pathway opens up, in that what seems unthinkable today may become acceptable tomorrow in the name of utility or security. In countries marked by serious social tensions, we cannot rule out the possibility that some leaders may consider armed conflict as an effective way of diverting attention from domestic problems and a cynical tool for managing difficulties.

blocks.en: A particular responsibility rests on the shoulders of those who work in the field of research. All the key players in this field — scientists, business owners, investors, academic authorities, politicians and others — must work with a transparent and responsible mindset, while maintaining an acute awareness of the broader context of the technological advancements they help to cultivate, including those related to AI. When people limit themselves to looking only at their own sector, they may deceive themselves into believing they are performing actions that are morally neutral and avoid questions about the ultimate ends that guide certain experiments. In this way, they risk cooperating — perhaps unknowingly — with questionable projects that fuel new forms of violence, manipulation and dominance.
blocks.zh: 在科研領域工作的人，肩上負有特別的責任。這個領域的所有關鍵參與者——科學家、企業持有人、投資者、學術權威、政治人物及其他人士——都必須以透明和負責任的心態工作，同時對他們協助培育的科技進步（包括與人工智能相關的進步）所處的更廣闊背景保持敏銳的覺察。當人們將目光侷限於自身領域時，他們可能會自欺欺人，以為自己正在進行道德上中立的行動，並迴避某些指導實驗的最終目的之問題。這樣一來，他們便有危險——或許是在不知不覺中——與一些助長新形式暴力、操縱和支配的可疑項目合作。
blocks.html: 209. A particular responsibility rests on the shoulders of those who work in the field of research. All the key players in this field — scientists, business owners, investors, academic authorities, politicians and others — must work with a transparent and responsible mindset, while maintaining an acute awareness of the broader context of the technological advancements they help to cultivate, including those related to AI. When people limit themselves to looking only at their own sector, they may deceive themselves into believing they are performing actions that are morally neutral and avoid questions about the ultimate ends that guide certain experiments. In this way, they risk cooperating — perhaps unknowingly — with questionable projects that fuel new forms of violence, manipulation and dominance.

blocks.en: Building the civilization of love
blocks.zh: 建設愛的文明
blocks.html: Building the civilization of love

blocks.en: The construction of a world in a state of perpetual conflict is an evil and must be named for what it is. This way of portraying our current situation may seem bleak or pessimistic, yet I consider it necessary to do so. The Christian perspective, however, is not limited to denouncing evil. We view history in the light of the crucified and risen Lord, to whom the Father has given “all authority in heaven and on earth” (Mt 28:18). We do not consider the present as a predetermined fate, but an opportunity for personal and collective conversion. Moreover, we believe in the power of the Kingdom, which grows from the tiny size of a mustard seed, which, once sown, sprouts and grows (cf. Mk 4:26-32). While the tumult of confusion is all around us, goodness grows silently from the earth. In the words of the prophet Isaiah: “Behold, I am doing a new thing; now it springs forth, do you not perceive it?” (Is 43:19).
blocks.zh: 建設一個處於無休止衝突中的世界，是邪惡之事，必須如實指陳。如此描繪我們當前處境的這種方式，或許顯得黯淡或悲觀，但我認為有這樣做的必要。然而，基督徒的視野並不只限於譴責邪惡。我們在十字架上死而復活的主的光照下看待歷史，父已將「天上地下的一切權柄」都交給了祂（參瑪 28:18）。我們不視當前為預先注定的命運，而是個人和集體皈依的契機。此外，我們相信天國的力量，這力量從微小的芥子般大小開始成長，一種下就發芽生長（參谷 4:26-32）。當混亂的喧囂在我們四周迴盪之際，美善正從大地默默生長。正如依撒意亞先知所言：「看哪！我要行一件新事，如今即要發生，你們不知道嗎？」（依 43:19）
blocks.html: 210. The construction of a world in a state of perpetual conflict is an evil and must be named for what it is. This way of portraying our current situation may seem bleak or pessimistic, yet I consider it necessary to do so. The Christian perspective, however, is not limited to denouncing evil. We view history in the light of the crucified and risen Lord, to whom the Father has given “all authority in heaven and on earth” (Mt 28:18). We do not consider the present as a predetermined fate, but an opportunity for personal and collective conversion. Moreover, we believe in the power of the Kingdom, which grows from the tiny size of a mustard seed, which, once sown, sprouts and grows (cf. Mk 4:26-32). While the tumult of confusion is all around us, goodness grows silently from the earth. In the words of the prophet Isaiah: “Behold, I am doing a new thing; now it springs forth, do you not perceive it?” (Is 43:19).

blocks.en: A closer analysis of history confirms this. Even in the darkest nights, the Lord raises up men and women who refuse to give up, who persevere in doing good, who protect the vulnerable and open pathways to reconciliation. The memory of the saints, righteous people and the oft-forgotten peacemakers, show us that grace does not magically eliminate conflict, but instead it inspires active resistance to evil and an astonishing creativity in doing good. Christians see the darkness and acknowledge it for what it is, yet they do not merely gaze upon it passively, for they know the light and understand that the darkness has not overcome it and cannot defeat it (cf. Jn 1:5). For this reason, even when suffering seems to have the last word, Christians serve the good and are sustained by a theological hope that gives reality both meaning and direction.
blocks.zh: 對歷史的更仔細分析證實了這一點。即使在最黑暗的夜晚，上主也會興起那些拒絕放棄的男男女女，他們堅持行善，保護弱小，並開闢通往修和的道路。對聖人、義人以及那些常被遺忘的和平締造者的記憶，向我們表明：恩寵並非以魔法消除衝突，而是激發對惡的主動抵抗，以及在行善上令人驚嘆的創造力。基督徒看見黑暗，並如實承認它的存在，但他們並不只是被動地凝視黑暗，因為他們認識那光，並明白黑暗絕不能勝過光，也無法戰勝光（參 Jn 1:5）。為此，即使苦難似乎說了最後一句話，基督徒依然服侍美善，並由那賦予現實意義與方向的超性望德所支撐。
blocks.html: 211. A closer analysis of history confirms this. Even in the darkest nights, the Lord raises up men and women who refuse to give up, who persevere in doing good, who protect the vulnerable and open pathways to reconciliation. The memory of the saints, righteous people and the oft-forgotten peacemakers, show us that grace does not magically eliminate conflict, but instead it inspires active resistance to evil and an astonishing creativity in doing good. Christians see the darkness and acknowledge it for what it is, yet they do not merely gaze upon it passively, for they know the light and understand that the darkness has not overcome it and cannot defeat it (cf. Jn 1:5). For this reason, even when suffering seems to have the last word, Christians serve the good and are sustained by a theological hope that gives reality both meaning and direction.

blocks.en: We can all do our part
blocks.zh: 我們人人皆可盡其一分
blocks.html: We can all do our part

blocks.en: At this point, however, a subtle temptation may emerge, namely the thought that the problems are too big and we are too small, and that our choices, therefore, cannot make a difference. This is a polite form of resignation, often disguised as realism. Certainly, not everyone has the same power to make a difference. There are those who govern, make investment decisions, lead institutions, conduct research, educate, produce or provide information, and then there are those who only seem to live their daily lives. Yet, no one is without responsibility. We all have our own areas for action, and it is precisely there — and nowhere else — that we must choose whether to fuel the mentality of force (even if only through indifference, cynicism, lies or hatred), or to preserve the mindset of peace (with truth, moderation, closeness and care).
blocks.zh: 然而，在此刻，一個微妙的誘惑可能會浮現，即認為問題太大，而我們太渺小，因此我們的選擇無法帶來改變。這是一種客氣的放棄，往往偽裝成現實主義。誠然，並非每個人都有同樣的力量去產生改變。有些人執政掌權、作出投資決定、領導機構、進行研究、從事教育、生產或提供資訊，而另一些人似乎只是在度他們的日常生活。然而，沒有人可以免於責任。我們每個人都有自己的行動領域，而恰恰是在那裡——而非別處——我們必須作出抉擇：是助長那以力壓人的心態（即便只是通過冷漠、犬儒主義、謊言或仇恨），還是保存那締造和平的心志（以真理、節制、親近和關懷）。
blocks.html: 212. At this point, however, a subtle temptation may emerge, namely the thought that the problems are too big and we are too small, and that our choices, therefore, cannot make a difference. This is a polite form of resignation, often disguised as realism. Certainly, not everyone has the same power to make a difference. There are those who govern, make investment decisions, lead institutions, conduct research, educate, produce or provide information, and then there are those who only seem to live their daily lives. Yet, no one is without responsibility. We all have our own areas for action, and it is precisely there — and nowhere else — that we must choose whether to fuel the mentality of force (even if only through indifference, cynicism, lies or hatred), or to preserve the mindset of peace (with truth, moderation, closeness and care).

blocks.en: The twentieth-century Catholic author J.R.R. Tolkien, in the words of a protagonist in one of his novels, described our responsibility in this way: “It is not our part to master all the tides of the world, but to do what is in us for the succour of those years wherein we are set, uprooting the evil in the fields that we know, so that those who live after may have clean earth to till.” [187] The civilization of love will not arise from a single or spectacular gesture, but from the sum total of small and steadfast acts of fidelity that serve as a bulwark against dehumanization. For this reason, it is worthwhile pausing to reflect on some aspects of how we, each in our own way, can cooperate in building the civilization of love. Without presuming to exhaust this theme, I would like to propose five paths toward daily and public responsibility: the need to disarm words, building peace through justice, adopting the perspective of victims, cultivating a healthy realism and reviving dialogue and multilateralism.
blocks.zh: 二十世紀的公教作家托爾金（J.R.R. Tolkien），曾藉其小說中一位主角之口，這樣描述我們的責任：「我們的本分並非駕馭世上一切的浪潮，而是要在我們所處的年月裡，盡我們所能去救助那些歲月，剷除我們所認識的田地中的邪惡，好讓後來的人能有潔淨的土地去耕種。」[187] 愛的文明不會由單一或驚天動地的舉動而產生，而是來自那些細小而堅定不移的忠信行動的總和，這些行動構成對抗非人化的一道壁壘。為此緣故，值得稍作停留，反思我們各人能以何種方式，協力建設愛的文明的某些面向。我不擬窮盡此一主題，但願提出五條通往日常及公共責任的道路：解除言詞武裝的必要、透過正義建設和平、採取受害者的視野、培養健全的現實主義，以及重振對話與多邊主義。
blocks.html: 213. The twentieth-century Catholic author J.R.R. Tolkien, in the words of a protagonist in one of his novels, described our responsibility in this way: “It is not our part to master all the tides of the world, but to do what is in us for the succour of those years wherein we are set, uprooting the evil in the fields that we know, so that those who live after may have clean earth to till.” [187] The civilization of love will not arise from a single or spectacular gesture, but from the sum total of small and steadfast acts of fidelity that serve as a bulwark against dehumanization. For this reason, it is worthwhile pausing to reflect on some aspects of how we, each in our own way, can cooperate in building the civilization of love. Without presuming to exhaust this theme, I would like to propose five paths toward daily and public responsibility: the need to disarm words, building peace through justice, adopting the perspective of victims, cultivating a healthy realism and reviving dialogue and multilateralism.

blocks.en: The need to disarm words
blocks.zh: 言語繳械之必要
blocks.html: The need to disarm words

blocks.en: The first contribution we can make toward a more humane civilization is to be mindful of our words. “Let us disarm words and we will help to disarm the world.” [188] Words have enormous power, something we experience in our daily interactions; for example, spoken words can change our mood for better or for worse. “Peace begins with each one of us: in the way we look at others, listen to others and speak about others. In this sense, the way we communicate is of fundamental importance: we must say ‘no’ to the war of words and images, we must reject the paradigm of war.” [189] We must all, therefore, examine our conscience regarding the words we use, the prejudices we have and the explicit or implicit aggression that lies within them. We have a real opportunity to contribute to the common good each time we speak the truth, offer wise advice, support those in need of comfort, denounce injustice and give a voice to the voiceless.
blocks.zh: 我們能為更富人性的文明所作的首要貢獻，就是謹慎我們的言語。「讓我們解除言語的武裝，我們就將有助於解除世界的武裝。」[188] 言語具有巨大的力量，這是我們在日常互動中親身經歷的事實；舉例而言，說出口的話語能夠改變我們的情緒，使之更好或更壞。「和平始於我們每一個人：始於我們看待他人的方式、聆聽他人的方式，以及談論他人的方式。從這層意義上說，我們溝通的方式具有根本的重要性：我們必須對言語和圖像的戰爭說『不』，我們必須拒斥戰爭的範式。」[189] 因此，我們所有人都必須就我們所使用的言語、我們心懷的偏見，以及潛藏其中的或明或暗的攻擊性，進行良心省察。每當我們講述真理、給予明智的勸告、支持需要安慰的人、譴責不義，並為無聲者發聲時，我們都擁有一個為公益作出貢獻的真實機會。
blocks.html: 214. The first contribution we can make toward a more humane civilization is to be mindful of our words. “Let us disarm words and we will help to disarm the world.” [188] Words have enormous power, something we experience in our daily interactions; for example, spoken words can change our mood for better or for worse. “Peace begins with each one of us: in the way we look at others, listen to others and speak about others. In this sense, the way we communicate is of fundamental importance: we must say ‘no’ to the war of words and images, we must reject the paradigm of war.” [189] We must all, therefore, examine our conscience regarding the words we use, the prejudices we have and the explicit or implicit aggression that lies within them. We have a real opportunity to contribute to the common good each time we speak the truth, offer wise advice, support those in need of comfort, denounce injustice and give a voice to the voiceless.

blocks.en: Building peace through justice
blocks.zh: 藉正義建設和平
blocks.html: Building peace through justice

blocks.en: All of us, at every level, can contribute to building the foundation of peace, which is justice. We do not merely seek any kind of peace — such as an absence of conflict at any cost — but instead, the true peace born of justice. “There exists a very close connection between the justice of the individual and the peace of everyone.” [190] Commenting on the psalm verse “justice and peace have embraced” ( Ps 84:11), Saint Augustine wrote: “There is no one who shuns the desire for peace, yet not everyone is willing to practice justice… But perform the works of justice, keeping in mind that justice and peace have embraced; they are not at odds with one another. Why do you set yourself against justice? Here, for example, is justice telling you not to steal, but you pay no heed; not to commit adultery, and you turn a deaf ear; not to do to others what you would not want done to yourself; not to say about your neighbor the things you would not want said about yourself… Do you therefore wish to attain peace? Then practice justice!” [191] Let us never grow weary of seeking justice!
blocks.zh: 我們每一個人，在各個層面上，都可以為奠定和平的基礎——即正義——作出貢獻。我們所尋求的，不僅是任何一種和平——例如不惜一切代價消除衝突——而是那源於正義的真正和平。「個人的正義與眾人的和平之間，存在著非常密切的聯繫。」[190] 聖奧斯定在詮釋聖詠詩句「正義與和平彼此相擁」（詠 84:11）時寫道：「沒有人會迴避對和平的渴望，然而並非每一個人都願意實踐正義……但你要踐行正義的事工，並緊記正義與和平彼此相擁；它們並非彼此對立。你為何要與正義作對呢？比方說，正義在此告訴你不要偷盜，你卻置若罔聞；不要犯姦淫，你卻充耳不聞；不要對別人做你不願別人對你做的事；不要談論你的近人那些你不願別人談論你的事……那麼，你渴望獲得和平嗎？那就實踐正義吧！」[191] 讓我們永不言倦地去追求正義！
blocks.html: 215. All of us, at every level, can contribute to building the foundation of peace, which is justice. We do not merely seek any kind of peace — such as an absence of conflict at any cost — but instead, the true peace born of justice. “There exists a very close connection between the justice of the individual and the peace of everyone.” [190] Commenting on the psalm verse “justice and peace have embraced” ( Ps 84:11), Saint Augustine wrote: “There is no one who shuns the desire for peace, yet not everyone is willing to practice justice… But perform the works of justice, keeping in mind that justice and peace have embraced; they are not at odds with one another. Why do you set yourself against justice? Here, for example, is justice telling you not to steal, but you pay no heed; not to commit adultery, and you turn a deaf ear; not to do to others what you would not want done to yourself; not to say about your neighbor the things you would not want said about yourself… Do you therefore wish to attain peace? Then practice justice!” [191] Let us never grow weary of seeking justice!

blocks.en: Adopting the perspective of victims
blocks.zh: 採納受害者的視角
blocks.html: Adopting the perspective of victims

blocks.en: There are times when, in order to remain human, we must set aside our reservations and take a stand. In some conflicts, it is unjust to remain neutral, nor is it enough merely to claim that we are not complicit. [192] When we witness the bombing of civilians, attacks on hospitals, schools or vital infrastructure, and violence that affects children, we are confronted with scandals that wound humanity itself. For this reason, we cannot limit ourselves to the level of abstract analysis. Pope Francis encouraged us to “touch the wounded flesh” [193] of those who suffer, look at their faces, listen to their stories and acknowledge their wounds. Painful events require both history and memory, the former to recount the facts, the latter to bear witness to lived experiences.
blocks.zh: 有些時候，為了保持人性，我們必須放下顧慮，表明立場。在某些衝突中，保持中立是不公義的，僅僅聲稱自己並非同謀，亦是不足夠的。[192] 當我們目睹平民遭受轟炸、醫院、學校或重要基建遭到襲擊，以及影響兒童的暴力時，我們所面對的是傷害人性本身的惡表。為此，我們不能只停留在抽象分析的層面。教宗方濟各鼓勵我們去「觸摸」受苦者「受傷的血肉」[193]，正視他們的面容，聆聽他們的故事，並承認他們的創傷。痛苦的事件既需要歷史，也需要記憶——前者用以敘述事實，後者則為活生生的經驗作見證。
blocks.html: 216. There are times when, in order to remain human, we must set aside our reservations and take a stand. In some conflicts, it is unjust to remain neutral, nor is it enough merely to claim that we are not complicit. [192] When we witness the bombing of civilians, attacks on hospitals, schools or vital infrastructure, and violence that affects children, we are confronted with scandals that wound humanity itself. For this reason, we cannot limit ourselves to the level of abstract analysis. Pope Francis encouraged us to “touch the wounded flesh” [193] of those who suffer, look at their faces, listen to their stories and acknowledge their wounds. Painful events require both history and memory, the former to recount the facts, the latter to bear witness to lived experiences.

blocks.en: Giving space to the perspectives and voices of victims through communication and education helps us to become aware of the abyss of evil inherent in war, and generally in all forms of violence. It helps us to reject the normalization of conflict; not to turn away when human dignity is violated; and to restore to victims the dignity of being recognized and heard. [194] Paying attention to these voices strengthens the conviction that, apart from violent minorities, humanity does not desire war. In a particular way, the Church can be a place of living memory for victims. As Saint Paul VI recalled, the Church feels she must make her own both the voice of those who died in past wars and the voice of the living who still bear wounds today, so that their cries may become an appeal for peace and harmony and not a prelude to new conflicts. [195]
blocks.zh: 透過傳播與教育為受害者的觀點和聲音留出空間，有助於我們意識到戰爭中——以及普遍存在於一切形式的暴力中——所隱含的邪惡深淵。這有助於我們拒絕將衝突「正常化」；在人性尊嚴遭受踐踏時不予轉面；並使受害者重獲被承認和被傾聽的尊嚴。[194] 關注這些聲音，會使我們更加堅信：除了一小撮暴力分子之外，人類並不渴望戰爭。教會尤其可以成為受害者的活的記憶之所。正如聖保祿六世所提醒的，教會意識到自己必須同時為那些在過往戰爭中死去的人發聲，也為今天仍背負著創傷的活人發聲，好使他們的呼喊能成為對和平與和諧的呼籲，而不是新衝突的序曲。[195]
blocks.html: 217. Giving space to the perspectives and voices of victims through communication and education helps us to become aware of the abyss of evil inherent in war, and generally in all forms of violence. It helps us to reject the normalization of conflict; not to turn away when human dignity is violated; and to restore to victims the dignity of being recognized and heard. [194] Paying attention to these voices strengthens the conviction that, apart from violent minorities, humanity does not desire war. In a particular way, the Church can be a place of living memory for victims. As Saint Paul VI recalled, the Church feels she must make her own both the voice of those who died in past wars and the voice of the living who still bear wounds today, so that their cries may become an appeal for peace and harmony and not a prelude to new conflicts. [195]

blocks.en: Cultivating a healthy realism
blocks.zh: 培育健康的現實主義
blocks.html: Cultivating a healthy realism

blocks.en: We are in need of a healthy realism that avoids both political idealism and cynicism. There is a kind of idealism that, in order to preserve its own worldview, tends to choose facts selectively, distorting and renaming them. Its proponents eventually, inhabit a reality constructed to fit their own convictions. Conversely, there is also a debased form of realism that confuses observation with resignation, arguing that since force prevails, it will always prevail. Authentic realism does not give up on changing the world; indeed, it starts by clearly identifying interests, fears, constraints and power dynamics, precisely in order to determine what can be achieved, and the measures needed to achieve it. It does not reduce politics to morality; neither does it surrender to violence. Instead, it seeks viable paths for making peace more than a mere word, through credible institutions, verifiable guarantees, patient negotiations, conflict prevention and the protection of civilians.
blocks.zh: 我們需要一種健全的現實主義，既擺脫政治理想主義，也擺脫犬儒主義。有一種理想主義，為了維護其自身世界觀，往往選擇性地揀取事實，加以扭曲和重新命名。其倡導者最終會棲身於一個為符合其自身信念而建構出來的現實之中。反之，也有一種墮落形式的現實主義，將觀察與認命混為一談，主張既然武力佔了上風，武力就永遠會佔上風。真正的現實主義不會放棄改變世界；事實上，它恰恰是從清晰辨明各種利益、恐懼、制約和權力動態開始，為要確定何事能夠實現，以及實現該事所需的措施。它既不將政治化約為道德，也不向暴力屈服。取而代之的是，它尋求可行的途徑，透過可信的機制、可驗證的保障、耐心的談判、衝突預防以及保護平民，使和平不僅僅是一個詞語。
blocks.html: 218. We are in need of a healthy realism that avoids both political idealism and cynicism. There is a kind of idealism that, in order to preserve its own worldview, tends to choose facts selectively, distorting and renaming them. Its proponents eventually, inhabit a reality constructed to fit their own convictions. Conversely, there is also a debased form of realism that confuses observation with resignation, arguing that since force prevails, it will always prevail. Authentic realism does not give up on changing the world; indeed, it starts by clearly identifying interests, fears, constraints and power dynamics, precisely in order to determine what can be achieved, and the measures needed to achieve it. It does not reduce politics to morality; neither does it surrender to violence. Instead, it seeks viable paths for making peace more than a mere word, through credible institutions, verifiable guarantees, patient negotiations, conflict prevention and the protection of civilians.

blocks.en: Reviving dialogue
blocks.zh: 重振對話
blocks.html: Reviving dialogue

blocks.en: In order to build the civilization of love, we must engage in dialogue, for this is the primary means of coexistence between people and nations, and it is the alternative to open conflict. On the eve of the Second World War, Pius XII affirmed that nothing is lost with peace, whereas with war everything can be lost. He insisted that people must return to speaking with one another, because a sincere and persevering dialogue always opens up the possibility of an honorable solution. [196]
blocks.zh: 為了建設愛的文明，我們必須展開對話，因為這是人與人、民族與民族之間共存的首要途徑，也是公開衝突的替代方案。在第二次世界大戰前夕，教宗庇護十二世申明，和平不會失去任何東西，而戰爭卻可能失去一切。他堅持人們必須重新彼此交談，因為真誠而持之以恆的對話，總能開啟達成光榮解決方案的可能性。[196]
blocks.html: 219. In order to build the civilization of love, we must engage in dialogue, for this is the primary means of coexistence between people and nations, and it is the alternative to open conflict. On the eve of the Second World War, Pius XII affirmed that nothing is lost with peace, whereas with war everything can be lost. He insisted that people must return to speaking with one another, because a sincere and persevering dialogue always opens up the possibility of an honorable solution. [196]

blocks.en: Indeed, dialogue is an ordinary part of human life and does not only concern relations between States. It involves acquiring an attitude that seeks to forge bonds of fraternity built on listening, an open demeanor, making time for each other and even wasting time together. For if we experience authentic encounters with others, with those who are different, strangers and migrants, it becomes much more difficult even to imagine war.
blocks.zh: 事實上，對話是人類日常生活中平常的一部分，不單只涉及國與國之間的關係。對話牽涉培養一種態度，這種態度尋求在聆聽、開放的舉止、為彼此騰出時間，甚至是一起「浪費」時間的基礎上，締造兄弟情誼的聯繫。因為如果我們與他人、與那些不同的人、陌生人和移民，經驗到真誠的相遇，那麼即使是想像戰爭也會變得困難得多。
blocks.html: 220. Indeed, dialogue is an ordinary part of human life and does not only concern relations between States. It involves acquiring an attitude that seeks to forge bonds of fraternity built on listening, an open demeanor, making time for each other and even wasting time together. For if we experience authentic encounters with others, with those who are different, strangers and migrants, it becomes much more difficult even to imagine war.

blocks.en: At the political level, there is an urgent need to shift from the “culture of power” to a genuine “culture of negotiation,” in which dialogue and diplomacy become the standard means of resolving conflicts. Giorgio La Pira expressed the hope that “the method of war be replaced by the method of peace: the method of negotiation, of encounter, of convergence, that is, the authentically human method!” [197] The awareness that all peoples share a common future demands that the “culture of negotiation” become an increasingly shared political and cultural commitment, capable of gradually leading humanity away from the cycle of violence.
blocks.zh: 在政治層面，迫切需要從「權力文化」轉向真正的「協商文化」，使對話和外交成為解決衝突的常規方式。喬治．拉皮拉曾表達這樣的希望：「願戰爭的方法被和平的方法所取代：即協商、相遇、趨同的方法，也就是真正合乎人性的方法！」[197] 意識到所有民族共享一個共同的未來，這要求「協商文化」成為日益普遍的政治和文化承諾，能夠逐步引領人類擺脫暴力的循環。
blocks.html: 221. At the political level, there is an urgent need to shift from the “culture of power” to a genuine “culture of negotiation,” in which dialogue and diplomacy become the standard means of resolving conflicts. Giorgio La Pira expressed the hope that “the method of war be replaced by the method of peace: the method of negotiation, of encounter, of convergence, that is, the authentically human method!” [197] The awareness that all peoples share a common future demands that the “culture of negotiation” become an increasingly shared political and cultural commitment, capable of gradually leading humanity away from the cycle of violence.

blocks.en: To those who have the honor and responsibility of governing, I would like to repeat the words that I spoke at the start of my Pontificate: “The peoples of our world desire peace, and to their leaders I appeal with all my heart: Let us meet, let us talk, let us negotiate! War is never inevitable. Weapons can and must be silenced, for they do not resolve problems but only increase them. Those who make history are the peacemakers, not those who sow seeds of suffering. Our neighbors are not first our enemies, but our fellow human beings; not criminals to be hated, but other men and women with whom we can speak. Let us reject the Manichean notions so typical of that mindset of violence that divides the world into those who are good and those who are evil.” [198]
blocks.zh: 對那些享有治理之榮譽與責任的人，我願重複我在就任教宗之初所說的話：「我們世界的人民渴望和平，我全心向他們的領袖呼籲：讓我們相會，讓我們交談，讓我們談判！戰爭絕非無可避免。武器能夠也必須緘默，因為它們不解決問題，只會加增問題。締造歷史的是和平的締造者，而非播種苦難種子的人。我們的鄰人首先不是我們的敵人，而是我們的同類；不是該被仇恨的罪犯，而是我們能夠與之交談的男男女女。讓我們摒棄那種將世界劃分為善者與惡者的摩尼教式觀念──那是暴力思維的典型特徵。」[198]
blocks.html: 222. To those who have the honor and responsibility of governing, I would like to repeat the words that I spoke at the start of my Pontificate: “The peoples of our world desire peace, and to their leaders I appeal with all my heart:  Let us meet, let us talk, let us negotiate! War is never inevitable. Weapons can and must be silenced, for they do not resolve problems but only increase them. Those who make history are the peacemakers, not those who sow seeds of suffering. Our neighbors are not first our enemies, but our fellow human beings; not criminals to be hated, but other men and women with whom we can speak. Let us reject the Manichean notions so typical of that mindset of violence that divides the world into those who are good and those who are evil.” [198]

blocks.en: In rejecting the mindset of violence, interreligious dialogue plays a decisive role, because at the heart of the great spiritual paths lies a message of peace. [199] Whereas those who use the name of God to legitimize terrorism, violence or war betray his true nature, for to fight in the name of religion means attacking religion itself. [200] The “spirit of Assisi,” evoked by Saint John Paul II and carried forward by Pope Francis — for example, through his dialogue with the Grand Imam of Al-Azhar — shows that believers can draw upon the most authentic sources of their particular spiritual traditions, where there is no room for “sanctified hatred.”
blocks.zh: 在摒棄暴力的思維模式方面，宗教交談扮演著決定性的角色，因為在各大靈修傳統的核心，都存在著和平的訊息。[199] 那些利用天主的名義來將恐怖主義、暴力或戰爭合法化的人，正是背棄了祂的真實本性，因為以宗教的名義作戰，意味著攻擊宗教本身。[200] 聖若望保祿二世所提及、並由教宗方濟各——例如透過他與阿茲哈爾大伊瑪目的交談——所延續的「亞西西精神」，表明信眾能夠從各自靈修傳統最本真的泉源中汲取力量；在這些泉源中，絕無「神聖化的仇恨」的立足之地。
blocks.html: 223. In rejecting the mindset of violence, interreligious dialogue plays a decisive role, because at the heart of the great spiritual paths lies a message of peace. [199] Whereas those who use the name of God to legitimize terrorism, violence or war betray his true nature, for to fight in the name of religion means attacking religion itself. [200] The “spirit of Assisi,” evoked by Saint John Paul II and carried forward by Pope Francis — for example, through his dialogue with the Grand Imam of Al-Azhar — shows that believers can draw upon the most authentic sources of their particular spiritual traditions, where there is no room for “sanctified hatred.”

blocks.en: The necessity of diplomacy and multilateralism
blocks.zh: 外交與多邊主義的必要性
blocks.html: The necessity of diplomacy and multilateralism

blocks.en: In international relations, dialogue is an irreplaceable diplomatic tool for preventing conflicts and rebuilding bonds of trust. Faced with the impulsive broadcasts, aggressive rhetoric and power politics that characterize our time, “the vocation of diplomacy is to foster dialogue with all parties, including those interlocutors considered less ‘convenient’ or not considered legitimized to negotiate.” [201] Therefore, every ounce of humility and patience should be employed in order to nurture even the faintest signs of goodwill among parties in conflict, so as to advance the process of peace.
blocks.zh: 在國際關係中，對話是預防衝突和重建信任紐帶的不可替代的外交工具。面對我們這個時代特有的衝動廣播、咄咄逼人的言辭和強權政治，「外交的使命在於促進與各方的對話，包括那些被認為不太『方便』或不被視為具有談判合法性的對話者在內。」[201] 因此，應當懷著全然的謙遜和耐心，去培養衝突各方之間哪怕是最微弱的善意跡象，從而推動和平的進程。
blocks.html: 224.  In international relations, dialogue is an irreplaceable diplomatic tool for preventing conflicts and rebuilding bonds of trust. Faced with the impulsive broadcasts, aggressive rhetoric and power politics that characterize our time, “the vocation of diplomacy is to foster dialogue with all parties, including those interlocutors considered less ‘convenient’ or not considered legitimized to negotiate.” [201] Therefore, every ounce of humility and patience should be employed in order to nurture even the faintest signs of goodwill among parties in conflict, so as to advance the process of peace.

blocks.en: Cyberspace too has become a battleground. Cyberattacks, data manipulation and campaigns of influence, orchestrated with the help of AI, can destabilize entire countries even before open armed conflict erupts. Moreover, in this area, the attribution of responsibility is often uncertain. When it is unclear who carried out an attack, the risk of disproportionate reaction, miscalculation and escalation increases. For this reason, diplomacy must be capable of operating effectively in this new environment, negotiating shared regulations on the use of digital technologies, in order to protect civilians and the most vulnerable from “invisible” yet real forms of violence.
blocks.zh: 網絡空間也已成為戰場。借助人工智能策劃的網絡攻擊、數據操控和影響力宣傳，甚至在公開武裝衝突爆發之前，就能動搖整個國家。此外，在這領域內，責任的歸屬往往難以確定。當無法清楚識別攻擊的發動者時，反應失當、誤判和局勢升級的風險便會增加。為此，外交工作必須有能力在這個新環境中有效運作，就數碼技術的使用協商共同規範，以保護平民和最弱勢群體免受那些「看不見」卻真實存在的暴力形式之害。
blocks.html: 225. Cyberspace too has become a battleground. Cyberattacks, data manipulation and campaigns of influence, orchestrated with the help of AI, can destabilize entire countries even before open armed conflict erupts. Moreover, in this area, the attribution of responsibility is often uncertain. When it is unclear who carried out an attack, the risk of disproportionate reaction, miscalculation and escalation increases. For this reason, diplomacy must be capable of operating effectively in this new environment, negotiating shared regulations on the use of digital technologies, in order to protect civilians and the most vulnerable from “invisible” yet real forms of violence.

blocks.en: International organizations, particularly the United Nations, are essential instruments for promoting a civilization of love, for they can foster dialogue among nations and promote the peaceful resolution of conflicts, the integral development of peoples, the protection of the most vulnerable, disarmament and the care of creation. Through such efforts, the international community can work to reduce inequalities, defend the rights of refugees and minorities, reallocate resources from military spending to human development and protect our common home. The Holy See supports and accompanies these endeavors, while also recognizing that the current weaknesses of the UN and the international political system reveal the need for profound reforms. This is not simply a question of technical adjustments, for the crisis of convictions and values that also concerns the ethical foundations of nations makes it more difficult to direct multilateralism toward the true common good. [202]
blocks.zh: 國際組織，尤其是聯合國，是促進愛的文明不可或缺的工具，因為它們能夠促進各國之間的對話，推動衝突的和平解決、各民族的整體發展、對最弱小者的保護、裁軍以及對受造界的照料。透過這些努力，國際社會能夠致力於減少不平等、捍衛難民和少數群體的權利、將軍事開支的資源重新分配用於人類發展，並保護我們的共同家園。聖座支持並陪伴這些努力，同時也認識到聯合國和國際政治體系當前的弱點揭示了進行深刻改革的必要性。這不僅僅是技術調整的問題，因為關乎各國倫理基礎的信念與價值觀危機，使多邊主義更難朝向真正的公益邁進。[202]
blocks.html: 226. International organizations, particularly the United Nations, are essential instruments for promoting a civilization of love, for they can foster dialogue among nations and promote the peaceful resolution of conflicts, the integral development of peoples, the protection of the most vulnerable, disarmament and the care of creation. Through such efforts, the international community can work to reduce inequalities, defend the rights of refugees and minorities, reallocate resources from military spending to human development and protect our common home. The Holy See supports and accompanies these endeavors, while also recognizing that the current weaknesses of the UN and the international political system reveal the need for profound reforms. This is not simply a question of technical adjustments, for the crisis of convictions and values that also concerns the ethical foundations of nations makes it more difficult to direct multilateralism toward the true common good. [202]

blocks.en: In the international context, the Holy See’s diplomacy adopts the Gospel’s principle of mercy as a concrete criterion for political action. This is one of the ways in which the Holy See places itself at the service of humanity, thereby appealing to consciences in the name of charity and truth, defending the dignity of every person and speaking up on behalf of the poor, migrants and victims of war. In this way, papal diplomacy expresses the catholicity of the Church and contributes to the building of a civilization of love, where even new technologies can be oriented toward the common good.
blocks.zh: 在國際層面上，聖座的外交將福音的慈悲原則奉為政治行動的具體標準。這是聖座以這種方式服務人類的途徑之一，藉此以愛德和真理的名義觸動人們的良心，維護每一個人的尊嚴，並為窮人、移民和戰爭受害者發聲。教宗的這種外交方式，表達了教會的大公性，並為建設愛的文明作出了貢獻；在這樣的文明中，即使是新的科技，也能導向公益。
blocks.html: 227. In the international context, the Holy See’s diplomacy adopts the Gospel’s principle of mercy as a concrete criterion for political action. This is one of the ways in which the Holy See places itself at the service of humanity, thereby appealing to consciences in the name of charity and truth, defending the dignity of every person and speaking up on behalf of the poor, migrants and victims of war. In this way, papal diplomacy expresses the catholicity of the Church and contributes to the building of a civilization of love, where even new technologies can be oriented toward the common good.

blocks.en: Praying and hoping
blocks.zh: 祈禱與懷望
blocks.html: Praying and hoping

blocks.en: These avenues for exercising responsibility are sustained by prayer, and in turn nourish prayer. Indeed, for each of us, peace primarily comes “from God, God who loves us all, unconditionally.” [203] It is a gift given by Jesus to his disciples on the day of Easter: “Peace be with you! It is the peace of the risen Christ. A peace that is unarmed and disarming, humble and persevering.” [204] With these words, I greeted the Church and the world on the day of my election to the See of Peter. I wish to repeat them now, and to invite everyone to pray for this gift. Let us never tire of praying for peace and of committing ourselves to achieving it in our relationships and in society.
blocks.zh: 這些履行責任的途徑，皆由祈禱所支撐，亦反過來滋養祈禱。事實上，為我們每一個人而言，和平首先是「來自天主，那位無條件地愛我們眾人的天主」。[203] 這是耶穌在復活節當天賜予祂門徒的禮物：「願你們平安！這是復活基督的平安。一種手無寸鐵且使人放下武器的平安、謙卑而又堅韌的平安。」[204] 在我當選伯多祿宗座繼承人的當天，我曾以這些話向教會和世界致候。如今我願再次重申這些話，並邀請所有人為這份恩賜祈禱。願我們永不言倦地為和平祈禱，並致力在我們的關係中及在社會內實現和平。
blocks.html: 228. These avenues for exercising responsibility are sustained by prayer, and in turn nourish prayer. Indeed, for each of us, peace primarily comes “from God, God who loves us all, unconditionally.” [203] It is a gift given by Jesus to his disciples on the day of Easter: “Peace be with you! It is the peace of the risen Christ. A peace that is unarmed and disarming, humble and persevering.” [204] With these words, I greeted the Church and the world on the day of my election to the See of Peter. I wish to repeat them now, and to invite everyone to pray for this gift. Let us never tire of praying for peace and of committing ourselves to achieving it in our relationships and in society.

blocks.en: “Let each builder choose with care how to build” (1 Cor 3:10). With these words, Saint Paul encouraged the Christians of Corinth to preserve unity. Dear brothers and sisters, we have reflected on the world we are building, and we asked ourselves what it means to safeguard the human person in the era of artificial intelligence. At the end of this reflection, I would like to propose a sober yet demanding program of Christian life with which we can navigate this epochal change in the light of the Gospel. This avenue emerges through contemplating God’s plan, living ecclesial unity by partaking of the Eucharist, building a world centered on the common good and praying in union with the Blessed Virgin Mary.
blocks.zh: 「各人應該注意怎樣在上面建築」（格前 3:10）。聖保祿宗徒以這句話勉勵格林多的基督徒保持合一。親愛的弟兄姊妹們，我們已就我們正在建造的世界作了反省，並自問：在人工智能的時代，「守護人性」意味着甚麼。在這份反省的末了，我願提出一個樸實卻具挑戰性的基督徒生活計劃，使我們可以在福音的光照下，航行於這場時代性的轉變：這條路徑，藉默觀天主的計劃、藉領受聖體而活出教會的合一、藉建設一個以公益為中心的世界、並藉與童貞聖母瑪利亞偕同祈禱而呈現於我們眼前。
blocks.html: 229. “Let each builder choose with care how to build” (1 Cor 3:10). With these words, Saint Paul encouraged the Christians of Corinth to preserve unity. Dear brothers and sisters, we have reflected on the world we are building, and we asked ourselves what it means to safeguard the human person in the era of artificial intelligence. At the end of this reflection, I would like to propose a sober yet demanding program of Christian life with which we can navigate this epochal change in the light of the Gospel. This avenue emerges through contemplating God’s plan, living ecclesial unity by partaking of the Eucharist, building a world centered on the common good and praying in union with the Blessed Virgin Mary.

blocks.en: Our world is filled with attempts to seize control of markets and spheres of influence, often shrouded in reassuring rhetoric and seductive ideologies. Yet our hearts yearn for an approach that is wise and benevolent, akin to that which Mary praises in her Magnificat, when she proclaims that God’s mercy extends in every generation to those who fear him. [205] This plan of mercy continues to unfold throughout history today, even amid the rapid and unsettling changes brought by algorithms and global networks, and it becomes a compass in the digital era for living our lives according to the Gospel.
blocks.zh: 我們的世界，充斥着奪取市場與影響力的種種企圖，往往隱身於令人安心的修辭和迷人的意識形態之下。然而我們的心，渴求的是一種智慧而仁愛的態度——一如瑪利亞在《謝主曲》中所讚頌的：她宣告天主的仁慈於世世代代施與敬畏祂的人[205]。這仁慈的計劃，今日仍貫穿於歷史中，即使在算法和全球網絡所帶來的迅疾而不安的變動之中亦然；它在數碼時代成為我們依福音而活的指南針。
blocks.html: 230. Our world is filled with attempts to seize control of markets and spheres of influence, often shrouded in reassuring rhetoric and seductive ideologies. Yet our hearts yearn for an approach that is wise and benevolent, akin to that which Mary praises in her Magnificat, when she proclaims that God’s mercy extends in every generation to those who fear him. [205] This plan of mercy continues to unfold throughout history today, even amid the rapid and unsettling changes brought by algorithms and global networks, and it becomes a compass in the digital era for living our lives according to the Gospel.

blocks.en: At the heart of everything is the mystery of the Incarnation, the Word who became flesh and dwelt among us. The flesh of the Son, poor and vulnerable, evokes the flesh of so many brothers and sisters stripped of their dignity and reduced to silence. [206] Through the Lord’s closeness, the gift of peace enters into the world in a paradoxical way. It does so through the power to become children of God, and is awakened when we allow ourselves to be moved by the tears of the little ones, the fragility of the elderly, the silence of victims and the struggle of those who fight against the evil they do not wish to commit. [207] In this wounded yet beloved flesh, the Father shows us the true humanity of a life fulfilled through openness and communion, which leads us to desire that his will be done on earth as it is in heaven. [208]
blocks.zh: 這一切的核心，是「降生」的奧蹟——聖言成了血肉，居住在我們中間。聖子那貧窮而脆弱的肉軀，喚起許許多多被剝奪了尊嚴、被噤聲的弟兄姊妹的肉軀[206]。藉着上主的臨近，平安的恩賜以一種弔詭的方式進入世界。它藉着我們成為天主子女的權柄而到來；它在我們容讓自己被小者的眼淚、長者的脆弱、受害者的沉默、那些為抵抗自己不願犯下之惡而掙扎之人的奮鬥所觸動時，被喚醒[207]。在這受傷卻深受珍愛的血肉裏，天父向我們展示了一個藉開放與共融而成全的生命所擁有的、真正的人性，這人性引領我們渴望「願祂的旨意奉行於地，如同在天上」[208]。
blocks.html: 231. At the heart of everything is the mystery of the Incarnation, the Word who became flesh and dwelt among us. The flesh of the Son, poor and vulnerable, evokes the flesh of so many brothers and sisters stripped of their dignity and reduced to silence. [206] Through the Lord’s closeness, the gift of peace enters into the world in a paradoxical way. It does so through the power to become children of God, and is awakened when we allow ourselves to be moved by the tears of the little ones, the fragility of the elderly, the silence of victims and the struggle of those who fight against the evil they do not wish to commit. [207] In this wounded yet beloved flesh, the Father shows us the true humanity of a life fulfilled through openness and communion, which leads us to desire that his will be done on earth as it is in heaven. [208]

blocks.en: In the promises of transhumanism and some posthumanist currents of thought, which seek an enhanced and almost disembodied humanity, we recognize a yearning that is of concern to us, namely the need for a fuller life, less exposed to limitations and suffering. Yet the Incarnation opens a different pathway. On the one hand, old and new ideologies alike urge humanity to overcome limitations through technology, and to rise above others by asserting dominance. Contrary to this, the mystery of the Son of God entering into our human condition promises something quite different. The living God descends into our history in order to free us from all forms of slavery. [209] He takes upon himself our weakness and transforms it into a setting for salvation. There is no moment or human situation that is not worthy of God. “According to the teaching of our faith, we have and adore, in our mysteries, a God who is born in a manger, a God who lives and travels in Judea, a God who dies on the cross, a dead God who lies in the tomb.” [210] The future of humanity, therefore, finds its standard in the ability to welcome this divine way of drawing near, of sharing the burden of the world, of transforming relationships from within. “O wonder... man is God and this God-Man passes through all those stages, endures all those states and ennobles them, sanctifies them, deifies them in himself!” [211] What saves humanity is the divine love that descends into the most fragile point of our history and renews it from within.
blocks.zh: 在超人類主義的承諾，以及一些後人類主義的思潮——它們追求一種被增強、近乎脫離形體的人性——當中，我們也察覺到一份令我們關注的渴望，即對一種更圓滿、較少受限與痛苦的生命的需求。然而，「降生」開啟了另一條道路。一方面，新舊意識形態都在催促人類藉科技超越限度，並藉強加宰制而高過他人。與此相反，聖子進入我們人性處境的奧蹟，許下了截然不同的事。永生的天主下降到我們的歷史中，為要把我們從一切奴役中釋放出來[209]。祂將我們的軟弱承擔在自己身上，並把它轉化為救恩的場景。沒有一個時刻、沒有一種人性處境，是不配得到天主的。「按照我們信仰的訓導，我們在我們的奧蹟中擁有並朝拜：一位生於馬槽的天主、一位行走於猶太地的天主、一位死於十字架的天主、一位死了被安葬在墳墓中的天主」[210]。故此，人類的未來，其衡量便在於：能否歡迎這神性的「親近之道」——共擔世界的重擔，從內部轉化關係。「噢，何等奇妙……人就是天主，而這天主-人經歷了所有那些階段，承受了所有那些境況，並在自身內把它們高貴化、聖化、神化！」[211] 拯救人類的，是那下降到我們歷史中最脆弱之處、並由內部更新它的神性的愛。
blocks.html: 232. In the promises of transhumanism and some posthumanist currents of thought, which seek an enhanced and almost disembodied humanity, we recognize a yearning that is of concern to us, namely the need for a fuller life, less exposed to limitations and suffering. Yet the Incarnation opens a different pathway. On the one hand, old and new ideologies alike urge humanity to overcome limitations through technology, and to rise above others by asserting dominance. Contrary to this, the mystery of the Son of God entering into our human condition promises something quite different. The living God descends into our history in order to free us from all forms of slavery. [209] He takes upon himself our weakness and transforms it into a setting for salvation. There is no moment or human situation that is not worthy of God. “According to the teaching of our faith, we have and adore, in our mysteries, a God who is born in a manger, a God who lives and travels in Judea, a God who dies on the cross, a dead God who lies in the tomb.” [210] The future of humanity, therefore, finds its standard in the ability to welcome this divine way of drawing near, of sharing the burden of the world, of transforming relationships from within. “O wonder... man is God and this God-Man passes through all those stages, endures all those states and ennobles them, sanctifies them, deifies them in himself!” [211] What saves humanity is the divine love that descends into the most fragile point of our history and renews it from within.

blocks.en: For this reason, as a believer among believers, I invite everyone to contemplate, in the face of the Son of God, the grandeur of humanity that shines a light also on the era of AI. In Christ, we are called to cooperate in the work of creation, rather than be disinterested observers of technological processes that limit our freedom and responsibility. [212] The dignity inscribed in each of us by the Holy Spirit can also be seen in our capacity to reflect critically, choose and love freely, and form authentic relationships. No computational system, however sophisticated, can create a heart that gives itself, or a conscience that discerns good from evil. Even when machines excel in efficiency, a human face that asks to be gazed upon remains the center of our history. This human face is the fullness toward which history is moving. It is the mystery of “recapitulation”: the certainty that the Father has decreed to bring all things, those in heaven and those on earth, back to Christ, the one Head (cf. Eph 1:10). In this plan, nothing will be lost that is authentically human. Indeed, everything will be purified and reunited in the One, who gathers every fragment of life, every tear and every authentically human achievement, rescuing them from nothingness and delivering them, redeemed, to the Father.
blocks.zh: 為此，作為信眾中的一位信徒，我邀請所有人在天主子的容顏中默觀：那同樣照亮人工智能時代的人性壯麗。在基督內，我們被召叫去與創造的工程同工，而不是做一個漠不關心的旁觀者，眼看科技進程限制我們的自由與責任[212]。聖神鐫刻於我們每一個人身上的尊嚴，亦顯現於我們批判性反思、自由抉擇與愛、並結成真實關係的能力上。無論如何精密的計算系統，都無法造就一顆自我交付的心，或一份能分辨善惡的良心。即使機器在效率上勝過我們，那渴望被注目的「人面」，仍是我們歷史的中心。這人面便是歷史正趨向的圓滿。這就是「萬有歸總」的奧蹟：天父決意「使天上和地上的萬有，總歸於基督元首」（參弗 1:10）。在這計劃中，凡真正屬於人性的，將無一失落；相反，一切將在那位「在基督內」者內被淨化並重新合一，祂收集生命的每一片碎屑、每一滴眼淚、每一份真實人性的成就，將它們從虛無中救出，並把它們作為被救贖的果實獻給天父。
blocks.html: 233. For this reason, as a believer among believers, I invite everyone to contemplate, in the face of the Son of God, the grandeur of humanity that shines a light also on the era of AI. In Christ, we are called to cooperate in the work of creation, rather than be disinterested observers of technological processes that limit our freedom and responsibility. [212] The dignity inscribed in each of us by the Holy Spirit can also be seen in our capacity to reflect critically, choose and love freely, and form authentic relationships. No computational system, however sophisticated, can create a heart that gives itself, or a conscience that discerns good from evil. Even when machines excel in efficiency, a human face that asks to be gazed upon remains the center of our history. This human face is the fullness toward which history is moving. It is the mystery of “recapitulation”: the certainty that the Father has decreed to bring all things, those in heaven and those on earth, back to Christ, the one Head (cf. Eph 1:10). In this plan, nothing will be lost that is authentically human. Indeed, everything will be purified and reunited in the One, who gathers every fragment of life, every tear and every authentically human achievement, rescuing them from nothingness and delivering them, redeemed, to the Father.

blocks.en: One body in Christ
blocks.zh: 在基督內為一個身體
blocks.html: One body in Christ

blocks.en: The spirituality that we need is a Eucharistic spirituality, that is, a spirituality of ecclesial unity in love. The Incarnation and the Paschal Mystery reveal God entering into our human condition and transforming it through the gift of himself. This gift remains present and active in the Eucharist, in which the Lord gives himself and gathers the Church together, so that his offering becomes the principle of unity and source of new life. It is from this communion that Christian solidarity also arises, since “union with Christ is also union with all those to whom he gives himself.” [213] As Saint Augustine explained to the new Christians of his local Church, the bread and wine on the altar are the sacrament of the unity of the faithful in Christ: “What is seen is a mere physical likeness; what is grasped bears spiritual fruit. So now, if you want to understand the body of Christ, listen to the Apostle Paul speaking to the faithful: together you are the body of Christ ( 1 Cor 12:27). If you are the body and members of Christ, then it is your sacrament that is placed on the table of the Lord; it is your sacrament that you receive. You respond ‘Amen,’ and by responding in this way you assent to it. For you hear the words, ‘the Body of Christ’ and respond ‘Amen.’ Be then a member of the Body of Christ that your Amen may be true!” [214]
blocks.zh: 我們所需要的靈修，是聖體聖事的靈修——亦即在愛內的教會合一的靈修。「降生」奧蹟與逾越奧蹟，啟示了天主進入我們人性處境，並藉自我的恩賜而將其轉化。這份恩賜在聖體聖事中持續活現：上主在聖體中自我交付，並聚集教會於一，使祂的祭獻成為合一的根源和新生命的泉源。基督徒的團結，正是由這份共融湧出，因為「與基督的合一，也就是與祂所自我交付的眾人的合一」[213]。正如聖奧斯定向他自己地方教會的新信友所解釋：祭台上的餅與酒，是信眾在基督內合一的聖事：「人所看見的，僅是外在的相似；人所領受的，卻結出屬神的果實。如果你願意明白基督的身體，請聽宗徒保祿對信眾所說：『你們便是基督的身體，各自都是肢體』（格前 12:27）。如果你們是基督的身體和肢體，那麼置於主餐桌上的，便是你們的聖事；你們所領的，便是你們的聖事。你們答覆『阿們』，並藉此而同意它。因為你們聽到『基督的身體』，便答覆『阿們』。那麼，要成為基督身體的肢體，使你們的『阿們』是真實的！」[214]
blocks.html: 234. The spirituality that we need is a Eucharistic spirituality, that is, a spirituality of ecclesial unity in love. The Incarnation and the Paschal Mystery reveal God entering into our human condition and transforming it through the gift of himself. This gift remains present and active in the Eucharist, in which the Lord gives himself and gathers the Church together, so that his offering becomes the principle of unity and source of new life. It is from this communion that Christian solidarity also arises, since “union with Christ is also union with all those to whom he gives himself.” [213] As Saint Augustine explained to the new Christians of his local Church, the bread and wine on the altar are the sacrament of the unity of the faithful in Christ: “What is seen is a mere physical likeness; what is grasped bears spiritual fruit. So now, if you want to understand the body of Christ, listen to the Apostle Paul speaking to the faithful: together you are the body of Christ ( 1 Cor 12:27). If you are the body and members of Christ, then it is your sacrament that is placed on the table of the Lord; it is your sacrament that you receive. You respond ‘Amen,’ and by responding in this way you assent to it. For you hear the words, ‘the Body of Christ’ and respond ‘Amen.’ Be then a member of the Body of Christ that your Amen may be true!” [214]

blocks.en: The “Amen” that we say in the liturgy, the Body we eat and the Blood we drink shape our entire lives. The Eucharist “is an extremely personal encounter with the Lord and yet never simply an act of individual piety.” [215] In the Eucharist we find a visible manifestation of the reality that we “are the Church of Christ, his members, his body. We are brothers and sisters in him. And in Christ, though many and diverse, we are one: In Illo uno unum.” [216] The Eucharist opens us to justice and sharing, with a preferential concern for those who are burdened by poverty or marginalization. And while new economic and technological networks can generate exclusion, isolation and dependencies, the Church — nourished by the Eucharist — is called to make visible a different paradigm, one that preserves human connections, gives a voice to the invisible and ensures that processes are aimed at respecting people’s dignity.
blocks.zh: 我們在禮儀中所說的「阿們」，所吃的聖體和所飲的聖血，塑造着我們的整個生命。聖體聖事「是一次極個人的、與上主的相遇，但又從來不僅僅是一個個人虔誠的行動」[215]。在聖體聖事中，我們看見一個有形可見的事實：我們「是基督的教會、祂的肢體、祂的身體。我們在祂內是弟兄姊妹。我們在基督內，雖眾而多元，卻是一體：In Illo uno unum」[216]。聖體聖事使我們向正義和分享開放，並對那些被貧窮或邊緣化所拖累的人，懷抱優先的關懷。當新的經濟和科技網絡可能催生排斥、孤立與依賴時，那以聖體為養料的教會，被召喚去彰顯另一種典範：一個保存人與人之間連結、為無形者發聲、確保各種進程以尊重人的尊嚴為目標的典範。
blocks.html: 235. The “Amen” that we say in the liturgy, the Body we eat and the Blood we drink shape our entire lives. The Eucharist “is an extremely personal encounter with the Lord and yet never simply an act of individual piety.” [215] In the Eucharist we find a visible manifestation of the reality that we “are the Church of Christ, his members, his body. We are brothers and sisters in him. And in Christ, though many and diverse, we are one: In Illo uno unum.” [216] The Eucharist opens us to justice and sharing, with a preferential concern for those who are burdened by poverty or marginalization. And while new economic and technological networks can generate exclusion, isolation and dependencies, the Church — nourished by the Eucharist — is called to make visible a different paradigm, one that preserves human connections, gives a voice to the invisible and ensures that processes are aimed at respecting people’s dignity.

blocks.en: The construction site of our time
blocks.zh: 我們這時代的工地
blocks.html: The construction site of our time

blocks.en: The spirituality I wish to commend is that of the “wise architect” who, driven by hope for the Kingdom of God, is committed to building the world for the common good (cf. 1 Cor 3:10). As I mentioned at the beginning of this reflection, [217] the task of building in our time must place our relationship with God at its center. Our rule must be the acceptance of human limitations as a natural and positive reality, and should be characterized by shared responsibility and a language characterized by the Gospel. At the end of this reflection, the plan for a civilization of love can be seen more clearly, and the construction site appears to be already up and running, thanks especially to the many living stones solidly united to Christ the cornerstone (cf. 1 Pet 2:4-6). In this task, we are called to assume an active role, without taking refuge in spiritual sentimentality or retreating into our own little worlds. We must be faithful to the truth, invest in education, cultivate relationships and love justice and peace.
blocks.zh: 我所願推薦的靈修，是「精明建築師」的靈修：祂為天主之國的希望所驅動，致力於為公益而建設世界（參格前 3:10）。誠如我在本反省之初所提及的[217]，我們這時代的「建設」工作，必須以我們與天主的關係為中心。我們的準則必須是：接納人性的限度為一項自然而正面的事實；並以共同的責任、以一種具福音色彩的語言為特徵。在這份反省的末了，「愛的文明」的計劃顯得更為清晰；而那「工地」似乎已經開工——這特別歸功於眾多堅實連結於基督這棟梁石之上的「活石」（參伯前 2:4-6）。在這項任務中，我們被召叫承擔積極的角色，而不躲入屬靈的傷感之中，也不退到自己的小天地。我們必須對真理忠信、投資於教育、培育關係、熱愛正義與和平。
blocks.html: 236.  The spirituality I wish to commend is that of the “wise architect” who, driven by hope for the Kingdom of God, is committed to building the world for the common good (cf. 1 Cor 3:10). As I mentioned at the beginning of this reflection, [217] the task of building in our time must place our relationship with God at its center. Our rule must be the acceptance of human limitations as a natural and positive reality, and should be characterized by shared responsibility and a language characterized by the Gospel.
